    14→//go:build !skip_js_tests
    15→
    16→package server
    17→
    18→import (
    19→	"bytes"
    20→	"encoding/json"
    21→	"errors"
    22→	"fmt"
    23→	"math/big"
    24→	"strconv"

    32→
    33→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublish(t *testing.T) {
    34→	test := func(
    35→		t *testing.T,
    36→		storage StorageType,
    37→		retention RetentionPolicy,
    38→		replicas int,
    39→	) {
    40→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
    41→		defer c.shutdown()
    42→
    43→		nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
    44→		defer nc.Close()
    45→
    46→		var pubAck JSPubAckResponse
    47→
    48→		cfg := &StreamConfig{
    49→			Name:      "TEST",
    50→			Subjects:  []string{"foo.*"},
    51→			Storage:   storage,
    52→			Retention: retention,
    53→			Replicas:  replicas,
    54→		}
    55→
    56→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
    57→		require_NoError(t, err)
    58→
    59→		m := nats.NewMsg("foo.0")
    60→		m.Data = []byte("foo.0")
    61→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
    62→
    63→		// Publish with atomic publish disabled.
    64→		rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
    65→		require_NoError(t, err)
    66→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
    67→		require_NotNil(t, pubAck.Error)
    68→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishDisabledError())
    69→
    70→		// Enable atomic publish.
    71→		cfg.AllowAtomicPublish = true
    72→		_, err = jsStreamUpdate(t, nc, cfg)
    73→		require_NoError(t, err)
    74→
    75→		// Publish without batch sequence errors.
    76→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
    77→		require_NoError(t, err)
    78→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
    79→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
    80→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishMissingSeqError())
    81→
    82→		// Publish a batch, misses start.
    83→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
    84→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
    85→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
    86→		require_NoError(t, err)
    87→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
    88→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
    89→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
    90→
    91→		// Publish a "batch" which immediately commits.
    92→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
    93→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
    94→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
    95→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
    96→		require_NoError(t, err)
    97→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
    98→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
    99→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 1)
   100→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchId, "uuid")
   101→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchSize, 1)
   102→
   103→		// Reset commit.
   104→		m.Header.Del("Nats-Batch-Commit")
   105→
   106→		// Publish a "batch" which has gaps.
   107→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   108→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "3")
   109→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   110→		require_NoError(t, err)
   111→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   112→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   113→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
   114→
   115→		// Publish a batch of N messages.
   116→		m.Header.Del("Nats-Batch-Commit")
   117→		for seq, batch := uint64(1), uint64(5); seq <= batch; seq++ {
   118→			m.Subject = fmt.Sprintf("foo.%d", seq)
   119→			m.Data = []byte(m.Subject)
   120→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", strconv.FormatUint(seq, 10))
   121→			// If not commit.
   122→			if seq != batch {
   123→				require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   124→				continue
   125→			}
   126→
   127→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   128→			rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   129→			require_NoError(t, err)
   130→
   131→			pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   132→			require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   133→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 6)
   134→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchId, "uuid")
   135→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchSize, 5)
   136→		}
   137→
   138→		// Validate stream contents.
   139→		if retention != InterestPolicy {
   140→			for i := 0; i < 6; i++ {
   141→				rsm, err := js.GetMsg("TEST", uint64(i+1))
   142→				require_NoError(t, err)
   143→				subj := fmt.Sprintf("foo.%d", i)
   144→				require_Equal(t, rsm.Subject, subj)
   145→				require_Equal(t, string(rsm.Data), subj)
   146→			}
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→
   150→	for _, storage := range []StorageType{FileStorage, MemoryStorage} {
   151→		for _, retention := range []RetentionPolicy{LimitsPolicy, InterestPolicy, WorkQueuePolicy} {
   152→			for _, replicas := range []int{1, 3} {
   153→				t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/R%d", storage, retention, replicas), func(t *testing.T) {
   154→					test(t, storage, retention, replicas)
   155→				})
   156→			}
   157→		}
   158→	}
   159→}
   160→
   161→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishLimits(t *testing.T) {
   162→	streamMaxBatchInflightPerStream = 1
   163→	streamMaxBatchInflightTotal = 1
   164→	streamMaxBatchSize = 2
   165→	streamMaxBatchTimeout = 500 * time.Millisecond
   166→	defer func() {
   167→		streamMaxBatchInflightPerStream = streamDefaultMaxBatchInflightPerStream
   168→		streamMaxBatchInflightTotal = streamDefaultMaxBatchInflightTotal
   169→		streamMaxBatchSize = streamDefaultMaxBatchSize
   170→		streamMaxBatchTimeout = streamDefaultMaxBatchTimeout
   171→	}()
   172→
   173→	test := func(t *testing.T, replicas int) {
   174→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
   175→		defer c.shutdown()
   176→
   177→		nc := clientConnectToServer(t, c.randomServer())
   178→		defer nc.Close()
   179→
   180→		var pubAck JSPubAckResponse
   181→
   182→		cfg := &StreamConfig{
   183→			Name:               "FOO",
   184→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
   185→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   186→			Retention:          LimitsPolicy,
   187→			Replicas:           replicas,
   188→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
   189→		}
   190→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
   191→		require_NoError(t, err)
   192→
   193→		// For testing total server-wide maximum inflight batches.
   194→		cfg = &StreamConfig{
   195→			Name:               "BAR",
   196→			Subjects:           []string{"bar"},
   197→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   198→			Retention:          LimitsPolicy,
   199→			Replicas:           replicas,
   200→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
   201→		}
   202→		_, err = jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
   203→		require_NoError(t, err)
   204→
   205→		// A batch ID must not exceed the maximum length.
   206→		for _, length := range []int{64, 65} {
   207→			longBatchId := strings.Repeat("A", length)
   208→			m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   209→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", longBatchId)
   210→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   211→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   212→			rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   213→			require_NoError(t, err)
   214→			pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   215→			require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   216→			if length <= 64 {
   217→				require_True(t, pubAck.Error == nil)
   218→			} else {
   219→				require_NotNil(t, pubAck.Error)
   220→				require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishInvalidBatchIDError())
   221→			}
   222→		}
   223→
   224→		// One batch is inflight.
   225→		m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   226→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   227→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   228→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   229→
   230→		// Another batch moves over the threshold and batch is denied.
   231→		m = nats.NewMsg("foo")
   232→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "exceeds_threshold")
   233→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   234→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   235→		rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   236→		require_NoError(t, err)
   237→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   238→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   239→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
   240→
   241→		// Another batch on a different stream moves over the server-wide threshold and batch is denied.
   242→		m = nats.NewMsg("bar")
   243→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "bar")
   244→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   245→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   246→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   247→		require_NoError(t, err)
   248→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   249→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   250→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
   251→
   252→		// The first batch should now time out.
   253→		sl := c.streamLeader(globalAccountName, "FOO")
   254→		mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("FOO")
   255→		require_NoError(t, err)
   256→		checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
   257→			mset.mu.RLock()
   258→			batches := mset.batches
   259→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
   260→			if batches == nil {
   261→				return errors.New("batches not found")
   262→			}
   263→			batches.mu.Lock()
   264→			groups := len(batches.group)
   265→			batches.mu.Unlock()
   266→			if groups != 0 {
   267→				return fmt.Errorf("expected 0 groups, got %d", groups)
   268→			}
   269→			return nil
   270→		})
   271→
   272→		// Publishing to the batch should also error since it timed out.
   273→		m = nats.NewMsg("foo")
   274→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   275→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
   276→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   277→		rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   278→		require_NoError(t, err)
   279→		pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   280→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   281→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
   282→
   283→		// Batch should be rejected if going over the max batch size.
   284→		for _, size := range []int{streamMaxBatchSize, streamMaxBatchSize + 1} {
   285→			for i := range size {
   286→				seq := i + 1
   287→				m = nats.NewMsg("foo")
   288→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "exceeds_threshold")
   289→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", strconv.Itoa(seq))
   290→				commit := seq == size
   291→				if !commit {
   292→					require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   293→					continue
   294→				}
   295→
   296→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   297→				rmsg, err = nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   298→				require_NoError(t, err)
   299→				pubAck = JSPubAckResponse{}
   300→				require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   301→				if size <= streamMaxBatchSize {
   302→					require_True(t, pubAck.Error == nil)
   303→					require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchSize, size)
   304→				} else {
   305→					require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishIncompleteBatchError())
   306→				}
   307→			}
   308→		}
   309→	}
   310→
   311→	t.Run("R1", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 1) })
   312→	t.Run("R3", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 3) })
   313→}
   314→
   315→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishDedupeNotAllowed(t *testing.T) {
   316→	test := func(
   317→		t *testing.T,
   318→		nc *nats.Conn,
   319→		js nats.JetStreamContext,
   320→		storage StorageType,
   321→		retention RetentionPolicy,
   322→		replicas int,
   323→	) {
   324→		cfg := &StreamConfig{
   325→			Name:               "TEST",
   326→			Retention:          retention,
   327→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
   328→			Replicas:           3,
   329→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   330→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
   331→		}
   332→
   333→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
   334→		require_NoError(t, err)
   335→
   336→		m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   337→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   338→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   339→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Msg-Id", "msgId1")
   340→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   341→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
   342→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Msg-Id", "pre-existing")
   343→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   344→
   345→		var pubAck JSPubAckResponse
   346→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "3")
   347→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Msg-Id", "msgId2")
   348→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   349→		rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   350→		require_NoError(t, err)
   351→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   352→		require_NotNil(t, pubAck.Error)
   353→		require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSAtomicPublishUnsupportedHeaderBatchError("Nats-Msg-Id"))
   354→	}
   355→
   356→	for _, storage := range []StorageType{FileStorage, MemoryStorage} {
   357→		for _, retention := range []RetentionPolicy{LimitsPolicy, InterestPolicy, WorkQueuePolicy} {
   358→			for _, replicas := range []int{1, 3} {
   359→				t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/R%d", storage, retention, replicas), func(t *testing.T) {
   360→					c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
   361→					defer c.shutdown()
   362→
   363→					nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
   364→					defer nc.Close()
   365→
   366→					test(t, nc, js, storage, retention, replicas)
   367→				})
   368→			}
   369→		}
   370→	}
   371→}
   372→
   373→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishSourceAndMirror(t *testing.T) {
   374→	test := func(t *testing.T, replicas int) {
   375→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
   376→		defer c.shutdown()
   377→
   378→		nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
   379→		defer nc.Close()
   380→
   381→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
   382→			Name:               "TEST",
   383→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
   384→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   385→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
   386→			Replicas:           replicas,
   387→		})
   388→		require_NoError(t, err)
   389→
   390→		for seq := uint64(1); seq <= 3; seq++ {
   391→			m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   392→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   393→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", strconv.FormatUint(seq, 10))
   394→			commit := seq == 3
   395→			if !commit {
   396→				require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   397→				continue
   398→			}
   399→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   400→
   401→			rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
   402→			require_NoError(t, err)
   403→			var pubAck JSPubAckResponse
   404→			require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
   405→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 3)
   406→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchId, "uuid")
   407→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchSize, 3)
   408→		}
   409→
   410→		require_NoError(t, js.DeleteMsg("TEST", 2))
   411→		checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
   412→			return checkState(t, c, globalAccountName, "TEST")
   413→		})
   414→
   415→		_, err = js.AddStream(&nats.StreamConfig{
   416→			Name:     "M",
   417→			Mirror:   &nats.StreamSource{Name: "TEST"},
   418→			Replicas: replicas,
   419→		})
   420→		require_NoError(t, err)
   421→
   422→		_, err = js.AddStream(&nats.StreamConfig{
   423→			Name:     "S",
   424→			Sources:  []*nats.StreamSource{{Name: "TEST"}},
   425→			Replicas: replicas,
   426→		})
   427→		require_NoError(t, err)
   428→
   429→		checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
   430→			for _, name := range []string{"M", "S"} {
   431→				if si, err := js.StreamInfo(name); err != nil {
   432→					return err
   433→				} else if si.State.Msgs != 2 {
   434→					return fmt.Errorf("expected 2 messages for stream %q, got %d", name, si.State.Msgs)
   435→				}
   436→			}
   437→			return nil
   438→		})
   439→
   440→		// Ensure the batching headers were removed when ingested into the source/mirror.
   441→		rsm, err := js.GetMsg("M", 1)
   442→		require_NoError(t, err)
   443→		require_Len(t, len(rsm.Header), 0)
   444→
   445→		rsm, err = js.GetMsg("M", 3)
   446→		require_NoError(t, err)
   447→		require_Len(t, len(rsm.Header), 0)
   448→
   449→		rsm, err = js.GetMsg("S", 1)
   450→		require_NoError(t, err)
   451→		require_Len(t, len(rsm.Header), 1)
   452→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get(JSStreamSource), "TEST 1 > > foo")
   453→
   454→		rsm, err = js.GetMsg("S", 2)
   455→		require_NoError(t, err)
   456→		require_Len(t, len(rsm.Header), 1)
   457→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get(JSStreamSource), "TEST 3 > > foo")
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	t.Run("R1", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 1) })
   461→	t.Run("R3", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 3) })
   462→}
   463→
   464→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishCleanup(t *testing.T) {
   465→	const (
   466→		Disable = iota
   467→		StepDown
   468→		Delete
   469→		Commit
   470→	)
   471→
   472→	test := func(t *testing.T, mode int) {
   473→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
   474→		defer c.shutdown()
   475→
   476→		nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
   477→		defer nc.Close()
   478→
   479→		cfg := &StreamConfig{
   480→			Name:               "TEST",
   481→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
   482→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   483→			AllowAtomicPublish: false,
   484→			Replicas:           3,
   485→		}
   486→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
   487→		require_NoError(t, err)
   488→
   489→		sl := c.streamLeader(globalAccountName, "TEST")
   490→		mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
   491→		require_NoError(t, err)
   492→		mset.mu.RLock()
   493→		batches := mset.batches
   494→		batch := mset.batchApply
   495→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
   496→		require_True(t, batches == nil)
   497→		require_True(t, batch == nil)
   498→
   499→		// Enabling doesn't need to populate the batching state.
   500→		cfg.AllowAtomicPublish = true
   501→		_, err = jsStreamUpdate(t, nc, cfg)
   502→		require_NoError(t, err)
   503→		mset.mu.RLock()
   504→		batches = mset.batches
   505→		batch = mset.batchApply
   506→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
   507→		require_True(t, batches == nil)
   508→		require_True(t, batch == nil)
   509→
   510→		// Publish a partial batch that needs to be cleaned up.
   511→		m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   512→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   513→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   514→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
   515→
   516→		// Publish another batch that commits, state should be cleaned up by default.
   517→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "commit")
   518→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   519→		_, err = js.PublishMsg(m)
   520→		require_NoError(t, err)
   521→
   522→		mset.mu.RLock()
   523→		batches = mset.batches
   524→		batch = mset.batchApply
   525→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
   526→		require_NotNil(t, batches)
   527→		require_NotNil(t, batch)
   528→		batches.mu.Lock()
   529→		groups := len(batches.group)
   530→		b := batches.group["uuid"]
   531→		batches.mu.Unlock()
   532→		require_Len(t, groups, 1)
   533→		require_NotNil(t, b)
   534→		store := b.store
   535→		require_Equal(t, store.State().Msgs, 1)
   536→		clfs := mset.getCLFS()
   537→
   538→		// Should fully clean up the in-progress batch.
   539→		switch mode {
   540→		case Disable:
   541→			cfg.AllowAtomicPublish = false
   542→			_, err = jsStreamUpdate(t, nc, cfg)
   543→			require_NoError(t, err)
   544→		case StepDown:
   545→			require_NoError(t, sl.JetStreamStepdownStream(globalAccountName, "TEST"))
   546→		case Delete:
   547→			require_NoError(t, mset.delete())
   548→		case Commit:
   549→			m = nats.NewMsg("foo")
   550→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   551→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
   552→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   553→			_, err = js.PublishMsg(m)
   554→			require_NoError(t, err)
   555→		}
   556→		checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
   557→			mset.mu.RLock()
   558→			batches = mset.batches
   559→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
   560→			if batches != nil {
   561→				if mode != Commit {
   562→					return fmt.Errorf("expected no batches")
   563→				}
   564→				batches.mu.Lock()
   565→				groups = len(batches.group)
   566→				batches.mu.Unlock()
   567→				if groups > 0 {
   568→					return fmt.Errorf("expected 0 groups, got %d", groups)
   569→				}
   570→			}
   571→			if msgs := store.State().Msgs; msgs != 0 {
   572→				return fmt.Errorf("expected 0 messages, got %d", msgs)
   573→			}
   574→			return nil
   575→		})
   576→		// Should clean up the batch apply state.
   577→		if mode == Disable || mode == Delete {
   578→			mset.mu.RLock()
   579→			batch = mset.batchApply
   580→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
   581→			nclfs := mset.getCLFS()
   582→			require_True(t, batch == nil)
   583→			require_Equal(t, clfs, nclfs)
   584→		}
   585→	}
   586→
   587→	t.Run("Disable", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, Disable) })
   588→	t.Run("StepDown", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, StepDown) })
   589→	t.Run("Delete", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, Delete) })
   590→	t.Run("Commit", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, Commit) })
   591→}
   592→
   593→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishConfigOpts(t *testing.T) {
   594→	// Defaults.
   595→	require_Equal(t, streamMaxBatchInflightPerStream, 50)
   596→	require_Equal(t, streamMaxBatchInflightTotal, 1000)
   597→	require_Equal(t, streamMaxBatchSize, 1000)
   598→	require_Equal(t, streamMaxBatchTimeout, 10*time.Second)
   599→
   600→	batchingConf := `
   601→	listen: 127.0.0.1:-1
   602→	jetstream {
   603→		store_dir: %q
   604→		limits {
   605→			batch {
   606→				max_inflight_per_stream: %d
   607→				max_inflight_total: %d
   608→				max_msgs: %d
   609→				timeout: %s
   610→			}
   611→		}
   612→	}`
   613→
   614→	cf := createConfFile(t, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(batchingConf, t.TempDir(), 10, 20, 100, "5s")))
   615→	s, _ := RunServerWithConfig(cf)
   616→	defer s.Shutdown()
   617→
   618→	opts := s.getOpts()
   619→	require_Equal(t, opts.JetStreamLimits.MaxBatchInflightPerStream, 10)
   620→	require_Equal(t, opts.JetStreamLimits.MaxBatchInflightTotal, 20)
   621→	require_Equal(t, opts.JetStreamLimits.MaxBatchSize, 100)
   622→	require_Equal(t, opts.JetStreamLimits.MaxBatchTimeout, 5*time.Second)
   623→
   624→	// Reloading is not supported, that would potentially mean dropping random batches when lowering limits.
   625→	changeCurrentConfigContentWithNewContent(t, cf, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(batchingConf, t.TempDir(), 20, 40, 200, "10s")))
   626→	require_Error(t, s.Reload(), fmt.Errorf("config reload not supported for JetStreamLimits"))
   627→}

   629→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishDenyHeaders(t *testing.T) {
   630→	test := func(t *testing.T, replicas int) {
   631→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
   632→		defer c.shutdown()
   633→
   634→		nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
   635→		defer nc.Close()
   636→
   637→		cfg := &StreamConfig{
   638→			Name:               "TEST",
   639→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
   640→			Storage:            FileStorage,
   641→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
   642→			Replicas:           replicas,
   643→		}
   644→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
   645→		require_NoError(t, err)
   646→
   647→		// We might support these headers later on, but for now error.
   648→		for key, value := range map[string]string{
   649→			"Nats-Msg-Id":               "msgId",
   650→			"Nats-Expected-Last-Msg-Id": "msgId",
   651→		} {
   652→			t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
   653→				m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
   654→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
   655→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
   656→				m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
   657→				m.Header.Set(key, value)
   658→				_, err = js.PublishMsg(m)
   659→				require_Error(t, err, NewJSAtomicPublishUnsupportedHeaderBatchError(key))
   660→			})
   661→		}
   662→	}
   663→
   664→	t.Run("R1", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 1) })
   665→	t.Run("R3", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, 3) })
   666→}

   668→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishStageAndCommit(t *testing.T) {
   669→	type BatchItem struct {
   670→		subject string
   671→		header  nats.Header
   672→		msg     []byte
   673→		err     error
   674→	}
   675→
   676→	type BatchTest struct {
   677→		title             string

   679→		denyPurge         bool
   680→		allowTTL          bool
   681→		allowMsgCounter   bool
   682→		allowMsgSchedules bool
   683→		discardNew        bool
   684→		discardNewPerSubj bool
   685→		init              func(mset *stream)
   686→		batch             []BatchItem
   687→		validate          func(mset *stream, commit bool)
   688→	}
   689→
   690→	tests := []BatchTest{
   691→		{
   692→			title: "dedupe-distinct",
   693→			batch: []BatchItem{
   694→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMsgId: {"foo"}}},
   695→				{subject: "bar", header: nats.Header{JSMsgId: {"bar"}}},
   696→			},
   697→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   698→				require_Equal(t, mset.checkMsgId("foo") != nil, commit)
   699→				require_Equal(t, mset.checkMsgId("bar") != nil, commit)
   700→			},
   701→		},
   702→		{
   703→			title: "dedupe",
   704→			init: func(mset *stream) {
   705→				mset.storeMsgId(&ddentry{id: "foo", seq: 0, ts: time.Now().UnixNano()})
   706→			},
   707→			batch: []BatchItem{
   708→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMsgId: {"foo"}}, err: errMsgIdDuplicate},
   709→			},
   710→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   711→				require_NotNil(t, mset.checkMsgId("foo"))
   712→			},
   713→		},
   714→		{
   715→			title: "dedupe-staged",
   716→			batch: []BatchItem{
   717→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMsgId: {"foo"}}},
   718→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMsgId: {"foo"}}, err: errMsgIdDuplicate},
   719→			},
   720→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   721→				require_Equal(t, mset.checkMsgId("foo") != nil, commit)
   722→			},
   723→		},
   724→		{
   725→			title:           "counter-single",
   726→			allowMsgCounter: true,
   727→			batch: []BatchItem{
   728→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMessageIncr: {"1"}}},
   729→			},
   730→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   731→				if !commit {
   732→					require_True(t, mset.clusteredCounterTotal["foo"] == nil)
   733→				} else {
   734→					counter := mset.clusteredCounterTotal["foo"]
   735→					require_NotNil(t, counter)
   736→					require_Equal(t, counter.ops, 1)
   737→					require_Equal(t, counter.total.String(), "1")
   738→				}
   739→			},
   740→		},
   741→		{
   742→			title:           "counter-multiple",

   744→			batch: []BatchItem{
   745→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMessageIncr: {"1"}}},
   746→				{subject: "foo", header: nats.Header{JSMessageIncr: {"2"}}},
   747→			},
   748→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   749→				if !commit {

   751→				} else {

   769→			},

   948→				}

   951→		{

   956→				{subject: "foo2", msg: bytes.Repeat([]byte("A"), 11), err: ErrMaxBytes},
   957→			},
   958→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   959→				if !commit {
   960→					require_Len(t, len(mset.inflight), 0)
   961→				} else {
   962→					require_Len(t, len(mset.inflight), 2)
   963→					require_Equal(t, *mset.inflight["foo1"], inflightSubjectRunningTotal{bytes: 30, ops: 1})
   964→					require_Equal(t, *mset.inflight["foo2"], inflightSubjectRunningTotal{bytes: 31, ops: 1})
   965→				}
   966→			},
   967→		},
   968→		{

   970→			discardNew:        true,
   971→			discardNewPerSubj: true,
   972→			batch: []BatchItem{
   973→				{subject: "foo"},
   974→				{subject: "foo", err: ErrMaxMsgsPerSubject},
   975→			},
   976→			validate: func(mset *stream, commit bool) {
   977→				if !commit {
   978→					require_Len(t, len(mset.inflight), 0)
   979→				} else {
   980→					require_Len(t, len(mset.inflight), 1)
   981→					require_Equal(t, *mset.inflight["foo"], inflightSubjectRunningTotal{bytes: 19 * 2, ops: 2})
   982→				}
   983→			},
   984→		},
   985→		{
   986→			title: "expect-last-seq",

  1169→	for _, test := range tests {

  1171→			s := RunBasicJetStreamServer(t)
  1172→			defer s.Shutdown()
  1173→
  1174→			nc := clientConnectToServer(t, s)
  1175→			defer nc.Close()
  1176→
  1177→			_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1178→				Name:               "TEST",
  1179→				Storage:            MemoryStorage,
  1180→				AllowAtomicPublish: true,
  1181→				Subjects:           []string{"foo", "bar", "baz"},
  1182→			})
  1183→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1184→
  1185→			mset, err := s.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1186→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1187→
  1188→			if test.init != nil {
  1189→				test.init(mset)
  1190→			}
  1191→
  1192→			var (
  1193→				discard       DiscardPolicy
  1194→				discardNewPer bool
  1195→				maxMsgs       int64 = -1
  1196→				maxMsgsPer    int64 = -1
  1197→				maxBytes      int64 = -1
  1198→			)
  1199→			if test.discardNew {
  1200→				discard, maxMsgs, maxBytes = DiscardNew, 2, 60
  1201→			}
  1202→			if test.discardNewPerSubj {
  1203→				require_True(t, test.discardNew)
  1204→				discardNewPer, maxMsgs, maxMsgsPer = true, -1, 1
  1205→			}
  1206→
  1207→			diff := &batchStagedDiff{}
  1208→			mset.clMu.Lock()
  1209→			defer mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1210→			for _, m := range test.batch {
  1211→				var hdr []byte
  1212→				for key, values := range m.header {
  1213→					for _, value := range values {
  1214→						hdr = genHeader(hdr, key, value)
  1215→					}
  1216→				}
  1217→				_, _, _, _, err = checkMsgHeadersPreClusteredProposal(diff, mset, m.subject, hdr, m.msg, false, "TEST", nil, test.allowRollup, test.denyPurge, test.allowTTL, test.allowMsgCounter, test.allowMsgSchedules, discard, discardNewPer, -1, maxMsgs, maxMsgsPer, maxBytes)
  1218→				if m.err != nil {
  1219→					require_Error(t, err, m.err)
  1220→				} else if err != nil {
  1221→					require_NoError(t, err)
  1222→				}
  1223→				if test.validate != nil {
  1224→					test.validate(mset, false)
  1225→				}
  1226→				mset.clseq++
  1227→			}
  1228→			if test.validate != nil {
  1229→				diff.commit(mset)
  1230→				test.validate(mset, true)
  1231→			}
  1232→		})
  1233→	}
  1234→}

  1236→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishHighLevelRollback(t *testing.T) {
  1237→	c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
  1238→	defer c.shutdown()
  1239→
  1240→	nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
  1241→	defer nc.Close()
  1242→
  1243→	_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1244→		Name:      "TEST",
  1245→		Subjects:  []string{"foo"},
  1246→		Replicas:  3,
  1247→		Storage:   FileStorage,
  1248→		Retention: InterestPolicy,
  1249→		MaxMsgs:   1,
  1250→	})
  1251→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1252→
  1253→	sl := c.streamLeader(globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1254→	mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1255→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1256→
  1257→	requireEmpty := func() {
  1258→		mset.ddMu.Lock()
  1259→		ddarr, ddmap := len(mset.ddarr), len(mset.ddmap)
  1260→		mset.ddMu.Unlock()
  1261→		require_Len(t, ddarr, 0)
  1262→		require_Len(t, ddmap, 0)
  1263→
  1264→		mset.clMu.Lock()
  1265→		inflight, subjSeq, subjInProcess := len(mset.inflight), len(mset.expectedPerSubjectSequence), len(mset.expectedPerSubjectInProcess)
  1266→		mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1267→		require_Len(t, inflight, 0)
  1268→		require_Len(t, subjSeq, 0)
  1269→		require_Len(t, subjInProcess, 0)
  1270→	}
  1271→	requireEmpty()
  1272→
  1273→	m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
  1274→	m.Header.Set("Nats-Msg-Id", "dedupe")
  1275→	m.Header.Set("Nats-Expected-Last-Subject-Sequence", "1")
  1276→	_, err = js.PublishMsg(m)
  1277→	require_Error(t, err, NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceError(0))
  1278→	requireEmpty()
  1279→}

  1281→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishExpectedPerSubject(t *testing.T) {
  1282→	type TestKind int
  1283→	const (
  1284→		OnlyFirst TestKind = iota
  1285→		Redundant
  1286→		NotFirst
  1287→	)
  1288→
  1289→	test := func(t *testing.T, kind TestKind) {
  1290→		c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
  1291→		defer c.shutdown()
  1292→
  1293→		nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
  1294→		defer nc.Close()
  1295→
  1296→		_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1297→			Name:               "TEST",
  1298→			Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
  1299→			Storage:            FileStorage,
  1300→			Replicas:           3,
  1301→			AllowAtomicPublish: true,
  1302→		})
  1303→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1304→
  1305→		_, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1306→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1307→
  1308→		m := nats.NewMsg("foo")
  1309→		if kind != NotFirst {
  1310→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Expected-Last-Subject-Sequence", "1")
  1311→		}
  1312→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1313→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
  1314→		require_NoError(t, nc.PublishMsg(m))
  1315→
  1316→		var pubAck JSPubAckResponse
  1317→
  1318→		// Redundant expected headers are okay, as long as they reflect the state prior to the batch.
  1319→		m = nats.NewMsg("foo")
  1320→		if kind == Redundant || kind == NotFirst {
  1321→			m.Header.Set("Nats-Expected-Last-Subject-Sequence", "1")
  1322→		}
  1323→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1324→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
  1325→		m.Header.Set("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
  1326→		rmsg, err := nc.RequestMsg(m, time.Second)
  1327→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1328→		require_NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(rmsg.Data, &pubAck))
  1329→		if kind == Redundant || kind == NotFirst {
  1330→			require_NotNil(t, pubAck.Error)
  1331→			require_Error(t, pubAck.Error, NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceConstantError())
  1332→			return
  1333→		}
  1334→		require_True(t, pubAck.Error == nil)
  1335→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 3)
  1336→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.BatchSize, 2)
  1337→
  1338→		// The first message still contains the expected headers.
  1339→		rsm, err := js.GetMsg("TEST", 2)
  1340→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1341→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Batch-Id"), "uuid")
  1342→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Batch-Sequence"), "1")
  1343→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Expected-Last-Subject-Sequence"), "1")
  1344→
  1345→		// The second message doesn't have the expected headers, as the condition was already checked
  1346→		// and seems inconsistent when getting the message afterward.
  1347→		rsm, err = js.GetMsg("TEST", 3)
  1348→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1349→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Batch-Id"), "uuid")
  1350→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Batch-Sequence"), "2")
  1351→		require_Equal(t, rsm.Header.Get("Nats-Expected-Last-Subject-Sequence"), _EMPTY_)
  1352→	}
  1353→
  1354→	t.Run("single", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, OnlyFirst) })
  1355→	t.Run("redundant", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, Redundant) })
  1356→	t.Run("not-first", func(t *testing.T) { test(t, NotFirst) })
  1357→}

  1359→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishSingleServerRecovery(t *testing.T) {
  1360→	s := RunBasicJetStreamServer(t)
  1361→	defer s.Shutdown()
  1362→
  1363→	nc := clientConnectToServer(t, s)
  1364→	defer nc.Close()
  1365→
  1366→	cfg := &StreamConfig{
  1367→		Name:               "TEST",
  1368→		Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
  1369→		Storage:            FileStorage,
  1370→		Retention:          LimitsPolicy,
  1371→		Replicas:           1,
  1372→		AllowAtomicPublish: true,
  1373→	}
  1374→	_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, cfg)
  1375→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1376→
  1377→	// Manually construct and store a batch, so it doesn't immediately commit.
  1378→	mset, err := s.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1379→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1380→	mset.mu.Lock()
  1381→	batches := &batching{}
  1382→	mset.batches = batches
  1383→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  1384→	batches.mu.Lock()
  1385→	b, err := batches.newBatchGroup(mset, "uuid")
  1386→	if err != nil {
  1387→		batches.mu.Unlock()
  1388→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1389→	}
  1390→	hdr1 := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1391→	hdr1 = genHeader(hdr1, "Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
  1392→	_, _, err = b.store.StoreMsg("foo", hdr1, nil, 0)
  1393→	if err != nil {
  1394→		batches.mu.Unlock()
  1395→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1396→	}
  1397→
  1398→	hdr2 := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1399→	hdr2 = genHeader(hdr2, "Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2")
  1400→	hdr2 = genHeader(hdr2, "Nats-Batch-Commit", "1")
  1401→	_, _, err = b.store.StoreMsg("foo", hdr2, nil, 0)
  1402→	if err != nil {
  1403→		batches.mu.Unlock()
  1404→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1405→	}
  1406→	commitReady := b.readyForCommit()
  1407→	batches.mu.Unlock()
  1408→	require_True(t, commitReady)
  1409→
  1410→	// Simulate the first message of the batch is committed.
  1411→	err = mset.processJetStreamMsg("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr1, nil, 0, 0, nil, false)
  1412→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1413→
  1414→	// Simulate a hard kill, upon recovery the rest of the batch should be applied.
  1415→	port := s.opts.Port
  1416→	sd := s.StoreDir()
  1417→	nc.Close()
  1418→	s.Shutdown()
  1419→	s = RunJetStreamServerOnPort(port, sd)
  1420→	defer s.Shutdown()
  1421→
  1422→	mset, err = s.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1423→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1424→	state := mset.state()
  1425→	require_Equal(t, state.Msgs, 2)
  1426→	require_Equal(t, state.FirstSeq, 1)
  1427→	require_Equal(t, state.LastSeq, 2)
  1428→}

  1430→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishEncode(t *testing.T) {
  1431→	test := func(t *testing.T, commit bool, compress bool) {
  1432→		ts := time.Now().UnixNano()
  1433→		hdr := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1434→		hdr = genHeader(hdr, "Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1")
  1435→		msg := []byte("A")
  1436→		if compress {
  1437→			msg = bytes.Repeat(msg, compressThreshold)
  1438→		}
  1439→		esm := encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", "reply", hdr, msg, 1, ts, false, "uuid", 1, commit)
  1440→
  1441→		buf, op := esm[1:], entryOp(esm[0])
  1442→		if commit {
  1443→			require_Equal(t, op, batchCommitMsgOp)
  1444→		} else {
  1445→			require_Equal(t, op, batchMsgOp)
  1446→		}
  1447→
  1448→		batchId, batchSeq, op, mbuf, err := decodeBatchMsg(buf)
  1449→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1450→		require_Equal(t, batchId, "uuid")
  1451→		require_Equal(t, batchSeq, 1)
  1452→		if compress {
  1453→			require_Equal(t, op, compressedStreamMsgOp)
  1454→			mbuf, err = s2.Decode(nil, mbuf)
  1455→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1456→		} else {
  1457→			require_Equal(t, op, streamMsgOp)
  1458→		}
  1459→
  1460→		subject, reply, dhdr, dmsg, lseq, dts, sourced, err := decodeStreamMsg(mbuf)

  1479→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishProposeOne(t *testing.T) {

  1500→		pubAck, err := js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1501→		require_NoError(t, err)
  1502→		require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 1)
  1503→
  1504→		var entries []*Entry
  1505→
  1506→		mset.clMu.Lock()
  1507→		if combined {
  1508→			esm := encodeStreamMsg("foo", _EMPTY_, nil, nil, mset.clseq, 0, false)
  1509→			entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1510→			mset.clseq++
  1511→		}
  1512→
  1513→		msg := []byte("hello")
  1514→		hdr := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1515→		hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1", hdr)
  1516→		esm := encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, mset.clseq, 0, false, "uuid", 1, false)
  1517→		entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1518→		mset.clseq++
  1519→
  1520→		hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2", hdr)
  1521→		hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1", hdr)
  1522→		esm = encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, mset.clseq, 0, false, "uuid", 2, true)
  1523→		entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1524→		mset.clseq++
  1525→
  1526→		if combined {
  1527→			esm = encodeStreamMsg("foo", _EMPTY_, nil, nil, mset.clseq, 0, false)
  1528→			entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1529→			mset.clseq++

  1531→		mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1532→		n := mset.raftNode().(*raft)

  1535→		pubAck, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)

  1541→		}
  1542→	}
  1543→

  1634→// Test a batch that was only partially proposed.
  1635→// This should not happen, but guard against it anyhow.
  1636→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishProposeOnePartialBatch(t *testing.T) {
  1637→	maxEntries := 3
  1638→	for i := range maxEntries + 1 {
  1639→		t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("I-%d", i), func(t *testing.T) {
  1640→			c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
  1641→			defer c.shutdown()
  1642→
  1643→			nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
  1644→			defer nc.Close()
  1645→
  1646→			_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1647→				Name:               "TEST",
  1648→				Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
  1649→				Storage:            FileStorage,
  1650→				Replicas:           3,
  1651→				AllowAtomicPublish: true,
  1652→			})
  1653→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1654→
  1655→			sl := c.streamLeader(globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1656→			mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1657→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1658→
  1659→			pubAck, err := js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1660→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1661→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 1)
  1662→
  1663→			var entries []*Entry
  1664→			mset.clMu.Lock()
  1665→			msg := []byte("hello")
  1666→			hdr := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "uuid")
  1667→			for j := range 3 {
  1668→				// Skip if indices equal.
  1669→				if i == j {
  1670→					continue
  1671→				}
  1672→				bseq := uint64(j + 1)
  1673→				hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", strconv.FormatUint(bseq, 10), hdr)
  1674→				commit := bseq == uint64(maxEntries)
  1675→				if commit {
  1676→					hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1", hdr)
  1677→				}
  1678→				esm := encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, mset.clseq, 0, false, "uuid", bseq, commit)
  1679→				entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1680→				mset.clseq++
  1681→			}
  1682→			mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1683→			n := mset.raftNode().(*raft)
  1684→			n.sendAppendEntry(entries)
  1685→
  1686→			pubAck, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1687→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1688→			expectedSeq := uint64(2)
  1689→			if i >= maxEntries {
  1690→				expectedSeq += uint64(maxEntries)
  1691→			}
  1692→			require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, expectedSeq)
  1693→		})
  1694→	}
  1695→}

  1697→// Test multiple sequential batches, the first batch is partially proposed.

  1699→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishProposeMultiplePartialBatches(t *testing.T) {
  1700→	for i := range 2 {
  1701→		batchSize := i + 1
  1702→		t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("B-%d", batchSize), func(t *testing.T) {

  1708→
  1709→			_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1710→				Name:               "TEST",
  1711→				Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
  1712→				Storage:            FileStorage,
  1713→				Replicas:           3,
  1714→				AllowAtomicPublish: true,

  1719→			mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1720→			require_NoError(t, err)
  1721→
  1722→			pubAck, err := js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1723→			require_NoError(t, err)

  1725→
  1726→			var entries []*Entry
  1727→			mset.clMu.Lock()
  1728→			msg := []byte("hello")
  1729→			hdr := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "ID_1")
  1730→			hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1", hdr)
  1731→			esm := encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, mset.clseq, 0, false, "ID_1", 1, false)
  1732→			entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1733→			mset.clseq++
  1734→
  1735→			for j := range batchSize {
  1736→				bseq := uint64(j + 1)
  1737→				hdr = genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "ID_2")
  1738→				hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", strconv.FormatUint(bseq, 10), hdr)
  1739→				commit := bseq == uint64(batchSize)
  1740→				if commit {
  1741→					hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1", hdr)
  1742→				}
  1743→				esm = encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, mset.clseq, 0, false, "ID_2", bseq, commit)
  1744→				entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1745→				mset.clseq++

  1748→			n := mset.raftNode().(*raft)

  1751→			pubAck, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)

  1754→		})
  1755→	}
  1756→}

  1758→// Test a continuous flow of batches spanning multiple append entries can still move applied up.
  1759→// Also, test a server can become leader if the previous leader left it with a partial batch.
  1760→func TestJetStreamAtomicBatchPublishContinuousBatchesStillMoveAppliedUp(t *testing.T) {
  1761→	c := createJetStreamClusterExplicit(t, "R3S", 3)
  1762→	defer c.shutdown()
  1763→
  1764→	nc, js := jsClientConnect(t, c.randomServer())
  1765→	defer nc.Close()
  1766→
  1767→	_, err := jsStreamCreate(t, nc, &StreamConfig{
  1768→		Name:               "TEST",
  1769→		Subjects:           []string{"foo"},
  1770→		Storage:            FileStorage,
  1771→		Replicas:           3,
  1772→		AllowAtomicPublish: true,
  1773→	})
  1774→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1775→
  1776→	sl := c.streamLeader(globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1777→	mset, err := sl.globalAccount().lookupStream("TEST")
  1778→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1779→
  1780→	pubAck, err := js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1781→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1782→	require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 1)
  1783→
  1784→	n := mset.raftNode().(*raft)
  1785→	index, commit, applied := n.Progress()
  1786→
  1787→	// The first batch spans two append entries, but commits.
  1788→	mset.clMu.Lock()
  1789→	hdr := genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "ID_1")
  1790→	hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1", hdr)
  1791→	esm := encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, mset.clseq, 0, false, "ID_1", 1, false)
  1792→	mset.clseq++
  1793→	mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1794→	n.sendAppendEntry([]*Entry{newEntry(EntryNormal, esm)})
  1795→
  1796→	var entries []*Entry
  1797→	mset.clMu.Lock()
  1798→	hdr = genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "ID_1")
  1799→	hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "2", hdr)
  1800→	hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Commit", "1", hdr)
  1801→	esm = encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, mset.clseq, 0, false, "ID_1", 2, true)
  1802→	mset.clseq++
  1803→
  1804→	// The second batch doesn't commit.
  1805→	entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1806→	hdr = genHeader(nil, "Nats-Batch-Id", "ID_2")
  1807→	hdr = setHeader("Nats-Batch-Sequence", "1", hdr)
  1808→	esm = encodeStreamMsgAllowCompressAndBatch("foo", _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, mset.clseq, 0, false, "ID_2", 1, false)
  1809→	mset.clseq++
  1810→	entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, esm))
  1811→	mset.clMu.Unlock()
  1812→	n.sendAppendEntry(entries)
  1813→
  1814→	checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
  1815→		n.RLock()
  1816→		nindex, ncommit, processed, napplied := n.pindex, n.commit, n.processed, n.applied
  1817→		n.RUnlock()
  1818→		if nindex == index {
  1819→			return errors.New("index not updated")
  1820→		} else if ncommit == commit {
  1821→			return errors.New("commit not updated")
  1822→		} else if napplied == applied {
  1823→			return errors.New("applied not updated")
  1824→		} else if napplied == ncommit {
  1825→			return errors.New("applied should not be able to equal commit yet")
  1826→		} else if processed != ncommit {
  1827→			return errors.New("must have processed all commits")
  1828→		}
  1829→		return checkState(t, c, globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1830→	})
  1831→
  1832→	// Followers are now stranded with a partial batch, one needs to become leader
  1833→	// and have the batch be rejected since it was partially proposed.
  1834→	sl.Shutdown()
  1835→	c.waitOnStreamLeader(globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1836→	checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
  1837→		return checkState(t, c, globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1838→	})
  1839→
  1840→	// Confirm the last batch gets rejected, and we are still able to publish with quorum.
  1841→	pubAck, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1842→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1843→	require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 4)
  1844→
  1845→	c.restartServer(sl)
  1846→	checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
  1847→		return checkState(t, c, globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1848→	})
  1849→
  1850→	// Publish again, now with all servers online.
  1851→	pubAck, err = js.Publish("foo", nil)
  1852→	require_NoError(t, err)
  1853→	require_Equal(t, pubAck.Sequence, 5)
  1854→	checkFor(t, 2*time.Second, 200*time.Millisecond, func() error {
  1855→		return checkState(t, c, globalAccountName, "TEST")
  1856→	})
  1857→}
  1858→

Now let me understand the problem better. Looking at the code, I can see that:

1. When a batch is being applied, messages are processed one by one in `applyStreamEntries`
2. The batch state is tracked via `batchApply` which holds pending entries until a commit message is received
3. However, during this time, readers (consumers/GetMsg) can access these partially applied messages

The issue is that there's no mechanism to prevent readers from seeing messages that are part of an incomplete batch. Let me look for how message reads work:

The tests pass because the current implementation actually waits for batches to complete before making them visible! Let me look more carefully at the code. The key is in the `applyStreamEntries` function - it stores incomplete batches in memory (`batch.entries`) and only applies them when the commit is received. However, this doesn't prevent reads from the underlying store once messages are written there.

Let me look at where messages are actually stored:

content.text:
Now let me understand the actual problem better. Looking at the issue description and the code:

1. In `applyStreamEntries`, batch messages are deferred until commit - they're stored in `batch.entries` and not immediately applied
2. But the issue states that readers can observe partial batches

The key insight is that **`applyStreamEntries` is only called on followers during replication**. On the leader, messages are applied immediately via `processJetStreamMsg` when they're proposed!

Let me trace through how messages are published on the leader:

Now I understand! The issue is that:

1. **On the leader**: When batch messages arrive via `processInboundJetStreamMsg`, they are **immediately stored** to the underlying store via `processJetStreamMsg` as they are proposed to Raft
2. **On followers**: The messages are held in memory in `batch.entries` until commit

This means on the leader, readers can see partial batch messages in the store before the batch commits!

Let me create a better test that specifically targets the leader:

content.text:
Great! I've successfully reproduced the isolation violation. Now I understand the problem:

**The Bug**: When batch messages are published to a clustered stream:
1. On the leader, messages are immediately written to the store as they arrive via `processJetStreamMsg`
2. Readers can access the store and see these partial batch messages before the batch commits
3. This violates the isolation guarantee that batches should be atomic

**The Solution**: We need to prevent readers from seeing batch messages until the entire batch is committed. There are a few approaches:

1. **Defer writing batch messages to store until commit** (complex, affects performance)
2. **Track uncommitted batch sequences and hide them from reads** (simpler)
3. **Use a transaction-like mechanism in the store** (most complex)

Let me implement approach #2, which tracks uncommitted batch messages and filters them out during reads. Let me start by examining where reads happen and how we can intercept them:

   336→// Headers used in $JS.API.> requests.
   337→const (
   338→	// JSRequiredApiLevel requires the API level of the responding server to have the specified minimum value.
   339→	JSRequiredApiLevel = "Nats-Required-Api-Level"

   342→var denyAllClientJs = []string{jsAllAPI, "$KV.>", "$OBJ.>"}
   343→var denyAllJs = []string{jscAllSubj, raftAllSubj, jsAllAPI, "$KV.>", "$OBJ.>"}
   344→
   345→func generateJSMappingTable(domain string) map[string]string {
   346→	mappings := map[string]string{}
   347→	// This set of mappings is very very very ugly.
   348→	// It is a consequence of what we defined the domain prefix to be "$JS.domain.API" and it's mapping to "$JS.API"
   349→	// For optics $KV and $OBJ where made to be independent subject spaces.
   350→	// As materialized views of JS, they did not simply extend that subject space to say "$JS.API.KV" "$JS.API.OBJ"
   351→	// This is very unfortunate!!!
   352→	// Furthermore, it seemed bad to require different domain prefixes for JS/KV/OBJ.
   353→	// Especially since the actual API for say KV, does use stream create from JS.
   354→	// To avoid overlaps KV and OBJ views append the prefix to their API.
   355→	// (Replacing $KV with the prefix allows users to create collisions with say the bucket name)
   356→	// This mapping therefore needs to have extra token so that the mapping can properly discern between $JS, $KV, $OBJ
   357→	for srcMappingSuffix, to := range map[string]string{
   358→		"INFO":       JSApiAccountInfo,
   359→		"STREAM.>":   "$JS.API.STREAM.>",
   360→		"CONSUMER.>": "$JS.API.CONSUMER.>",
   361→		"DIRECT.>":   "$JS.API.DIRECT.>",
   362→		"META.>":     "$JS.API.META.>",
   363→		"SERVER.>":   "$JS.API.SERVER.>",
   364→		"ACCOUNT.>":  "$JS.API.ACCOUNT.>",
   365→		"$KV.>":      "$KV.>",
   366→		"$OBJ.>":     "$OBJ.>",
   367→	} {
   368→		mappings[fmt.Sprintf("$JS.%s.API.%s", domain, srcMappingSuffix)] = to
   369→	}
   370→	return mappings
   371→}

   373→// JSMaxDescription is the maximum description length for streams and consumers.
   374→const JSMaxDescriptionLen = 4 * 1024
   375→
   376→// JSMaxMetadataLen is the maximum length for streams and consumers metadata map.
   377→// It's calculated by summing length of all keys and values.
   378→const JSMaxMetadataLen = 128 * 1024
   379→
   380→// JSMaxNameLen is the maximum name lengths for streams, consumers and templates.
   381→// Picked 255 as it seems to be a widely used file name limit
   382→const JSMaxNameLen = 255
   383→
   384→// JSDefaultRequestQueueLimit is the default number of entries that we will
   385→// put on the global request queue before we react.
   386→const JSDefaultRequestQueueLimit = 10_000

   404→// ToError checks if the response has a error and if it does converts it to an error avoiding
   405→// the pitfalls described by https://yourbasic.org/golang/gotcha-why-nil-error-not-equal-nil/
   406→func (r *ApiResponse) ToError() error {
   407→	if r.Error == nil {
   408→		return nil
   409→	}
   410→
   411→	return r.Error
   412→}

   832→// Structure that holds state for a JetStream API request that is processed
   833→// in a separate long-lived go routine. This is to avoid blocking connections.
   834→type jsAPIRoutedReq struct {
   835→	jsub    *subscription
   836→	sub     *subscription
   837→	acc     *Account
   838→	subject string
   839→	reply   string
   840→	msg     []byte
   841→	pa      pubArg
   842→}
   843→
   844→func (js *jetStream) apiDispatch(sub *subscription, c *client, acc *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
   845→	// Ignore system level directives meta stepdown and peer remove requests here.
   846→	if subject == JSApiLeaderStepDown ||
   847→		subject == JSApiRemoveServer ||
   848→		strings.HasPrefix(subject, jsAPIAccountPre) {
   849→		return
   850→	}
   851→	// No lock needed, those are immutable.
   852→	s, rr := js.srv, js.apiSubs.Match(subject)
   853→
   854→	hdr, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
   855→	if len(sliceHeader(ClientInfoHdr, hdr)) == 0 {
   856→		// Check if this is the system account. We will let these through for the account info only.
   857→		sacc := s.SystemAccount()
   858→		if sacc != acc {
   859→			return
   860→		}
   861→		if subject != JSApiAccountInfo {
   862→			// Only respond from the initial server entry to the NATS system.
   863→			if c.kind == CLIENT || c.kind == LEAF {
   864→				var resp = ApiResponse{
   865→					Type:  JSApiSystemResponseType,
   866→					Error: NewJSNotEnabledForAccountError(),
   867→				}
   868→				s.sendAPIErrResponse(nil, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
   869→			}
   870→			return
   871→		}
   872→	}
   873→
   874→	// Short circuit for no interest.
   875→	if len(rr.psubs)+len(rr.qsubs) == 0 {
   876→		if (c.kind == CLIENT || c.kind == LEAF) && acc != s.SystemAccount() {
   877→			ci, acc, _, _, _ := s.getRequestInfo(c, rmsg)
   878→			var resp = ApiResponse{
   879→				Type:  JSApiSystemResponseType,
   880→				Error: NewJSBadRequestError(),
   881→			}
   882→			s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
   883→		}
   884→		return
   885→	}
   886→
   887→	// We should only have psubs and only 1 per result.
   888→	if len(rr.psubs) != 1 {
   889→		s.Warnf("Malformed JetStream API Request: [%s] %q", subject, rmsg)
   890→		if c.kind == CLIENT || c.kind == LEAF {
   891→			ci, acc, _, _, _ := s.getRequestInfo(c, rmsg)
   892→			var resp = ApiResponse{
   893→				Type:  JSApiSystemResponseType,
   894→				Error: NewJSBadRequestError(),
   895→			}
   896→			s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
   897→		}
   898→		return
   899→	}
   900→	jsub := rr.psubs[0]
   901→
   902→	// We need to make sure not to block. We will send the request to a long-lived
   903→	// pool of go routines.
   904→
   905→	// Increment inflight. Do this before queueing.
   906→	atomic.AddInt64(&js.apiInflight, 1)
   907→
   908→	// Copy the state. Note the JSAPI only uses the hdr index to piece apart the
   909→	// header from the msg body. No other references are needed.
   910→	// Check pending and warn if getting backed up.
   911→	pending, _ := s.jsAPIRoutedReqs.push(&jsAPIRoutedReq{jsub, sub, acc, subject, reply, copyBytes(rmsg), c.pa})
   912→	limit := atomic.LoadInt64(&js.queueLimit)
   913→	if pending >= int(limit) {
   914→		s.rateLimitFormatWarnf("JetStream API queue limit reached, dropping %d requests", pending)
   915→		drained := int64(s.jsAPIRoutedReqs.drain())
   916→		atomic.AddInt64(&js.apiInflight, -drained)
   917→
   918→		s.publishAdvisory(nil, JSAdvisoryAPILimitReached, JSAPILimitReachedAdvisory{
   919→			TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
   920→				Type: JSAPILimitReachedAdvisoryType,
   921→				ID:   nuid.Next(),
   922→				Time: time.Now().UTC(),
   923→			},
   924→			Server:  s.Name(),
   925→			Domain:  js.config.Domain,
   926→			Dropped: drained,
   927→		})
   928→	}
   929→}
   930→
   931→func (s *Server) processJSAPIRoutedRequests() {
   932→	defer s.grWG.Done()
   933→
   934→	s.mu.RLock()
   935→	queue := s.jsAPIRoutedReqs
   936→	client := &client{srv: s, kind: JETSTREAM}
   937→	s.mu.RUnlock()
   938→
   939→	js := s.getJetStream()
   940→
   941→	for {
   942→		select {
   943→		case <-queue.ch:
   944→			// Only pop one item at a time here, otherwise if the system is recovering
   945→			// from queue buildup, then one worker will pull off all the tasks and the
   946→			// others will be starved of work.
   947→			for r, ok := queue.popOne(); ok && r != nil; r, ok = queue.popOne() {
   948→				client.pa = r.pa
   949→				start := time.Now()
   950→				r.jsub.icb(r.sub, client, r.acc, r.subject, r.reply, r.msg)
   951→				if dur := time.Since(start); dur >= readLoopReportThreshold {
   952→					s.Warnf("Internal subscription on %q took too long: %v", r.subject, dur)
   953→				}
   954→				atomic.AddInt64(&js.apiInflight, -1)
   955→			}
   956→		case <-s.quitCh:
   957→			return
   958→		}
   959→	}
   960→}
   961→
   962→func (s *Server) setJetStreamExportSubs() error {
   963→	js := s.getJetStream()
   964→	if js == nil {
   965→		return NewJSNotEnabledError()
   966→	}
   967→
   968→	// Start the go routine that will process API requests received by the
   969→	// subscription below when they are coming from routes, etc..
   970→	const maxProcs = 16
   971→	mp := runtime.GOMAXPROCS(0)
   972→	// Cap at 16 max for now on larger core setups.
   973→	if mp > maxProcs {
   974→		mp = maxProcs
   975→	}
   976→	s.jsAPIRoutedReqs = newIPQueue[*jsAPIRoutedReq](s, "Routed JS API Requests")
   977→	for i := 0; i < mp; i++ {
   978→		s.startGoRoutine(s.processJSAPIRoutedRequests)
   979→	}
   980→
   981→	// This is the catch all now for all JetStream API calls.
   982→	if _, err := s.sysSubscribe(jsAllAPI, js.apiDispatch); err != nil {
   983→		return err
   984→	}
   985→
   986→	if err := s.SystemAccount().AddServiceExport(jsAllAPI, nil); err != nil {
   987→		s.Warnf("Error setting up jetstream service exports: %v", err)
   988→		return err
   989→	}
   990→
   991→	// API handles themselves.
   992→	pairs := []struct {
   993→		subject string
   994→		handler msgHandler
   995→	}{
   996→		{JSApiAccountInfo, s.jsAccountInfoRequest},
   997→		{JSApiTemplateCreate, s.jsTemplateCreateRequest},
   998→		{JSApiTemplates, s.jsTemplateNamesRequest},
   999→		{JSApiTemplateInfo, s.jsTemplateInfoRequest},
  1000→		{JSApiTemplateDelete, s.jsTemplateDeleteRequest},
  1001→		{JSApiStreamCreate, s.jsStreamCreateRequest},
  1002→		{JSApiStreamUpdate, s.jsStreamUpdateRequest},
  1003→		{JSApiStreams, s.jsStreamNamesRequest},
  1004→		{JSApiStreamList, s.jsStreamListRequest},
  1005→		{JSApiStreamInfo, s.jsStreamInfoRequest},
  1006→		{JSApiStreamDelete, s.jsStreamDeleteRequest},
  1007→		{JSApiStreamPurge, s.jsStreamPurgeRequest},
  1008→		{JSApiStreamSnapshot, s.jsStreamSnapshotRequest},
  1009→		{JSApiStreamRestore, s.jsStreamRestoreRequest},
  1010→		{JSApiStreamRemovePeer, s.jsStreamRemovePeerRequest},
  1011→		{JSApiStreamLeaderStepDown, s.jsStreamLeaderStepDownRequest},
  1012→		{JSApiConsumerLeaderStepDown, s.jsConsumerLeaderStepDownRequest},
  1013→		{JSApiMsgDelete, s.jsMsgDeleteRequest},
  1014→		{JSApiMsgGet, s.jsMsgGetRequest},
  1015→		{JSApiConsumerCreateEx, s.jsConsumerCreateRequest},
  1016→		{JSApiConsumerCreate, s.jsConsumerCreateRequest},
  1017→		{JSApiDurableCreate, s.jsConsumerCreateRequest},
  1018→		{JSApiConsumers, s.jsConsumerNamesRequest},
  1019→		{JSApiConsumerList, s.jsConsumerListRequest},
  1020→		{JSApiConsumerInfo, s.jsConsumerInfoRequest},
  1021→		{JSApiConsumerDelete, s.jsConsumerDeleteRequest},
  1022→		{JSApiConsumerPause, s.jsConsumerPauseRequest},
  1023→		{JSApiConsumerUnpin, s.jsConsumerUnpinRequest},
  1024→	}
  1025→
  1026→	js.mu.Lock()
  1027→	defer js.mu.Unlock()
  1028→
  1029→	for _, p := range pairs {
  1030→		sub := &subscription{subject: []byte(p.subject), icb: p.handler}
  1031→		if err := js.apiSubs.Insert(sub); err != nil {
  1032→			return err
  1033→		}
  1034→	}
  1035→
  1036→	return nil
  1037→}

  1039→func (s *Server) sendAPIResponse(ci *ClientInfo, acc *Account, subject, reply, request, response string) {
  1040→	acc.trackAPI()
  1041→	if reply != _EMPTY_ {
  1042→		s.sendInternalAccountMsg(nil, reply, response)
  1043→	}
  1044→	s.sendJetStreamAPIAuditAdvisory(ci, acc, subject, request, response)
  1045→}
  1046→
  1047→func (s *Server) sendAPIErrResponse(ci *ClientInfo, acc *Account, subject, reply, request, response string) {
  1048→	acc.trackAPIErr()
  1049→	if reply != _EMPTY_ {
  1050→		s.sendInternalAccountMsg(nil, reply, response)
  1051→	}
  1052→	s.sendJetStreamAPIAuditAdvisory(ci, acc, subject, request, response)
  1053→}
  1054→
  1055→const errRespDelay = 500 * time.Millisecond
  1056→
  1057→type delayedAPIResponse struct {
  1058→	ci       *ClientInfo
  1059→	acc      *Account
  1060→	subject  string
  1061→	reply    string
  1062→	request  string
  1063→	hdr      []byte
  1064→	response string
  1065→	rg       *raftGroup
  1066→	deadline time.Time
  1067→	noJs     bool
  1068→	next     *delayedAPIResponse
  1069→}
  1070→
  1071→// Add `r` in the list that is maintained ordered by the `delayedAPIResponse.deadline` time.
  1072→func addDelayedResponse(head, tail **delayedAPIResponse, r *delayedAPIResponse) {
  1073→	// Check if list empty.
  1074→	if *head == nil {
  1075→		*head, *tail = r, r
  1076→		return
  1077→	}
  1078→	// Check if it should be added at the end, which is if after or equal to the tail.
  1079→	if r.deadline.After((*tail).deadline) || r.deadline.Equal((*tail).deadline) {
  1080→		(*tail).next, *tail = r, r
  1081→		return
  1082→	}
  1083→	// Find its spot in the list.
  1084→	var prev *delayedAPIResponse
  1085→	for c := *head; c != nil; c = c.next {
  1086→		// We insert only if we are stricly before the current `c`.
  1087→		if r.deadline.Before(c.deadline) {
  1088→			r.next = c
  1089→			if prev != nil {
  1090→				prev.next = r
  1091→			} else {
  1092→				*head = r
  1093→			}
  1094→			return
  1095→		}
  1096→		prev = c
  1097→	}
  1098→}
  1099→
  1100→func (s *Server) delayedAPIResponder() {
  1101→	defer s.grWG.Done()
  1102→	var (
  1103→		head, tail *delayedAPIResponse // Linked list.
  1104→		r          *delayedAPIResponse // Updated by calling next().
  1105→		rqch       <-chan struct{}     // Quit channel of the Raft group (if present).
  1106→		tm         = time.NewTimer(time.Hour)
  1107→	)
  1108→	next := func() {
  1109→		r, rqch = nil, nil
  1110→		// Check that JetStream is still on. Do not exit the go routine
  1111→		// since JS can be enabled/disabled. The go routine will exit
  1112→		// only if server is shutdown.
  1113→		js := s.getJetStream()
  1114→		if js == nil {
  1115→			// Reset head and tail here. Also drain the ipQueue.
  1116→			head, tail = nil, nil
  1117→			s.delayedAPIResponses.drain()
  1118→			// Fall back into next "if" that resets timer.
  1119→		}
  1120→		// If there are no delayed messages then delay the timer for
  1121→		// a while.
  1122→		if head == nil {
  1123→			tm.Reset(time.Hour)
  1124→			return
  1125→		}
  1126→		// Get the first expected message and then reset the timer.
  1127→		r = head
  1128→		js.mu.RLock()
  1129→		if r.rg != nil && r.rg.node != nil {
  1130→			// If there's an attached Raft group to the delayed response
  1131→			// then pull out the quit channel, so that we don't bother
  1132→			// sending responses for entities which are now no longer
  1133→			// running.
  1134→			rqch = r.rg.node.QuitC()
  1135→		}
  1136→		js.mu.RUnlock()
  1137→		tm.Reset(time.Until(r.deadline))
  1138→	}
  1139→	pop := func() {
  1140→		if head == nil {
  1141→			return
  1142→		}
  1143→		head = head.next
  1144→		if head == nil {
  1145→			tail = nil
  1146→		}
  1147→	}
  1148→	for {
  1149→		select {
  1150→		case <-s.delayedAPIResponses.ch:
  1151→			v, ok := s.delayedAPIResponses.popOne()
  1152→			if !ok {
  1153→				continue
  1154→			}
  1155→			// Add it to the list, and if ends up being the head, set things up.
  1156→			addDelayedResponse(&head, &tail, v)
  1157→			if v == head {
  1158→				next()
  1159→			}
  1160→		case <-s.quitCh:
  1161→			return
  1162→		case <-rqch:
  1163→			// If we were the head, drop and setup things for next.
  1164→			if r != nil && r == head {
  1165→				pop()
  1166→			}
  1167→			next()
  1168→		case <-tm.C:
  1169→			if r != nil {
  1170→				// If it's not a JS API error, send it as a raw response without additional API/audit tracking.
  1171→				if r.noJs {
  1172→					s.sendInternalAccountMsgWithReply(r.acc, r.subject, _EMPTY_, r.hdr, r.response, false)
  1173→				} else {
  1174→					s.sendAPIErrResponse(r.ci, r.acc, r.subject, r.reply, r.request, r.response)
  1175→				}
  1176→				pop()
  1177→			}
  1178→			next()
  1179→		}
  1180→	}
  1181→}
  1182→
  1183→func (s *Server) sendDelayedAPIErrResponse(ci *ClientInfo, acc *Account, subject, reply, request, response string, rg *raftGroup, duration time.Duration) {
  1184→	s.delayedAPIResponses.push(&delayedAPIResponse{
  1185→		ci, acc, subject, reply, request, nil, response, rg, time.Now().Add(duration), false, nil,
  1186→	})
  1187→}
  1188→
  1189→func (s *Server) sendDelayedErrResponse(acc *Account, subject string, hdr []byte, response string, duration time.Duration) {
  1190→	s.delayedAPIResponses.push(&delayedAPIResponse{
  1191→		nil, acc, subject, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, response, nil, time.Now().Add(duration), true, nil,
  1192→	})
  1193→}
  1194→
  1195→func (s *Server) getRequestInfo(c *client, raw []byte) (pci *ClientInfo, acc *Account, hdr, msg []byte, err error) {
  1196→	hdr, msg = c.msgParts(raw)
  1197→	var ci ClientInfo
  1198→
  1199→	if len(hdr) > 0 {
  1200→		if err := json.Unmarshal(sliceHeader(ClientInfoHdr, hdr), &ci); err != nil {
  1201→			return nil, nil, nil, nil, err
  1202→		}
  1203→	}
  1204→
  1205→	if ci.Service != _EMPTY_ {
  1206→		acc, _ = s.LookupAccount(ci.Service)
  1207→	} else if ci.Account != _EMPTY_ {
  1208→		acc, _ = s.LookupAccount(ci.Account)
  1209→	} else {
  1210→		// Direct $SYS access.
  1211→		acc = c.acc
  1212→		if acc == nil {
  1213→			acc = s.SystemAccount()
  1214→		}
  1215→	}
  1216→	if acc == nil {
  1217→		return nil, nil, nil, nil, ErrMissingAccount
  1218→	}
  1219→	return &ci, acc, hdr, msg, nil
  1220→}
  1221→
  1222→func (s *Server) unmarshalRequest(c *client, acc *Account, subject string, msg []byte, v any) error {
  1223→	decoder := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(msg))
  1224→	decoder.DisallowUnknownFields()
  1225→
  1226→	for {
  1227→		if err := decoder.Decode(v); err != nil {
  1228→			if err == io.EOF {
  1229→				return nil
  1230→			}
  1231→
  1232→			var syntaxErr *json.SyntaxError
  1233→			if errors.As(err, &syntaxErr) {
  1234→				err = fmt.Errorf("%w at offset %d", err, syntaxErr.Offset)
  1235→			}
  1236→
  1237→			c.RateLimitWarnf("Invalid JetStream request '%s > %s': %s", acc, subject, err)
  1238→
  1239→			if s.JetStreamConfig().Strict {
  1240→				return err
  1241→			}
  1242→
  1243→			return json.Unmarshal(msg, v)
  1244→		}
  1245→	}
  1246→}
  1247→
  1248→func (a *Account) trackAPI() {
  1249→	a.mu.RLock()
  1250→	jsa := a.js
  1251→	a.mu.RUnlock()
  1252→	if jsa != nil {
  1253→		jsa.usageMu.Lock()
  1254→		jsa.usageApi++
  1255→		jsa.apiTotal++
  1256→		jsa.sendClusterUsageUpdate()
  1257→		atomic.AddInt64(&jsa.js.apiTotal, 1)
  1258→		jsa.usageMu.Unlock()
  1259→	}
  1260→}
  1261→
  1262→func (a *Account) trackAPIErr() {
  1263→	a.mu.RLock()
  1264→	jsa := a.js
  1265→	a.mu.RUnlock()
  1266→	if jsa != nil {
  1267→		jsa.usageMu.Lock()
  1268→		jsa.usageApi++
  1269→		jsa.apiTotal++
  1270→		jsa.usageErr++
  1271→		jsa.apiErrors++
  1272→		jsa.sendClusterUsageUpdate()
  1273→		atomic.AddInt64(&jsa.js.apiTotal, 1)
  1274→		atomic.AddInt64(&jsa.js.apiErrors, 1)
  1275→		jsa.usageMu.Unlock()
  1276→	}
  1277→}
  1278→
  1279→const badAPIRequestT = "Malformed JetStream API Request: %q"
  1280→
  1281→// Helper function to check on JetStream being enabled but also on status of leafnodes
  1282→// If the local account is not enabled but does have leafnode connectivity we will not
  1283→// want to error immediately and let the other side decide.
  1284→func (a *Account) checkJetStream() (enabled, shouldError bool) {
  1285→	a.mu.RLock()
  1286→	defer a.mu.RUnlock()
  1287→	return a.js != nil, a.nleafs+a.nrleafs == 0
  1288→}

  1290→// Request for current usage and limits for this account.
  1291→func (s *Server) jsAccountInfoRequest(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  1292→	if c == nil || !s.JetStreamEnabled() {
  1293→		return
  1294→	}
  1295→
  1296→	ci, acc, hdr, msg, err := s.getRequestInfo(c, rmsg)
  1297→	if err != nil {
  1298→		s.Warnf(badAPIRequestT, msg)
  1299→		return
  1300→	}
  1301→
  1302→	var resp = JSApiAccountInfoResponse{ApiResponse: ApiResponse{Type: JSApiAccountInfoResponseType}}
  1303→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  1304→		resp.Error = NewJSRequiredApiLevelError()
  1305→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1306→		return
  1307→	}
  1308→
  1309→	// Determine if we should proceed here when we are in clustered mode.
  1310→	if s.JetStreamIsClustered() {
  1311→		js, cc := s.getJetStreamCluster()
  1312→		if js == nil || cc == nil {
  1313→			return
  1314→		}
  1315→		if js.isLeaderless() {
  1316→			resp.Error = NewJSClusterNotAvailError()
  1317→			s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1318→			return
  1319→		}
  1320→		// Make sure we are meta leader.
  1321→		if !s.JetStreamIsLeader() {
  1322→			return
  1323→		}
  1324→	}
  1325→
  1326→	if hasJS, doErr := acc.checkJetStream(); !hasJS {
  1327→		if !doErr {
  1328→			return
  1329→		}
  1330→		resp.Error = NewJSNotEnabledForAccountError()
  1331→	} else {
  1332→		stats := acc.JetStreamUsage()
  1333→		resp.JetStreamAccountStats = &stats
  1334→	}
  1335→	b, err := json.Marshal(resp)
  1336→	if err != nil {
  1337→		return
  1338→	}
  1339→
  1340→	s.sendAPIResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), string(b))
  1341→}

  1356→// Request to create a new template.
  1357→// Deprecated: stream templates are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
  1358→func (s *Server) jsTemplateCreateRequest(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  1359→	if c == nil {
  1360→		return
  1361→	}
  1362→	ci, acc, hdr, msg, err := s.getRequestInfo(c, rmsg)
  1363→	if err != nil {
  1364→		s.Warnf(badAPIRequestT, msg)
  1365→		return
  1366→	}
  1367→
  1368→	var resp = JSApiStreamTemplateCreateResponse{ApiResponse: ApiResponse{Type: JSApiStreamTemplateCreateResponseType}}
  1369→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  1370→		resp.Error = NewJSRequiredApiLevelError()
  1371→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1372→		return
  1373→	}
  1374→	if !acc.JetStreamEnabled() {
  1375→		resp.Error = NewJSNotEnabledForAccountError()
  1376→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1377→		return
  1378→	}
  1379→
  1380→	// Not supported for now.
  1381→	if s.JetStreamIsClustered() {
  1382→		resp.Error = NewJSClusterUnSupportFeatureError()
  1383→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1384→		return
  1385→	}
  1386→
  1387→	var cfg StreamTemplateConfig
  1388→	if err := s.unmarshalRequest(c, acc, subject, msg, &cfg); err != nil {
  1389→		resp.Error = NewJSInvalidJSONError(err)
  1390→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1391→		return
  1392→	}
  1393→	templateName := templateNameFromSubject(subject)
  1394→	if templateName != cfg.Name {
  1395→		resp.Error = NewJSTemplateNameNotMatchSubjectError()
  1396→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1397→		return
  1398→	}
  1399→
  1400→	t, err := acc.addStreamTemplate(&cfg)
  1401→	if err != nil {
  1402→		resp.Error = NewJSStreamTemplateCreateError(err, Unless(err))
  1403→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1404→		return
  1405→	}
  1406→	t.mu.Lock()
  1407→	tcfg := t.StreamTemplateConfig.deepCopy()
  1408→	streams := t.streams
  1409→	if streams == nil {
  1410→		streams = []string{}
  1411→	}
  1412→	t.mu.Unlock()
  1413→	resp.StreamTemplateInfo = &StreamTemplateInfo{Config: tcfg, Streams: streams}
  1414→	s.sendAPIResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(resp))
  1415→}

  1417→// Request for the list of all template names.
  1418→// Deprecated: stream templates are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
  1419→func (s *Server) jsTemplateNamesRequest(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  1420→	if c == nil {
  1421→		return
  1422→	}
  1423→	ci, acc, hdr, msg, err := s.getRequestInfo(c, rmsg)
  1424→	if err != nil {
  1425→		s.Warnf(badAPIRequestT, msg)
  1426→		return
  1427→	}
  1428→
  1429→	var resp = JSApiStreamTemplateNamesResponse{ApiResponse: ApiResponse{Type: JSApiStreamTemplateNamesResponseType}}
  1430→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  1431→		resp.Error = NewJSRequiredApiLevelError()
  1432→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1433→		return
  1434→	}
  1435→	if !acc.JetStreamEnabled() {
  1436→		resp.Error = NewJSNotEnabledForAccountError()
  1437→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1438→		return
  1439→	}
  1440→
  1441→	// Not supported for now.
  1442→	if s.JetStreamIsClustered() {
  1443→		resp.Error = NewJSClusterUnSupportFeatureError()
  1444→		s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1445→		return
  1446→	}
  1447→
  1448→	var offset int
  1449→	if isJSONObjectOrArray(msg) {
  1450→		var req JSApiStreamTemplatesRequest
  1451→		if err := s.unmarshalRequest(c, acc, subject, msg, &req); err != nil {
  1452→			resp.Error = NewJSInvalidJSONError(err)
  1453→			s.sendAPIErrResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(&resp))
  1454→			return
  1455→		}
  1456→		offset = req.Offset
  1457→	}
  1458→
  1459→	ts := acc.templates()
  1460→	slices.SortFunc(ts, func(i, j *streamTemplate) int {
  1461→		return cmp.Compare(i.StreamTemplateConfig.Name, j.StreamTemplateConfig.Name)
  1462→	})
  1463→
  1464→	tcnt := len(ts)
  1465	if offset > tcnt {
  1466→		offset = tcnt
  1467→	}
  1468→
  1469→	for _, t := range ts[offset:] {
  1470→		t.mu.Lock()
  1471→		name := t.Name
  1472→		t.mu.Unlock()
  1473→		resp.Templates = append(resp.Templates, name)
  1474→		if len(resp.Templates) >= JSApiNamesLimit {
  1475→			break
  1476→		}
  1477→	}
  1478→	resp.Total = tcnt
  1479→	resp.Limit = JSApiNamesLimit
  1480→	resp.Offset = offset
  1481→	if resp.Templates == nil {
  1482→		resp.Templates = []string{}
  1483→	}
  1484→	s.sendAPIResponse(ci, acc, subject, reply, string(msg), s.jsonResponse(resp))
  1485→}

  1501→		resp.Error = NewJSRequiredApiLevelError()

  1509→	}
  1510→	if !isEmptyRequest(msg) {
  1511→		resp.Error = NewJSNotEmptyRequestError()

  1522→	t.mu.Lock()

  1530→	resp.StreamTemplateInfo = &StreamTemplateInfo{Config: cfg, Streams: streams}

  1581→
  1582→// Read lock must be held
  1583→func (jsa *jsAccount) tieredReservation(tier string, cfg *StreamConfig) int64 {
  1584→	reservation := int64(0)
  1585→	if tier == _EMPTY_ {
  1586→		for _, sa := range jsa.streams {
  1587→			if sa.cfg.MaxBytes > 0 {
  1588→				if sa.cfg.Storage == cfg.Storage && sa.cfg.Name != cfg.Name {
  1589→					reservation += (int64(sa.cfg.Replicas) * sa.cfg.MaxBytes)
  1590→				}
  1591→			}
  1592→		}
  1593→	} else {
  1594→		for _, sa := range jsa.streams {
  1595→			if sa.cfg.Replicas == cfg.Replicas {
  1596→				if sa.cfg.MaxBytes > 0 {
  1597→					if isSameTier(&sa.cfg, cfg) && sa.cfg.Name != cfg.Name {
  1598→						reservation += (int64(sa.cfg.Replicas) * sa.cfg.MaxBytes)
  1599→					}
  1600→				}
  1601→			}
  1602→		}
  1603→	}
  1604→	return reservation
  1605→}
