   567→// Helper function to set consumer config defaults from above.
   568→func setConsumerConfigDefaults(config *ConsumerConfig, streamCfg *StreamConfig, lim *JSLimitOpts, accLim *JetStreamAccountLimits, pedantic bool) *ApiError {
   569→	// Setup default of -1, meaning no limit for MaxDeliver.
   570→	if config.MaxDeliver == 0 || config.MaxDeliver < -1 {
   571→		if pedantic && config.MaxDeliver < -1 {
   572→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_deliver must be set to -1"))
   573→		}
   574→		config.MaxDeliver = -1
   575→	}
   576→	// Setup zero defaults.
   577→	if config.MaxWaiting < 0 {
   578→		if pedantic {
   579→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_waiting must not be negative"))
   580→		}
   581→		config.MaxWaiting = 0
   582→	}
   583→	if config.MaxAckPending < -1 {
   584→		if pedantic {
   585→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_ack_pending must be set to -1"))
   586→		}
   587→		config.MaxAckPending = -1
   588→	}
   589→	if config.MaxRequestBatch < 0 {
   590→		if pedantic {
   591→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_batch must not be negative"))
   592→		}
   593→		config.MaxRequestBatch = 0
   594→	}
   595→	if config.MaxRequestExpires < 0 {
   596→		if pedantic {
   597→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_expires must not be negative"))
   598→		}
   599→		config.MaxRequestExpires = 0
   600→	}
   601→	if config.MaxRequestMaxBytes < 0 {
   602→		if pedantic {
   603→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_bytes must not be negative"))
   604→		}
   605→		config.MaxRequestMaxBytes = 0
   606→	}
   607→	if config.Heartbeat < 0 {
   608→		if pedantic {
   609→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("idle_heartbeat must not be negative"))
   610→		}
   611→		config.Heartbeat = 0
   612→	}
   613→	if config.InactiveThreshold < 0 {
   614→		if pedantic {
   615→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("inactive_threshold must not be negative"))
   616→		}
   617→		config.InactiveThreshold = 0
   618→	}
   619→	if config.PinnedTTL < 0 {
   620→		if pedantic {
   621→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("priority_timeout must not be negative"))
   622→		}
   623→		config.PinnedTTL = 0
   624→	}
   625→
   626→	// Set to default if not specified.
   627→	if config.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ && config.MaxWaiting == 0 {
   628→		config.MaxWaiting = JSWaitQueueDefaultMax
   629→	}
   630→	// Setup proper default for ack wait if we are in explicit ack mode.
   631→	if config.AckWait == 0 && (config.AckPolicy == AckExplicit || config.AckPolicy == AckAll) {
   632→		config.AckWait = JsAckWaitDefault
   633→	}
   634→	// If BackOff was specified that will override the AckWait and the MaxDeliver.
   635→	if len(config.BackOff) > 0 {
   636→		if pedantic && config.AckWait != config.BackOff[0] {
   637→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("first backoff value has to equal batch AckWait"))
   638→		}
   639→		config.AckWait = config.BackOff[0]
   640→	}
   641→	if config.MaxAckPending == 0 {
   642→		if pedantic && streamCfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending > 0 {
   643→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_ack_pending must be set if it's configured in stream limits"))
   644→		}
   645→		config.MaxAckPending = streamCfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending
   646→	}
   647→	if config.InactiveThreshold == 0 {
   648→		if pedantic && streamCfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold > 0 {
   649→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("inactive_threshold must be set if it's configured in stream limits"))
   650→		}
   651→		config.InactiveThreshold = streamCfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold
   652→	}
   653→	// Set proper default for max ack pending if we are ack explicit and none has been set.
   654→	if (config.AckPolicy == AckExplicit || config.AckPolicy == AckAll) && config.MaxAckPending == 0 {
   655→		ackPending := JsDefaultMaxAckPending
   656→		if lim.MaxAckPending > 0 && lim.MaxAckPending < ackPending {
   657→			ackPending = lim.MaxAckPending
   658→		}
   659→		if accLim.MaxAckPending > 0 && accLim.MaxAckPending < ackPending {
   660→			ackPending = accLim.MaxAckPending
   661→		}
   662→		config.MaxAckPending = ackPending
   663→	}
   664→	// if applicable set max request batch size
   665→	if config.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ && config.MaxRequestBatch == 0 && lim.MaxRequestBatch > 0 {
   666→		if pedantic {
   667→			return NewJSPedanticError(errors.New("max_request_batch must be set if it's JetStream limits are set"))
   668→		}
   669→		config.MaxRequestBatch = lim.MaxRequestBatch
   670→	}
   671→
   672→	// set the default value only if pinned policy is used.
   673→	if config.PriorityPolicy == PriorityPinnedClient && config.PinnedTTL == 0 {
   674→		config.PinnedTTL = JsDefaultPinnedTTL
   675→	}
   676→	return nil
   677→}
   678→
   679→// Check the consumer config. If we are recovering don't check filter subjects.
   680→func checkConsumerCfg(
   681→	config *ConsumerConfig,
   682→	srvLim *JSLimitOpts,
   683→	cfg *StreamConfig,
   684→	_ *Account,
   685→	accLim *JetStreamAccountLimits,
   686→	isRecovering bool,
   687→) *ApiError {
   688→
   689→	// Check if replicas is defined but exceeds parent stream.
   690→	if config.Replicas > 0 && config.Replicas > cfg.Replicas {
   691→		return NewJSConsumerReplicasExceedsStreamError()
   692→	}
   693→	// Check that it is not negative
   694→	if config.Replicas < 0 {
   695→		return NewJSReplicasCountCannotBeNegativeError()
   696→	}
   697→	// If the stream is interest or workqueue retention make sure the replicas
   698→	// match that of the stream. This is REQUIRED for now.
   699→	if cfg.Retention == InterestPolicy || cfg.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy {
   700→		// Only error here if not recovering.
   701→		// We handle recovering in a different spot to allow consumer to come up
   702→		// if previous version allowed it to be created. We do not want it to not come up.
   703→		if !isRecovering && config.Replicas != 0 && config.Replicas != cfg.Replicas {
   704→			return NewJSConsumerReplicasShouldMatchStreamError()
   705→		}
   706→	}
   707→
   708→	if _, err := config.AckPolicy.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
   709→		return NewJSConsumerAckPolicyInvalidError()
   710→	}
   711→	if _, err := config.ReplayPolicy.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
   712→		return NewJSConsumerReplayPolicyInvalidError()
   713→	}
   714→
   715→	// Check not negative AckWait/BackOff
   716→	for _, backoff := range config.BackOff {
   717→		if backoff < 0 {
   718→			return NewJSConsumerBackOffNegativeError()
   719→		}
   720→	}
   721→	if config.AckWait < 0 {
   722→		return NewJSConsumerAckWaitNegativeError()
   723→	}
   724→
   725→	// Check if we have a BackOff defined that MaxDeliver is within range etc.
   726→	if lbo := len(config.BackOff); lbo > 0 && config.MaxDeliver != -1 && lbo > config.MaxDeliver {
   727→		return NewJSConsumerMaxDeliverBackoffError()
   728→	}
   729→
   730→	if len(config.Description) > JSMaxDescriptionLen {
   731→		return NewJSConsumerDescriptionTooLongError(JSMaxDescriptionLen)
   732→	}
   733→
   734→	// For now expect a literal subject if its not empty. Empty means work queue mode (pull mode).
   735→	if config.DeliverSubject != _EMPTY_ {
   736→		if !subjectIsLiteral(config.DeliverSubject) {
   737→			return NewJSConsumerDeliverToWildcardsError()
   738→		}
   739→		if !IsValidSubject(config.DeliverSubject) {
   740→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidDeliverSubjectError()
   741→		}
   742→		if deliveryFormsCycle(cfg, config.DeliverSubject) {
   743→			return NewJSConsumerDeliverCycleError()
   744→		}
   745→		if config.MaxWaiting != 0 {
   746→			return NewJSConsumerPushMaxWaitingError()
   747→		}
   748→		if config.MaxAckPending > 0 && config.AckPolicy == AckNone {
   749→			return NewJSConsumerMaxPendingAckPolicyRequiredError()
   750→		}
   751→		if config.Heartbeat > 0 && config.Heartbeat < 100*time.Millisecond {
   752→			return NewJSConsumerSmallHeartbeatError()
   753→		}
   754→	} else {
   755→		// Pull mode with work queue retention from the stream requires an explicit ack.
   756→		if config.AckPolicy == AckNone && cfg.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy {
   757→			return NewJSConsumerPullRequiresAckError()
   758→		}
   759→		if config.RateLimit > 0 {
   760→			return NewJSConsumerPullWithRateLimitError()
   761→		}
   762→		if config.MaxWaiting < 0 {
   763→			return NewJSConsumerMaxWaitingNegativeError()
   764→		}
   765→		if config.Heartbeat > 0 {
   766→			return NewJSConsumerHBRequiresPushError()
   767→		}
   768→		if config.FlowControl {
   769→			return NewJSConsumerFCRequiresPushError()
   770→		}
   771→		if config.MaxRequestBatch < 0 {
   772→			return NewJSConsumerMaxRequestBatchNegativeError()
   773→		}
   774→		if config.MaxRequestExpires != 0 && config.MaxRequestExpires < time.Millisecond {
   775→			return NewJSConsumerMaxRequestExpiresTooSmallError()
   776→		}
   777→		if srvLim.MaxRequestBatch > 0 && config.MaxRequestBatch > srvLim.MaxRequestBatch {
   778→			return NewJSConsumerMaxRequestBatchExceededError(srvLim.MaxRequestBatch)
   779→		}
   780→	}
   781→	if srvLim.MaxAckPending > 0 && config.MaxAckPending > srvLim.MaxAckPending {
   782→		return NewJSConsumerMaxPendingAckExcessError(srvLim.MaxAckPending)
   783→	}
   784→	if accLim.MaxAckPending > 0 && config.MaxAckPending > accLim.MaxAckPending {
   785→		return NewJSConsumerMaxPendingAckExcessError(accLim.MaxAckPending)
   786→	}
   787→	if cfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending > 0 && config.MaxAckPending > cfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending {
   788→		return NewJSConsumerMaxPendingAckExcessError(cfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending)
   789→	}
   790→	if cfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold > 0 && config.InactiveThreshold > cfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold {
   791→		return NewJSConsumerInactiveThresholdExcessError(cfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold)
   792→	}
   793→
   794→	// Direct need to be non-mapped ephemerals.
   795→	if config.Direct {
   796→		if config.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ {
   797→			return NewJSConsumerDirectRequiresPushError()
   798→		}
   799→		if isDurableConsumer(config) {
   800→			return NewJSConsumerDirectRequiresEphemeralError()
   801→		}
   802→	}
   803→
   804→	// Do not allow specifying both FilterSubject and FilterSubjects,
   805→	// as that's probably unintentional without any difference from passing
   806→	// all filters in FilterSubjects.
   807→	if config.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && len(config.FilterSubjects) > 0 {
   808→		return NewJSConsumerDuplicateFilterSubjectsError()
   809→	}
   810→
   811→	if config.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(config.FilterSubject) {
   812→		return NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(ErrBadSubject)
   813→	}
   814→
   815→	// We treat FilterSubjects: []string{""} as a misconfig, so we validate against it.
   816→	for _, filter := range config.FilterSubjects {
   817→		if filter == _EMPTY_ {
   818→			return NewJSConsumerEmptyFilterError()
   819→		}
   820→	}
   821→	subjectFilters := gatherSubjectFilters(config.FilterSubject, config.FilterSubjects)
   822→
   823→	// Check subject filters do not overlap.
   824→	for outer, subject := range subjectFilters {
   825→		if !IsValidSubject(subject) {
   826→			return NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(ErrBadSubject)
   827→		}
   828→		for inner, ssubject := range subjectFilters {
   829→			if inner != outer && SubjectsCollide(subject, ssubject) {
   830→				return NewJSConsumerOverlappingSubjectFiltersError()
   831→			}
   832→		}
   833→	}
   834→
   835→	// Helper function to formulate similar errors.
   836→	badStart := func(dp, start string) error {
   837→		return fmt.Errorf("consumer delivery policy is deliver %s, but optional start %s is also set", dp, start)
   838→	}
   839→	notSet := func(dp, notSet string) error {
   840→		return fmt.Errorf("consumer delivery policy is deliver %s, but optional %s is not set", dp, notSet)
   841→	}
   842→
   843→	// Check on start position conflicts.
   844→	switch config.DeliverPolicy {
   845→	case DeliverAll:
   846→		if config.OptStartSeq > 0 {
   847→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("all", "sequence"))
   848→		}
   849→		if config.OptStartTime != nil {
   850→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("all", "time"))
   851→		}
   852→	case DeliverLast:
   853→		if config.OptStartSeq > 0 {
   854→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("last", "sequence"))
   855→		}
   856→		if config.OptStartTime != nil {
   857→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("last", "time"))
   858→		}
   859→	case DeliverLastPerSubject:
   860→		if config.OptStartSeq > 0 {
   861→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("last per subject", "sequence"))
   862→		}
   863→		if config.OptStartTime != nil {
   864→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("last per subject", "time"))
   865→		}
   866→		if config.FilterSubject == _EMPTY_ && len(config.FilterSubjects) == 0 {
   867→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(notSet("last per subject", "filter subject"))
   868→		}
   869→	case DeliverNew:
   870→		if config.OptStartSeq > 0 {
   871→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("new", "sequence"))
   872→		}
   873→		if config.OptStartTime != nil {
   874→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("new", "time"))
   875→		}
   876→	case DeliverByStartSequence:
   877→		if config.OptStartSeq == 0 {
   878→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(notSet("by start sequence", "start sequence"))
   879→		}
   880→		if config.OptStartTime != nil {
   881→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("by start sequence", "time"))
   882→		}
   883→	case DeliverByStartTime:
   884→		if config.OptStartTime == nil {
   885→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(notSet("by start time", "start time"))
   886→		}
   887→		if config.OptStartSeq != 0 {
   888→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidPolicyError(badStart("by start time", "start sequence"))
   889→		}
   890→	}
   891→
   892→	if config.SampleFrequency != _EMPTY_ {
   893→		s := strings.TrimSuffix(config.SampleFrequency, "%")
   894→		if sampleFreq, err := strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil || sampleFreq < 0 {
   895→			return NewJSConsumerInvalidSamplingError(err)
   896→		}
   897→	}
   898→
   899→	// We reject if flow control is set without heartbeats.
   900→	if config.FlowControl && config.Heartbeat == 0 {
   901→		return NewJSConsumerWithFlowControlNeedsHeartbeatsError()
   902→	}
   903→
   904→	if config.Durable != _EMPTY_ && config.Name != _EMPTY_ {
   905→		if config.Name != config.Durable {
   906→			return NewJSConsumerCreateDurableAndNameMismatchError()
   907→		}
   908→	}
   909→
   910→	var metadataLen int
   911→	for k, v := range config.Metadata {
   912→		metadataLen += len(k) + len(v)
   913→	}
   914→	if metadataLen > JSMaxMetadataLen {
   915→		return NewJSConsumerMetadataLengthError(fmt.Sprintf("%dKB", JSMaxMetadataLen/1024))
   916→	}
   917→
   918→	if config.PriorityPolicy != PriorityNone {
   919→		if config.DeliverSubject != "" {
   920→			return NewJSConsumerPushWithPriorityGroupError()
   921→		}
   922→		if len(config.PriorityGroups) == 0 {
   923→			return NewJSConsumerPriorityPolicyWithoutGroupError()
   924→		}
   925→
   926→		for _, group := range config.PriorityGroups {
   927→			if group == _EMPTY_ {
   928→				return NewJSConsumerEmptyGroupNameError()
   929→			}
   930→			if !validGroupName.MatchString(group) {
   931→				return NewJSConsumerInvalidGroupNameError()
   932→			}
   933→		}
   934→	} else {
   935→		// If PriorityPolicy is None or not set, reject if PriorityGroups or PinnedTTL are set
   936→		if len(config.PriorityGroups) > 0 {
   937→			return NewJSConsumerPriorityGroupWithPolicyNoneError()
   938→		}
   939→		if config.PinnedTTL > 0 {
   940→			return NewJSConsumerPinnedTTLWithoutPriorityPolicyNoneError()
   941→		}
   942→	}
   943→
   944→	// For now don't allow preferred server in placement.
   945→	if cfg.Placement != nil && cfg.Placement.Preferred != _EMPTY_ {
   946→		return NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("preferred server not permitted in placement"))
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	return nil
   950→}
   951→
   952→func (mset *stream) addConsumerWithAction(config *ConsumerConfig, action ConsumerAction, pedantic bool) (*consumer, error) {
   953→	return mset.addConsumerWithAssignment(config, _EMPTY_, nil, false, action, pedantic)
   954→}
   955→
   956→func (mset *stream) addConsumer(config *ConsumerConfig) (*consumer, error) {
   957→	return mset.addConsumerWithAction(config, ActionCreateOrUpdate, false)
   958→}
   959→
   960→func (mset *stream) addConsumerWithAssignment(config *ConsumerConfig, oname string, ca *consumerAssignment, isRecovering bool, action ConsumerAction, pedantic bool) (*consumer, error) {
   961→	// Check if this stream has closed.
   962→	if mset.closed.Load() {
   963→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidError()
   964→	}
   965→
   966→	mset.mu.RLock()
   967→	s, jsa, cfg, acc := mset.srv, mset.jsa, mset.cfg, mset.acc
   968→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
   969→
   970→	// If we do not have the consumer currently assigned to us in cluster mode we will proceed but warn.
   971→	// This can happen on startup with restored state where on meta replay we still do not have
   972→	// the assignment. Running in single server mode this always returns true.
   973→	if oname != _EMPTY_ && !jsa.consumerAssigned(mset.name(), oname) {
   974→		s.Debugf("Consumer %q > %q does not seem to be assigned to this server", mset.name(), oname)
   975→	}
   976→
   977→	if config == nil {
   978→		return nil, NewJSConsumerConfigRequiredError()
   979→	}
   980→
   981→	selectedLimits, _, _, _ := acc.selectLimits(config.replicas(&cfg))
   982→	if selectedLimits == nil {
   983→		return nil, NewJSNoLimitsError()
   984→	}
   985→
   986→	srvLim := &s.getOpts().JetStreamLimits
   987→	// Make sure we have sane defaults. Do so with the JS lock, otherwise a
   988→	// badly timed meta snapshot can result in a race condition.
   989→	mset.js.mu.Lock()
   990→	err := setConsumerConfigDefaults(config, &cfg, srvLim, selectedLimits, pedantic)
   991→	mset.js.mu.Unlock()
   992→	if err != nil {
   993→		return nil, err
   994→	}
   995→
   996→	if err := checkConsumerCfg(config, srvLim, &cfg, acc, selectedLimits, isRecovering); err != nil {
   997→		return nil, err
   998→	}
   999→	sampleFreq := 0
  1000→	if config.SampleFrequency != _EMPTY_ {
  1001→		// Can't fail as checkConsumerCfg checks correct format
  1002→		sampleFreq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSuffix(config.SampleFrequency, "%"))
  1003→	}
  1004→
  1005→	// Grab the client, account and server reference.
  1006→	c := mset.client
  1007→	if c == nil {
  1008→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidError()
  1009→	}
  1010→	var accName string
  1011→	c.mu.Lock()
  1012→	s, a := c.srv, c.acc
  1013→	if a != nil {
  1014→		accName = a.Name
  1015→	}
  1016→	c.mu.Unlock()
  1017→
  1018→	// Hold mset lock here.
  1019→	mset.mu.Lock()
  1020→	if mset.client == nil || mset.store == nil || mset.consumers == nil {
  1021→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  1022→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidError()
  1023→	}
  1024→
  1025→	// If this one is durable and already exists, we let that be ok as long as only updating what should be allowed.
  1026→	var cName string
  1027→	if isDurableConsumer(config) {
  1028→		cName = config.Durable
  1029→	} else if config.Name != _EMPTY_ {
  1030→		cName = config.Name
  1031→	}
  1032→	if cName != _EMPTY_ {
  1033→		if eo, ok := mset.consumers[cName]; ok {
  1034→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1035→			if action == ActionCreate {
  1036→				ocfg := eo.config()
  1037→				copyConsumerMetadata(config, &ocfg)
  1038→				if !reflect.DeepEqual(config, &ocfg) {
  1039→					return nil, NewJSConsumerAlreadyExistsError()
  1040→				}
  1041→			}
  1042→			// Check for overlapping subjects if we are a workqueue
  1043→			if cfg.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy {
  1044→				subjects := gatherSubjectFilters(config.FilterSubject, config.FilterSubjects)
  1045→				if !mset.partitionUnique(cName, subjects) {
  1046→					return nil, NewJSConsumerWQConsumerNotUniqueError()
  1047→				}
  1048→			}
  1049→			err := eo.updateConfig(config)
  1050→			if err == nil {
  1051→				return eo, nil
  1052→			}
  1053→			return nil, NewJSConsumerCreateError(err, Unless(err))
  1054→		}
  1055→	}
  1056→	if action == ActionUpdate {
  1057→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  1058→		return nil, NewJSConsumerDoesNotExistError()
  1059→	}
  1060→
  1061→	// Check for any limits, if the config for the consumer sets a limit we check against that
  1062→	// but if not we use the value from account limits, if account limits is more restrictive
  1063→	// than stream config we prefer the account limits to handle cases where account limits are
  1064→	// updated during the lifecycle of the stream
  1065→	maxc := cfg.MaxConsumers
  1066→	if maxc <= 0 || (selectedLimits.MaxConsumers > 0 && selectedLimits.MaxConsumers < maxc) {
  1067→		maxc = selectedLimits.MaxConsumers
  1068→	}
  1069→	if maxc > 0 && mset.numPublicConsumers() >= maxc {
  1070→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  1071→		return nil, NewJSMaximumConsumersLimitError()
  1072→	}
  1073→
  1074→	// Check on stream type conflicts with WorkQueues.
  1075→	if cfg.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy && !config.Direct {
  1076→		// Force explicit acks here.
  1077→		if config.AckPolicy != AckExplicit {
  1078→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1079→			return nil, NewJSConsumerWQRequiresExplicitAckError()
  1080→		}
  1081→
  1082→		if len(mset.consumers) > 0 {
  1083→			subjects := gatherSubjectFilters(config.FilterSubject, config.FilterSubjects)
  1084→			if len(subjects) == 0 {
  1085→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  1086→				return nil, NewJSConsumerWQMultipleUnfilteredError()
  1087→			} else if !mset.partitionUnique(cName, subjects) {
  1088→				// Prior to v2.9.7, on a stream with WorkQueue policy, the servers
  1089→				// were not catching the error of having multiple consumers with
  1090→				// overlapping filter subjects depending on the scope, for instance
  1091→				// creating "foo.*.bar" and then "foo.>" was not detected, while
  1092→				// "foo.>" and then "foo.*.bar" would have been. Failing here
  1093→				// in recovery mode would leave the rejected consumer in a bad state,
  1094→				// so we will simply warn here, asking the user to remove this
  1095→				// consumer administratively. Otherwise, if this is the creation
  1096→				// of a new consumer, we will return the error.
  1097→				if isRecovering {
  1098→					s.Warnf("Consumer %q > %q has a filter subject that overlaps "+
  1099→						"with other consumers, which is not allowed for a stream "+
  1100→						"with WorkQueue policy, it should be administratively deleted",
  1101→						cfg.Name, cName)
  1102→				} else {
  1103→					// We have a partition but it is not unique amongst the others.
  1104→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  1105→					return nil, NewJSConsumerWQConsumerNotUniqueError()
  1106→				}
  1107→			}
  1108→		}
  1109→		if config.DeliverPolicy != DeliverAll {
  1110→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1111→			return nil, NewJSConsumerWQConsumerNotDeliverAllError()
  1112→		}
  1113→	}
  1114→
  1115→	// Set name, which will be durable name if set, otherwise we create one at random.
  1116→	o := &consumer{
  1117→		mset:      mset,
  1118→		js:        s.getJetStream(),
  1119→		acc:       a,
  1120→		srv:       s,
  1121→		client:    s.createInternalJetStreamClient(),
  1122→		sysc:      s.createInternalJetStreamClient(),
  1123→		cfg:       *config,
  1124→		dsubj:     config.DeliverSubject,
  1125→		outq:      mset.outq,
  1126→		active:    true,
  1127→		qch:       make(chan struct{}),
  1128→		uch:       make(chan struct{}, 1),
  1129→		mch:       make(chan struct{}, 1),
  1130→		sfreq:     int32(sampleFreq),
  1131→		maxdc:     uint64(max(config.MaxDeliver, 0)), // MaxDeliver is negative (-1) when infinite.
  1132→		maxp:      config.MaxAckPending,
  1133→		retention: cfg.Retention,
  1134→		created:   time.Now().UTC(),
  1135→	}
  1136→
  1137→	// Bind internal client to the user account.
  1138→	o.client.registerWithAccount(a)
  1139→	// Bind to the system account.
  1140→	o.sysc.registerWithAccount(s.SystemAccount())
  1141→
  1142→	if isDurableConsumer(config) {
  1143→		if len(config.Durable) > JSMaxNameLen {
  1144→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1145→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1146→			return nil, NewJSConsumerNameTooLongError(JSMaxNameLen)
  1147→		}
  1148→		o.name = config.Durable
  1149→	} else if oname != _EMPTY_ {
  1150→		o.name = oname
  1151→	} else {
  1152→		if config.Name != _EMPTY_ {
  1153→			o.name = config.Name
  1154→		} else {
  1155→			// Legacy ephemeral auto-generated.
  1156→			for {
  1157→				o.name = createConsumerName()
  1158→				if _, ok := mset.consumers[o.name]; !ok {
  1159→					break
  1160→				}
  1161→			}
  1162→			config.Name = o.name
  1163→		}
  1164→	}
  1165→	// Create ackMsgs queue now that we have a consumer name
  1166→	o.ackMsgs = newIPQueue[*jsAckMsg](s, fmt.Sprintf("[ACC:%s] consumer '%s' on stream '%s' ackMsgs", accName, o.name, cfg.Name))
  1167→
  1168→	// Create our request waiting queue.
  1169→	if o.isPullMode() {
  1170→		o.waiting = newWaitQueue(config.MaxWaiting)
  1171→		// Create our internal queue for next msg requests.
  1172→		o.nextMsgReqs = newIPQueue[*nextMsgReq](s, fmt.Sprintf("[ACC:%s] consumer '%s' on stream '%s' pull requests", accName, o.name, cfg.Name))
  1173→	}
  1174→
  1175→	// already under lock, mset.Name() would deadlock
  1176→	o.stream = cfg.Name
  1177→	o.ackEventT = JSMetricConsumerAckPre + "." + o.stream + "." + o.name
  1178→	o.nakEventT = JSAdvisoryConsumerMsgNakPre + "." + o.stream + "." + o.name
  1179→	o.deliveryExcEventT = JSAdvisoryConsumerMaxDeliveryExceedPre + "." + o.stream + "." + o.name
  1180→
  1181→	if !isValidName(o.name) {
  1182→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  1183→		o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1184→		return nil, NewJSConsumerBadDurableNameError()
  1185→	}
  1186→
  1187→	// Setup our storage if not a direct consumer.
  1188→	if !config.Direct {
  1189→		store, err := mset.store.ConsumerStore(o.name, config)
  1190→		if err != nil {
  1191→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1192→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1193→			return nil, NewJSConsumerStoreFailedError(err)
  1194→		}
  1195→		o.store = store
  1196→	}
  1197→
  1198→	for _, filter := range gatherSubjectFilters(o.cfg.FilterSubject, o.cfg.FilterSubjects) {
  1199→		sub := &subjectFilter{
  1200→			subject:          filter,
  1201→			hasWildcard:      subjectHasWildcard(filter),
  1202→			tokenizedSubject: tokenizeSubjectIntoSlice(nil, filter),
  1203→		}
  1204→		o.subjf = append(o.subjf, sub)
  1205→	}
  1206→
  1207→	// If we have multiple filter subjects, create a sublist which we will use
  1208→	// in calling store.LoadNextMsgMulti.
  1209→	if len(o.cfg.FilterSubjects) > 0 {
  1210→		o.filters = gsl.NewSublist[struct{}]()
  1211→		for _, filter := range o.cfg.FilterSubjects {
  1212→			o.filters.Insert(filter, struct{}{})
  1213→		}
  1214→	} else {
  1215→		// Make sure this is nil otherwise.
  1216→		o.filters = nil
  1217→	}
  1218→
  1219→	if o.store != nil && o.store.HasState() {
  1220→		// Restore our saved state.
  1221→		o.mu.Lock()
  1222→		o.readStoredState()
  1223→		o.mu.Unlock()
  1224→	} else {
  1225→		// Select starting sequence number
  1226→		o.selectStartingSeqNo()
  1227→	}
  1228→
  1229→	// Now register with mset and create the ack subscription.
  1230→	// Check if we already have this one registered.
  1231→	if eo, ok := mset.consumers[o.name]; ok {
  1232→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  1233→		if !o.isDurable() || !o.isPushMode() {
  1234→			o.name = _EMPTY_ // Prevent removal since same name.
  1235→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1236→			return nil, NewJSConsumerNameExistError()
  1237→		}
  1238→		// If we are here we have already registered this durable. If it is still active that is an error.
  1239→		if eo.isActive() {
  1240→			o.name = _EMPTY_ // Prevent removal since same name.
  1241→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1242→			return nil, NewJSConsumerExistingActiveError()
  1243→		}
  1244→		// Since we are here this means we have a potentially new durable so we should update here.
  1245→		// Check that configs are the same.
  1246→		if !configsEqualSansDelivery(o.cfg, eo.cfg) {
  1247→			o.name = _EMPTY_ // Prevent removal since same name.
  1248→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1249→			return nil, NewJSConsumerReplacementWithDifferentNameError()
  1250→		}
  1251→		// Once we are here we have a replacement push-based durable.
  1252→		eo.updateDeliverSubject(o.cfg.DeliverSubject)
  1253→		return eo, nil
  1254→	}
  1255→
  1256→	// Set up the ack subscription for this consumer. Will use wildcard for all acks.
  1257→	// We will remember the template to generate replies with sequence numbers and use
  1258→	// that to scanf them back in.
  1259→	// Escape '%' in consumer and stream names, as `pre` is used as a template later
  1260→	// in consumer.ackReply(), resulting in erroneous formatting of the ack subject.
  1261→	mn := strings.ReplaceAll(cfg.Name, "%", "%%")
  1262→	pre := fmt.Sprintf(jsAckT, mn, strings.ReplaceAll(o.name, "%", "%%"))
  1263→	o.ackReplyT = fmt.Sprintf("%s.%%d.%%d.%%d.%%d.%%d", pre)
  1264→	o.ackSubj = fmt.Sprintf("%s.*.*.*.*.*", pre)
  1265→	o.nextMsgSubj = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiRequestNextT, mn, o.name)
  1266→
  1267→	// Check/update the inactive threshold
  1268→	o.updateInactiveThreshold(&o.cfg)
  1269→
  1270→	if o.isPushMode() {
  1271→		// Check if we are running only 1 replica and that the delivery subject has interest.
  1272→		// Check in place here for interest. Will setup properly in setLeader.
  1273→		if config.replicas(&cfg) == 1 {
  1274→			interest := o.acc.sl.HasInterest(o.cfg.DeliverSubject)
  1275→			if !o.hasDeliveryInterest(interest) {
  1276→				// Let the interest come to us eventually, but setup delete timer.
  1277→				o.updateDeliveryInterest(false)
  1278→			}
  1279→		}
  1280→	}
  1281→
  1282→	// Set our ca.
  1283→	if ca != nil {
  1284→		o.setConsumerAssignment(ca)
  1285→	}
  1286→
  1287→	// Check if we have a rate limit set.
  1288→	if config.RateLimit != 0 {
  1289→		o.setRateLimit(config.RateLimit)
  1290→	}
  1291→
  1292→	mset.setConsumer(o)
  1293→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  1294→
  1295→	if config.Direct || (!s.JetStreamIsClustered() && s.standAloneMode()) {
  1296→		o.setLeader(true)
  1297→	}
  1298→
  1299→	// This is always true in single server mode.
  1300→	if o.IsLeader() {
  1301→		// Send advisory.
  1302→		var suppress bool
  1303→		if !s.standAloneMode() && ca == nil {
  1304→			suppress = true
  1305→		} else if ca != nil {
  1306→			suppress = ca.responded
  1307→		}
  1308→		if !suppress {
  1309→			o.sendCreateAdvisory()
  1310→		}
  1311→	}
  1312→
  1313→	return o, nil
  1314→}
  1315→
  1316→// Updates the consumer `dthresh` delete timer duration and set
  1317→// cfg.InactiveThreshold to JsDeleteWaitTimeDefault for ephemerals
  1318→// if not explicitly already specified by the user.
  1319→// Lock should be held.
  1320→func (o *consumer) updateInactiveThreshold(cfg *ConsumerConfig) {
  1321→	// Ephemerals will always have inactive thresholds.
  1322→	if !o.isDurable() && cfg.InactiveThreshold <= 0 {
  1323→		// Add in 1 sec of jitter above and beyond the default of 5s.
  1324→		o.dthresh = JsDeleteWaitTimeDefault + 100*time.Millisecond + time.Duration(rand.Int63n(900))*time.Millisecond
  1325→		// Only stamp config with default sans jitter.
  1326→		cfg.InactiveThreshold = JsDeleteWaitTimeDefault
  1327→	} else if cfg.InactiveThreshold > 0 {
  1328→		// Add in up to 1 sec of jitter if pull mode.
  1329→		if o.isPullMode() {
  1330→			o.dthresh = cfg.InactiveThreshold + 100*time.Millisecond + time.Duration(rand.Int63n(900))*time.Millisecond
  1331→		} else {
  1332→			o.dthresh = cfg.InactiveThreshold
  1333→		}
  1334→	} else if cfg.InactiveThreshold <= 0 {
  1335→		// We accept InactiveThreshold be set to 0 (for durables)
  1336→		o.dthresh = 0
  1337→	}
  1338→}
  1339→
  1340→// Updates the paused state. If we are the leader and the pause deadline
  1341→// hasn't passed yet then we will start a timer to kick the consumer once
  1342→// that deadline is reached. Lock should be held.
  1343→func (o *consumer) updatePauseState(cfg *ConsumerConfig) {
  1344→	if o.uptmr != nil {
  1345→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.uptmr)
  1346→	}
  1347→	if !o.isLeader() {
  1348→		// Only the leader will run the timer as only the leader will run
  1349→		// loopAndGatherMsgs.
  1350→		return
  1351→	}
  1352→	if cfg.PauseUntil == nil || cfg.PauseUntil.IsZero() || cfg.PauseUntil.Before(time.Now()) {
  1353→		// Either the PauseUntil is unset (is effectively zero) or the
  1354→		// deadline has already passed, in which case there is nothing
  1355→		// to do.
  1356→		return
  1357→	}
  1358→	o.uptmr = time.AfterFunc(time.Until(*cfg.PauseUntil), func() {
  1359→		o.mu.Lock()
  1360→		defer o.mu.Unlock()
  1361→
  1362→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.uptmr)
  1363→		o.sendPauseAdvisoryLocked(&o.cfg)
  1364→		o.signalNewMessages()
  1365→	})
  1366→}
  1367→
  1368→func (o *consumer) consumerAssignment() *consumerAssignment {
  1369→	o.mu.RLock()
  1370→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  1371→	return o.ca
  1372→}
  1373→
  1374→func (o *consumer) setConsumerAssignment(ca *consumerAssignment) {
  1375→	o.mu.Lock()
  1376→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  1377→
  1378→	o.ca = ca
  1379→	if ca == nil {
  1380→		return
  1381→	}
  1382→	// Set our node.
  1383→	o.node = ca.Group.node
  1384→
  1385→	// Trigger update chan.
  1386→	select {
  1387→	case o.uch <- struct{}{}:
  1388→	default:
  1389→	}
  1390→}
  1391→
  1392→func (o *consumer) updateC() <-chan struct{} {
  1393→	o.mu.RLock()
  1394→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  1395→	return o.uch
  1396→}
  1397→
  1398→// checkQueueInterest will check on our interest's queue group status.
  1399→// Lock should be held.
  1400→func (o *consumer) checkQueueInterest() {
  1401→	if !o.active || o.cfg.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ {
  1402→		return
  1403→	}
  1404→	subj := o.dsubj
  1405→	if subj == _EMPTY_ {
  1406→		subj = o.cfg.DeliverSubject
  1407→	}
  1408→
  1409→	if rr := o.acc.sl.Match(subj); len(rr.qsubs) > 0 {
  1410→		// Just grab first
  1411→		if qsubs := rr.qsubs[0]; len(qsubs) > 0 {
  1412→			if sub := rr.qsubs[0][0]; len(sub.queue) > 0 {
  1413→				o.qgroup = string(sub.queue)
  1414→			}
  1415→		}
  1416→	}
  1417→}
  1418→
  1419→// clears our node if we have one. When we scale down to 1.
  1420→func (o *consumer) clearNode() {
  1421→	o.mu.Lock()
  1422→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  1423→	if o.node != nil {
  1424→		o.node.Delete()
  1425→		o.node = nil
  1426→	}
  1427→}
  1428→
  1429→// IsLeader will return if we are the current leader.
  1430→func (o *consumer) IsLeader() bool {
  1431→	return o.isLeader()
  1432→}
  1433→
  1434→// Lock should be held.
  1435→func (o *consumer) isLeader() bool {
  1436→	return o.leader.Load()
  1437→}
  1438→
  1439→func (o *consumer) setLeader(isLeader bool) {
  1440→	o.mu.RLock()
  1441→	mset, closed := o.mset, o.closed
  1442→	movingToClustered := o.node != nil && o.pch == nil
  1443→	movingToNonClustered := o.node == nil && o.pch != nil
  1444→	wasLeader := o.leader.Swap(isLeader)
  1445→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  1446→
  1447→	// If we are here we have a change in leader status.
  1448→	if isLeader {
  1449→		if closed || mset == nil {
  1450→			return
  1451→		}
  1452→
  1453→		if wasLeader {
  1454→			// If we detect we are scaling up, make sure to create clustered routines and channels.
  1455→			if movingToClustered {
  1456→				o.mu.Lock()
  1457→				// We are moving from R1 to clustered.
  1458→				o.pch = make(chan struct{}, 1)
  1459→				go o.loopAndForwardProposals(o.qch)
  1460→				if o.phead != nil {
  1461→					select {
  1462→					case o.pch <- struct{}{}:
  1463→					default:
  1464→					}
  1465→				}
  1466→				o.mu.Unlock()
  1467→			} else if movingToNonClustered {
  1468→				// We are moving from clustered to non-clustered now.
  1469→				// Set pch to nil so if we scale back up we will recreate the loopAndForward from above.
  1470→				o.mu.Lock()
  1471→				pch := o.pch
  1472→				o.pch = nil
  1473→				select {
  1474→				case pch <- struct{}{}:
  1475→				default:
  1476→				}
  1477→				o.mu.Unlock()
  1478→			}
  1479→			return
  1480→		}
  1481→
  1482→		mset.mu.RLock()
  1483→		s, jsa, stream := mset.srv, mset.jsa, mset.getCfgName()
  1484→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
  1485→
  1486→		o.mu.Lock()
  1487→		o.rdq = nil
  1488→		o.rdqi.Empty()
  1489→
  1490→		// Restore our saved state.
  1491→		// During non-leader status we just update our underlying store when not clustered.
  1492→		// If clustered we need to propose our initial (possibly skipped ahead) o.sseq to the group.
  1493→		if o.node == nil || o.dseq > 1 || (o.store != nil && o.store.HasState()) {
  1494→			o.readStoredState()
  1495→		} else if o.node != nil && o.sseq >= 1 {
  1496→			o.updateSkipped(o.sseq)
  1497→		}
  1498→
  1499→		// Setup initial num pending.
  1500→		o.streamNumPending()
  1501→
  1502→		// Cleanup lss when we take over in clustered mode.
  1503→		if o.hasSkipListPending() && o.sseq >= o.lss.resume {
  1504→			o.lss = nil
  1505→		}
  1506→
  1507→		// Do info sub.
  1508→		if o.infoSub == nil && jsa != nil {
  1509→			isubj := fmt.Sprintf(clusterConsumerInfoT, jsa.acc(), stream, o.name)
  1510→			// Note below the way we subscribe here is so that we can send requests to ourselves.
  1511→			o.infoSub, _ = s.systemSubscribe(isubj, _EMPTY_, false, o.sysc, o.handleClusterConsumerInfoRequest)
  1512→		}
  1513→
  1514→		var err error
  1515→		if o.cfg.AckPolicy != AckNone {
  1516→			if o.ackSub, err = o.subscribeInternal(o.ackSubj, o.pushAck); err != nil {
  1517→				o.mu.Unlock()
  1518→				o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1519→				return
  1520→			}
  1521→		}
  1522→
  1523→		// Setup the internal sub for next message requests regardless.
  1524→		// Will error if wrong mode to provide feedback to users.
  1525→		if o.reqSub, err = o.subscribeInternal(o.nextMsgSubj, o.processNextMsgReq); err != nil {
  1526→			o.mu.Unlock()
  1527→			o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1528→			return
  1529→		}
  1530→
  1531→		// Check on flow control settings.
  1532→		if o.cfg.FlowControl {
  1533→			o.setMaxPendingBytes(JsFlowControlMaxPending)
  1534→			fcsubj := fmt.Sprintf(jsFlowControl, stream, o.name)
  1535→			if o.fcSub, err = o.subscribeInternal(fcsubj, o.processFlowControl); err != nil {
  1536→				o.mu.Unlock()
  1537→				o.deleteWithoutAdvisory()
  1538→				return
  1539→			}
  1540→		}
  1541→
  1542→		// If push mode, register for notifications on interest.
  1543→		if o.isPushMode() {
  1544→			o.inch = make(chan bool, 8)
  1545→			o.acc.sl.registerNotification(o.cfg.DeliverSubject, o.cfg.DeliverGroup, o.inch)
  1546→			if o.active = <-o.inch; o.active {
  1547→				o.checkQueueInterest()
  1548→			}
  1549→
  1550→			// Check gateways in case they are enabled.
  1551→			if s.gateway.enabled {
  1552→				if !o.active {
  1553→					o.active = s.hasGatewayInterest(o.acc.Name, o.cfg.DeliverSubject)
  1554→				}
  1555→				stopAndClearTimer(&o.gwdtmr)
  1556→				o.gwdtmr = time.AfterFunc(time.Second, func() { o.watchGWinterest() })
  1557→			}
  1558→		}
  1559→
  1560→		if o.dthresh > 0 && (o.isPullMode() || !o.active) {
  1561→			// Pull consumer. We run the dtmr all the time for this one.
  1562→			stopAndClearTimer(&o.dtmr)
  1563→			o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(o.dthresh, o.deleteNotActive)
  1564→		}
  1565→
  1566→		// Update the consumer pause tracking.
  1567→		o.updatePauseState(&o.cfg)
  1568→
  1569→		// If we are not in ReplayInstant mode mark us as in replay state until resolved.
  1570→		if o.cfg.ReplayPolicy != ReplayInstant {
  1571→			o.replay = true
  1572→		}
  1573→
  1574→		// Recreate quit channel.
  1575→		o.qch = make(chan struct{})
  1576→		qch := o.qch
  1577→		node := o.node
  1578→		if node != nil && o.pch == nil {
  1579→			o.pch = make(chan struct{}, 1)
  1580→		}
  1581→		pullMode := o.isPullMode()
  1582→		o.mu.Unlock()
  1583→
  1584→		// Check if there are any pending we might need to clean up etc.
  1585→		o.checkPending()
  1586→
  1587→		// Snapshot initial info.
  1588→		o.infoWithSnap(true)
  1589→
  1590→		// These are the labels we will use to annotate our goroutines.
  1591→		labels := pprofLabels{
  1592→			"type":     "consumer",
  1593→			"account":  mset.accName(),
  1594→			"stream":   mset.name(),
  1595→			"consumer": o.name,
  1596→		}
  1597→
  1598→		// Now start up Go routine to deliver msgs.
  1599→		go func() {
  1600→			setGoRoutineLabels(labels)
  1601→			o.loopAndGatherMsgs(qch)
  1602→		}()
  1603→
  1604→		// Now start up Go routine to process acks.
  1605→		go func() {
  1606→			setGoRoutineLabels(labels)
  1607→			o.processInboundAcks(qch)
  1608→		}()
  1609→
  1610→		if pullMode {
  1611→			// Now start up Go routine to process inbound next message requests.
  1612→			go func() {
  1613→				setGoRoutineLabels(labels)
  1614→				o.processInboundNextMsgReqs(qch)
  1615→			}()
  1616→		}
  1617→
  1618→		// If we are R>1 spin up our proposal loop.
  1619→		if node != nil {
  1620→			// Determine if we can send pending requests info to the group.
  1621→			// They must be on server versions >= 2.7.1
  1622→			o.checkAndSetPendingRequestsOk()
  1623→			o.checkPendingRequests()
  1624→			go func() {
  1625→				setGoRoutineLabels(labels)
  1626→				o.loopAndForwardProposals(qch)
  1627→			}()
  1628→		}
  1629→
  1630→	} else {
  1631→		// Shutdown the go routines and the subscriptions.
  1632→		o.mu.Lock()
  1633→		if o.qch != nil {
  1634→			close(o.qch)
  1635→			o.qch = nil
  1636→		}
  1637→		// Stop any inactivity timers. Should only be running on leaders.
  1638→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.dtmr)
  1639→		// Stop any unpause timers. Should only be running on leaders.
  1640→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.uptmr)
  1641→		// Make sure to clear out any re-deliver queues
  1642→		o.stopAndClearPtmr()
  1643→		o.rdq = nil
  1644→		o.rdqi.Empty()
  1645→		o.pending = nil
  1646→		o.resetPendingDeliveries()
  1647→		// ok if they are nil, we protect inside unsubscribe()
  1648→		o.unsubscribe(o.ackSub)
  1649→		o.unsubscribe(o.reqSub)
  1650→		o.unsubscribe(o.fcSub)
  1651→		o.ackSub, o.reqSub, o.fcSub = nil, nil, nil
  1652→		if o.infoSub != nil {
  1653→			o.srv.sysUnsubscribe(o.infoSub)
  1654→			o.infoSub = nil
  1655→		}
  1656→		// Reset waiting if we are in pull mode.
  1657→		if o.isPullMode() {
  1658→			o.waiting = newWaitQueue(o.cfg.MaxWaiting)
  1659→			o.nextMsgReqs.drain()
  1660→		} else if o.srv.gateway.enabled {
  1661→			stopAndClearTimer(&o.gwdtmr)
  1662→		}
  1663→		// If we were the leader make sure to drain queued up acks.
  1664→		if wasLeader {
  1665→			o.ackMsgs.drain()
  1666→			// Reset amount of acks that need to be processed.
  1667→			atomic.StoreInt64(&o.awl, 0)
  1668→			// Also remove any pending replies since we should not be the one to respond at this point.
  1669→			o.replies = nil
  1670→		}
  1671→		o.mu.Unlock()
  1672→	}
  1673→}
  1674→
  1675→// This is coming on the wire so do not block here.
  1676→func (o *consumer) handleClusterConsumerInfoRequest(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, msg []byte) {
  1677→	go o.infoWithSnapAndReply(false, reply)
  1678→}
  1679→
  1680→// Lock should be held.
  1681→func (o *consumer) subscribeInternal(subject string, cb msgHandler) (*subscription, error) {
  1682→	c := o.client
  1683→	if c == nil {
  1684→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid consumer")
  1685→	}
  1686→	if !c.srv.EventsEnabled() {
  1687→		return nil, ErrNoSysAccount
  1688→	}
  1689→	if cb == nil {
  1690→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("undefined message handler")
  1691→	}
  1692→
  1693→	o.sid++
  1694→
  1695→	// Now create the subscription
  1696→	return c.processSub([]byte(subject), nil, []byte(strconv.Itoa(o.sid)), cb, false)
  1697→}
  1698→
  1699→// Unsubscribe from our subscription.
  1700→// Lock should be held.
  1701→func (o *consumer) unsubscribe(sub *subscription) {
  1702→	if sub == nil || o.client == nil {
  1703→		return
  1704→	}
  1705→	o.client.processUnsub(sub.sid)
  1706→}
  1707→
  1708→// We need to make sure we protect access to the outq.
  1709→// Do all advisory sends here.
  1710→func (o *consumer) sendAdvisory(subject string, e any) {
  1711→	if o.acc == nil {
  1712→		return
  1713→	}
  1714→
  1715→	// If there is no one listening for this advisory then save ourselves the effort
  1716→	// and don't bother encoding the JSON or sending it.
  1717→	if sl := o.acc.sl; (sl != nil && !sl.HasInterest(subject)) && !o.srv.hasGatewayInterest(o.acc.Name, subject) {
  1718→		return
  1719→	}
  1720→
  1721→	j, err := json.Marshal(e)
  1722→	if err != nil {
  1723→		return
  1724→	}
  1725→
  1726→	o.outq.sendMsg(subject, j)
  1727→}

  1840→
  1841→// This will check for extended interest in a subject. If we have local interest we just return
  1842→// that, but in the absence of local interest and presence of gateways or service imports we need
  1843→// to check those as well.
  1844→func (o *consumer) hasDeliveryInterest(localInterest bool) bool {
  1845→	o.mu.RLock()
  1846→	mset := o.mset
  1847→	if mset == nil {
  1848→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  1849→		return false
  1850→	}
  1851→	acc := o.acc
  1852→	deliver := o.cfg.DeliverSubject
  1853→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  1854→
  1855→	if localInterest {
  1856→		return true
  1857→	}
  1858→
  1859→	// If we are here check gateways.
  1860→	if s := acc.srv; s != nil && s.hasGatewayInterest(acc.Name, deliver) {
  1861→		return true
  1862→	}
  1863→	return false
  1864→}
  1865→
  1866→func (s *Server) hasGatewayInterest(account, subject string) bool {
  1867→	gw := s.gateway
  1868→	if !gw.enabled {
  1869→		return false
  1870→	}
  1871→	gw.RLock()
  1872→	defer gw.RUnlock()
  1873→	for _, gwc := range gw.outo {
  1874→		psi, qr := gwc.gatewayInterest(account, stringToBytes(subject))
  1875→		if psi || qr != nil {
  1876→			return true
  1877→		}
  1878→	}
  1879→	return false
  1880→}
  1881→
  1882→// This processes an update to the local interest for a deliver subject.
  1883→func (o *consumer) updateDeliveryInterest(localInterest bool) bool {
  1884→	interest := o.hasDeliveryInterest(localInterest)
  1885→
  1886→	o.mu.Lock()
  1887→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  1888→
  1889→	mset := o.mset
  1890→	if mset == nil || o.isPullMode() {
  1891→		return false
  1892→	}
  1893→
  1894→	if interest && !o.active {
  1895→		o.signalNewMessages()
  1896→	}
  1897→	// Update active status, if not active clear any queue group we captured.
  1898→	if o.active = interest; !o.active {
  1899→		o.qgroup = _EMPTY_
  1900→	} else {
  1901→		o.checkQueueInterest()
  1902→	}
  1903→
  1904→	// If the delete timer has already been set do not clear here and return.
  1905→	// Note that durable can now have an inactive threshold, so don't check
  1906→	// for durable status, instead check for dthresh > 0.
  1907→	if o.dtmr != nil && o.dthresh > 0 && !interest {
  1908→		return true
  1909→	}
  1910→
  1911→	// Stop and clear the delete timer always.
  1912→	stopAndClearTimer(&o.dtmr)
  1913→
  1914→	// If we do not have interest anymore and have a delete threshold set, then set
  1915→	// a timer to delete us. We wait for a bit in case of server reconnect.
  1916→	if !interest && o.dthresh > 0 {
  1917→		o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(o.dthresh, o.deleteNotActive)
  1918→		return true
  1919→	}
  1920→	return false
  1921→}
  1922→
  1923→const (
  1924→	defaultConsumerNotActiveStartInterval = 30 * time.Second
  1925→	defaultConsumerNotActiveMaxInterval   = 5 * time.Minute
  1926→)
  1927→
  1928→var (
  1929→	consumerNotActiveStartInterval = defaultConsumerNotActiveStartInterval
  1930→	consumerNotActiveMaxInterval   = defaultConsumerNotActiveMaxInterval
  1931→)
  1932→
  1933→// deleteNotActive must only be called from time.AfterFunc or in its own
  1934→// goroutine, as it can block on clean-up.
  1935→func (o *consumer) deleteNotActive() {
  1936→	// Take a copy of these when the goroutine starts, mostly it avoids a
  1937→	// race condition with tests that modify these consts, such as
  1938→	// TestJetStreamClusterGhostEphemeralsAfterRestart.
  1939→	cnaMax := consumerNotActiveMaxInterval
  1940→	cnaStart := consumerNotActiveStartInterval
  1941→
  1942→	o.mu.Lock()
  1943→	if o.mset == nil {
  1944→		o.mu.Unlock()
  1945→		return
  1946→	}
  1947→	// Push mode just look at active.
  1948→	if o.isPushMode() {
  1949→		// If we are active simply return.
  1950→		if o.active {
  1951→			o.mu.Unlock()
  1952→			return
  1953→		}
  1954→	} else {
  1955→		// Pull mode.
  1956→		elapsed := time.Since(o.waiting.last)
  1957→		if elapsed < o.dthresh {
  1958→			// These need to keep firing so reset but use delta.
  1959→			if o.dtmr != nil {
  1960→				o.dtmr.Reset(o.dthresh - elapsed)
  1961→			} else {
  1962→				o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(o.dthresh-elapsed, o.deleteNotActive)
  1963→			}
  1964→			o.mu.Unlock()
  1965→			return
  1966→		}
  1967→		// Check if we still have valid requests waiting.
  1968→		if o.checkWaitingForInterest() {
  1969→			if o.dtmr != nil {
  1970→				o.dtmr.Reset(o.dthresh)
  1971→			} else {
  1972→				o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(o.dthresh, o.deleteNotActive)
  1973→			}
  1974→			o.mu.Unlock()
  1975→			return
  1976→		}
  1977→
  1978→		// We now know we have no waiting requests, and our last request was long ago.
  1979→		// However, based on AckWait the consumer could still be actively processing,
  1980→		// even if we haven't been informed if there were no acks in the meantime.
  1981→		// We must wait for the message that expires last and start counting down the
  1982→		// inactive threshold from there.
  1983→		now := time.Now().UnixNano()
  1984→		l := len(o.cfg.BackOff)
  1985→		var delay time.Duration
  1986→		var ackWait time.Duration
  1987→		for _, p := range o.pending {
  1988→			if l == 0 {
  1989→				ackWait = o.ackWait(0)
  1990→			} else {
  1991→				bi := int(o.rdc[p.Sequence])
  1992→				if bi < 0 {
  1993→					bi = 0
  1994→				} else if bi >= l {
  1995→					bi = l - 1
  1996→				}
  1997→				ackWait = o.ackWait(o.cfg.BackOff[bi])
  1998→			}
  1999→			if ts := p.Timestamp + ackWait.Nanoseconds() + o.dthresh.Nanoseconds(); ts > now {
  2000→				delay = max(delay, time.Duration(ts-now))
  2001→			}
  2002→		}
  2003→		// We'll wait for the latest time we expect an ack, plus the inactive threshold.
  2004→		// Acknowledging a message will reset this back down to just the inactive threshold.
  2005→		if delay > 0 {
  2006→			if o.dtmr != nil {
  2007→				o.dtmr.Reset(delay)
  2008→			} else {
  2009→				o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(delay, o.deleteNotActive)
  2010→			}
  2011→			o.mu.Unlock()
  2012→			return
  2013→		}
  2014→	}
  2015→
  2016→	s, js := o.mset.srv, o.srv.js.Load()
  2017→	acc, stream, name, isDirect := o.acc.Name, o.stream, o.name, o.cfg.Direct
  2018→	var qch, cqch chan struct{}
  2019→	if o.srv != nil {
  2020→		qch = o.srv.quitCh
  2021→	}
  2022→	o.mu.Unlock()
  2023→	if js != nil {
  2024→		cqch = js.clusterQuitC()
  2025→	}
  2026→
  2027→	// Useful for pprof.
  2028→	setGoRoutineLabels(pprofLabels{
  2029→		"account":  acc,
  2030→		"stream":   stream,
  2031→		"consumer": name,
  2032→	})
  2033→
  2034→	// We will delete locally regardless.
  2035→	defer o.delete()
  2036→
  2037→	// If we are clustered, check if we still have this consumer assigned.
  2038→	// If we do forward a proposal to delete ourselves to the metacontroller leader.
  2039→	if !isDirect && s.JetStreamIsClustered() {
  2040→		js.mu.RLock()
  2041→		var (
  2042→			cca         consumerAssignment
  2043→			meta        RaftNode
  2044→			removeEntry []byte
  2045→		)
  2046→		ca, cc := js.consumerAssignment(acc, stream, name), js.cluster
  2047→		if ca != nil && cc != nil {
  2048→			meta = cc.meta
  2049→			cca = *ca
  2050→			cca.Reply = _EMPTY_
  2051→			removeEntry = encodeDeleteConsumerAssignment(&cca)
  2052→			meta.ForwardProposal(removeEntry)
  2053→		}
  2054→		js.mu.RUnlock()
  2055→
  2056→		if ca != nil && cc != nil {
  2057→			// Check to make sure we went away.
  2058→			// Don't think this needs to be a monitored go routine.
  2059→			jitter := time.Duration(rand.Int63n(int64(cnaStart)))
  2060→			interval := cnaStart + jitter
  2061→			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
  2062→			defer ticker.Stop()
  2063→			for {
  2064→				select {
  2065→				case <-ticker.C:
  2066→				case <-qch:
  2067→					return
  2068→				case <-cqch:
  2069→					return
  2070→				}
  2071→				js.mu.RLock()
  2072→				if js.shuttingDown {
  2073→					js.mu.RUnlock()
  2074→					return
  2075→				}
  2076→				nca := js.consumerAssignment(acc, stream, name)
  2077→				js.mu.RUnlock()
  2078→				// Make sure this is the same consumer assignment, and not a new consumer with the same name.
  2079→				if nca != nil && reflect.DeepEqual(nca, ca) {
  2080→					s.Warnf("Consumer assignment for '%s > %s > %s' not cleaned up, retrying", acc, stream, name)
  2081→					meta.ForwardProposal(removeEntry)
  2082→					if interval < cnaMax {
  2083→						interval *= 2
  2084→						ticker.Reset(interval)
  2085→					}
  2086→					continue
  2087→				}
  2088→				// We saw that consumer has been removed, all done.
  2089→				return
  2090→			}
  2091→		}
  2092→	}
  2093→}
  2094→
  2095→func (o *consumer) watchGWinterest() {
  2096→	pa := o.isActive()
  2097→	// If there is no local interest...
  2098→	if o.hasNoLocalInterest() {
  2099→		o.updateDeliveryInterest(false)
  2100→		if !pa && o.isActive() {
  2101→			o.signalNewMessages()
  2102→		}
  2103→	}
  2104→
  2105→	// We want this to always be running so we can also pick up on interest returning.
  2106→	o.mu.Lock()
  2107→	if o.gwdtmr != nil {
  2108→		o.gwdtmr.Reset(time.Second)
  2109→	} else {
  2110→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.gwdtmr)
  2111→		o.gwdtmr = time.AfterFunc(time.Second, func() { o.watchGWinterest() })
  2112→	}
  2113→	o.mu.Unlock()
  2114→}
  2115→
  2116→// Config returns the consumer's configuration.
  2117→func (o *consumer) config() ConsumerConfig {
  2118→	o.mu.Lock()
  2119→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2120→	return o.cfg
  2121→}
  2122→
  2123→// Check if we have hit max deliveries. If so do notification and cleanup.
  2124→// Return whether or not the max was hit.
  2125→// Lock should be held.
  2126→func (o *consumer) hasMaxDeliveries(seq uint64) bool {
  2127→	if o.maxdc == 0 {
  2128→		return false
  2129→	}
  2130→	if dc := o.deliveryCount(seq); dc >= o.maxdc {
  2131→		// We have hit our max deliveries for this sequence.
  2132→		// Only send the advisory once.
  2133→		if dc == o.maxdc {
  2134→			o.notifyDeliveryExceeded(seq, dc)
  2135→		}
  2136→		// Determine if we signal to start flow of messages again.
  2137→		if o.maxp > 0 && len(o.pending) >= o.maxp {
  2138→			o.signalNewMessages()
  2139→		}
  2140→		// Make sure to remove from pending.
  2141→		if p, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok && p != nil {
  2142→			delete(o.pending, seq)
  2143→			o.updateDelivered(p.Sequence, seq, dc, p.Timestamp)
  2144→		}
  2145→		// Ensure redelivered state is set, if not already.
  2146→		if o.rdc == nil {
  2147→			o.rdc = make(map[uint64]uint64)
  2148→		}
  2149→		o.rdc[seq] = dc
  2150→		return true
  2151→	}
  2152→	return false
  2153→}
  2154→
  2155→// Force expiration of all pending.
  2156→// Lock should be held.
  2157→func (o *consumer) forceExpirePending() {
  2158→	var expired []uint64
  2159→	for seq := range o.pending {
  2160→		if !o.onRedeliverQueue(seq) && !o.hasMaxDeliveries(seq) {
  2161→			expired = append(expired, seq)
  2162→		}
  2163→	}
  2164→	if len(expired) > 0 {
  2165→		slices.Sort(expired)
  2166→		o.addToRedeliverQueue(expired...)
  2167→		// Now we should update the timestamp here since we are redelivering.
  2168→		// We will use an incrementing time to preserve order for any other redelivery.
  2169→		off := time.Now().UnixNano() - o.pending[expired[0]].Timestamp
  2170→		for _, seq := range expired {
  2171→			if p, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok && p != nil {
  2172→				p.Timestamp += off
  2173→			}
  2174→		}
  2175→		o.resetPtmr(o.ackWait(0))
  2176→	}
  2177→	o.signalNewMessages()
  2178→}
  2179→
  2180→// Acquire proper locks and update rate limit.
  2181→// Will use what is in config.
  2182→func (o *consumer) setRateLimitNeedsLocks() {
  2183→	o.mu.RLock()
  2184→	mset := o.mset
  2185→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  2186→
  2187→	if mset == nil {
  2188→		return
  2189→	}
  2190→
  2191→	mset.mu.RLock()
  2192→	o.mu.Lock()
  2193→	o.setRateLimit(o.cfg.RateLimit)
  2194→	o.mu.Unlock()
  2195→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2196→}
  2197→
  2198→// Set the rate limiter
  2199→// Both mset and consumer lock should be held.
  2200→func (o *consumer) setRateLimit(bps uint64) {
  2201→	if bps == 0 {
  2202→		o.rlimit = nil
  2203→		return
  2204→	}
  2205→
  2206→	// TODO(dlc) - Make sane values or error if not sane?
  2207→	// We are configured in bits per sec so adjust to bytes.
  2208→	rl := rate.Limit(bps / 8)
  2209→	mset := o.mset
  2210→
  2211→	// Burst should be set to maximum msg size for this account, etc.
  2212→	var burst int
  2213→	// We don't need to get cfgMu's rlock here since this function
  2214→	// is already invoked under mset.mu.RLock(), which superseeds cfgMu.
  2215→	if mset.cfg.MaxMsgSize > 0 {
  2216→		burst = int(mset.cfg.MaxMsgSize)
  2217→	} else if mset.jsa.account.limits.mpay > 0 {
  2218→		burst = int(mset.jsa.account.limits.mpay)
  2219→	} else {
  2220→		s := mset.jsa.account.srv
  2221→		burst = int(s.getOpts().MaxPayload)
  2222→	}
  2223→
  2224→	o.rlimit = rate.NewLimiter(rl, burst)
  2225→}
  2226→
  2227→// Check if new consumer config allowed vs old.
  2228→func (acc *Account) checkNewConsumerConfig(cfg, ncfg *ConsumerConfig) error {
  2229→	if reflect.DeepEqual(cfg, ncfg) {
  2230→		return nil
  2231→	}
  2232→	// Something different, so check since we only allow certain things to be updated.
  2233→	if cfg.DeliverPolicy != ncfg.DeliverPolicy {
  2234→		return errors.New("deliver policy can not be updated")
  2235→	}
  2236→	if cfg.OptStartSeq != ncfg.OptStartSeq {
  2237→		return errors.New("start sequence can not be updated")
  2238→	}
  2239→	if cfg.OptStartTime != nil && ncfg.OptStartTime != nil {
  2240→		// Both have start times set, compare them directly:
  2241→		if !cfg.OptStartTime.Equal(*ncfg.OptStartTime) {
  2242→			return errors.New("start time can not be updated")
  2243→		}
  2244→	} else if cfg.OptStartTime != nil || ncfg.OptStartTime != nil {
  2245→		// At least one start time is set and the other is not
  2246→		return errors.New("start time can not be updated")
  2247→	}
  2248→	if cfg.AckPolicy != ncfg.AckPolicy {
  2249→		return errors.New("ack policy can not be updated")
  2250→	}
  2251→	if cfg.ReplayPolicy != ncfg.ReplayPolicy {
  2252→		return errors.New("replay policy can not be updated")
  2253→	}
  2254→	if cfg.Heartbeat != ncfg.Heartbeat {
  2255→		return errors.New("heart beats can not be updated")
  2256→	}
  2257→	if cfg.FlowControl != ncfg.FlowControl {
  2258→		return errors.New("flow control can not be updated")
  2259→	}
  2260→
  2261→	// Deliver Subject is conditional on if its bound.
  2262→	if cfg.DeliverSubject != ncfg.DeliverSubject {
  2263→		if cfg.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ {
  2264→			return errors.New("can not update pull consumer to push based")
  2265→		}
  2266→		if ncfg.DeliverSubject == _EMPTY_ {
  2267→			return errors.New("can not update push consumer to pull based")
  2268→		}
  2269→		if acc.sl.HasInterest(cfg.DeliverSubject) {
  2270→			return NewJSConsumerNameExistError()
  2271→		}
  2272→	}
  2273→
  2274→	if cfg.MaxWaiting != ncfg.MaxWaiting {
  2275→		return errors.New("max waiting can not be updated")
  2276→	}
  2277→
  2278→	// Check if BackOff is defined, MaxDeliver is within range.
  2279→	if lbo := len(ncfg.BackOff); lbo > 0 && ncfg.MaxDeliver != -1 && lbo > ncfg.MaxDeliver {
  2280→		return NewJSConsumerMaxDeliverBackoffError()
  2281→	}
  2282→
  2283→	return nil
  2284→}
  2285→
  2286→// Update the config based on the new config, or error if update not allowed.
  2287→func (o *consumer) updateConfig(cfg *ConsumerConfig) error {
  2288→	o.mu.Lock()
  2289→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2290→
  2291→	if o.closed || o.mset == nil {
  2292→		return NewJSConsumerDoesNotExistError()
  2293→	}
  2294→
  2295→	if err := o.acc.checkNewConsumerConfig(&o.cfg, cfg); err != nil {
  2296→		return err
  2297→	}
  2298→
  2299→	// Make sure we always store PauseUntil in UTC.
  2300→	if cfg.PauseUntil != nil {
  2301→		utc := (*cfg.PauseUntil).UTC()
  2302→		cfg.PauseUntil = &utc
  2303→	}
  2304→
  2305→	if o.store != nil {
  2306→		// Update local state always.
  2307→		if err := o.store.UpdateConfig(cfg); err != nil {
  2308→			return err
  2309→		}
  2310→	}
  2311→
  2312→	// DeliverSubject
  2313→	if cfg.DeliverSubject != o.cfg.DeliverSubject {
  2314→		o.updateDeliverSubjectLocked(cfg.DeliverSubject)
  2315→	}
  2316→
  2317→	// MaxAckPending
  2318→	if cfg.MaxAckPending != o.cfg.MaxAckPending {
  2319→		o.maxp = cfg.MaxAckPending
  2320→		o.signalNewMessages()
  2321→		// If MaxAckPending is lowered, we could have allocated a pending deliveries map of larger size.
  2322→		// Reset it here, so we can shrink the map.
  2323→		if cfg.MaxAckPending < o.cfg.MaxAckPending {
  2324→			o.resetPendingDeliveries()
  2325→		}
  2326→	}
  2327→	// AckWait
  2328→	if cfg.AckWait != o.cfg.AckWait {
  2329→		if o.ptmr != nil {
  2330→			o.resetPtmr(100 * time.Millisecond)
  2331→		}
  2332→	}
  2333→	// Rate Limit
  2334→	if cfg.RateLimit != o.cfg.RateLimit {
  2335→		// We need both locks here so do in Go routine.
  2336→		go o.setRateLimitNeedsLocks()
  2337→	}
  2338→	if cfg.SampleFrequency != o.cfg.SampleFrequency {
  2339→		s := strings.TrimSuffix(cfg.SampleFrequency, "%")
  2340→		if sampleFreq, err := strconv.ParseInt(s, 10, 32); err == nil {
  2341→			o.sfreq = int32(sampleFreq)
  2342→		}
  2343→	}
  2344→	// Set MaxDeliver if changed
  2345→	if cfg.MaxDeliver != o.cfg.MaxDeliver {
  2346→		// MaxDeliver is negative (-1) when infinite.
  2347→		o.maxdc = uint64(max(cfg.MaxDeliver, 0))
  2348→	}
  2349→	// Set InactiveThreshold if changed.
  2350→	if val := cfg.InactiveThreshold; val != o.cfg.InactiveThreshold {
  2351→		o.updateInactiveThreshold(cfg)
  2352→		stopAndClearTimer(&o.dtmr)
  2353→		// Restart timer only if we are the leader.
  2354→		if o.isLeader() && o.dthresh > 0 {
  2355→			o.dtmr = time.AfterFunc(o.dthresh, o.deleteNotActive)
  2356→		}
  2357→	}
  2358→	// Check whether the pause has changed
  2359→	{
  2360→		var old, new time.Time
  2361→		if o.cfg.PauseUntil != nil {
  2362→			old = *o.cfg.PauseUntil
  2363→		}
  2364→		if cfg.PauseUntil != nil {
  2365→			new = *cfg.PauseUntil
  2366→		}
  2367→		if !old.Equal(new) {
  2368→			o.updatePauseState(cfg)
  2369→			if o.isLeader() {
  2370→				o.sendPauseAdvisoryLocked(cfg)
  2371→			}
  2372→		}
  2373→	}
  2374→
  2375→	// Check for Subject Filters update.
  2376→	newSubjects := gatherSubjectFilters(cfg.FilterSubject, cfg.FilterSubjects)
  2377→	if !subjectSliceEqual(newSubjects, o.subjf.subjects()) {
  2378→		newSubjf := make(subjectFilters, 0, len(newSubjects))
  2379→		for _, newFilter := range newSubjects {
  2380→			fs := &subjectFilter{
  2381→				subject:          newFilter,
  2382→				hasWildcard:      subjectHasWildcard(newFilter),
  2383→				tokenizedSubject: tokenizeSubjectIntoSlice(nil, newFilter),
  2384→			}
  2385→			newSubjf = append(newSubjf, fs)
  2386→		}
  2387→		// Make sure we have correct signaling setup.
  2388→		// Consumer lock can not be held.
  2389→		mset := o.mset
  2390→		o.mu.Unlock()
  2391→		mset.swapSigSubs(o, newSubjf.subjects())
  2392→		o.mu.Lock()
  2393→
  2394→		// When we're done with signaling, we can replace the subjects.
  2395→		// If filters were removed, set `o.subjf` to nil.
  2396→		if len(newSubjf) == 0 {
  2397→			o.subjf = nil
  2398→			o.filters = nil
  2399→		} else {
  2400→			o.subjf = newSubjf
  2401→			if len(o.subjf) == 1 {
  2402→				o.filters = nil
  2403→			} else {
  2404→				o.filters = gsl.NewSublist[struct{}]()
  2405→				for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  2406→					o.filters.Insert(filter.subject, struct{}{})
  2407→				}
  2408→			}
  2409→		}
  2410→	}
  2411→
  2412→	// Record new config for others that do not need special handling.
  2413→	// Allowed but considered no-op, [Description, SampleFrequency, MaxWaiting, HeadersOnly]
  2414→	o.cfg = *cfg
  2415→
  2416→	// Cleanup messages that lost interest.
  2417→	if o.retention == InterestPolicy {
  2418→		o.mu.Unlock()
  2419→		o.cleanupNoInterestMessages(o.mset, false)
  2420→		o.mu.Lock()
  2421→	}
  2422→
  2423→	// Re-calculate num pending on update.
  2424→	o.streamNumPending()
  2425→
  2426→	return nil
  2427→}
  2428→
  2429→// This is a config change for the delivery subject for a
  2430→// push based consumer.
  2431→func (o *consumer) updateDeliverSubject(newDeliver string) {
  2432→	// Update the config and the dsubj
  2433→	o.mu.Lock()
  2434→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2435→	o.updateDeliverSubjectLocked(newDeliver)
  2436→}
  2437→
  2438→// This is a config change for the delivery subject for a
  2439→// push based consumer.
  2440→func (o *consumer) updateDeliverSubjectLocked(newDeliver string) {
  2441→	if o.closed || o.isPullMode() || o.cfg.DeliverSubject == newDeliver {
  2442→		return
  2443→	}
  2444→
  2445→	// Force redeliver of all pending on change of delivery subject.
  2446→	if len(o.pending) > 0 {
  2447→		o.forceExpirePending()
  2448→	}
  2449→
  2450→	o.acc.sl.clearNotification(o.dsubj, o.cfg.DeliverGroup, o.inch)
  2451→	o.dsubj, o.cfg.DeliverSubject = newDeliver, newDeliver
  2452→	// When we register new one it will deliver to update state loop.
  2453→	o.acc.sl.registerNotification(newDeliver, o.cfg.DeliverGroup, o.inch)
  2454→}
  2455→
  2456→// Check that configs are equal but allow delivery subjects to be different.
  2457→func configsEqualSansDelivery(a, b ConsumerConfig) bool {
  2458→	// These were copied in so can set Delivery here.
  2459→	a.DeliverSubject, b.DeliverSubject = _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_
  2460→	return reflect.DeepEqual(a, b)
  2461→}
  2462→
  2463→// Helper to send a reply to an ack.
  2464→func (o *consumer) sendAckReply(subj string) {
  2465→	o.mu.RLock()
  2466→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  2467→	o.outq.sendMsg(subj, nil)
  2468→}
  2469→
  2470→type jsAckMsg struct {
  2471→	subject string
  2472→	reply   string
  2473→	hdr     int
  2474→	msg     []byte
  2475→}
  2476→
  2477→var jsAckMsgPool sync.Pool
  2478→
  2479→func newJSAckMsg(subj, reply string, hdr int, msg []byte) *jsAckMsg {
  2480→	var m *jsAckMsg
  2481→	am := jsAckMsgPool.Get()
  2482→	if am != nil {
  2483→		m = am.(*jsAckMsg)
  2484→	} else {
  2485→		m = &jsAckMsg{}
  2486→	}
  2487→	// When getting something from a pool it is critical that all fields are
  2488→	// initialized. Doing this way guarantees that if someone adds a field to
  2489→	// the structure, the compiler will fail the build if this line is not updated.
  2490→	(*m) = jsAckMsg{subj, reply, hdr, msg}
  2491→	return m
  2492→}
  2493→
  2494→func (am *jsAckMsg) returnToPool() {
  2495→	if am == nil {
  2496→		return
  2497→	}
  2498→	am.subject, am.reply, am.hdr, am.msg = _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, -1, nil
  2499→	jsAckMsgPool.Put(am)
  2500→}
  2501→
  2502→// Push the ack message to the consumer's ackMsgs queue
  2503→func (o *consumer) pushAck(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  2504→	atomic.AddInt64(&o.awl, 1)
  2505→	o.ackMsgs.push(newJSAckMsg(subject, reply, c.pa.hdr, copyBytes(rmsg)))
  2506→}
  2507→
  2508→// Processes a message for the ack reply subject delivered with a message.
  2509→func (o *consumer) processAck(subject, reply string, hdr int, rmsg []byte) {
  2510→	defer atomic.AddInt64(&o.awl, -1)
  2511→
  2512→	var msg []byte
  2513→	if hdr > 0 {
  2514→		msg = rmsg[hdr:]
  2515→	} else {
  2516→		msg = rmsg
  2517→	}
  2518→
  2519→	sseq, dseq, dc := ackReplyInfo(subject)
  2520→
  2521→	skipAckReply := sseq == 0
  2522→
  2523→	switch {
  2524→	case len(msg) == 0, bytes.Equal(msg, AckAck), bytes.Equal(msg, AckOK):
  2525→		if !o.processAckMsg(sseq, dseq, dc, reply, true) {
  2526→			// We handle replies for acks in updateAcks
  2527→			skipAckReply = true
  2528→		}
  2529→	case bytes.HasPrefix(msg, AckNext):
  2530→		o.processAckMsg(sseq, dseq, dc, _EMPTY_, true)
  2531→		o.processNextMsgRequest(reply, msg[len(AckNext):])
  2532→		skipAckReply = true
  2533→	case bytes.HasPrefix(msg, AckNak):
  2534→		o.processNak(sseq, dseq, dc, msg)
  2535→	case bytes.Equal(msg, AckProgress):
  2536→		o.progressUpdate(sseq)
  2537→	case bytes.HasPrefix(msg, AckTerm):
  2538→		var reason string
  2539→		if buf := msg[len(AckTerm):]; len(buf) > 0 {
  2540→			reason = string(bytes.TrimSpace(buf))
  2541→		}
  2542→		if !o.processTerm(sseq, dseq, dc, reason, reply) {
  2543→			// We handle replies for acks in updateAcks
  2544→			skipAckReply = true
  2545→		}
  2546→	}
  2547→
  2548→	// Ack the ack if requested.
  2549→	if len(reply) > 0 && !skipAckReply {
  2550→		o.sendAckReply(reply)
  2551→	}
  2552→}
  2553→
  2554→// Used to process a working update to delay redelivery.
  2555→func (o *consumer) progressUpdate(seq uint64) {
  2556→	o.mu.Lock()
  2557→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2558→
  2559→	if p, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok {
  2560→		p.Timestamp = time.Now().UnixNano()
  2561→		// Update store system.
  2562→		o.updateDelivered(p.Sequence, seq, 1, p.Timestamp)
  2563→	}
  2564→}
  2565→
  2566→// Lock should be held.
  2567→func (o *consumer) updateSkipped(seq uint64) {
  2568→	// Clustered mode and R>1 only.
  2569→	if o.node == nil || !o.isLeader() {
  2570→		return
  2571→	}
  2572→	var b [1 + 8]byte
  2573→	b[0] = byte(updateSkipOp)
  2574→	var le = binary.LittleEndian
  2575→	le.PutUint64(b[1:], seq)
  2576→	o.propose(b[:])
  2577→}
  2578→
  2579→func (o *consumer) loopAndForwardProposals(qch chan struct{}) {
  2580→	// On exit make sure we nil out pch.
  2581→	defer func() {
  2582→		o.mu.Lock()
  2583→		o.pch = nil
  2584→		o.mu.Unlock()
  2585→	}()
  2586→
  2587→	o.mu.RLock()
  2588→	node, pch := o.node, o.pch
  2589→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  2590→
  2591→	if node == nil || pch == nil {
  2592→		return
  2593→	}
  2594→
  2595→	forwardProposals := func() error {
  2596→		o.mu.Lock()
  2597→		if o.node == nil || !o.node.Leader() {
  2598→			o.mu.Unlock()
  2599→			return errors.New("no longer leader")
  2600→		}
  2601→		proposal := o.phead
  2602→		o.phead, o.ptail = nil, nil
  2603→		o.mu.Unlock()
  2604→		// 256k max for now per batch.
  2605→		const maxBatch = 256 * 1024
  2606→		var entries []*Entry
  2607→		for sz := 0; proposal != nil; proposal = proposal.next {
  2608→			entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, proposal.data))
  2609→			sz += len(proposal.data)
  2610→			if sz > maxBatch {
  2611→				node.ProposeMulti(entries)
  2612→				// We need to re-create `entries` because there is a reference
  2613→				// to it in the node's pae map.
  2614→				sz, entries = 0, nil
  2615→			}
  2616→		}
  2617→		if len(entries) > 0 {
  2618→			node.ProposeMulti(entries)
  2619→		}
  2620→		return nil
  2621→	}
  2622→
  2623→	// In case we have anything pending on entry.
  2624→	forwardProposals()
  2625→
  2626→	for {
  2627→		select {
  2628→		case <-qch:
  2629→			forwardProposals()
  2630			return
  2631→		case <-pch:
  2632→			if err := forwardProposals(); err != nil {
  2633→				return
  2634→			}
  2635→		}
  2636→	}
  2637→}
  2638→
  2639→// Lock should be held.
  2640→func (o *consumer) propose(entry []byte) {
  2641→	p := &proposal{data: entry}
  2642→	if o.phead == nil {
  2643→		o.phead = p
  2644→	} else {
  2645→		o.ptail.next = p
  2646→	}
  2647→	o.ptail = p
  2648→
  2649→	// Kick our looper routine.
  2650→	select {
  2651→	case o.pch <- struct{}{}:
  2652→	default:
  2653→	}
  2654→}
  2655→
  2656→// Lock should be held.
  2657→func (o *consumer) updateDelivered(dseq, sseq, dc uint64, ts int64) {
  2658→	// Clustered mode and R>1.
  2659→	if o.node != nil {
  2660→		// Inline for now, use variable compression.
  2661→		var b [4*binary.MaxVarintLen64 + 1]byte
  2662→		b[0] = byte(updateDeliveredOp)
  2663→		n := 1
  2664→		n += binary.PutUvarint(b[n:], dseq)
  2665→		n += binary.PutUvarint(b[n:], sseq)
  2666→		n += binary.PutUvarint(b[n:], dc)
  2667→		n += binary.PutVarint(b[n:], ts)
  2668→		o.propose(b[:n])
  2669→	} else if o.store != nil {
  2670→		o.store.UpdateDelivered(dseq, sseq, dc, ts)
  2671→	}
  2672→	// Update activity.
  2673→	o.ldt = time.Now()
  2674→}
  2675→
  2676→// Used to remember a pending ack reply in a replicated consumer.
  2677→// Lock should be held.
  2678→func (o *consumer) addAckReply(sseq uint64, reply string) {
  2679→	if o.replies == nil {
  2680→		o.replies = make(map[uint64]string)
  2681→	}
  2682→	o.replies[sseq] = reply
  2683→}
  2684→
  2685→// Used to remember messages that need to be sent for a replicated consumer, after delivered quorum.
  2686→// Lock should be held.
  2687→func (o *consumer) addReplicatedQueuedMsg(pmsg *jsPubMsg) {
  2688→	// Is not explicitly limited in size, but will at most hold maximum ack pending.
  2689→	if o.pendingDeliveries == nil {
  2690→		o.pendingDeliveries = make(map[uint64]*jsPubMsg)
  2691→	}
  2692→	o.pendingDeliveries[pmsg.seq] = pmsg
  2693→
  2694→	// Is not explicitly limited in size, but will at most hold maximum waiting requests.
  2695→	if o.waitingDeliveries == nil {
  2696→		o.waitingDeliveries = make(map[string]*waitingDelivery)
  2697→	}
  2698→	if wd, ok := o.waitingDeliveries[pmsg.dsubj]; ok {
  2699→		wd.seq = pmsg.seq
  2700→	} else {
  2701→		wd := wdPool.Get().(*waitingDelivery)
  2702→		wd.seq = pmsg.seq
  2703→		o.waitingDeliveries[pmsg.dsubj] = wd
  2704→	}
  2705→}
  2706→
  2707→// Lock should be held.
  2708→func (o *consumer) updateAcks(dseq, sseq uint64, reply string) {
  2709→	if o.node != nil {
  2710→		// Inline for now, use variable compression.
  2711→		var b [2*binary.MaxVarintLen64 + 1]byte
  2712→		b[0] = byte(updateAcksOp)
  2713→		n := 1
  2714→		n += binary.PutUvarint(b[n:], dseq)
  2715→		n += binary.PutUvarint(b[n:], sseq)
  2716→		o.propose(b[:n])
  2717→		if reply != _EMPTY_ {
  2718→			o.addAckReply(sseq, reply)
  2719→		}
  2720→	} else if o.store != nil {
  2721→		o.store.UpdateAcks(dseq, sseq)
  2722→		if reply != _EMPTY_ {
  2723→			// Already locked so send direct.
  2724→			o.outq.sendMsg(reply, nil)
  2725→		}
  2726→	}
  2727→	// Update activity.
  2728→	o.lat = time.Now()
  2729→}
  2730→
  2731→// Communicate to the cluster an addition of a pending request.
  2732→// Lock should be held.
  2733→func (o *consumer) addClusterPendingRequest(reply string) {
  2734→	if o.node == nil || !o.pendingRequestsOk() {
  2735→		return
  2736→	}
  2737→	b := make([]byte, len(reply)+1)
  2738→	b[0] = byte(addPendingRequest)
  2739→	copy(b[1:], reply)
  2740→	o.propose(b)
  2741→}
  2742→
  2743→// Communicate to the cluster a removal of a pending request.
  2744→// Lock should be held.
  2745→func (o *consumer) removeClusterPendingRequest(reply string) {
  2746→	if o.node == nil || !o.pendingRequestsOk() {
  2747→		return
  2748→	}
  2749→	b := make([]byte, len(reply)+1)
  2750→	b[0] = byte(removePendingRequest)
  2751→	copy(b[1:], reply)
  2752→	o.propose(b)
  2753→}
  2754→
  2755→// Set whether or not we can send pending requests to followers.
  2756→func (o *consumer) setPendingRequestsOk(ok bool) {
  2757→	o.mu.Lock()
  2758→	o.prOk = ok
  2759→	o.mu.Unlock()
  2760→}
  2761→
  2762→// Lock should be held.
  2763→func (o *consumer) pendingRequestsOk() bool {
  2764→	return o.prOk
  2765→}
  2766→
  2767→// Set whether or not we can send info about pending pull requests to our group.
  2768→// Will require all peers have a minimum version.
  2769→func (o *consumer) checkAndSetPendingRequestsOk() {
  2770→	o.mu.RLock()
  2771→	s, isValid := o.srv, o.mset != nil
  2772→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  2773→	if !isValid {
  2774→		return
  2775→	}
  2776→
  2777→	if ca := o.consumerAssignment(); ca != nil && len(ca.Group.Peers) > 1 {
  2778→		for _, pn := range ca.Group.Peers {
  2779→			if si, ok := s.nodeToInfo.Load(pn); ok {
  2780→				if !versionAtLeast(si.(nodeInfo).version, 2, 7, 1) {
  2781→					// We expect all of our peers to eventually be up to date.
  2782→					// So check again in awhile.
  2783→					time.AfterFunc(eventsHBInterval, func() { o.checkAndSetPendingRequestsOk() })
  2784→					o.setPendingRequestsOk(false)
  2785→					return
  2786→				}
  2787→			}
  2788→		}
  2789→	}
  2790→	o.setPendingRequestsOk(true)
  2791→}
  2792→
  2793→// On leadership change make sure we alert the pending requests that they are no longer valid.
  2794→func (o *consumer) checkPendingRequests() {
  2795→	o.mu.Lock()
  2796→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2797→	if o.mset == nil || o.outq == nil {
  2798→		return
  2799→	}
  2800→	hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 409 Leadership Change\r\n\r\n")
  2801→	for reply := range o.prm {
  2802→		o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  2803→	}
  2804→	o.prm = nil
  2805→}
  2806→
  2807→// This will release any pending pull requests if applicable.
  2808→// Should be called only by the leader being deleted or stopped.
  2809→// Lock should be held.
  2810→func (o *consumer) releaseAnyPendingRequests(isAssigned bool) {
  2811→	if o.mset == nil || o.outq == nil || o.waiting.len() == 0 {
  2812→		return
  2813→	}
  2814→	var hdr []byte
  2815→	if !isAssigned {
  2816→		hdr = []byte("NATS/1.0 409 Consumer Deleted\r\n\r\n")
  2817→	}
  2818→
  2819→	wq := o.waiting
  2820→	for wr := wq.head; wr != nil; {
  2821→		if hdr != nil {
  2822→			o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  2823→		}
  2824→		next := wr.next
  2825→		wr.recycle()
  2826→		wr = next
  2827→	}
  2828→	// Nil out old queue.
  2829→	o.waiting = nil
  2830→}
  2831→
  2832→// Process a NAK.
  2833→func (o *consumer) processNak(sseq, dseq, dc uint64, nak []byte) {
  2834→	o.mu.Lock()
  2835→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2836→
  2837→	// Check for out of range.
  2838→	if dseq <= o.adflr || dseq > o.dseq {
  2839→		return
  2840→	}
  2841→	// If we are explicit ack make sure this is still on our pending list.
  2842→	if _, ok := o.pending[sseq]; !ok {
  2843→		return
  2844→	}
  2845→
  2846→	// Deliver an advisory
  2847→	e := JSConsumerDeliveryNakAdvisory{
  2848→		TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
  2849→			Type: JSConsumerDeliveryNakAdvisoryType,
  2850→			ID:   nuid.Next(),
  2851→			Time: time.Now().UTC(),
  2852→		},
  2853→		Stream:      o.stream,
  2854→		Consumer:    o.name,
  2855→		ConsumerSeq: dseq,
  2856→		StreamSeq:   sseq,
  2857→		Deliveries:  dc,
  2858→		Domain:      o.srv.getOpts().JetStreamDomain,
  2859→	}
  2860→
  2861→	o.sendAdvisory(o.nakEventT, e)
  2862→
  2863→	// Check to see if we have delays attached.
  2864→	if len(nak) > len(AckNak) {
  2865→		arg := bytes.TrimSpace(nak[len(AckNak):])
  2866→		if len(arg) > 0 {
  2867→			var d time.Duration
  2868→			var err error
  2869→			if arg[0] == '{' {
  2870→				var nd ConsumerNakOptions
  2871→				if err = json.Unmarshal(arg, &nd); err == nil {
  2872→					d = nd.Delay
  2873→				}
  2874→			} else {
  2875→				d, err = time.ParseDuration(string(arg))
  2876→			}
  2877→			if err != nil {
  2878→				// Treat this as normal NAK.
  2879→				o.srv.Warnf("JetStream consumer '%s > %s > %s' bad NAK delay value: %q", o.acc.Name, o.stream, o.name, arg)
  2880→			} else {
  2881→				// We have a parsed duration that the user wants us to wait before retrying.
  2882→				// Make sure we are not on the rdq.
  2883→				o.removeFromRedeliverQueue(sseq)
  2884→				if p, ok := o.pending[sseq]; ok {
  2885→					// now - ackWait is expired now, so offset from there.
