  2886→					p.Timestamp = time.Now().Add(-o.cfg.AckWait).Add(d).UnixNano()
  2887→					// Update store system which will update followers as well.
  2888→					o.updateDelivered(p.Sequence, sseq, dc, p.Timestamp)
  2889→					if o.ptmr != nil {
  2890→						// Want checkPending to run and figure out the next timer ttl.
  2891→						// TODO(dlc) - We could optimize this maybe a bit more and track when we expect the timer to fire.
  2892→						o.resetPtmr(10 * time.Millisecond)
  2893→					}
  2894→				}
  2895→				// Nothing else for use to do now so return.
  2896→				return
  2897→			}
  2898→		}
  2899→	}
  2900→
  2901→	// If already queued up also ignore.
  2902→	if !o.onRedeliverQueue(sseq) {
  2903→		o.addToRedeliverQueue(sseq)
  2904→	}
  2905→
  2906→	o.signalNewMessages()
  2907→}
  2908→
  2909→// Process a TERM
  2910→// Returns `true` if the ack was processed in place and the sender can now respond
  2911→// to the client, or `false` if there was an error or the ack is replicated (in which
  2912→// case the reply will be sent later).
  2913→func (o *consumer) processTerm(sseq, dseq, dc uint64, reason, reply string) bool {
  2914→	// Treat like an ack to suppress redelivery.
  2915→	ackedInPlace := o.processAckMsg(sseq, dseq, dc, reply, false)
  2916→
  2917→	o.mu.Lock()
  2918→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  2919→
  2920→	// Deliver an advisory
  2921→	e := JSConsumerDeliveryTerminatedAdvisory{
  2922→		TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
  2923→			Type: JSConsumerDeliveryTerminatedAdvisoryType,
  2924→			ID:   nuid.Next(),
  2925→			Time: time.Now().UTC(),
  2926→		},
  2927→		Stream:      o.stream,
  2928→		Consumer:    o.name,
  2929→		ConsumerSeq: dseq,
  2930→		StreamSeq:   sseq,
  2931→		Deliveries:  dc,
  2932→		Reason:      reason,
  2933→		Domain:      o.srv.getOpts().JetStreamDomain,
  2934→	}
  2935→
  2936→	subj := JSAdvisoryConsumerMsgTerminatedPre + "." + o.stream + "." + o.name
  2937→	o.sendAdvisory(subj, e)
  2938→	return ackedInPlace
  2939→}
  2940→
  2941→// Introduce a small delay in when timer fires to check pending.
  2942→// Allows bursts to be treated in same time frame.
  2943→const ackWaitDelay = time.Millisecond
  2944→
  2945→// ackWait returns how long to wait to fire the pending timer.
  2946→func (o *consumer) ackWait(next time.Duration) time.Duration {
  2947→	if next > 0 {
  2948→		return next + ackWaitDelay
  2949→	}
  2950→	return o.cfg.AckWait + ackWaitDelay
  2951→}
  2952→
  2953→// Due to bug in calculation of sequences on restoring redelivered let's do quick sanity check.
  2954→// Lock should be held.
  2955→func (o *consumer) checkRedelivered() {
  2956→	var shouldUpdateState bool
  2957→	for sseq := range o.rdc {
  2958→		if sseq <= o.asflr {
  2959→			delete(o.rdc, sseq)
  2960→			o.removeFromRedeliverQueue(sseq)
  2961→			shouldUpdateState = true
  2962→		}
  2963→	}
  2964→	if shouldUpdateState {
  2965→		if err := o.writeStoreStateUnlocked(); err != nil && o.srv != nil && o.mset != nil && !o.closed {
  2966→			s, acc, mset, name := o.srv, o.acc, o.mset, o.name
  2967→			s.Warnf("Consumer '%s > %s > %s' error on write store state from check redelivered: %v", acc, mset.getCfgName(), name, err)
  2968→		}
  2969→	}
  2970→}
  2971→
  2972→// This will restore the state from disk.
  2973→// Lock should be held.
  2974→func (o *consumer) readStoredState() error {
  2975→	if o.store == nil {
  2976→		return nil
  2977→	}
  2978→	state, err := o.store.State()
  2979→	if err == nil {
  2980→		o.applyState(state)
  2981→		if len(o.rdc) > 0 {
  2982→			o.checkRedelivered()
  2983→		}
  2984→	}
  2985→	return err
  2986→}
  2987→
  2988→// Apply the consumer stored state.
  2989→// Lock should be held.
  2990→func (o *consumer) applyState(state *ConsumerState) {
  2991→	if state == nil {
  2992→		return
  2993→	}
  2994→
  2995→	o.sseq = state.Delivered.Stream + 1
  2996→	o.dseq = state.Delivered.Consumer + 1
  2997→	o.adflr = state.AckFloor.Consumer
  2998→	o.asflr = state.AckFloor.Stream
  2999→	o.pending = state.Pending
  3000→	o.rdc = state.Redelivered
  3001→
  3002→	// Setup tracking timer if we have restored pending.
  3003→	if o.isLeader() && len(o.pending) > 0 {
  3004→		// This is on startup or leader change. We want to check pending
  3005→		// sooner in case there are inconsistencies etc. Pick between 500ms - 1.5s
  3006→		delay := 500*time.Millisecond + time.Duration(rand.Int63n(1000))*time.Millisecond
  3007→
  3008→		// If normal is lower than this just use that.
  3009→		if o.cfg.AckWait < delay {
  3010→			delay = o.ackWait(0)
  3011→		}
  3012→		o.resetPtmr(delay)
  3013→	}
  3014→}
  3015→
  3016→// Sets our store state from another source. Used in clustered mode on snapshot restore.
  3017→// Lock should be held.
  3018→func (o *consumer) setStoreState(state *ConsumerState) error {
  3019→	if state == nil || o.store == nil {
  3020→		return nil
  3021→	}
  3022→	err := o.store.Update(state)
  3023→	if err == nil {
  3024→		o.applyState(state)
  3025→	}
  3026→	return err
  3027→}
  3028→
  3029→// Update our state to the store.
  3030→func (o *consumer) writeStoreState() error {
  3031→	o.mu.Lock()
  3032→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  3033→	return o.writeStoreStateUnlocked()
  3034→}
  3035→
  3036→// Update our state to the store.
  3037→// Lock should be held.
  3038→func (o *consumer) writeStoreStateUnlocked() error {
  3039→	if o.store == nil {
  3040→		return nil
  3041→	}
  3042→	state := ConsumerState{
  3043→		Delivered: SequencePair{
  3044→			Consumer: o.dseq - 1,
  3045→			Stream:   o.sseq - 1,
  3046→		},
  3047→		AckFloor: SequencePair{
  3048→			Consumer: o.adflr,
  3049→			Stream:   o.asflr,
  3050→		},
  3051→		Pending:     o.pending,
  3052→		Redelivered: o.rdc,
  3053→	}
  3054→	return o.store.Update(&state)
  3055→}
  3056→
  3057→// Returns an initial info. Only applicable for non-clustered consumers.
  3058→// We will clear after we return it, so one shot.
  3059→func (o *consumer) initialInfo() *ConsumerInfo {
  3060→	o.mu.Lock()
  3061→	ici := o.ici
  3062→	o.ici = nil // gc friendly
  3063→	o.mu.Unlock()
  3064→	if ici == nil {
  3065→		ici = o.info()
  3066→	}
  3067→	return ici
  3068→}
  3069→
  3070→// Clears our initial info.
  3071→// Used when we have a leader change in cluster mode but do not send a response.
  3072→func (o *consumer) clearInitialInfo() {
  3073→	o.mu.Lock()
  3074→	o.ici = nil // gc friendly
  3075→	o.mu.Unlock()
  3076→}
  3077→
  3078→// Info returns our current consumer state.
  3079→func (o *consumer) info() *ConsumerInfo {
  3080→	return o.infoWithSnap(false)
  3081→}
  3082→
  3083→func (o *consumer) infoWithSnap(snap bool) *ConsumerInfo {
  3084→	return o.infoWithSnapAndReply(snap, _EMPTY_)
  3085→}
  3086→
  3087→func (o *consumer) infoWithSnapAndReply(snap bool, reply string) *ConsumerInfo {
  3088→	o.mu.Lock()
  3089→	mset := o.mset
  3090→	if o.closed || mset == nil || mset.srv == nil {
  3091→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3092→		return nil
  3093→	}
  3094→	js := o.js
  3095→	if js == nil {
  3096→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3097→		return nil
  3098→	}
  3099→
  3100→	// Capture raftGroup.
  3101→	var rg *raftGroup
  3102→	if o.ca != nil {
  3103→		rg = o.ca.Group
  3104→	}
  3105→
  3106→	priorityGroups := []PriorityGroupState{}
  3107→	// TODO(jrm): when we introduce supporting many priority groups, we need to update assigning `o.currentNuid` for each group.
  3108→	if len(o.cfg.PriorityGroups) > 0 {
  3109→		priorityGroups = append(priorityGroups, PriorityGroupState{
  3110→			Group:          o.cfg.PriorityGroups[0],
  3111→			PinnedClientID: o.currentPinId,
  3112→			PinnedTS:       o.pinnedTS,
  3113→		})
  3114→	}
  3115→
  3116→	cfg := o.cfg
  3117→	info := &ConsumerInfo{
  3118→		Stream:  o.stream,
  3119→		Name:    o.name,
  3120→		Created: o.created,
  3121→		Config:  &cfg,
  3122→		Delivered: SequenceInfo{
  3123→			Consumer: o.dseq - 1,
  3124→			Stream:   o.sseq - 1,
  3125→		},
  3126→		AckFloor: SequenceInfo{
  3127→			Consumer: o.adflr,
  3128→			Stream:   o.asflr,
  3129→		},
  3130→		NumAckPending:  len(o.pending),
  3131→		NumRedelivered: len(o.rdc),
  3132→		NumPending:     o.checkNumPending(),
  3133→		PushBound:      o.isPushMode() && o.active,
  3134→		TimeStamp:      time.Now().UTC(),
  3135→		PriorityGroups: priorityGroups,
  3136→	}
  3137→	// Reset redelivered for MaxDeliver 1. Redeliveries are disabled so must not report it (is confusing otherwise).
  3138→	// The state does still keep track of these messages.
  3139→	if o.cfg.MaxDeliver == 1 {
  3140→		info.NumRedelivered = 0
  3141→	}
  3142→	if o.cfg.PauseUntil != nil {
  3143→		p := *o.cfg.PauseUntil
  3144→		if info.Paused = time.Now().Before(p); info.Paused {
  3145→			info.PauseRemaining = time.Until(p)
  3146→		}
  3147→	}
  3148→
  3149→	// We always need to pull certain data from our store.
  3150→	if o.store != nil {
  3151→		state, err := o.store.BorrowState()
  3152→		if err != nil {
  3153→			o.mu.Unlock()
  3154→			return nil
  3155→		}
  3156→
  3157→		// If we are the leader we could have o.sseq that is skipped ahead.
  3158→		// To maintain consistency in reporting (e.g. jsz) we always take the state for our delivered/ackfloor stream sequence.
  3159→		// Only use skipped ahead o.sseq if we're a new consumer and have not yet replicated this state yet.
  3160→		leader := o.isLeader()
  3161→		if !leader || o.store.HasState() {
  3162→			info.Delivered.Consumer, info.Delivered.Stream = state.Delivered.Consumer, state.Delivered.Stream
  3163→		}
  3164→		info.AckFloor.Consumer, info.AckFloor.Stream = state.AckFloor.Consumer, state.AckFloor.Stream
  3165→		if !leader {
  3166→			info.NumAckPending = len(state.Pending)
  3167→			info.NumRedelivered = len(state.Redelivered)
  3168→		}
  3169→	}
  3170→
  3171→	// Adjust active based on non-zero etc. Also make UTC here.
  3172→	if !o.ldt.IsZero() {
  3173→		ldt := o.ldt.UTC() // This copies as well.
  3174→		info.Delivered.Last = &ldt
  3175→	}
  3176→	if !o.lat.IsZero() {
  3177→		lat := o.lat.UTC() // This copies as well.
  3178→		info.AckFloor.Last = &lat
  3179→	}
  3180→
  3181→	// If we are a pull mode consumer, report on number of waiting requests.
  3182→	if o.isPullMode() {
  3183→		o.processWaiting(false)
  3184→		info.NumWaiting = o.waiting.len()
  3185→	}
  3186→	// If we were asked to snapshot do so here.
  3187→	if snap {
  3188→		o.ici = info
  3189→	}
  3190→	sysc := o.sysc
  3191→	o.mu.Unlock()
  3192→
  3193→	// Do cluster.
  3194→	if rg != nil {
  3195→		info.Cluster = js.clusterInfo(rg)
  3196→	}
  3197→
  3198→	// If we have a reply subject send the response here.
  3199→	if reply != _EMPTY_ && sysc != nil {
  3200→		sysc.sendInternalMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, nil, info)
  3201→	}
  3202→
  3203→	return info
  3204→}
  3205→
  3206→// Will signal us that new messages are available. Will break out of waiting.
  3207→func (o *consumer) signalNewMessages() {
  3208→	// Kick our new message channel
  3209→	select {
  3210→	case o.mch <- struct{}{}:
  3211→	default:
  3212→	}
  3213→}
  3214→
  3215→// shouldSample lets us know if we are sampling metrics on acks.
  3216→func (o *consumer) shouldSample() bool {
  3217→	switch {
  3218→	case o.sfreq <= 0:
  3219→		return false
  3220→	case o.sfreq >= 100:
  3221→		return true
  3222→	}
  3223→
  3224→	// TODO(ripienaar) this is a tad slow so we need to rethink here, however this will only
  3225→	// hit for those with sampling enabled and its not the default
  3226→	return rand.Int31n(100) <= o.sfreq
  3227→}
  3228→
  3229→func (o *consumer) sampleAck(sseq, dseq, dc uint64) {
  3230→	if !o.shouldSample() {
  3231→		return
  3232→	}
  3233→
  3234→	now := time.Now().UTC()
  3235→	unow := now.UnixNano()
  3236→
  3237→	e := JSConsumerAckMetric{
  3238→		TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
  3239→			Type: JSConsumerAckMetricType,
  3240→			ID:   nuid.Next(),
  3241→			Time: now,
  3242→		},
  3243→		Stream:      o.stream,
  3244→		Consumer:    o.name,
  3245→		ConsumerSeq: dseq,
  3246→		StreamSeq:   sseq,
  3247→		Delay:       unow - o.pending[sseq].Timestamp,
  3248→		Deliveries:  dc,
  3249→		Domain:      o.srv.getOpts().JetStreamDomain,
  3250→	}
  3251→
  3252→	o.sendAdvisory(o.ackEventT, e)
  3253→}
  3254→
  3255→// Process an ACK.
  3256→// Returns `true` if the ack was processed in place and the sender can now respond
  3257→// to the client, or `false` if there was an error or the ack is replicated (in which
  3258→// case the reply will be sent later).
  3259→func (o *consumer) processAckMsg(sseq, dseq, dc uint64, reply string, doSample bool) bool {
  3260→	o.mu.Lock()
  3261→	if o.closed {
  3262→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3263→		return false
  3264→	}
  3265→
  3266→	mset := o.mset
  3267→	if mset == nil || mset.closed.Load() {
  3268→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3269→		return false
  3270→	}
  3271→
  3272→	// Check if this ack is above the current pointer to our next to deliver.
  3273→	if sseq >= o.sseq {
  3274→		// Let's make sure this is valid.
  3275→		// This is only received on the consumer leader, so should never be higher
  3276→		// than the last stream sequence. But could happen if we've just become
  3277→		// consumer leader, and we are not up-to-date on the stream yet.
  3278→		var ss StreamState
  3279→		mset.store.FastState(&ss)
  3280→		if sseq > ss.LastSeq {
  3281→			o.srv.Warnf("JetStream consumer '%s > %s > %s' ACK sequence %d past last stream sequence of %d",
  3282→				o.acc.Name, o.stream, o.name, sseq, ss.LastSeq)
  3283→			// FIXME(dlc) - For 2.11 onwards should we return an error here to the caller?
  3284→		}
  3285→		// Even though another leader must have delivered a message with this sequence, we must not adjust
  3286→		// the current pointer. This could otherwise result in a stuck consumer, where messages below this
  3287→		// sequence can't be redelivered, and we'll have incorrect pending state and ack floors.
  3288→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3289→		return false
  3290→	}
  3291→
  3292→	// Let the owning stream know if we are interest or workqueue retention based.
  3293→	// If this consumer is clustered (o.node != nil) this will be handled by
  3294→	// processReplicatedAck after the ack has propagated.
  3295→	ackInPlace := o.node == nil && o.retention != LimitsPolicy
  3296→
  3297→	var sgap, floor uint64
  3298→	var needSignal bool
  3299→
  3300→	switch o.cfg.AckPolicy {
  3301→	case AckExplicit:
  3302→		if p, ok := o.pending[sseq]; ok {
  3303→			if doSample {
  3304→				o.sampleAck(sseq, dseq, dc)
  3305→			}
  3306→			if o.maxp > 0 && len(o.pending) >= o.maxp {
  3307→				needSignal = true
  3308→			}
  3309→			delete(o.pending, sseq)
  3310→			// Use the original deliver sequence from our pending record.
  3311→			dseq = p.Sequence
  3312→
  3313→			// Only move floors if we matched an existing pending.
  3314→			if len(o.pending) == 0 {
  3315→				o.adflr = o.dseq - 1
  3316→				o.asflr = o.sseq - 1
  3317→			} else if dseq == o.adflr+1 {
  3318→				o.adflr, o.asflr = dseq, sseq
  3319→				for ss := sseq + 1; ss < o.sseq; ss++ {
  3320→					if p, ok := o.pending[ss]; ok {
  3321→						if p.Sequence > 0 {
  3322→							o.adflr, o.asflr = p.Sequence-1, ss-1
  3323→						}
  3324→						break
  3325→					}
  3326→				}
  3327→			}
  3328→		}
  3329→		delete(o.rdc, sseq)
  3330→		o.removeFromRedeliverQueue(sseq)
  3331→	case AckAll:
  3332→		// no-op
  3333→		if dseq <= o.adflr || sseq <= o.asflr {
  3334→			o.mu.Unlock()
  3335→			// Return true to let caller respond back to the client.
  3336→			return true
  3337→		}
  3338→		if o.maxp > 0 && len(o.pending) >= o.maxp {
  3339→			needSignal = true
  3340→		}
  3341→		sgap = sseq - o.asflr
  3342→		floor = sseq // start at same and set lower as we go.
  3343→		o.adflr, o.asflr = dseq, sseq
  3344→
  3345→		remove := func(seq uint64) {
  3346→			delete(o.pending, seq)
  3347→			delete(o.rdc, seq)
  3348→			o.removeFromRedeliverQueue(seq)
  3349→			if seq < floor {
  3350→				floor = seq
  3351→			}
  3352→		}
  3353→		// Determine if smarter to walk all of pending vs the sequence range.
  3354→		if sgap > uint64(len(o.pending)) {
  3355→			for seq := range o.pending {
  3356→				if seq <= sseq {
  3357→					remove(seq)
  3358→				}
  3359→			}
  3360→		} else {
  3361→			for seq := sseq; seq > sseq-sgap && len(o.pending) > 0; seq-- {
  3362→				remove(seq)
  3363→			}
  3364→		}
  3365→	case AckNone:
  3366→		// FIXME(dlc) - This is error but do we care?
  3367→		o.mu.Unlock()
  3368→		return ackInPlace
  3369→	}
  3370→
  3371→	// No ack replication, so we set reply to "" so that updateAcks does not
  3372→	// send the reply. The caller will.
  3373→	if ackInPlace {
  3374→		reply = _EMPTY_
  3375→	}
  3376→	// Update underlying store.
  3377→	o.updateAcks(dseq, sseq, reply)
  3378→	o.mu.Unlock()
  3379→
  3380→	if ackInPlace {
  3381→		if sgap > 1 {
  3382→			// FIXME(dlc) - This can very inefficient, will need to fix.
  3383→			for seq := sseq; seq >= floor; seq-- {
  3384→				mset.ackMsg(o, seq)
  3385→			}
  3386→		} else {
  3387→			mset.ackMsg(o, sseq)
  3388→		}
  3389→	}
  3390→
  3391→	// If we had max ack pending set and were at limit we need to unblock ourselves.
  3392→	if needSignal {
  3393→		o.signalNewMessages()
  3394→	}
  3395→	return ackInPlace
  3396→}
  3397→
  3398→// Determine if this is a truly filtered consumer. Modern clients will place filtered subjects
  3399→// even if the stream only has a single non-wildcard subject designation.
  3400→// Read lock should be held.
  3401→func (o *consumer) isFiltered() bool {
  3402→	if o.subjf == nil {
  3403→		return false
  3404→	}
  3405→	// If we are here we want to check if the filtered subject is
  3406→	// a direct match for our only listed subject.
  3407→	mset := o.mset
  3408→	if mset == nil {
  3409→		return true
  3410→	}
  3411→
  3412→	// Protect access to mset.cfg with the cfgMu mutex.
  3413→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  3414→	msetSubjects := mset.cfg.Subjects
  3415→	mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  3416→
  3417→	// `isFiltered` need to be performant, so we do
  3418→	// as any checks as possible to avoid unnecessary work.
  3419→	// Here we avoid iteration over slices if there is only one subject in stream
  3420→	// and one filter for the consumer.
  3421→	if len(msetSubjects) == 1 && len(o.subjf) == 1 {
  3422→		return msetSubjects[0] != o.subjf[0].subject
  3423→	}
  3424→
  3425→	// if the list is not equal length, we can return early, as this is filtered.
  3426→	if len(msetSubjects) != len(o.subjf) {
  3427→		return true
  3428→	}
  3429→
  3430→	// if in rare case scenario that user passed all stream subjects as consumer filters,
  3431→	// we need to do a more expensive operation.
  3432→	// reflect.DeepEqual would return false if the filters are the same, but in different order
  3433→	// so it can't be used here.
  3434→	cfilters := make(map[string]struct{}, len(o.subjf))
  3435→	for _, val := range o.subjf {
  3436→		cfilters[val.subject] = struct{}{}
  3437→	}
  3438→	for _, val := range msetSubjects {
  3439→		if _, ok := cfilters[val]; !ok {
  3440→			return true
  3441→		}
  3442→	}
  3443→	return false
  3444→}
  3445→
  3446→// Check if we need an ack for this store seq.
  3447→// This is called for interest based retention streams to remove messages.
  3448→func (o *consumer) needAck(sseq uint64, subj string) bool {
  3449→	var needAck bool
  3450→	var asflr, osseq uint64
  3451→	var pending map[uint64]*Pending
  3452→	var rdc map[uint64]uint64
  3453→
  3454→	o.mu.RLock()
  3455→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  3456→
  3457→	isFiltered := o.isFiltered()
  3458→	if isFiltered && o.mset == nil {
  3459→		return false
  3460→	}
  3461→
  3462→	// Check if we are filtered, and if so check if this is even applicable to us.
  3463→	if isFiltered {
  3464→		if subj == _EMPTY_ {
  3465→			var err error
  3466→			if subj, err = o.mset.store.SubjectForSeq(sseq); err != nil {
  3467→				return false
  3468→			}
  3469→		}
  3470→		if !o.isFilteredMatch(subj) {
  3471→			return false
  3472→		}
  3473→	}
  3474→	if o.isLeader() {
  3475→		asflr, osseq = o.asflr, o.sseq
  3476→		pending, rdc = o.pending, o.rdc
  3477→	} else {
  3478→		if o.store == nil {
  3479→			return false
  3480→		}
  3481→		state, err := o.store.BorrowState()
  3482→		if err != nil || state == nil {
  3483→			// Fall back to what we track internally for now.
  3484→			return sseq > o.asflr && !o.isFiltered()
  3485→		}
  3486→		// If loading state as here, the osseq is +1.
  3487→		asflr, osseq, pending, rdc = state.AckFloor.Stream, state.Delivered.Stream+1, state.Pending, state.Redelivered
  3488→	}
  3489→
  3490→	switch o.cfg.AckPolicy {
  3491→	case AckNone, AckAll:
  3492→		needAck = sseq > asflr
  3493→	case AckExplicit:
  3494→		if sseq > asflr {
  3495→			if sseq >= osseq {
  3496→				needAck = true
  3497→			} else {
  3498→				_, needAck = pending[sseq]
  3499→			}
  3500→		}
  3501→	}
  3502→
  3503→	// Finally check if redelivery of this message is tracked.
  3504→	// If the message is not pending, it should be preserved if it reached max delivery.
  3505→	if !needAck {
  3506→		_, needAck = rdc[sseq]
  3507→	}
  3508→
  3509→	return needAck
  3510→}
  3511→
  3512→type PriorityGroup struct {
  3513→	Group         string `json:"group,omitempty"`
  3514→	MinPending    int64  `json:"min_pending,omitempty"`
  3515→	MinAckPending int64  `json:"min_ack_pending,omitempty"`
  3516→	Id            string `json:"id,omitempty"`
  3517→	Priority      int    `json:"priority,omitempty"`
  3518→}
  3519→
  3520→// Used in nextReqFromMsg, since the json.Unmarshal causes the request
  3521→// struct to escape to the heap always. This should reduce GC pressure.
  3522→var jsGetNextPool = sync.Pool{
  3523→	New: func() any {
  3524→		return &JSApiConsumerGetNextRequest{}
  3525→	},
  3526→}
  3527→
  3528→// Helper for the next message requests.
  3529→func nextReqFromMsg(msg []byte) (time.Time, int, int, bool, time.Duration, time.Time, *PriorityGroup, error) {
  3530→	req := bytes.TrimSpace(msg)
  3531→
  3532→	switch {
  3533→	case len(req) == 0:
  3534→		return time.Time{}, 1, 0, false, 0, time.Time{}, nil, nil
  3535→
  3536→	case req[0] == '{':
  3537→		cr := jsGetNextPool.Get().(*JSApiConsumerGetNextRequest)
  3538→		defer func() {
  3539→			*cr = JSApiConsumerGetNextRequest{}
  3540→			jsGetNextPool.Put(cr)
  3541→		}()
  3542→		if err := json.Unmarshal(req, &cr); err != nil {
  3543→			return time.Time{}, -1, 0, false, 0, time.Time{}, nil, err
  3544→		}
  3545→		var hbt time.Time
  3546→		if cr.Heartbeat > 0 {
  3547→			if cr.Heartbeat*2 > cr.Expires {
  3548→				return time.Time{}, 1, 0, false, 0, time.Time{}, nil, errors.New("heartbeat value too large")
  3549→			}
  3550→			hbt = time.Now().Add(cr.Heartbeat)
  3551→		}
  3552→		priorityGroup := cr.PriorityGroup
  3553→		if cr.Expires == time.Duration(0) {
  3554→			return time.Time{}, cr.Batch, cr.MaxBytes, cr.NoWait, cr.Heartbeat, hbt, &priorityGroup, nil
  3555→		}
  3556→		return time.Now().Add(cr.Expires), cr.Batch, cr.MaxBytes, cr.NoWait, cr.Heartbeat, hbt, &priorityGroup, nil
  3557→	default:
  3558→		if n, err := strconv.Atoi(string(req)); err == nil {
  3559→			return time.Time{}, n, 0, false, 0, time.Time{}, nil, nil
  3560→		}
  3561→	}
  3562→
  3563→	return time.Time{}, 1, 0, false, 0, time.Time{}, nil, nil
  3564→}
  3565→
  3566→// Represents a request that is on the internal waiting queue
  3567→type waitingRequest struct {
  3568→	next          *waitingRequest
  3569→	acc           *Account
  3570→	interest      string
  3571→	reply         string
  3572→	n             int // For batching
  3573→	d             int // num delivered
  3574→	b             int // For max bytes tracking
  3575→	expires       time.Time
  3576→	received      time.Time
  3577→	hb            time.Duration
  3578→	hbt           time.Time
  3579→	noWait        bool
  3580→	priorityGroup *PriorityGroup
  3581→}
  3582→
  3583→// sync.Pool for waiting requests.
  3584→var wrPool = sync.Pool{
  3585→	New: func() any {
  3586→		return new(waitingRequest)
  3587→	},
  3588→}
  3589→
  3590→// Recycle this request. This request can not be accessed after this call.
  3591→func (wr *waitingRequest) recycleIfDone() bool {
  3592→	if wr != nil && wr.n <= 0 {
  3593→		wr.recycle()
  3594→		return true
  3595→	}
  3596→	return false
  3597→}
  3598→
  3599→// Force a recycle.
  3600→func (wr *waitingRequest) recycle() {
  3601→	if wr != nil {
  3602→		wr.next, wr.acc, wr.interest, wr.reply = nil, nil, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_
  3603→		wrPool.Put(wr)
  3604→	}
  3605→}
  3606→
  3607→// Represents an (optional) request timeout that's sent after waiting for replicated deliveries.
  3608→type waitingDelivery struct {
  3609→	seq uint64
  3610→	pn  int // Pending messages.
  3611→	pb  int // Pending bytes.
  3612→}
  3613→
  3614→// sync.Pool for waiting deliveries.
  3615→var wdPool = sync.Pool{
  3616→	New: func() any {
  3617→		return new(waitingDelivery)
  3618→	},
  3619→}
  3620→
  3621→// Force a recycle.
  3622→func (wd *waitingDelivery) recycle() {
  3623→	if wd != nil {
  3624→		wd.seq, wd.pn, wd.pb = 0, 0, 0
  3625→		wdPool.Put(wd)
  3626→	}
  3627→}
  3628→
  3629→// waiting queue for requests that are waiting for new messages to arrive.
  3630→type waitQueue struct {
  3631→	n, max int
  3632→	last   time.Time
  3633→	head   *waitingRequest
  3634→	tail   *waitingRequest
  3635→}
  3636→
  3637→// Create a new ring buffer with at most max items.
  3638→func newWaitQueue(max int) *waitQueue {
  3639→	return &waitQueue{max: max}
  3640→}
  3641→
  3642→var (
  3643→	errWaitQueueFull = errors.New("wait queue is full")
  3644→	errWaitQueueNil  = errors.New("wait queue is nil")
  3645→)
  3646→
  3647→// insertSorted inserts wr at the correct position based on priority
  3648→func (wq *waitQueue) insertSorted(wr *waitingRequest) {
  3649→
  3650→	// Handle empty queue
  3651→	if wq.head == nil {
  3652→		wq.head = wr
  3653→		wq.tail = wr
  3654→		wr.next = nil
  3655→		return
  3656→	}
  3657→	insertAtPosition(wr, wq)
  3658→}
  3659→
  3660→func (wq *waitQueue) addPrioritized(wr *waitingRequest) error {
  3661→	if wq == nil {
  3662→		return errWaitQueueNil
  3663→	}
  3664→	if wq.isFull() {
  3665→		return errWaitQueueFull
  3666→	}
  3667→
  3668→	wq.insertSorted(wr)
  3669→	wq.n++
  3670→	wq.last = wr.received
  3671→	return nil
  3672→}
  3673→
  3674→// Adds in a new request.
  3675→func (wq *waitQueue) add(wr *waitingRequest) error {
  3676→	if wq == nil {
  3677→		return errWaitQueueNil
  3678→	}
  3679→	if wq.isFull() {
  3680→		return errWaitQueueFull
  3681→	}
  3682→	if wq.head == nil {
  3683→		wq.head = wr
  3684→	} else {
  3685→		wq.tail.next = wr
  3686→	}
  3687→	// Always set tail.
  3688→	wq.tail = wr
  3689→	// Make sure nil
  3690→	wr.next = nil
  3691→
  3692→	// Track last active via when we receive a request.
  3693→	wq.last = wr.received
  3694→	wq.n++
  3695→	return nil
  3696→}
  3697→
  3698→func (wq *waitQueue) isFull() bool {
  3699→	if wq == nil {
  3700→		return false
  3701→	}
  3702→	return wq.n == wq.max
  3703→}
  3704→
  3705→func (wq *waitQueue) isEmpty() bool {
  3706→	if wq == nil {
  3707→		return true
  3708→	}
  3709→	return wq.n == 0
  3710→}
  3711→
  3712→func (wq *waitQueue) len() int {
  3713→	if wq == nil {
  3714→		return 0
  3715→	}
  3716→	return wq.n
  3717→}
  3718→
  3719→// Peek will return the next request waiting or nil if empty.
  3720→func (wq *waitQueue) peek() *waitingRequest {
  3721→	if wq == nil {
  3722→		return nil
  3723→	}
  3724→	return wq.head
  3725→}
  3726→
  3727→func (wq *waitQueue) cycle() {
  3728→	wr := wq.peek()
  3729→	if wr != nil {
  3730→		// Always remove current now on a pop, and move to end if still valid.
  3731→		// If we were the only one don't need to remove since this can be a no-op.
  3732→		wq.removeCurrent()
  3733→		wq.add(wr)
  3734→	}
  3735→}
  3736→
  3737→func (wq *waitQueue) popOrPopAndRequeue(priority PriorityPolicy) *waitingRequest {
  3738→	if wq == nil || wq.head == nil {
  3739→		return nil
  3740→	}
  3741→
  3742→	if priority == PriorityPrioritized {
  3743→		return wq.popAndRequeue()
  3744→	}
  3745→	return wq.pop()
  3746→}
  3747→
  3748→// pop will return the next request and move the read cursor.
  3749→// This will now place a request that still has pending items at the ends of the list.
  3750→func (wq *waitQueue) pop() *waitingRequest {
  3751→	wr := wq.peek()
  3752→	if wr != nil {
  3753→		wr.d++
  3754→		wr.n--
  3755→		// Always remove current now on a pop, and move to end if still valid.
  3756→		// If we were the only one don't need to remove since this can be a no-op.
  3757→		if wr.n > 0 && wq.n > 1 {
  3758→			wq.removeCurrent()
  3759→			wq.add(wr)
  3760→		} else if wr.n <= 0 {
  3761→			wq.removeCurrent()
  3762→		}
  3763→	}
  3764→	return wr
  3765→}
  3766→
  3767→// popAndRequeue pops the head element and requeues it at the end of its priority group.
  3768→// This maintains FIFO order within the same priority level.
  3769→func (wq *waitQueue) popAndRequeue() *waitingRequest {
  3770→	if wq == nil || wq.head == nil {
  3771→		return nil
  3772→	}
  3773→
  3774→	// Save the head
  3775→	wr := wq.head
  3776→
  3777→	if wr == nil {
  3778→		return wr
  3779→	}
  3780→
  3781→	wr.d++
  3782→	wr.n--
  3783→
  3784→	if wr.n > 0 && wq.n > 1 {
  3785→		if wr.next == nil {
  3786→			return wr
  3787→		}
  3788→		wq.head = wq.head.next
  3789→		wr.next = nil
  3790→
  3791→		insertAtPosition(wr, wq)
  3792→
  3793→	} else if wr.n <= 0 {
  3794→		wq.removeCurrent()
  3795→		return wr
  3796→
  3797→	}
  3798→
  3799→	return wr
  3800→}
  3801→
  3802→func insertAtPosition(wr *waitingRequest, wq *waitQueue) {
  3803→	priority := math.MaxInt32
  3804→	if wr.priorityGroup != nil {
  3805→		priority = wr.priorityGroup.Priority
  3806→	}
  3807→
  3808→	var prev *waitingRequest
  3809→	current := wq.head
  3810→	for current != nil {
  3811→		currentPriority := math.MaxInt32
  3812→		if current.priorityGroup != nil {
  3813→			currentPriority = current.priorityGroup.Priority
  3814→		}
  3815→		if currentPriority > priority {
  3816→			break
  3817→		}
  3818→		prev = current
  3819→		current = current.next
  3820→	}
  3821→
  3822→	if prev == nil {
  3823→		// All remaining elements have higher priority
  3824→		wr.next = wq.head
  3825→		wq.head = wr
  3826→	} else {
  3827→		wr.next = prev.next
  3828→		prev.next = wr
  3829→		if wr.next == nil {
  3830→			wq.tail = wr
  3831→		}
  3832→	}
  3833→}
  3834→
  3835→// Removes the current read pointer (head FIFO) entry.
  3836→func (wq *waitQueue) removeCurrent() {
  3837→	wq.remove(nil, wq.head)
  3838→}
  3839→
  3840→// Remove the wr element from the wait queue.
  3841→func (wq *waitQueue) remove(pre, wr *waitingRequest) {
  3842→	if wr == nil {
  3843→		return
  3844→	}
  3845→	if pre != nil {
  3846→		pre.next = wr.next
  3847→	} else if wr == wq.head {
  3848→		// We are removing head here.
  3849→		wq.head = wr.next
  3850→	}
  3851→	// Check if wr was our tail.
  3852→	if wr == wq.tail {
  3853→		// Check if we need to assign to pre.
  3854→		if wr.next == nil {
  3855→			wq.tail = pre
  3856→		} else {
  3857→			wq.tail = wr.next
  3858→		}
  3859→	}
  3860→	wq.n--
  3861→}
  3862→
  3863→// Return the map of pending requests keyed by the reply subject.
  3864→// No-op if push consumer or invalid etc.
  3865→func (o *consumer) pendingRequests() map[string]*waitingRequest {
  3866→	if o.waiting == nil {
  3867→		return nil
  3868→	}
  3869→	wq, m := o.waiting, make(map[string]*waitingRequest)
  3870→	for wr := wq.head; wr != nil; wr = wr.next {
  3871→		m[wr.reply] = wr
  3872→	}
  3873→
  3874→	return m
  3875→}
  3876→
  3877→func (o *consumer) setPinnedTimer(priorityGroup string) {
  3878→	if o.pinnedTtl != nil {
  3879→		o.pinnedTtl.Reset(o.cfg.PinnedTTL)
  3880→	} else {
  3881→		o.pinnedTtl = time.AfterFunc(o.cfg.PinnedTTL, func() {
  3882→			o.mu.Lock()
  3883→			o.currentPinId = _EMPTY_
  3884→			o.sendUnpinnedAdvisoryLocked(priorityGroup, "timeout")
  3885→			o.mu.Unlock()
  3886→			o.signalNewMessages()
  3887→		})
  3888→	}
  3889→}
  3890→
  3891→// Return next waiting request. This will check for expirations but not noWait or interest.
  3892→// That will be handled by processWaiting.
  3893→// Lock should be held.
  3894→func (o *consumer) nextWaiting(sz int) *waitingRequest {
  3895→	if o.waiting == nil || o.waiting.isEmpty() {
  3896→		return nil
  3897→	}
  3898→
  3899→	// Check if server needs to assign a new pin id.
  3900→	needNewPin := o.currentPinId == _EMPTY_ && o.cfg.PriorityPolicy == PriorityPinnedClient
  3901→	// As long as we support only one priority group, we can capture  that group here and reuse it.
  3902→	var priorityGroup string
  3903→	if len(o.cfg.PriorityGroups) > 0 {
  3904→		priorityGroup = o.cfg.PriorityGroups[0]
  3905→	}
  3906→
  3907→	numCycled := 0
  3908→	for wr := o.waiting.peek(); !o.waiting.isEmpty(); wr = o.waiting.peek() {
  3909→		if wr == nil {
  3910→			break
  3911→		}
  3912→		// Check if we have max bytes set.
  3913→		if wr.b > 0 {
  3914→			if sz <= wr.b {
  3915→				wr.b -= sz
  3916→				// If we are right now at zero, set batch to 1 to deliver this one but stop after.
  3917→				if wr.b == 0 {
  3918→					wr.n = 1
  3919→				}
  3920→			} else {
  3921→				// Since we can't send that message to the requestor, we need to
  3922→				// notify that we are closing the request.
  3923→				const maxBytesT = "NATS/1.0 409 Message Size Exceeds MaxBytes\r\n%s: %d\r\n%s: %d\r\n\r\n"
  3924→				hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, maxBytesT, JSPullRequestPendingMsgs, wr.n, JSPullRequestPendingBytes, wr.b)
  3925→				o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  3926→				// Remove the current one, no longer valid due to max bytes limit.
  3927→				o.waiting.removeCurrent()
  3928→				if o.node != nil {
  3929→					o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  3930→				}
  3931→				wr.recycle()
  3932→				continue
  3933→			}
  3934→		}
  3935→
  3936→		if wr.expires.IsZero() || time.Now().Before(wr.expires) {
  3937→			if needNewPin {
  3938→				if wr.priorityGroup.Id == _EMPTY_ {
  3939→					o.currentPinId = nuid.Next()
  3940→					o.pinnedTS = time.Now().UTC()
  3941→					wr.priorityGroup.Id = o.currentPinId
  3942→					o.setPinnedTimer(priorityGroup)
  3943→
  3944→				} else {
  3945→					// There is pin id set, but not a matching one. Send a notification to the client and remove the request.
  3946→					// Probably this is the old pin id.
  3947→					o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, []byte(JSPullRequestWrongPinID), nil, nil, 0))
  3948→					o.waiting.removeCurrent()
  3949→					if o.node != nil {
  3950→						o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  3951→					}
  3952→					wr.recycle()
  3953→					continue
  3954→				}
  3955→			} else if o.currentPinId != _EMPTY_ {
  3956→				// Check if we have a match on the currentNuid
  3957→				if wr.priorityGroup != nil && wr.priorityGroup.Id == o.currentPinId {
  3958→					// If we have a match, we do nothing here and will deliver the message later down the code path.
  3959→				} else if wr.priorityGroup.Id == _EMPTY_ {
  3960→					o.waiting.cycle()
  3961→					numCycled++
  3962→					if numCycled >= o.waiting.len() {
  3963→						return nil
  3964→					}
  3965→					continue
  3966→				} else {
  3967→					// There is pin id set, but not a matching one. Send a notification to the client and remove the request.
  3968→					o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, []byte(JSPullRequestWrongPinID), nil, nil, 0))
  3969→					o.waiting.removeCurrent()
  3970→					if o.node != nil {
  3971→						o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  3972→					}
  3973→					wr.recycle()
  3974→					continue
  3975→				}
  3976→			}
  3977→
  3978→			if o.cfg.PriorityPolicy == PriorityOverflow {
  3979→				if wr.priorityGroup != nil &&
  3980→					// We need to check o.npc+1, because before calling nextWaiting, we do o.npc--
  3981→					(wr.priorityGroup.MinPending > 0 && wr.priorityGroup.MinPending > o.npc+1 ||
  3982→						wr.priorityGroup.MinAckPending > 0 && wr.priorityGroup.MinAckPending > int64(len(o.pending))) {
  3983→					o.waiting.cycle()
  3984→					numCycled++
  3985→					// We're done cycling through the requests.
  3986→					if numCycled >= o.waiting.len() {
  3987→						return nil
  3988→					}
  3989→					continue
  3990→				}
  3991→			}
  3992→			if wr.acc.sl.HasInterest(wr.interest) {
  3993→				if needNewPin {
  3994→					o.sendPinnedAdvisoryLocked(priorityGroup)
  3995→				}
  3996→				return o.waiting.popOrPopAndRequeue(o.cfg.PriorityPolicy)
  3997→			} else if time.Since(wr.received) < defaultGatewayRecentSubExpiration && (o.srv.leafNodeEnabled || o.srv.gateway.enabled) {
  3998→				if needNewPin {
  3999→					o.sendPinnedAdvisoryLocked(priorityGroup)
  4000→				}
  4001→				return o.waiting.popOrPopAndRequeue(o.cfg.PriorityPolicy)
  4002→			} else if o.srv.gateway.enabled && o.srv.hasGatewayInterest(wr.acc.Name, wr.interest) {
  4003→				if needNewPin {
  4004→					o.sendPinnedAdvisoryLocked(priorityGroup)
  4005→				}
  4006→				return o.waiting.popOrPopAndRequeue(o.cfg.PriorityPolicy)
  4007→			}
  4008→		} else {
  4009→			// We do check for expiration in `processWaiting`, but it is possible to hit the expiry here, and not there.
  4010→			rdWait := o.replicateDeliveries()
  4011→			if rdWait {
  4012→				// Check if we need to send the timeout after pending replicated deliveries, or can do so immediately.
  4013→				if wd, ok := o.waitingDeliveries[wr.reply]; !ok {
  4014→					rdWait = false
  4015→				} else {
  4016→					wd.pn, wd.pb = wr.n, wr.b
  4017→				}
  4018→			}
  4019→			if !rdWait {
  4020→				hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, "NATS/1.0 408 Request Timeout\r\n%s: %d\r\n%s: %d\r\n\r\n", JSPullRequestPendingMsgs, wr.n, JSPullRequestPendingBytes, wr.b)
  4021→				o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4022→			}
  4023→			o.waiting.removeCurrent()
  4024→			if o.node != nil {
  4025→				o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  4026→			}
  4027→			wr.recycle()
  4028→			continue
  4029→
  4030→		}
  4031→		if wr.interest != wr.reply {
  4032→			const intExpT = "NATS/1.0 408 Interest Expired\r\n%s: %d\r\n%s: %d\r\n\r\n"
  4033→			hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, intExpT, JSPullRequestPendingMsgs, wr.n, JSPullRequestPendingBytes, wr.b)
  4034→			o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4035→		}
  4036→		// Remove the current one, no longer valid.
  4037→		o.waiting.removeCurrent()
  4038→		if o.node != nil {
  4039→			o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  4040→		}
  4041→		wr.recycle()
  4042→	}
  4043→
  4044→	return nil
  4045→}
  4046→
  4047→// Next message request.
  4048→type nextMsgReq struct {
  4049→	reply string
  4050→	msg   []byte
  4051→}
  4052→
  4053→var nextMsgReqPool sync.Pool
  4054→
  4055→func newNextMsgReq(reply string, msg []byte) *nextMsgReq {
  4056→	var nmr *nextMsgReq
  4057→	m := nextMsgReqPool.Get()
  4058→	if m != nil {
  4059→		nmr = m.(*nextMsgReq)
  4060→	} else {
  4061→		nmr = &nextMsgReq{}
  4062→	}
  4063→	// When getting something from a pool it is critical that all fields are
  4064→	// initialized. Doing this way guarantees that if someone adds a field to
  4065→	// the structure, the compiler will fail the build if this line is not updated.
  4066→	(*nmr) = nextMsgReq{reply, msg}
  4067→	return nmr
  4068→}
  4069→
  4070→func (nmr *nextMsgReq) returnToPool() {
  4071→	if nmr == nil {
  4072→		return
  4073→	}
  4074→	nmr.reply, nmr.msg = _EMPTY_, nil
  4075→	nextMsgReqPool.Put(nmr)
  4076→}
  4077→
  4078→// processNextMsgReq will process a request for the next message available. A nil message payload means deliver
  4079→// a single message. If the payload is a formal request or a number parseable with Atoi(), then we will send a
  4080→// batch of messages without requiring another request to this endpoint, or an ACK.
  4081→func (o *consumer) processNextMsgReq(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, _, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  4082→	if reply == _EMPTY_ {
  4083→		return
  4084→	}
  4085→
  4086→	// Short circuit error here.
  4087→	if o.nextMsgReqs == nil {
  4088→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 409 Consumer is push based\r\n\r\n")
  4089→		o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4090→		return
  4091→	}
  4092→
  4093→	hdr, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  4094→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  4095→		hdr = []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Required Api Level\r\n\r\n")
  4096→		o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4097→		return
  4098→	}
  4099→	o.nextMsgReqs.push(newNextMsgReq(reply, copyBytes(msg)))
  4100→}
  4101→
  4102→func (o *consumer) processNextMsgRequest(reply string, msg []byte) {
  4103→	o.mu.Lock()
  4104→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  4105→
  4106→	mset := o.mset
  4107→	if mset == nil {
  4108→		return
  4109→	}
  4110→
  4111→	sendErr := func(status int, description string) {
  4112→		hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, "NATS/1.0 %d %s\r\n\r\n", status, description)
  4113→		o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4114→	}
  4115→
  4116→	if o.isPushMode() || o.waiting == nil {
  4117→		sendErr(409, "Consumer is push based")
  4118→		return
  4119→	}
  4120→
  4121→	// Check payload here to see if they sent in batch size or a formal request.
  4122→	expires, batchSize, maxBytes, noWait, hb, hbt, priorityGroup, err := nextReqFromMsg(msg)
  4123→	if err != nil {
  4124→		sendErr(400, fmt.Sprintf("Bad Request - %v", err))
  4125→		return
  4126→	}
  4127→
  4128→	// Check for request limits
  4129→	if o.cfg.MaxRequestBatch > 0 && batchSize > o.cfg.MaxRequestBatch {
  4130→		sendErr(409, fmt.Sprintf("Exceeded MaxRequestBatch of %d", o.cfg.MaxRequestBatch))
  4131→		return
  4132→	}
  4133→
  4134→	if !expires.IsZero() && o.cfg.MaxRequestExpires > 0 && expires.After(time.Now().Add(o.cfg.MaxRequestExpires)) {
  4135→		sendErr(409, fmt.Sprintf("Exceeded MaxRequestExpires of %v", o.cfg.MaxRequestExpires))
  4136→		return
  4137→	}
  4138→
  4139→	if maxBytes > 0 && o.cfg.MaxRequestMaxBytes > 0 && maxBytes > o.cfg.MaxRequestMaxBytes {
  4140→		sendErr(409, fmt.Sprintf("Exceeded MaxRequestMaxBytes of %v", o.cfg.MaxRequestMaxBytes))
  4141→		return
  4142→	}
  4143→
  4144→	if priorityGroup != nil {
  4145→		if (priorityGroup.MinPending != 0 || priorityGroup.MinAckPending != 0) && o.cfg.PriorityPolicy != PriorityOverflow {
  4146→			sendErr(400, "Bad Request - Not a Overflow Priority consumer")
  4147→		}
  4148→
  4149→		if priorityGroup.Id != _EMPTY_ && o.cfg.PriorityPolicy != PriorityPinnedClient {
  4150→			sendErr(400, "Bad Request - Not a Pinned Client Priority consumer")
  4151→		}
  4152→		if priorityGroup.Priority < 0 || priorityGroup.Priority > 9 {
  4153→			sendErr(400, "Bad Request - Priority must be between 0 and 9")
  4154→			return
  4155→		}
  4156→	}
  4157→
  4158→	if priorityGroup != nil && o.cfg.PriorityPolicy != PriorityNone {
  4159→		if priorityGroup.Group == _EMPTY_ {
  4160→			sendErr(400, "Bad Request - Priority Group missing")
  4161→			return
  4162→		}
  4163→
  4164→		found := false
  4165→		for _, group := range o.cfg.PriorityGroups {
  4166→			if group == priorityGroup.Group {
  4167→				found = true
  4168→				break
  4169→			}
  4170→		}
  4171→		if !found {
  4172→			sendErr(400, "Bad Request - Invalid Priority Group")
  4173→			return
  4174→		}
  4175→
  4176→		if o.currentPinId != _EMPTY_ {
  4177→			if priorityGroup.Id == o.currentPinId {
  4178→				o.setPinnedTimer(priorityGroup.Group)
  4179→			} else if priorityGroup.Id != _EMPTY_ {
  4180→				sendErr(423, "Nats-Pin-Id mismatch")
  4181→				return
  4182→			}
  4183→		}
  4184→	}
  4185→
  4186→	// If we have the max number of requests already pending try to expire.
  4187→	if o.waiting.isFull() {
  4188→		// Try to expire some of the requests.
  4189→		// We do not want to push too hard here so at maximum process once per sec.
  4190→		if time.Since(o.lwqic) > time.Second {
  4191→			o.processWaiting(false)
  4192→		}
  4193→	}
  4194→
  4195→	// If the request is for noWait and we have pending requests already, check if we have room.
  4196→	if noWait {
  4197→		msgsPending := o.numPending() + uint64(len(o.rdq))
  4198→		// If no pending at all, decide what to do with request.
  4199→		// If no expires was set then fail.
  4200→		if msgsPending == 0 && expires.IsZero() {
  4201→			o.waiting.last = time.Now()
  4202→			sendErr(404, "No Messages")
  4203→			return
  4204→		}
  4205→		if msgsPending > 0 {
  4206→			_, _, batchPending, _ := o.processWaiting(false)
  4207→			if msgsPending < uint64(batchPending) {
  4208→				o.waiting.last = time.Now()
  4209→				sendErr(408, "Requests Pending")
  4210→				return
  4211→			}
  4212→		}
  4213→		// If we are here this should be considered a one-shot situation.
  4214→		// We will wait for expires but will return as soon as we have any messages.
  4215→	}
  4216→
  4217→	// If we receive this request though an account export, we need to track that interest subject and account.
  4218→	acc, interest := trackDownAccountAndInterest(o.acc, reply)
  4219→
  4220→	// Create a waiting request.
  4221→	wr := wrPool.Get().(*waitingRequest)
  4222→	wr.acc, wr.interest, wr.reply, wr.n, wr.d, wr.noWait, wr.expires, wr.hb, wr.hbt, wr.priorityGroup = acc, interest, reply, batchSize, 0, noWait, expires, hb, hbt, priorityGroup
  4223→	wr.b = maxBytes
  4224→	wr.received = time.Now()
  4225→
  4226→	if o.cfg.PriorityPolicy == PriorityPrioritized {
  4227→		if err := o.waiting.addPrioritized(wr); err != nil {
  4228→			if hb == 0 {
  4229→				sendErr(409, "Exceeded MaxWaiting")
  4230→			}
  4231→			wr.recycle()
  4232→			return
  4233→		}
  4234→	} else {
  4235→		if err := o.waiting.add(wr); err != nil {
  4236→			// If the client has a heartbeat interval set, don't bother responding with a 409,
  4237→			// otherwise we can end up in a hot loop with the client re-requesting instead of
  4238→			// waiting for the missing heartbeats instead and retrying.
  4239→			if hb == 0 {
  4240→				sendErr(409, "Exceeded MaxWaiting")
  4241→			}
  4242→			wr.recycle()
  4243→			return
  4244→		}
  4245→	}
  4246→	o.signalNewMessages()
  4247→	// If we are clustered update our followers about this request.
  4248→	if o.node != nil {
  4249→		o.addClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  4250→	}
  4251→}
  4252→
  4253→func trackDownAccountAndInterest(acc *Account, interest string) (*Account, string) {
  4254→	for strings.HasPrefix(interest, replyPrefix) {
  4255→		oa := acc
  4256→		oa.mu.RLock()
  4257→		if oa.exports.responses == nil {
  4258→			oa.mu.RUnlock()
  4259→			break
  4260→		}
  4261→		si := oa.exports.responses[interest]
  4262→		if si == nil {
  4263→			oa.mu.RUnlock()
  4264→			break
  4265→		}
  4266→		acc, interest = si.acc, si.to
  4267→		oa.mu.RUnlock()
  4268→	}
  4269→	return acc, interest
  4270→}
  4271→
  4272→// Return current delivery count for a given sequence.
  4273→func (o *consumer) deliveryCount(seq uint64) uint64 {
  4274→	if o.rdc == nil {
  4275→		return 1
  4276→	}
  4277→	if dc := o.rdc[seq]; dc >= 1 {
  4278→		return dc
  4279→	}
  4280→	return 1
  4281→}
  4282→
  4283→// Increase the delivery count for this message.
  4284→// ONLY used on redelivery semantics.
  4285→// Lock should be held.
  4286→func (o *consumer) incDeliveryCount(sseq uint64) uint64 {
  4287→	if o.rdc == nil {
  4288→		o.rdc = make(map[uint64]uint64)
  4289→	}
  4290→	o.rdc[sseq] += 1
  4291→	return o.rdc[sseq] + 1
  4292→}
  4293→
  4294→// Used if we have to adjust on failed delivery or bad lookups.
  4295→// Those failed attempts should not increase deliver count.
  4296→// Lock should be held.
  4297→func (o *consumer) decDeliveryCount(sseq uint64) {
  4298→	if o.rdc == nil {
  4299→		o.rdc = make(map[uint64]uint64)
  4300→	}
  4301→	o.rdc[sseq] -= 1
  4302→}
  4303→
  4304→// send a delivery exceeded advisory.
  4305→func (o *consumer) notifyDeliveryExceeded(sseq, dc uint64) {
  4306→	e := JSConsumerDeliveryExceededAdvisory{
  4307→		TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
  4308→			Type: JSConsumerDeliveryExceededAdvisoryType,
  4309→			ID:   nuid.Next(),
  4310→			Time: time.Now().UTC(),
  4311→		},
  4312→		Stream:     o.stream,
  4313→		Consumer:   o.name,
  4314→		StreamSeq:  sseq,
  4315→		Deliveries: dc,
  4316→		Domain:     o.srv.getOpts().JetStreamDomain,
  4317→	}
  4318→
  4319→	o.sendAdvisory(o.deliveryExcEventT, e)
  4320→}
  4321→
  4322→// Check if the candidate subject matches a filter if its present.
  4323→// Lock should be held.
  4324→func (o *consumer) isFilteredMatch(subj string) bool {
  4325→	// No filter is automatic match.
  4326→	if o.subjf == nil {
  4327→		return true
  4328→	}
  4329→	for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  4330→		if !filter.hasWildcard && subj == filter.subject {
  4331→			return true
  4332→		}
  4333→	}
  4334→	// It's quicker to first check for non-wildcard filters, then
  4335→	// iterate again to check for subset match.
  4336→	tsa := [32]string{}
  4337→	tts := tokenizeSubjectIntoSlice(tsa[:0], subj)
  4338→	for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  4339→		if isSubsetMatchTokenized(tts, filter.tokenizedSubject) {
  4340→			return true
  4341→		}
  4342→	}
  4343→	return false
  4344→}
  4345→
  4346→// Check if the candidate filter subject is equal to or a subset match
  4347→// of one of the filter subjects.
  4348→// Lock should be held.
  4349→func (o *consumer) isEqualOrSubsetMatch(subj string) bool {
  4350→	for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  4351→		if !filter.hasWildcard && subj == filter.subject {
  4352→			return true
  4353→		}
  4354→	}
  4355→	tsa := [32]string{}
  4356→	tts := tokenizeSubjectIntoSlice(tsa[:0], subj)
  4357→	for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  4358→		if isSubsetMatchTokenized(filter.tokenizedSubject, tts) {
  4359→			return true
  4360→		}
  4361→	}
  4362→	return false
  4363→}
  4364→
  4365→var (
  4366→	errMaxAckPending = errors.New("max ack pending reached")
  4367→	errBadConsumer   = errors.New("consumer not valid")
  4368→	errNoInterest    = errors.New("consumer requires interest for delivery subject when ephemeral")
  4369→)
  4370→
  4371→// Get next available message from underlying store.
  4372→// Is partition aware and redeliver aware.
  4373→// Lock should be held.
  4374→func (o *consumer) getNextMsg() (*jsPubMsg, uint64, error) {
  4375→	if o.mset == nil || o.mset.store == nil {
  4376→		return nil, 0, errBadConsumer
  4377→	}
  4378→	// Process redelivered messages before looking at possibly "skip list" (deliver last per subject)
  4379→	if o.hasRedeliveries() {
  4380→		var seq, dc uint64
  4381→		for seq = o.getNextToRedeliver(); seq > 0; seq = o.getNextToRedeliver() {
  4382→			dc = o.incDeliveryCount(seq)
  4383→			if o.maxdc > 0 && dc > o.maxdc {
  4384→				// Only send once
  4385→				if dc == o.maxdc+1 {
  4386→					o.notifyDeliveryExceeded(seq, dc-1)
  4387→				}
  4388→				// Make sure to remove from pending.
  4389→				if p, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok && p != nil {
  4390→					delete(o.pending, seq)
  4391→					o.updateDelivered(p.Sequence, seq, dc, p.Timestamp)
  4392→				}
  4393→				continue
  4394→			}
  4395→			pmsg := getJSPubMsgFromPool()
  4396→			sm, err := o.mset.store.LoadMsg(seq, &pmsg.StoreMsg)
  4397→			if sm == nil || err != nil {
  4398→				pmsg.returnToPool()
  4399→				pmsg, dc = nil, 0
  4400→				// Adjust back deliver count.
  4401→				o.decDeliveryCount(seq)
  4402→			}
  4403→			// Message was scheduled for redelivery but was removed in the meantime.
  4404→			if err == ErrStoreMsgNotFound || err == errDeletedMsg {
  4405→				// This is a race condition where the message is still in o.pending and
  4406→				// scheduled for redelivery, but it has been removed from the stream.
  4407→				// o.processTerm is called in a goroutine so could run after we get here.
  4408→				// That will correct the pending state and delivery/ack floors, so just skip here.
  4409→				continue
  4410→			}
  4411→			return pmsg, dc, err
  4412→		}
  4413→	}
  4414→
  4415→	// Check if we have max pending.
  4416→	if o.maxp > 0 && len(o.pending) >= o.maxp {
  4417→		// maxp only set when ack policy != AckNone and user set MaxAckPending
  4418→		// Stall if we have hit max pending.
  4419→		return nil, 0, errMaxAckPending
  4420→	}
  4421→
  4422→	if o.hasSkipListPending() {
  4423→		seq := o.lss.seqs[0]
  4424→		if len(o.lss.seqs) == 1 {
  4425→			o.sseq = o.lss.resume
  4426→			o.lss = nil
  4427→			o.updateSkipped(o.sseq)
  4428→		} else {
  4429→			o.lss.seqs = o.lss.seqs[1:]
  4430→			o.sseq = seq
  4431→		}
  4432→		pmsg := getJSPubMsgFromPool()
  4433→		sm, err := o.mset.store.LoadMsg(seq, &pmsg.StoreMsg)
  4434→		if sm == nil || err != nil {
  4435→			pmsg.returnToPool()
  4436→		}
  4437→		o.sseq++
  4438→		return pmsg, 1, err
  4439→	}
  4440→
  4441→	// Hold onto this since we release the lock.
  4442→	store := o.mset.store
  4443→
  4444→	var sseq uint64
  4445→	var err error
  4446→	var sm *StoreMsg
  4447→	var pmsg = getJSPubMsgFromPool()
  4448→
  4449→	// Grab next message applicable to us.
  4450→	filters, subjf, fseq := o.filters, o.subjf, o.sseq
  4451→	// Check if we are multi-filtered or not.
  4452→	if filters != nil {
  4453→		sm, sseq, err = store.LoadNextMsgMulti(filters, fseq, &pmsg.StoreMsg)
  4454→	} else if len(subjf) > 0 { // Means single filtered subject since o.filters means > 1.
  4455→		filter, wc := subjf[0].subject, subjf[0].hasWildcard
  4456→		sm, sseq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(filter, wc, fseq, &pmsg.StoreMsg)
  4457→	} else {
  4458→		// No filter here.
  4459→		sm, sseq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(_EMPTY_, false, fseq, &pmsg.StoreMsg)
  4460→	}
  4461→	if sm == nil {
  4462→		pmsg.returnToPool()
  4463→		pmsg = nil
  4464→	}
  4465→	// Check if we should move our o.sseq.
  4466→	if sseq >= o.sseq {
  4467→		// If we are moving step by step then sseq == o.sseq.
  4468→		// If we have jumped we should update skipped for other replicas.
  4469→		if sseq != o.sseq && err == ErrStoreEOF {
  4470→			o.updateSkipped(sseq + 1)
  4471→		}
  4472→		o.sseq = sseq + 1
  4473→	}
  4474→	return pmsg, 1, err
  4475→}
  4476→
  4477→// Will check for expiration and lack of interest on waiting requests.
  4478→// Will also do any heartbeats and return the next expiration or HB interval.
  4479→func (o *consumer) processWaiting(eos bool) (int, int, int, time.Time) {
  4480→	var fexp time.Time
  4481→	if o.srv == nil || o.waiting.isEmpty() {
  4482→		return 0, 0, 0, fexp
  4483→	}
  4484→	// Mark our last check time.
  4485→	o.lwqic = time.Now()
  4486→
  4487→	var expired, brp int
  4488→	s, now := o.srv, time.Now()
  4489→
  4490→	wq := o.waiting
  4491→	remove := func(pre, wr *waitingRequest) *waitingRequest {
  4492→		expired++
  4493→		if o.node != nil {
  4494→			o.removeClusterPendingRequest(wr.reply)
  4495→		}
  4496→		next := wr.next
  4497→		wq.remove(pre, wr)
  4498→		wr.recycle()
  4499→		return next
  4500→	}
  4501→
  4502→	var pre *waitingRequest
  4503→	for wr := wq.head; wr != nil; {
  4504→		// Check expiration.
  4505→		if (eos && wr.noWait && wr.d > 0) || (!wr.expires.IsZero() && now.After(wr.expires)) {
  4506→			rdWait := o.replicateDeliveries()
  4507→			if rdWait {
  4508→				// Check if we need to send the timeout after pending replicated deliveries, or can do so immediately.
  4509→				if wd, ok := o.waitingDeliveries[wr.reply]; !ok {
  4510→					rdWait = false
  4511→				} else {
  4512→					wd.pn, wd.pb = wr.n, wr.b
  4513→				}
  4514→			}
  4515→			if !rdWait {
  4516→				hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, "NATS/1.0 408 Request Timeout\r\n%s: %d\r\n%s: %d\r\n\r\n", JSPullRequestPendingMsgs, wr.n, JSPullRequestPendingBytes, wr.b)
  4517→				o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(wr.reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4518→			}
  4519→			wr = remove(pre, wr)
  4520→			continue
  4521→		}
  4522→		// Now check interest.
  4523→		interest := wr.acc.sl.HasInterest(wr.interest)
  4524→		if !interest && (s.leafNodeEnabled || s.gateway.enabled) {
  4525→			// If we are here check on gateways and leaf nodes (as they can mask gateways on the other end).
  4526→			// If we have interest or the request is too young break and do not expire.
  4527→			if time.Since(wr.received) < defaultGatewayRecentSubExpiration {
  4528→				interest = true
  4529→			} else if s.gateway.enabled && s.hasGatewayInterest(wr.acc.Name, wr.interest) {
  4530→				interest = true
  4531→			}
  4532→		}
  4533→		// Check if we have interest.
  4534→		if !interest {
  4535→			// No more interest here so go ahead and remove this one from our list.
  4536→			wr = remove(pre, wr)
  4537→			continue
  4538→		}
  4539→
  4540→		// If interest, update batch pending requests counter and update fexp timer.
  4541→		brp += wr.n
  4542→		if !wr.hbt.IsZero() {
  4543→			if now.After(wr.hbt) {
  4544→				// Fire off a heartbeat here.
  4545→				o.sendIdleHeartbeat(wr.reply)
  4546→				// Update next HB.
  4547→				wr.hbt = now.Add(wr.hb)
  4548→			}
  4549→			if fexp.IsZero() || wr.hbt.Before(fexp) {
  4550→				fexp = wr.hbt
  4551→			}
  4552→		}
  4553→		if !wr.expires.IsZero() && (fexp.IsZero() || wr.expires.Before(fexp)) {
  4554→			fexp = wr.expires
  4555→		}
  4556→		// Update pre and wr here.
  4557→		pre = wr
  4558→		wr = wr.next
  4559→	}
  4560→
  4561→	return expired, wq.len(), brp, fexp
  4562→}
  4563→
  4564→// Will check to make sure those waiting still have registered interest.
  4565→func (o *consumer) checkWaitingForInterest() bool {
  4566→	o.processWaiting(true)
  4567→	return o.waiting.len() > 0
  4568→}
  4569→
  4570→// Lock should be held.
  4571→func (o *consumer) hbTimer() (time.Duration, *time.Timer) {
  4572→	if o.cfg.Heartbeat == 0 {
  4573→		return 0, nil
  4574→	}
  4575→	return o.cfg.Heartbeat, time.NewTimer(o.cfg.Heartbeat)
  4576→}
  4577→
  4578→// Check here for conditions when our ack floor may have drifted below the streams first sequence.
  4579→// In general this is accounted for in normal operations, but if the consumer misses the signal from
  4580→// the stream it will not clear the message and move the ack state.
  4581→// Should only be called from consumer leader.
  4582→func (o *consumer) checkAckFloor() {
  4583→	o.mu.RLock()
  4584→	mset, closed, asflr, numPending := o.mset, o.closed, o.asflr, len(o.pending)
  4585→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  4586→
  4587→	if asflr == 0 || closed || mset == nil {
  4588→		return
  4589→	}
  4590→
  4591→	var ss StreamState
  4592→	mset.store.FastState(&ss)
  4593→
  4594→	// If our floor is equal or greater that is normal and nothing for us to do.
  4595→	if ss.FirstSeq == 0 || asflr >= ss.FirstSeq-1 {
  4596→		return
  4597→	}
  4598→
  4599→	// Check which linear space is less to walk.
  4600→	if ss.FirstSeq-asflr-1 < uint64(numPending) {
  4601→		// Process all messages that no longer exist.
  4602→		for seq := asflr + 1; seq < ss.FirstSeq; seq++ {
  4603→			// Check if this message was pending.
  4604→			o.mu.RLock()
  4605→			p, isPending := o.pending[seq]
  4606→			rdc := o.deliveryCount(seq)
  4607→			o.mu.RUnlock()
  4608→			// If it was pending for us, get rid of it.
  4609→			if isPending {
  4610→				o.processTerm(seq, p.Sequence, rdc, ackTermLimitsReason, _EMPTY_)
  4611→			}
  4612→		}
  4613→	} else if numPending > 0 {
  4614→		// here it is shorter to walk pending.
  4615→		// toTerm is seq, dseq, rcd for each entry.
  4616→		toTerm := make([]uint64, 0, numPending*3)
  4617→		o.mu.RLock()
  4618→		for seq, p := range o.pending {
  4619→			if seq < ss.FirstSeq {
  4620→				var dseq uint64 = 1
  4621→				if p != nil {
  4622→					dseq = p.Sequence
  4623→				}
  4624→				rdc := o.deliveryCount(seq)
  4625→				toTerm = append(toTerm, seq, dseq, rdc)
  4626→			}
  4627→		}
  4628→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  4629→
  4630→		for i := 0; i < len(toTerm); i += 3 {
  4631→			seq, dseq, rdc := toTerm[i], toTerm[i+1], toTerm[i+2]
  4632→			o.processTerm(seq, dseq, rdc, ackTermLimitsReason, _EMPTY_)
  4633→		}
  4634→	}
  4635→
  4636→	// Do one final check here.
  4637→	o.mu.Lock()
  4638→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  4639→
  4640→	// If we are closed do not change anything and simply return.
  4641→	if o.closed {
  4642→		return
  4643→	}
  4644→
  4645→	// If we are here, and this should be rare, we still are off with our ack floor.
  4646→	// We will make sure we are not doing un-necessary work here if only off by a bit
  4647→	// since this could be normal for a high activity wq or stream.
  4648→	// We will set it explicitly to 1 behind our current lowest in pending, or if
  4649→	// pending is empty, to our current delivered -1.
  4650→	const minOffThreshold = 50
  4651→	if ss.FirstSeq >= minOffThreshold && o.asflr < ss.FirstSeq-minOffThreshold {
  4652→		var psseq, pdseq uint64
  4653→		for seq, p := range o.pending {
  4654→			if psseq == 0 || seq < psseq {
  4655→				psseq, pdseq = seq, p.Sequence
  4656→			}
  4657→		}
  4658→		// If we still have none, set to current delivered -1.
  4659→		if psseq == 0 {
  4660→			psseq, pdseq = o.sseq-1, o.dseq-1
  4661→			// If still not adjusted.
  4662→			if psseq < ss.FirstSeq-1 {
  4663→				psseq = ss.FirstSeq - 1
  4664→			}
  4665→		} else {
  4666→			// Since this was set via the pending, we should not include
  4667→			// it directly but set floors to -1.
  4668→			psseq, pdseq = psseq-1, pdseq-1
  4669→		}
  4670→		o.asflr, o.adflr = psseq, pdseq
  4671→	}
  4672→}
  4673→
  4674→func (o *consumer) processInboundAcks(qch chan struct{}) {
  4675→	// Grab the server lock to watch for server quit.
  4676→	o.mu.RLock()
  4677→	s, mset := o.srv, o.mset
  4678→	hasInactiveThresh := o.cfg.InactiveThreshold > 0
  4679→
  4680→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  4681→
  4682→	if s == nil || mset == nil {
  4683→		return
  4684→	}
  4685→
  4686→	// We will check this on entry and periodically.
  4687→	o.checkAckFloor()
  4688→
  4689→	// How often we will check for ack floor drift.
  4690→	// Spread these out for large numbers on a server restart.
  4691→	delta := time.Duration(rand.Int63n(int64(time.Minute)))
  4692→	ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Minute + delta)
  4693→	defer ticker.Stop()
  4694→
  4695→	for {
  4696→		select {
  4697→		case <-o.ackMsgs.ch:
  4698→			// If we have an inactiveThreshold set, mark our activity.
  4699→			// Do this before processing acks, otherwise we might race if there are no pending messages
  4700→			// anymore and the inactivity threshold kicks in before we're able to mark activity.
  4701→			if hasInactiveThresh {
  4702→				o.suppressDeletion()
  4703→			}
  4704→
  4705→			acks := o.ackMsgs.pop()
  4706→			for _, ack := range acks {
  4707→				o.processAck(ack.subject, ack.reply, ack.hdr, ack.msg)
  4708→				ack.returnToPool()
  4709→			}
  4710→			o.ackMsgs.recycle(&acks)
  4711→		case <-ticker.C:
  4712→			o.checkAckFloor()
  4713→		case <-qch:
  4714→			return
  4715→		case <-s.quitCh:
  4716→			return
  4717→		}
  4718→	}
  4719→}
  4720→
  4721→// Process inbound next message requests.
  4722→func (o *consumer) processInboundNextMsgReqs(qch chan struct{}) {
  4723→	// Grab the server lock to watch for server quit.
  4724→	o.mu.RLock()
  4725→	s := o.srv
  4726→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  4727→
  4728→	for {
  4729→		select {
  4730→		case <-o.nextMsgReqs.ch:
  4731→			reqs := o.nextMsgReqs.pop()
  4732→			for _, req := range reqs {
  4733→				o.processNextMsgRequest(req.reply, req.msg)
  4734→				req.returnToPool()
  4735→			}
  4736→			o.nextMsgReqs.recycle(&reqs)
  4737→		case <-qch:
  4738→			return
  4739→		case <-s.quitCh:
  4740→			return
  4741→		}
  4742→	}
  4743→}
  4744→
  4745→// Suppress auto cleanup on ack activity of any kind.
  4746→func (o *consumer) suppressDeletion() {
  4747→	o.mu.Lock()
  4748→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  4749→
  4750→	if o.closed {
  4751→		return
  4752→	}
  4753→
  4754→	if o.isPushMode() && o.dtmr != nil {
  4755→		// if dtmr is not nil we have started the countdown, simply reset to threshold.
  4756→		o.dtmr.Reset(o.dthresh)
  4757→	} else if o.isPullMode() && o.waiting != nil {
  4758→		// Pull mode always has timer running, update last on waiting queue.
  4759→		o.waiting.last = time.Now()
  4760→		if o.dtmr != nil {
  4761→			o.dtmr.Reset(o.dthresh)
  4762→		}
  4763→	}
  4764→}
  4765→
  4766→// loopAndGatherMsgs waits for messages for the consumer. qch is the quit channel,
  4767→// upch is the unpause channel which fires when the PauseUntil deadline is reached.
  4768→func (o *consumer) loopAndGatherMsgs(qch chan struct{}) {
  4769→	// On startup check to see if we are in a reply situation where replay policy is not instant.
  4770→	var (
  4771→		lts  int64 // last time stamp seen, used for replay.
  4772→		lseq uint64
  4773→	)
  4774→
  4775→	o.mu.RLock()
  4776→	mset := o.mset
  4777→	getLSeq := o.replay
  4778→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  4779→	// consumer is closed when mset is set to nil.
  4780→	if mset == nil {
  4781→		return
  4782→	}
  4783→	if getLSeq {
  4784→		lseq = mset.state().LastSeq
  4785→	}
  4786→
  4787→	o.mu.Lock()
  4788→	s := o.srv
  4789→	// need to check again if consumer is closed
  4790→	if o.mset == nil {
  4791→		o.mu.Unlock()
  4792→		return
  4793→	}
  4794→	// For idle heartbeat support.
  4795→	var hbc <-chan time.Time
  4796→	hbd, hb := o.hbTimer()
  4797→	if hb != nil {
  4798→		hbc = hb.C
  4799→	}
  4800→	// Interest changes.
  4801→	inch := o.inch
  4802→	o.mu.Unlock()
  4803→
  4804→	// Grab the stream's retention policy and name
  4805→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  4806→	stream, rp := mset.cfg.Name, mset.cfg.Retention
  4807→	mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  4808→
  4809→	var err error
  4810→
  4811→	// Deliver all the msgs we have now, once done or on a condition, we wait for new ones.
  4812→	for {
  4813→		var (
  4814→			pmsg     *jsPubMsg
  4815→			dc       uint64
  4816→			dsubj    string
  4817→			ackReply string
  4818→			delay    time.Duration
  4819→			sz       int
  4820→			wrn, wrb int
  4821→		)
  4822→
  4823→		o.mu.Lock()
  4824→
  4825→		// consumer is closed when mset is set to nil.
  4826→		if o.closed || o.mset == nil {
  4827→			o.mu.Unlock()
  4828→			return
  4829→		}
  4830→
  4831→		// Clear last error.
  4832→		err = nil
  4833→
  4834→		// If the consumer is paused then stop sending.
  4835→		if o.cfg.PauseUntil != nil && !o.cfg.PauseUntil.IsZero() && time.Now().Before(*o.cfg.PauseUntil) {
  4836→			// If the consumer is paused and we haven't reached the deadline yet then
  4837→			// go back to waiting.
  4838→			goto waitForMsgs
  4839→		}
  4840→
  4841→		// If we are in push mode and not active or under flowcontrol let's stop sending.
  4842→		if o.isPushMode() {
  4843→			if !o.active || (o.maxpb > 0 && o.pbytes > o.maxpb) {
  4844→				goto waitForMsgs
  4845→			}
  4846→		} else if o.waiting.isEmpty() {
  4847→			// If we are in pull mode and no one is waiting already break and wait.
  4848→			goto waitForMsgs
  4849→		}
  4850→
  4851→		// Grab our next msg.
  4852→		pmsg, dc, err = o.getNextMsg()
  4853→
  4854→		// We can release the lock now under getNextMsg so need to check this condition again here.
  4855→		if o.closed || o.mset == nil {
  4856→			o.mu.Unlock()
  4857→			return
  4858→		}
  4859→
  4860→		// On error either wait or return.
  4861→		if err != nil || pmsg == nil {
  4862→			// On EOF we can optionally fast sync num pending state.
  4863→			if err == ErrStoreEOF {
  4864→				o.checkNumPendingOnEOF()
  4865→			}
  4866→			if err == ErrStoreMsgNotFound || err == errDeletedMsg || err == ErrStoreEOF || err == errMaxAckPending {
  4867→				goto waitForMsgs
  4868→			} else if err == errPartialCache {
  4869→				s.Warnf("Unexpected partial cache error looking up message for consumer '%s > %s > %s'",
  4870→					o.mset.acc, stream, o.cfg.Name)
  4871→				goto waitForMsgs
  4872→
  4873→			} else {
  4874→				s.Errorf("Received an error looking up message for consumer '%s > %s > %s': %v",
  4875→					o.mset.acc, stream, o.cfg.Name, err)
  4876→				goto waitForMsgs
  4877→			}
  4878→		}
  4879→
  4880→		// If a minimum last sequence was specified, we need to check if the
  4881→		// underlying stream has sufficient data. As an optimization, we only
  4882→		// do this if what we want to deliver is below this floor.
  4883→		if o.cfg.MinLastSeq > 0 && pmsg.seq < o.cfg.MinLastSeq {
  4884→			var state StreamState
  4885→			o.mset.store.FastState(&state)
  4886→			if state.LastSeq < o.cfg.MinLastSeq {
  4887→				// We only block deliveries at the start until we reach min last seq,
  4888→				// so simply put back our pointer to account for the o.getNextMsg advancing it.
  4889→				o.sseq--
  4890→				goto waitForMsgs
  4891→			}
  4892→		}
  4893→
  4894→		// Update our cached num pending here first.
  4895→		if dc == 1 {
  4896→			o.npc--
  4897→		}
  4898→		// Pre-calculate ackReply
  4899→		ackReply = o.ackReply(pmsg.seq, o.dseq, dc, pmsg.ts, o.numPending())
  4900→
  4901→		// If headers only do not send msg payload.
  4902→		// Add in msg size itself as header.
  4903→		if o.cfg.HeadersOnly {
  4904→			convertToHeadersOnly(pmsg)
  4905→		}
  4906→		// Calculate payload size. This can be calculated on client side.
  4907→		// We do not include transport subject here since not generally known on client.
  4908→		sz = len(pmsg.subj) + len(ackReply) + len(pmsg.hdr) + len(pmsg.msg)
  4909→
  4910→		if o.isPushMode() {
  4911→			dsubj = o.dsubj
  4912→		} else if wr := o.nextWaiting(sz); wr != nil {
  4913→			wrn, wrb = wr.n, wr.b
  4914→			dsubj = wr.reply
  4915→			if o.cfg.PriorityPolicy == PriorityPinnedClient {
  4916→				// FIXME(jrm): Can we make this prettier?
  4917→				if len(pmsg.hdr) == 0 {
  4918→					pmsg.hdr = genHeader(pmsg.hdr, JSPullRequestNatsPinId, o.currentPinId)
  4919→					pmsg.buf = append(pmsg.hdr, pmsg.msg...)
  4920→				} else {
  4921→					pmsg.hdr = genHeader(pmsg.hdr, JSPullRequestNatsPinId, o.currentPinId)
  4922→					bufLen := len(pmsg.hdr) + len(pmsg.msg)
  4923→					pmsg.buf = make([]byte, bufLen)
  4924→					pmsg.buf = append(pmsg.hdr, pmsg.msg...)
  4925→				}
  4926→
  4927→				sz = len(pmsg.subj) + len(ackReply) + len(pmsg.hdr) + len(pmsg.msg)
  4928→
  4929→			}
  4930→			if done := wr.recycleIfDone(); done && o.node != nil {
  4931→				o.removeClusterPendingRequest(dsubj)
  4932→			} else if !done && wr.hb > 0 {
  4933→				wr.hbt = time.Now().Add(wr.hb)
  4934→			}
  4935→		} else {
  4936→			// We will redo this one as long as this is not a redelivery.
  4937→			// Need to also test that this is not going backwards since if
  4938→			// we fail to deliver we can end up here from rdq but we do not
  4939→			// want to decrement o.sseq if that is the case.
  4940→			if dc == 1 && pmsg.seq == o.sseq-1 {
  4941→				o.sseq--
  4942→				o.npc++
  4943→			} else if !o.onRedeliverQueue(pmsg.seq) {
  4944→				// We are not on the rdq so decrement the delivery count
  4945→				// and add it back.
  4946→				o.decDeliveryCount(pmsg.seq)
  4947→				o.addToRedeliverQueue(pmsg.seq)
  4948→			}
  4949→			pmsg.returnToPool()
  4950→			goto waitForMsgs
  4951→		}
  4952→
  4953→		// If we are in a replay scenario and have not caught up check if we need to delay here.
  4954→		if o.replay && lts > 0 {
  4955→			if delay = time.Duration(pmsg.ts - lts); delay > time.Millisecond {
  4956→				o.mu.Unlock()
  4957→				select {
  4958→				case <-qch:
  4959→					pmsg.returnToPool()
  4960→					return
  4961→				case <-time.After(delay):
  4962→				}
  4963→				o.mu.Lock()
  4964→			}
  4965→		}
  4966→
  4967→		// Track this regardless.
  4968→		lts = pmsg.ts
  4969→
  4970→		// If we have a rate limit set make sure we check that here.
  4971→		if o.rlimit != nil {
  4972→			now := time.Now()
  4973→			r := o.rlimit.ReserveN(now, sz)
  4974→			delay := r.DelayFrom(now)
  4975→			if delay > 0 {
  4976→				o.mu.Unlock()
  4977→				select {
  4978→				case <-qch:
  4979→					pmsg.returnToPool()
  4980→					return
  4981→				case <-time.After(delay):
  4982→				}
  4983→				o.mu.Lock()
  4984→			}
  4985→		}
  4986→
  4987→		// Do actual delivery.
  4988→		o.deliverMsg(dsubj, ackReply, pmsg, dc, rp)
  4989→
  4990→		// If given request fulfilled batch size, but there are still pending bytes, send information about it.
  4991→		if wrn <= 0 && wrb > 0 {
  4992→			msg := fmt.Appendf(nil, JsPullRequestRemainingBytesT, JSPullRequestPendingMsgs, wrn, JSPullRequestPendingBytes, wrb)
  4993→			o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(dsubj, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, msg, nil, nil, 0))
  4994→		}
  4995→		// Reset our idle heartbeat timer if set.
  4996→		if hb != nil {
  4997→			hb.Reset(hbd)
  4998→		}
  4999→
  5000→		o.mu.Unlock()
  5001→		continue
  5002→
  5003→	waitForMsgs:
  5004→		// If we were in a replay state check to see if we are caught up. If so clear.
  5005→		if o.replay && o.sseq > lseq {
  5006→			o.replay = false
  5007→		}
  5008→
  5009→		// Make sure to process any expired requests that are pending.
  5010→		var wrExp <-chan time.Time
  5011→		if o.isPullMode() {
  5012→			// Dont expire oneshots if we are here because of max ack pending limit.
  5013→			_, _, _, fexp := o.processWaiting(err != errMaxAckPending)
  5014→			if !fexp.IsZero() {
  5015→				expires := time.Until(fexp)
  5016→				if expires <= 0 {
  5017→					expires = time.Millisecond
  5018→				}
  5019→				wrExp = time.NewTimer(expires).C
  5020→			}
  5021→		}
  5022→
  5023→		// We will wait here for new messages to arrive.
  5024→		mch, odsubj := o.mch, o.cfg.DeliverSubject
  5025→		o.mu.Unlock()
  5026→
  5027→		select {
  5028→		case <-mch:
  5029→			// Messages are waiting.
  5030→		case interest := <-inch:
  5031→			// inch can be nil on pull-based, but then this will
  5032→			// just block and not fire.
  5033→			o.updateDeliveryInterest(interest)
  5034→		case <-qch:
  5035→			return
  5036→		case <-wrExp:
  5037→			o.mu.Lock()
  5038→			o.processWaiting(true)
  5039→			o.mu.Unlock()
  5040→		case <-hbc:
  5041→			if o.isActive() {
  5042→				o.mu.RLock()
  5043→				o.sendIdleHeartbeat(odsubj)
  5044→				o.mu.RUnlock()
  5045→			}
  5046→			// Reset our idle heartbeat timer.
  5047→			hb.Reset(hbd)
  5048→		}
  5049→	}
  5050→}
  5051→
  5052→// Lock should be held.
  5053→func (o *consumer) sendIdleHeartbeat(subj string) {
  5054→	const t = "NATS/1.0 100 Idle Heartbeat\r\n%s: %d\r\n%s: %d\r\n\r\n"
  5055→	sseq, dseq := o.sseq-1, o.dseq-1
  5056→	hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, t, JSLastConsumerSeq, dseq, JSLastStreamSeq, sseq)
  5057→	if fcp := o.fcid; fcp != _EMPTY_ {
  5058→		// Add in that we are stalled on flow control here.
  5059→		addOn := fmt.Appendf(nil, "%s: %s\r\n\r\n", JSConsumerStalled, fcp)
  5060→		hdr = append(hdr[:len(hdr)-LEN_CR_LF], []byte(addOn)...)
  5061→	}
  5062→	o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(subj, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5063→}
  5064→
  5065→func (o *consumer) ackReply(sseq, dseq, dc uint64, ts int64, pending uint64) string {
  5066→	return fmt.Sprintf(o.ackReplyT, dc, sseq, dseq, ts, pending)
  5067→}
  5068→
  5069→// Used mostly for testing. Sets max pending bytes for flow control setups.
  5070→func (o *consumer) setMaxPendingBytes(limit int) {
  5071→	o.pblimit = limit
  5072→	o.maxpb = limit / 16
  5073→	if o.maxpb == 0 {
  5074→		o.maxpb = 1
  5075→	}
  5076→}
  5077→
  5078→// Does some sanity checks to see if we should re-calculate.
  5079→// Since there is a race when decrementing when there is contention at the beginning of the stream.
  5080→// The race is a getNextMsg skips a deleted msg, and then the decStreamPending call fires.
  5081→// This does some quick sanity checks to see if we should re-calculate num pending.
  5082→// Lock should be held.
  5083→func (o *consumer) checkNumPending() uint64 {
  5084→	if o.mset != nil && o.mset.store != nil {
  5085→		var state StreamState
  5086→		o.mset.store.FastState(&state)
  5087→		npc := o.numPending()
  5088→		if o.sseq > state.LastSeq && npc > 0 || npc > state.Msgs {
  5089→			// Re-calculate.
  5090→			o.streamNumPending()
  5091→		}
  5092→	}
  5093→	return o.numPending()
  5094→}
  5095→
  5096→// Lock should be held.
  5097→func (o *consumer) numPending() uint64 {
  5098→	if o.npc < 0 {
  5099→		return 0
  5100→	}
  5101→	return uint64(o.npc)
  5102→}
  5103→
  5104→// This will do a quick sanity check on num pending when we encounter
  5105→// and EOF in the loop and gather.
  5106→// Lock should be held.
  5107→func (o *consumer) checkNumPendingOnEOF() {
  5108→	if o.mset == nil {
  5109→		return
  5110→	}
  5111→	var state StreamState
  5112→	o.mset.store.FastState(&state)
  5113→	if o.sseq > state.LastSeq && o.npc != 0 {
  5114→		// We know here we can reset our running state for num pending.
  5115→		o.npc, o.npf = 0, state.LastSeq
  5116→	}
  5117→}
  5118→
  5119→// Call into streamNumPending after acquiring the consumer lock.
  5120→func (o *consumer) streamNumPendingLocked() uint64 {
  5121→	o.mu.Lock()
  5122→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  5123→	return o.streamNumPending()
  5124→}
  5125→
  5126→// Will force a set from the stream store of num pending.
  5127→// Depends on delivery policy, for last per subject we calculate differently.
  5128→// Lock should be held.
  5129→func (o *consumer) streamNumPending() uint64 {
  5130→	if o.mset == nil || o.mset.store == nil {
  5131→		o.npc, o.npf = 0, 0
  5132→		return 0
  5133→	}
  5134→	npc, npf := o.calculateNumPending()
  5135→	o.npc, o.npf = int64(npc), npf
  5136→	return o.numPending()
  5137→}
  5138→
  5139→// Will calculate num pending but only requires a read lock.
  5140→// Depends on delivery policy, for last per subject we calculate differently.
  5141→// At least RLock should be held.
  5142→func (o *consumer) calculateNumPending() (npc, npf uint64) {
  5143→	if o.mset == nil || o.mset.store == nil {
  5144→		return 0, 0
  5145→	}
  5146→
  5147→	isLastPerSubject := o.cfg.DeliverPolicy == DeliverLastPerSubject
  5148→	filters, subjf := o.filters, o.subjf
  5149→
  5150→	if filters != nil {
  5151→		return o.mset.store.NumPendingMulti(o.sseq, filters, isLastPerSubject)
  5152→	} else if len(subjf) > 0 {
  5153→		filter := subjf[0].subject
  5154→		return o.mset.store.NumPending(o.sseq, filter, isLastPerSubject)
  5155→	}
  5156→	return o.mset.store.NumPending(o.sseq, _EMPTY_, isLastPerSubject)
  5157→}
  5158→
  5159→func convertToHeadersOnly(pmsg *jsPubMsg) {
  5160→	// If headers only do not send msg payload.
  5161→	// Add in msg size itself as header.
  5162→	hdr, msg := pmsg.hdr, pmsg.msg
  5163→	var bb bytes.Buffer
  5164→	if len(hdr) == 0 {
  5165→		bb.WriteString(hdrLine)
  5166→	} else {
  5167→		bb.Write(hdr)
  5168→		bb.Truncate(len(hdr) - LEN_CR_LF)
  5169→	}
  5170→	bb.WriteString(JSMsgSize)
  5171→	bb.WriteString(": ")
  5172→	bb.WriteString(strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(msg)), 10))
  5173→	bb.WriteString(CR_LF)
  5174→	bb.WriteString(CR_LF)
  5175→	// Replace underlying buf which we can use directly when we send.
  5176→	// TODO(dlc) - Probably just use directly when forming bytes.Buffer?
  5177→	pmsg.buf = pmsg.buf[:0]
  5178→	pmsg.buf = append(pmsg.buf, bb.Bytes()...)
  5179→	// Replace with new header.
  5180→	pmsg.hdr = pmsg.buf
  5181→	// Cancel msg payload
  5182→	pmsg.msg = nil
  5183→}
  5184→
  5185→// Deliver a msg to the consumer.
  5186→// Lock should be held and o.mset validated to be non-nil.
  5187→func (o *consumer) deliverMsg(dsubj, ackReply string, pmsg *jsPubMsg, dc uint64, rp RetentionPolicy) {
  5188→	if o.mset == nil {
  5189→		pmsg.returnToPool()
  5190→		return
  5191→	}
  5192→
  5193→	dseq := o.dseq
  5194→	o.dseq++
  5195→
  5196→	pmsg.dsubj, pmsg.reply, pmsg.o = dsubj, ackReply, o
  5197→	psz := pmsg.size()
  5198→
  5199→	if o.maxpb > 0 {
  5200→		o.pbytes += psz
  5201→	}
  5202→
  5203→	mset := o.mset
  5204→	ap := o.cfg.AckPolicy
  5205→
  5206→	// Cant touch pmsg after this sending so capture what we need.
  5207→	seq, ts := pmsg.seq, pmsg.ts
  5208→
