  5209→	// Update delivered first.
  5210→	o.updateDelivered(dseq, seq, dc, ts)
  5211→
  5212→	if ap == AckExplicit || ap == AckAll {
  5213→		o.trackPending(seq, dseq)
  5214→	} else if ap == AckNone {
  5215→		o.adflr = dseq
  5216→		o.asflr = seq
  5217→	}
  5218→
  5219→	// Send message.
  5220→	if o.replicateDeliveries() {
  5221→		o.addReplicatedQueuedMsg(pmsg)
  5222→	} else {
  5223→		o.outq.send(pmsg)
  5224→	}
  5225→
  5226→	// Flow control.
  5227→	if o.maxpb > 0 && o.needFlowControl(psz) {
  5228→		o.sendFlowControl()
  5229→	}
  5230→
  5231→	// If pull mode and we have inactivity threshold, signaled by dthresh, update last activity.
  5232→	if o.isPullMode() && o.dthresh > 0 {
  5233→		o.waiting.last = time.Now()
  5234→	}
  5235→
  5236→	// If we are ack none and mset is interest only we should make sure stream removes interest.
  5237→	if ap == AckNone && rp != LimitsPolicy {
  5238→		if mset != nil && mset.ackq != nil && (o.node == nil || o.cfg.Direct) {
  5239→			mset.ackq.push(seq)
  5240→		} else {
  5241→			o.updateAcks(dseq, seq, _EMPTY_)
  5242→		}
  5243→	}
  5244→}
  5245→
  5246→// replicateDeliveries returns whether deliveries should be replicated before sending them.
  5247→// If we're replicated we MUST only send the message AFTER we've got quorum for updating
  5248→// delivered state. Otherwise, we could be in an invalid state after a leader change.
  5249→// We can send immediately if not replicated, not using acks, or using flow control (incompatible).
  5250→// Lock should be held.
  5251→func (o *consumer) replicateDeliveries() bool {
  5252→	return o.node != nil && o.cfg.AckPolicy != AckNone && !o.cfg.FlowControl
  5253→}
  5254→
  5255→func (o *consumer) needFlowControl(sz int) bool {
  5256→	if o.maxpb == 0 {
  5257→		return false
  5258→	}
  5259→	// Decide whether to send a flow control message which we will need the user to respond.
  5260→	// We send when we are over 50% of our current window limit.
  5261→	if o.fcid == _EMPTY_ && o.pbytes > o.maxpb/2 {
  5262→		return true
  5263→	}
  5264→	// If we have an existing outstanding FC, check to see if we need to expand the o.fcsz
  5265→	if o.fcid != _EMPTY_ && (o.pbytes-o.fcsz) >= o.maxpb {
  5266→		o.fcsz += sz
  5267→	}
  5268→	return false
  5269→}
  5270→
  5271→func (o *consumer) processFlowControl(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subj, _ string, _ []byte) {
  5272→	o.mu.Lock()
  5273→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  5274→
  5275→	// Ignore if not the latest we have sent out.
  5276→	if subj != o.fcid {
  5277→		return
  5278→	}
  5279→
  5280→	// For slow starts and ramping up.
  5281→	if o.maxpb < o.pblimit {
  5282→		o.maxpb *= 2
  5283→		if o.maxpb > o.pblimit {
  5284→			o.maxpb = o.pblimit
  5285→		}
  5286→	}
  5287→
  5288→	// Update accounting.
  5289→	o.pbytes -= o.fcsz
  5290→	if o.pbytes < 0 {
  5291→		o.pbytes = 0
  5292→	}
  5293→	o.fcid, o.fcsz = _EMPTY_, 0
  5294→
  5295→	o.signalNewMessages()
  5296→}
  5297→
  5298→// Lock should be held.
  5299→func (o *consumer) fcReply() string {
  5300→	var sb strings.Builder
  5301→	sb.WriteString(jsFlowControlPre)
  5302→	sb.WriteString(o.stream)
  5303→	sb.WriteByte(btsep)
  5304→	sb.WriteString(o.name)
  5305→	sb.WriteByte(btsep)
  5306→	var b [4]byte
  5307→	rn := rand.Int63()
  5308→	for i, l := 0, rn; i < len(b); i++ {
  5309→		b[i] = digits[l%base]
  5310→		l /= base
  5311→	}
  5312→	sb.Write(b[:])
  5313→	return sb.String()
  5314→}
  5315→
  5316→// sendFlowControl will send a flow control packet to the consumer.
  5317→// Lock should be held.
  5318→func (o *consumer) sendFlowControl() {
  5319→	if !o.isPushMode() {
  5320→		return
  5321→	}
  5322→	subj, rply := o.cfg.DeliverSubject, o.fcReply()
  5323→	o.fcsz, o.fcid = o.pbytes, rply
  5324→	hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 100 FlowControl Request\r\n\r\n")
  5325→	o.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(subj, _EMPTY_, rply, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5326→}
  5327→
  5328→// Tracks our outstanding pending acks. Only applicable to AckExplicit mode.
  5329→// Lock should be held.
  5330→func (o *consumer) trackPending(sseq, dseq uint64) {
  5331→	if o.pending == nil {
  5332→		o.pending = make(map[uint64]*Pending)
  5333→	}
  5334→
  5335→	// We could have a backoff that set a timer higher than what we need for this message.
  5336→	// In that case, reset to lowest backoff required for a message redelivery.
  5337→	minDelay := o.ackWait(0)
  5338→	if l := len(o.cfg.BackOff); l > 0 {
  5339→		bi := int(o.rdc[sseq])
  5340→		if bi < 0 {
  5341→			bi = 0
  5342→		} else if bi >= l {
  5343→			bi = l - 1
  5344→		}
  5345→		minDelay = o.ackWait(o.cfg.BackOff[bi])
  5346→	}
  5347→	minDeadline := time.Now().Add(minDelay)
  5348→	if o.ptmr == nil || o.ptmrEnd.After(minDeadline) {
  5349→		o.resetPtmr(minDelay)
  5350→	}
  5351→
  5352→	if p, ok := o.pending[sseq]; ok {
  5353→		// Update timestamp but keep original consumer delivery sequence.
  5354→		// So do not update p.Sequence.
  5355→		p.Timestamp = time.Now().UnixNano()
  5356→	} else {
  5357→		o.pending[sseq] = &Pending{dseq, time.Now().UnixNano()}
  5358→	}
  5359→}
  5360→
  5361→// Credit back a failed delivery.
  5362→// lock should be held.
  5363→func (o *consumer) creditWaitingRequest(reply string) {
  5364→	wq := o.waiting
  5365→	for wr := wq.head; wr != nil; wr = wr.next {
  5366→		if wr.reply == reply {
  5367→			wr.n++
  5368→			wr.d--
  5369→			return
  5370→		}
  5371→	}
  5372→}
  5373→
  5374→// didNotDeliver is called when a delivery for a consumer message failed.
  5375→// Depending on our state, we will process the failure.
  5376→func (o *consumer) didNotDeliver(seq uint64, subj string) {
  5377→	o.mu.Lock()
  5378→	mset := o.mset
  5379→	if mset == nil {
  5380→		o.mu.Unlock()
  5381→		return
  5382→	}
  5383→	// Adjust back deliver count.
  5384→	o.decDeliveryCount(seq)
  5385→
  5386→	var checkDeliveryInterest bool
  5387→	if o.isPushMode() {
  5388→		o.active = false
  5389→		checkDeliveryInterest = true
  5390→	} else if o.pending != nil {
  5391→		// Good chance we did not deliver because no interest so force a check.
  5392→		o.processWaiting(false)
  5393→		// If it is still there credit it back.
  5394→		o.creditWaitingRequest(subj)
  5395→		// pull mode and we have pending.
  5396→		if _, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok {
  5397→			// We found this messsage on pending, we need
  5398→			// to queue it up for immediate redelivery since
  5399→			// we know it was not delivered
  5400→			if !o.onRedeliverQueue(seq) {
  5401→				o.addToRedeliverQueue(seq)
  5402→				if !o.waiting.isEmpty() {
  5403→					o.signalNewMessages()
  5404→				}
  5405→			}
  5406→		}
  5407→	}
  5408→	o.mu.Unlock()
  5409→
  5410→	if checkDeliveryInterest {
  5411→		localInterest := !o.hasNoLocalInterest()
  5412→		o.updateDeliveryInterest(localInterest)
  5413→	}
  5414→}
  5415→
  5416→// Lock should be held.
  5417→func (o *consumer) addToRedeliverQueue(seqs ...uint64) {
  5418→	o.rdq = append(o.rdq, seqs...)
  5419→	for _, seq := range seqs {
  5420→		o.rdqi.Insert(seq)
  5421→	}
  5422→}
  5423→
  5424→// Lock should be held.
  5425→func (o *consumer) hasRedeliveries() bool {
  5426→	return len(o.rdq) > 0
  5427→}
  5428→
  5429→func (o *consumer) getNextToRedeliver() uint64 {
  5430→	if len(o.rdq) == 0 {
  5431→		return 0
  5432→	}
  5433→	seq := o.rdq[0]
  5434→	if len(o.rdq) == 1 {
  5435→		o.rdq = nil
  5436→		o.rdqi.Empty()
  5437→	} else {
  5438→		o.rdq = append(o.rdq[:0], o.rdq[1:]...)
  5439→		o.rdqi.Delete(seq)
  5440→	}
  5441→	return seq
  5442→}
  5443→
  5444→// This checks if we already have this sequence queued for redelivery.
  5445→// FIXME(dlc) - This is O(n) but should be fast with small redeliver size.
  5446→// Lock should be held.
  5447→func (o *consumer) onRedeliverQueue(seq uint64) bool {
  5448→	return o.rdqi.Exists(seq)
  5449→}
  5450→
  5451→// Remove a sequence from the redelivery queue.
  5452→// Lock should be held.
  5453→func (o *consumer) removeFromRedeliverQueue(seq uint64) bool {
  5454→	if !o.onRedeliverQueue(seq) {
  5455→		return false
  5456→	}
  5457→	for i, rseq := range o.rdq {
  5458→		if rseq == seq {
  5459→			if len(o.rdq) == 1 {
  5460→				o.rdq = nil
  5461→				o.rdqi.Empty()
  5462→			} else {
  5463→				o.rdq = append(o.rdq[:i], o.rdq[i+1:]...)
  5464→				o.rdqi.Delete(seq)
  5465→			}
  5466→			return true
  5467→		}
  5468→	}
  5469→	return false
  5470→}
  5471→
  5472→// Checks the pending messages.
  5473→func (o *consumer) checkPending() {
  5474→	o.mu.Lock()
  5475→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  5476→
  5477→	mset := o.mset
  5478→	// On stop, mset and timer will be nil.
  5479→	if o.closed || mset == nil || o.ptmr == nil {
  5480→		o.stopAndClearPtmr()
  5481→		return
  5482→	}
  5483→
  5484→	var shouldUpdateState bool
  5485→	var state StreamState
  5486→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
  5487→	fseq := state.FirstSeq
  5488→
  5489→	now := time.Now().UnixNano()
  5490→	ttl := int64(o.cfg.AckWait)
  5491→	next := int64(o.ackWait(0))
  5492→	// However, if there is backoff, initializes with the largest backoff.
  5493→	// It will be adjusted as needed.
  5494→	if l := len(o.cfg.BackOff); l > 0 {
  5495→		next = int64(o.cfg.BackOff[l-1])
  5496→	}
  5497→
  5498→	// Since we can update timestamps, we have to review all pending.
  5499→	// We will now bail if we see an ack pending inbound to us via o.awl.
  5500→	var expired []uint64
  5501→	check := len(o.pending) > 1024
  5502→	for seq, p := range o.pending {
  5503→		if check && atomic.LoadInt64(&o.awl) > 0 {
  5504→			o.resetPtmr(100 * time.Millisecond)
  5505→			return
  5506→		}
  5507→		// Check if these are no longer valid.
  5508→		if seq < fseq || seq <= o.asflr {
  5509→			delete(o.pending, seq)
  5510→			delete(o.rdc, seq)
  5511→			o.removeFromRedeliverQueue(seq)
  5512→			shouldUpdateState = true
  5513→			// Check if we need to move ack floors.
  5514→			if seq > o.asflr {
  5515→				o.asflr = seq
  5516→			}
  5517→			if p.Sequence > o.adflr {
  5518→				o.adflr = p.Sequence
  5519→			}
  5520→			continue
  5521→		}
  5522→		elapsed, deadline := now-p.Timestamp, ttl
  5523→		if len(o.cfg.BackOff) > 0 {
  5524→			// This is ok even if o.rdc is nil, we would get dc == 0, which is what we want.
  5525→			dc := int(o.rdc[seq])
  5526→			if dc < 0 {
  5527→				// Prevent consumer backoff from going backwards.
  5528→				dc = 0
  5529→			}
  5530→			// This will be the index for the next backoff, will set to last element if needed.
  5531→			nbi := dc + 1
  5532→			if dc+1 >= len(o.cfg.BackOff) {
  5533→				dc = len(o.cfg.BackOff) - 1
  5534→				nbi = dc
  5535→			}
  5536→			deadline = int64(o.cfg.BackOff[dc])
  5537→			// Set `next` to the next backoff (if smaller than current `next` value).
  5538→			if nextBackoff := int64(o.cfg.BackOff[nbi]); nextBackoff < next {
  5539→				next = nextBackoff
  5540→			}
  5541→		}
  5542→		if elapsed >= deadline {
  5543→			// We will check if we have hit our max deliveries. Previously we would do this on getNextMsg() which
  5544→			// worked well for push consumers, but with pull based consumers would require a new pull request to be
  5545→			// present to process and redelivered could be reported incorrectly.
  5546→			if !o.onRedeliverQueue(seq) && !o.hasMaxDeliveries(seq) {
  5547→				expired = append(expired, seq)
  5548→			}
  5549→		} else if deadline-elapsed < next {
  5550→			// Update when we should fire next.
  5551→			next = deadline - elapsed
  5552→		}
  5553→	}
  5554→
  5555→	if len(expired) > 0 {
  5556→		// We need to sort.
  5557→		slices.Sort(expired)
  5558→		o.addToRedeliverQueue(expired...)
  5559→		// Now we should update the timestamp here since we are redelivering.
  5560→		// We will use an incrementing time to preserve order for any other redelivery.
  5561→		off := now - o.pending[expired[0]].Timestamp
  5562→		for _, seq := range expired {
  5563→			if p, ok := o.pending[seq]; ok {
  5564→				p.Timestamp += off
  5565→			}
  5566→		}
  5567→		o.signalNewMessages()
  5568→	}
  5569→
  5570→	if len(o.pending) > 0 {
  5571→		o.resetPtmr(time.Duration(next))
  5572→	} else {
  5573→		// Make sure to stop timer and clear out any re delivery queues
  5574→		o.stopAndClearPtmr()
  5575→		o.rdq = nil
  5576→		o.rdqi.Empty()
  5577→		o.pending = nil
  5578→		// Mimic behavior in processAckMsg when pending is empty.
  5579→		o.adflr, o.asflr = o.dseq-1, o.sseq-1
  5580→	}
  5581→
  5582→	// Update our state if needed.
  5583→	if shouldUpdateState {
  5584→		if err := o.writeStoreStateUnlocked(); err != nil && o.srv != nil && o.mset != nil && !o.closed {
  5585→			s, acc, mset, name := o.srv, o.acc, o.mset, o.name
  5586→			s.Warnf("Consumer '%s > %s > %s' error on write store state from check pending: %v", acc, mset.getCfgName(), name, err)
  5587→		}
  5588→	}
  5589→}

  5591→// SeqFromReply will extract a sequence number from a reply subject.
  5592→func (o *consumer) seqFromReply(reply string) uint64 {
  5593→	_, dseq, _ := ackReplyInfo(reply)
  5594→	return dseq
  5595→}
  5596→
  5597→// StreamSeqFromReply will extract the stream sequence from the reply subject.
  5598→func (o *consumer) streamSeqFromReply(reply string) uint64 {
  5599→	sseq, _, _ := ackReplyInfo(reply)
  5600→	return sseq
  5601→}
  5602→
  5603→// Quick parser for positive numbers in ack reply encoding.
  5604→func parseAckReplyNum(d string) (n int64) {
  5605→	if len(d) == 0 {
  5606→		return -1
  5607→	}
  5608→	for _, dec := range d {
  5609→		if dec < asciiZero || dec > asciiNine {
  5610→			return -1
  5611→		}
  5612→		n = n*10 + (int64(dec) - asciiZero)
  5613→	}
  5614→	return n
  5615→}
  5616→
  5617→const expectedNumReplyTokens = 9
  5618→
  5619→// Grab encoded information in the reply subject for a delivered message.
  5620→func replyInfo(subject string) (sseq, dseq, dc uint64, ts int64, pending uint64) {
  5621→	tsa := [expectedNumReplyTokens]string{}
  5622→	start, tokens := 0, tsa[:0]
  5623→	for i := 0; i < len(subject); i++ {
  5624→		if subject[i] == btsep {
  5625→			tokens = append(tokens, subject[start:i])
  5626→			start = i + 1
  5627→		}
  5628→	}
  5629→	tokens = append(tokens, subject[start:])
  5630→	if len(tokens) != expectedNumReplyTokens || tokens[0] != "$JS" || tokens[1] != "ACK" {
  5631→		return 0, 0, 0, 0, 0
  5632→	}
  5633→	// TODO(dlc) - Should we error if we do not match consumer name?
  5634→	// stream is tokens[2], consumer is 3.
  5635→	dc = uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[4]))
  5636→	sseq, dseq = uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[5])), uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[6]))
  5637→	ts = parseAckReplyNum(tokens[7])
  5638→	pending = uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[8]))
  5639→
  5640→	return sseq, dseq, dc, ts, pending
  5641→}
  5642→
  5643→func ackReplyInfo(subject string) (sseq, dseq, dc uint64) {
  5644→	tsa := [expectedNumReplyTokens]string{}
  5645→	start, tokens := 0, tsa[:0]
  5646→	for i := 0; i < len(subject); i++ {
  5647→		if subject[i] == btsep {
  5648→			tokens = append(tokens, subject[start:i])
  5649→			start = i + 1
  5650→		}
  5651→	}
  5652→	tokens = append(tokens, subject[start:])
  5653→	if len(tokens) != expectedNumReplyTokens || tokens[0] != "$JS" || tokens[1] != "ACK" {
  5654→		return 0, 0, 0
  5655→	}
  5656→	dc = uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[4]))
  5657→	sseq, dseq = uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[5])), uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[6]))
  5658→
  5659→	return sseq, dseq, dc
  5660→}
  5661→
  5662→// NextSeq returns the next delivered sequence number for this consumer.
  5663→func (o *consumer) nextSeq() uint64 {
  5664→	o.mu.RLock()
  5665→	dseq := o.dseq
  5666→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  5667→	return dseq

  5669→
  5670→// Used to hold skip list when deliver policy is last per subject.
  5671→type lastSeqSkipList struct {
  5672→	resume uint64
  5673→	seqs   []uint64
  5674→}
  5675→
  5676→// Let's us know we have a skip list, which is for deliver last per subject and we are just starting.
  5677→// Lock should be held.
  5678→func (o *consumer) hasSkipListPending() bool {
  5679→	return o.lss != nil && len(o.lss.seqs) > 0
  5680→}
  5681→
  5682→// Will select the starting sequence.
  5683→func (o *consumer) selectStartingSeqNo() {
  5684→	if o.mset == nil || o.mset.store == nil {
  5685→		o.sseq = 1
  5686→	} else {
  5687→		var state StreamState
  5688→		o.mset.store.FastState(&state)
  5689→		if o.cfg.OptStartSeq == 0 {
  5690→			if o.cfg.DeliverPolicy == DeliverAll {
  5691→				o.sseq = state.FirstSeq
  5692→			} else if o.cfg.DeliverPolicy == DeliverLast {
  5693→				if o.subjf == nil {
  5694→					o.sseq = state.LastSeq
  5695→				} else {
  5696→					// If we are partitioned here this will be properly set when we become leader.
  5697→					for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  5698→						ss := o.mset.store.FilteredState(1, filter.subject)
  5699→						if ss.Last > o.sseq {
  5700→							o.sseq = ss.Last
  5701→						}
  5702→					}
  5703→				}
  5704→			} else if o.cfg.DeliverPolicy == DeliverLastPerSubject {
  5705→				// If our parent stream is set to max msgs per subject of 1 this is just
  5706→				// a normal consumer at this point. We can avoid any heavy lifting.
  5707→				o.mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  5708→				mmp := o.mset.cfg.MaxMsgsPer
  5709→				o.mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  5710→				if mmp == 1 {
  5711→					o.sseq = state.FirstSeq
  5712→				} else {
  5713→					filters := make([]string, 0, len(o.subjf))
  5714→					if o.subjf == nil {
  5715→						filters = append(filters, o.cfg.FilterSubject)
  5716→					} else {
  5717→						for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  5718→							filters = append(filters, filter.subject)
  5719→						}
  5720→					}
  5721→
  5722→					lss := &lastSeqSkipList{resume: state.LastSeq}
  5723→					lss.seqs, _ = o.mset.store.MultiLastSeqs(filters, 0, 0)
  5724→
  5725→					if len(lss.seqs) == 0 {
  5726→						o.sseq = state.LastSeq
  5727→					} else {
  5728→						o.sseq = lss.seqs[0]
  5729→					}
  5730→					// Assign skip list.
  5731→					o.lss = lss
  5732→				}
  5733→			} else if o.cfg.OptStartTime != nil {
  5734→				// If we are here we are time based.
  5735→				// TODO(dlc) - Once clustered can't rely on this.
  5736→				o.sseq = o.mset.store.GetSeqFromTime(*o.cfg.OptStartTime)
  5737→				// Here we want to see if we are filtered, and if so possibly close the gap
  5738→				// to the nearest first given our starting sequence from time. This is so we do
  5739→				// not force the system to do a linear walk between o.sseq and the real first.
  5740→				if len(o.subjf) > 0 {
  5741→					nseq := state.LastSeq
  5742→					for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  5743→						// Use first sequence since this is more optimized atm.
  5744→						ss := o.mset.store.FilteredState(state.FirstSeq, filter.subject)
  5745→						if ss.First >= o.sseq && ss.First < nseq {
  5746→							nseq = ss.First
  5747→						}
  5748→					}
  5749→					// Skip ahead if possible.
  5750→					if nseq > o.sseq && nseq < state.LastSeq {
  5751→						o.sseq = nseq
  5752→					}
  5753→				}
  5754→			} else {
  5755→				// DeliverNew
  5756→				o.sseq = state.LastSeq + 1
  5757→			}
  5758→		} else {
  5759→			o.sseq = o.cfg.OptStartSeq
  5760→		}
  5761→
  5762→		if state.FirstSeq == 0 && (o.cfg.Direct || o.cfg.OptStartSeq == 0) {
  5763→			// If the stream is empty, deliver only new.
  5764→			// But only if mirroring/sourcing, or start seq is unset, otherwise need to respect provided value.
  5765→			o.sseq = 1
  5766→		} else if o.sseq > state.LastSeq && (o.cfg.Direct || o.cfg.OptStartSeq == 0) {
  5767→			// If selected sequence is in the future, clamp back down.
  5768→			// But only if mirroring/sourcing, or start seq is unset, otherwise need to respect provided value.
  5769→			o.sseq = state.LastSeq + 1
  5770→		} else if o.sseq < state.FirstSeq {
  5771→			// If the first sequence is further ahead than the starting sequence,
  5772→			// there are no messages there anymore, so move the sequence up.
  5773→			o.sseq = state.FirstSeq
  5774→		}
  5775→	}
  5776→
  5777→	// Always set delivery sequence to 1.
  5778→	o.dseq = 1
  5779→	// Set ack delivery floor to delivery-1
  5780→	o.adflr = o.dseq - 1
  5781→	// Set ack store floor to store-1
  5782→	o.asflr = o.sseq - 1
  5783→	// Set our starting sequence state.
  5784→	// But only if we're not clustered, if clustered we propose upon becoming leader.
  5785→	if o.store != nil && o.sseq > 0 && o.cfg.replicas(&o.mset.cfg) == 1 {
  5786→		o.store.SetStarting(o.sseq - 1)
  5787→	}
  5788→}

  5857→
  5858→// This is when the underlying stream has been purged.
  5859→// sseq is the new first seq for the stream after purge.
  5860→// Lock should NOT be held.
  5861→func (o *consumer) purge(sseq uint64, slseq uint64, isWider bool) {
  5862→	// Do not update our state unless we know we are the leader.
  5863→	if !o.isLeader() {
  5864→		return
  5865→	}
  5866→	// Signals all have been purged for this consumer.
  5867→	if sseq == 0 && !isWider {
  5868→		sseq = slseq + 1
  5869→	}
  5870→
  5871→	var store StreamStore
  5872→	if isWider {
  5873→		o.mu.RLock()
  5874→		if o.mset != nil {
  5875→			store = o.mset.store
  5876→		}
  5877→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  5878→	}
  5879→
  5880→	o.mu.Lock()
  5881→	// Do not go backwards
  5882→	if o.sseq < sseq {
  5883→		o.sseq = sseq
  5884→	}
  5885→
  5886→	if o.asflr < sseq {
  5887→		o.asflr = sseq - 1
  5888→		// We need to remove those no longer relevant from pending.
  5889→		for seq, p := range o.pending {
  5890→			if seq <= o.asflr {
  5891→				if p.Sequence > o.adflr {
  5892→					o.adflr = p.Sequence
  5893→					if o.adflr > o.dseq {
  5894→						o.dseq = o.adflr
  5895→					}
  5896→				}
  5897→				delete(o.pending, seq)
  5898→				delete(o.rdc, seq)
  5899→				// rdq handled below.
  5900→			}
  5901→			if isWider && store != nil {
  5902→				// Our filtered subject, which could be all, is wider than the underlying purge.
  5903→				// We need to check if the pending items left are still valid.
  5904→				var smv StoreMsg
  5905→				if _, err := store.LoadMsg(seq, &smv); err == errDeletedMsg || err == ErrStoreMsgNotFound {
  5906→					if p.Sequence > o.adflr {
  5907→						o.adflr = p.Sequence
  5908→						if o.adflr > o.dseq {
  5909→							o.dseq = o.adflr
  5910→						}
  5911→					}
  5912→					delete(o.pending, seq)
  5913→					delete(o.rdc, seq)
  5914→				}
  5915→			}
  5916→		}
  5917→	}
  5918→
  5919→	// This means we can reset everything at this point.
  5920→	if len(o.pending) == 0 {
  5921→		o.pending, o.rdc = nil, nil
  5922→		o.adflr, o.asflr = o.dseq-1, o.sseq-1
  5923→	}
  5924→
  5925→	// We need to remove all those being queued for redelivery under o.rdq
  5926→	if len(o.rdq) > 0 {
  5927→		rdq := o.rdq
  5928→		o.rdq = nil
  5929→		o.rdqi.Empty()
  5930→		for _, sseq := range rdq {
  5931→			if sseq >= o.sseq {
  5932→				o.addToRedeliverQueue(sseq)
  5933→			}
  5934→		}
  5935→	}
  5936→	// Grab some info in case of error below.
  5937→	s, acc, mset, name := o.srv, o.acc, o.mset, o.name
  5938→	o.mu.Unlock()
  5939→
  5940→	if err := o.writeStoreState(); err != nil && s != nil && mset != nil {
  5941→		s.Warnf("Consumer '%s > %s > %s' error on write store state from purge: %v", acc, mset.name(), name, err)
  5942→	}
  5943→}
  5944→
  5945→func stopAndClearTimer(tp **time.Timer) {
  5946→	if *tp == nil {
  5947→		return
  5948→	}
  5949→	// Will get drained in normal course, do not try to
  5950→	// drain here.
  5951→	(*tp).Stop()
  5952→	*tp = nil
  5953→}
  5954→
  5955→// Stop will shutdown  the consumer for the associated stream.
  5956→func (o *consumer) stop() error {
  5957→	return o.stopWithFlags(false, false, true, false)
  5958→}
  5959→
  5960→func (o *consumer) deleteWithoutAdvisory() error {
  5961→	return o.stopWithFlags(true, false, true, false)
  5962→}
  5963→
  5964→// Delete will delete the consumer for the associated stream and send advisories.
  5965→func (o *consumer) delete() error {
  5966→	return o.stopWithFlags(true, false, true, true)
  5967→}
  5968→
  5969→// To test for closed state.
  5970→func (o *consumer) isClosed() bool {
  5971→	o.mu.RLock()
  5972→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  5973→	return o.closed
  5974→}
  5975→
  5976→func (o *consumer) stopWithFlags(dflag, sdflag, doSignal, advisory bool) error {
  5977→	// If dflag is true determine if we are still assigned.
  5978→	var isAssigned bool
  5979→	if dflag {
  5980→		o.mu.RLock()
  5981→		acc, stream, consumer := o.acc, o.stream, o.name
  5982→		isClustered := o.js != nil && o.js.isClustered()
  5983→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  5984→		if isClustered {
  5985→			// Grab jsa to check assignment.
  5986→			var jsa *jsAccount
  5987→			if acc != nil {
  5988→				// Need lock here to avoid data race.
  5989→				acc.mu.RLock()
  5990→				jsa = acc.js
  5991→				acc.mu.RUnlock()
  5992→			}
  5993→			if jsa != nil {
  5994→				isAssigned = jsa.consumerAssigned(stream, consumer)
  5995→			}
  5996→		}
  5997→	}
  5998→
  5999→	o.mu.Lock()
  6000→	if o.closed {
  6001→		o.mu.Unlock()
  6002→		return nil
  6003→	}
  6004→	o.closed = true
  6005→
  6006→	// Check if we are the leader and are being deleted (as a node).
  6007→	if dflag && o.isLeader() {
  6008→		// If we are clustered and node leader (probable from above), stepdown.
  6009→		if node := o.node; node != nil {
  6010→			node.StepDown()
  6011→		}
  6012→
  6013→		// dflag does not necessarily mean that the consumer is being deleted,
  6014→		// just that the consumer node is being removed from this peer, so we
  6015→		// send delete advisories only if we are no longer assigned at the meta layer,
  6016→		// or we are not clustered.
  6017→		if !isAssigned && advisory {
  6018→			o.sendDeleteAdvisoryLocked()
  6019→		}
  6020→		if o.isPullMode() {
  6021→			// Release any pending.
  6022→			o.releaseAnyPendingRequests(isAssigned)
  6023→		}
  6024→	}
  6025→
  6026→	if o.qch != nil {
  6027→		close(o.qch)
  6028→		o.qch = nil
  6029→	}
  6030→
  6031→	a := o.acc
  6032→	store := o.store
  6033→	mset := o.mset
  6034→	o.mset = nil
  6035→	o.active = false
  6036→	o.unsubscribe(o.ackSub)
  6037→	o.unsubscribe(o.reqSub)
  6038→	o.unsubscribe(o.fcSub)
  6039→	o.ackSub = nil
  6040→	o.reqSub = nil
  6041→	o.fcSub = nil
  6042→	if o.infoSub != nil {
  6043→		o.srv.sysUnsubscribe(o.infoSub)
  6044→		o.infoSub = nil
  6045→	}
  6046→	c := o.client
  6047→	o.client = nil
  6048→	sysc := o.sysc
  6049→	o.sysc = nil
  6050→	o.stopAndClearPtmr()
  6051→	stopAndClearTimer(&o.dtmr)
  6052→	stopAndClearTimer(&o.gwdtmr)
  6053→	delivery := o.cfg.DeliverSubject
  6054→	o.waiting = nil
  6055→	// Break us out of the readLoop.
  6056→	if doSignal {
  6057→		o.signalNewMessages()
  6058→	}
  6059→	n := o.node
  6060→	qgroup := o.cfg.DeliverGroup
  6061→	o.ackMsgs.unregister()
  6062→	if o.nextMsgReqs != nil {
  6063→		o.nextMsgReqs.unregister()
  6064→	}
  6065→
  6066→	// For cleaning up the node assignment.
  6067→	var ca *consumerAssignment
  6068→	if dflag {
  6069→		ca = o.ca
  6070→	}
  6071→	js := o.js
  6072→	o.mu.Unlock()
  6073→
  6074→	if c != nil {
  6075→		c.closeConnection(ClientClosed)
  6076→	}
  6077→	if sysc != nil {
  6078→		sysc.closeConnection(ClientClosed)
  6079→	}
  6080→
  6081→	if delivery != _EMPTY_ {
  6082→		a.sl.clearNotification(delivery, qgroup, o.inch)
  6083→	}
  6084→
  6085→	var rp RetentionPolicy
  6086→	if mset != nil {
  6087→		mset.mu.Lock()
  6088→		mset.removeConsumer(o)
  6089→		// No need for cfgMu's lock since mset.mu.Lock superseeds it.
  6090→		rp = mset.cfg.Retention
  6091→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  6092→	}
  6093→
  6094→	// Cleanup messages that lost interest.
  6095→	if dflag && rp == InterestPolicy {
  6096→		o.cleanupNoInterestMessages(mset, true)
  6097→	}
  6098→
  6099→	// Cluster cleanup.
  6100→	if n != nil {
  6101→		if dflag {
  6102→			n.Delete()
  6103→		} else {
  6104→			n.Stop()
  6105→		}
  6106→	}
  6107→
  6108→	if ca != nil {
  6109→		js.mu.Lock()
  6110→		if ca.Group != nil {
  6111→			ca.Group.node = nil
  6112→		}
  6113→		js.mu.Unlock()
  6114→	}
  6115→
  6116→	// Clean up our store.
  6117→	var err error
  6118→	if store != nil {
  6119→		if dflag {
  6120→			if sdflag {
  6121→				err = store.StreamDelete()
  6122→			} else {
  6123→				err = store.Delete()
  6124→			}
  6125→		} else {
  6126→			err = store.Stop()
  6127→		}
  6128→	} else if dflag {
  6129→		// If there's no store (for example, when it's offline), manually delete the directories.
  6130→		o.mu.RLock()
  6131→		stream, consumer := o.stream, o.name
  6132→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  6133→		accDir := filepath.Join(js.config.StoreDir, a.GetName())
  6134→		consumersDir := filepath.Join(accDir, streamsDir, stream, consumerDir)
  6135→		os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(consumersDir, consumer))
  6136→	}
  6137→
  6138→	return err
  6139→}
  6140→
  6141→// We need to optionally remove all messages since we are interest based retention.
  6142→// We will do this consistently on all replicas. Note that if in clustered mode the non-leader
  6143→// consumers will need to restore state first.
  6144→// ignoreInterest marks whether the consumer should be ignored when determining interest.
  6145→// No lock held on entry.
  6146→func (o *consumer) cleanupNoInterestMessages(mset *stream, ignoreInterest bool) {
  6147→	o.mu.Lock()
  6148→	if !o.isLeader() {
  6149→		o.readStoredState()
  6150→	}
  6151→	start := o.asflr
  6152→	o.mu.Unlock()
  6153→
  6154→	// Make sure we start at worst with first sequence in the stream.
  6155→	state := mset.state()
  6156→	if start < state.FirstSeq {
  6157→		start = state.FirstSeq
  6158→	}
  6159→	stop := state.LastSeq
  6160→
  6161→	// Consumer's interests are ignored by default. If we should not ignore interest, unset.
  6162→	co := o
  6163→	if !ignoreInterest {
  6164→		co = nil
  6165→	}
  6166→
  6167→	var rmseqs []uint64
  6168→	mset.mu.RLock()
  6169→
  6170→	// If over this amount of messages to check, optimistically call to checkInterestState().
  6171→	// It will not always do the right thing in removing messages that lost interest, but ensures
  6172→	// we don't degrade performance by doing a linear scan through the whole stream.
  6173→	// Messages might need to expire based on limits to be cleaned up.
  6174→	// TODO(dlc) - Better way?
  6175→	const bailThresh = 100_000
  6176→
  6177→	// Check if we would be spending too much time here and defer to separate go routine.
  6178→	if len(mset.consumers) == 0 {
  6179→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
  6180→		mset.mu.Lock()
  6181→		defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  6182→		mset.store.Purge()
  6183→		var state StreamState
  6184→		mset.store.FastState(&state)
  6185→		mset.lseq = state.LastSeq
  6186→		// Also make sure we clear any pending acks.
  6187→		mset.clearAllPreAcksBelowFloor(state.FirstSeq)
  6188→		return
  6189→	} else if stop-start > bailThresh {
  6190→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
  6191→		go mset.checkInterestState()
  6192→		return
  6193→	}
  6194→
  6195→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  6196→	mset.mu.Lock()
  6197→	for seq := start; seq <= stop; seq++ {
  6198→		if mset.noInterest(seq, co) {
  6199→			rmseqs = append(rmseqs, seq)
  6200→		}
  6201→	}
  6202→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  6203→
  6204→	// These can be removed.
  6205→	for _, seq := range rmseqs {
  6206→		mset.store.RemoveMsg(seq)
  6207→	}
  6208→}
  6209→
  6210→// Check that we do not form a cycle by delivering to a delivery subject
  6211→// that is part of the interest group.
  6212→func deliveryFormsCycle(cfg *StreamConfig, deliverySubject string) bool {
  6213→	for _, subject := range cfg.Subjects {
  6214→		if subjectIsSubsetMatch(deliverySubject, subject) {
  6215→			return true
  6216→		}
  6217→	}
  6218→	return false
  6219→}
  6220→
  6221→// switchToEphemeral is called on startup when recovering ephemerals.
  6222→func (o *consumer) switchToEphemeral() {
  6223→	o.mu.Lock()
  6224→	o.cfg.Durable = _EMPTY_
  6225→	store, ok := o.store.(*consumerFileStore)
  6226→	interest := o.acc.sl.HasInterest(o.cfg.DeliverSubject)
  6227→	// Setup dthresh.
  6228→	o.updateInactiveThreshold(&o.cfg)
  6229→	o.updatePauseState(&o.cfg)
  6230→	o.mu.Unlock()
  6231→
  6232→	// Update interest
  6233→	o.updateDeliveryInterest(interest)
  6234→	// Write out new config
  6235→	if ok {
  6236→		store.updateConfig(o.cfg)
  6237→	}
  6238→}

  6240→// RequestNextMsgSubject returns the subject to request the next message when in pull or worker mode.
  6241→// Returns empty otherwise.
  6242→func (o *consumer) requestNextMsgSubject() string {
  6243→	return o.nextMsgSubj
  6244→}
  6245→
  6246→func (o *consumer) decStreamPending(sseq uint64, subj string) {
  6247→	o.mu.Lock()
  6248→
  6249→	// Update our cached num pending only if we think deliverMsg has not done so.
  6250→	if sseq >= o.sseq && o.isFilteredMatch(subj) {
  6251→		o.npc--
  6252→	}
  6253→
  6254→	// Check if this message was pending.
  6255→	p, wasPending := o.pending[sseq]
  6256→	var rdc uint64
  6257→	if wasPending {
  6258→		rdc = o.deliveryCount(sseq)
  6259→	}
  6260→
  6261→	o.mu.Unlock()
  6262→
  6263→	// If it was pending process it like an ack.
  6264→	if wasPending {
  6265→		// We could have the lock for the stream so do this in a go routine.
  6266→		// TODO(dlc) - We should do this with ipq vs naked go routines.
  6267→		go o.processTerm(sseq, p.Sequence, rdc, ackTermUnackedLimitsReason, _EMPTY_)
  6268→	}
  6269→}
  6270→
  6271→func (o *consumer) account() *Account {
  6272→	o.mu.RLock()
  6273→	a := o.acc
  6274→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  6275→	return a
  6276→}
  6277→
  6278→// Creates a sublist for consumer.
  6279→// All subjects share the same callback.
  6280→func (o *consumer) signalSubs() []string {
  6281→	o.mu.Lock()
  6282→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  6283→
  6284→	if o.sigSubs != nil {
  6285→		return o.sigSubs
  6286→	}
  6287→
  6288→	if len(o.subjf) == 0 {
  6289→		subs := []string{fwcs}
  6290→		o.sigSubs = subs
  6291→		return subs
  6292→	}
  6293→
  6294→	subs := make([]string, 0, len(o.subjf))
  6295→	for _, filter := range o.subjf {
  6296→		subs = append(subs, filter.subject)
  6297→	}
  6298→	o.sigSubs = subs
  6299→	return subs
  6300→}
  6301→
  6302→// This is what will be called when our parent stream wants to kick us regarding a new message.
  6303→// We know that this subject matches us by how the parent handles registering us with the signaling sublist,
  6304→// but we must check if we are leader.
  6305→// We do need the sequence of the message however and we use the msg as the encoded seq.
  6306→func (o *consumer) processStreamSignal(seq uint64) {
  6307→	// We can get called here now when not leader, so bail fast
  6308→	// and without acquiring any locks.
  6309→	if !o.leader.Load() {
  6310→		return
  6311→	}
  6312→	o.mu.Lock()
  6313→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  6314→	if o.mset == nil {
  6315→		return
  6316→	}
  6317→	if seq > o.npf {
  6318→		o.npc++
  6319→	}
  6320→	if seq < o.sseq {
  6321→		return
  6322→	}
  6323→	if o.isPushMode() && o.active || o.isPullMode() && !o.waiting.isEmpty() {
  6324→		o.signalNewMessages()
  6325→	}
  6326→}
  6327→
  6328→// Used to compare if two multiple filtered subject lists are equal.
  6329→func subjectSliceEqual(slice1 []string, slice2 []string) bool {
  6330→	if len(slice1) != len(slice2) {
  6331→		return false
  6332→	}
  6333→	set2 := make(map[string]struct{}, len(slice2))
  6334→	for _, val := range slice2 {
  6335→		set2[val] = struct{}{}
  6336→	}
  6337→	for _, val := range slice1 {
  6338→		if _, ok := set2[val]; !ok {
  6339→			return false
  6340→		}
  6341→	}
  6342→	return true
  6343→}
  6344→
  6345→// Utility for simpler if conditions in Consumer config checks.
  6346→// In future iteration, we can immediately create `o.subjf` and
  6347→// use it to validate things.
  6348→func gatherSubjectFilters(filter string, filters []string) []string {
  6349→	if filter != _EMPTY_ {
  6350→		filters = append(filters, filter)
  6351→	}
  6352→	// list of filters should never contain non-empty filter.
  6353→	return filters
  6354→}
  6355→
  6356→// shouldStartMonitor will return true if we should start a monitor
  6357→// goroutine or will return false if one is already running.
  6358→func (o *consumer) shouldStartMonitor() bool {
  6359→	o.mu.Lock()
  6360→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  6361→
  6362→	if o.inMonitor {
  6363→		return false
  6364→	}
  6365→	o.monitorWg.Add(1)
  6366→	o.inMonitor = true
  6367→	return true
  6368→}
  6369→
  6370→// Clear the monitor running state. The monitor goroutine should
  6371→// call this in a defer to clean up on exit.
  6372→func (o *consumer) clearMonitorRunning() {
  6373→	o.mu.Lock()
  6374→	defer o.mu.Unlock()
  6375→
  6376→	if o.inMonitor {
  6377→		o.monitorWg.Done()
  6378→		o.inMonitor = false
  6379→	}
  6380→}
  6381→
  6382→// Test whether we are in the monitor routine.
  6383→func (o *consumer) isMonitorRunning() bool {
  6384→	o.mu.RLock()
  6385→	defer o.mu.RUnlock()
  6386→	return o.inMonitor
  6387→}
  6388→
  6389→// If we detect that our ackfloor is higher than the stream's last sequence, return this error.
  6390→var errAckFloorHigherThanLastSeq = errors.New("consumer ack floor is higher than streams last sequence")
  6391→var errAckFloorInvalid = errors.New("consumer ack floor is invalid")
  6392→
  6393→// If we are a consumer of an interest or workqueue policy stream, process that state and make sure consistent.
  6394→func (o *consumer) checkStateForInterestStream(ss *StreamState) error {
  6395→	o.mu.RLock()
  6396→	// See if we need to process this update if our parent stream is not a limits policy stream.
  6397→	mset := o.mset
  6398→	shouldProcessState := mset != nil && o.retention != LimitsPolicy
  6399→	if o.closed || !shouldProcessState || o.store == nil || ss == nil {
  6400→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  6401→		return nil
  6402→	}
  6403→	store := mset.store
  6404→	state, err := o.store.State()
  6405→
  6406→	filters, subjf, filter := o.filters, o.subjf, _EMPTY_
  6407→	var wc bool
  6408→	if filters == nil && subjf != nil {
  6409→		filter, wc = subjf[0].subject, subjf[0].hasWildcard
  6410→	}
  6411→	chkfloor := o.chkflr
  6412→	o.mu.RUnlock()
  6413→
  6414→	if err != nil {
  6415→		return err
  6416→	}
  6417→
  6418→	asflr := state.AckFloor.Stream
  6419→	// Protect ourselves against rolling backwards.
  6420→	if asflr&(1<<63) != 0 {
  6421→		return errAckFloorInvalid
  6422→	}
  6423→
  6424→	// Check if the underlying stream's last sequence is less than our floor.
  6425→	// This can happen if the stream has been reset and has not caught up yet.
  6426→	if asflr > ss.LastSeq {
  6427→		return errAckFloorHigherThanLastSeq
  6428→	}
  6429→
  6430→	var smv StoreMsg
  6431→	var seq, nseq uint64
  6432→	// Start at first stream seq or a previous check floor, whichever is higher.
  6433→	// Note this will really help for interest retention, with WQ the loadNextMsg
  6434→	// gets us a long way already since it will skip deleted msgs not for our filter.
  6435→	fseq := ss.FirstSeq
  6436→	if chkfloor > fseq {
  6437→		fseq = chkfloor
  6438→	}
  6439→
  6440→	var retryAsflr uint64
  6441→	for seq = fseq; asflr > 0 && seq <= asflr; seq++ {
  6442→		if filters != nil {
  6443→			_, nseq, err = store.LoadNextMsgMulti(filters, seq, &smv)
  6444→		} else {
  6445→			_, nseq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(filter, wc, seq, &smv)
  6446→		}
  6447→		// if we advanced sequence update our seq. This can be on no error and EOF.
  6448→		if nseq > seq {
  6449→			seq = nseq
  6450→		}
  6451→		// Only ack though if no error and seq <= ack floor.
  6452		if err == nil && seq <= asflr {
  6453→			didRemove := mset.ackMsg(o, seq)
  6454→			// Removing the message could fail. For example if clustered since we need to propose it.
  6455→			// Overwrite retry floor (only the first time) to allow us to check next time if the removal was successful.
  6456→			if didRemove && retryAsflr == 0 {
  6457→				retryAsflr = seq
  6458→			}
  6459→		}
  6460→	}
  6461→	// If retry floor was not overwritten, set to ack floor+1, we don't need to account for any retries below it.
  6462→	if retryAsflr == 0 {
  6463→		retryAsflr = asflr + 1
  6464→	}
  6465→
  6466→	o.mu.Lock()
  6467→	// Update our check floor.
  6468→	// Check floor must never be greater than ack floor+1, otherwise subsequent calls to this function would skip work.
  6469→	if retryAsflr > o.chkflr {
  6470→		o.chkflr = retryAsflr
  6471→	}
  6472→	// See if we need to process this update if our parent stream is not a limits policy stream.
  6473→	state, _ = o.store.State()
  6474→	o.mu.Unlock()
  6475→
  6476→	// If we have pending, we will need to walk through to delivered in case we missed any of those acks as well.
  6477→	if state != nil && len(state.Pending) > 0 && state.AckFloor.Stream > 0 {
  6478→		for seq := state.AckFloor.Stream + 1; seq <= state.Delivered.Stream; seq++ {
  6479→			if _, ok := state.Pending[seq]; !ok {
  6480→				// Want to call needAck since it is filter aware.
  6481→				if o.needAck(seq, _EMPTY_) {
  6482→					mset.ackMsg(o, seq)
  6483→				}
  6484→			}
  6485→		}
  6486→	}
  6487→	return nil
  6488→}
  6489→
  6490→func (o *consumer) resetPtmr(delay time.Duration) {
  6491→	if o.ptmr == nil {
  6492→		o.ptmr = time.AfterFunc(delay, o.checkPending)
  6493→	} else {
  6494→		o.ptmr.Reset(delay)
  6495→	}
  6496→	o.ptmrEnd = time.Now().Add(delay)
  6497→}
  6498→
  6499→func (o *consumer) stopAndClearPtmr() {
  6500→	stopAndClearTimer(&o.ptmr)
  6501→	o.ptmrEnd = time.Time{}
  6502→}
  6503→
  6504→func (o *consumer) resetPendingDeliveries() {
  6505→	for _, pmsg := range o.pendingDeliveries {
  6506→		pmsg.returnToPool()
  6507→	}
  6508→	o.pendingDeliveries = nil
  6509→	for _, wd := range o.waitingDeliveries {
  6510→		wd.recycle()
  6511→	}
  6512→	o.waitingDeliveries = nil
  6513→}
  6514→

    41→// StreamConfigRequest is used to create or update a stream.

    49→// StreamConfig will determine the name, subjects and retention policy
    50→// for a given stream. If subjects is empty the name will be used.
    51→type StreamConfig struct {
    52→	Name         string           `json:"name"`
    53→	Description  string           `json:"description,omitempty"`
    54→	Subjects     []string         `json:"subjects,omitempty"`
    55→	Retention    RetentionPolicy  `json:"retention"`
    56→	MaxConsumers int              `json:"max_consumers"`
    57→	MaxMsgs      int64            `json:"max_msgs"`
    58→	MaxBytes     int64            `json:"max_bytes"`
    59→	MaxAge       time.Duration    `json:"max_age"`
    60→	MaxMsgsPer   int64            `json:"max_msgs_per_subject"`
    61→	MaxMsgSize   int32            `json:"max_msg_size,omitempty"`
    62→	Discard      DiscardPolicy    `json:"discard"`
    63→	Storage      StorageType      `json:"storage"`
    64→	Replicas     int              `json:"num_replicas"`
    65→	NoAck        bool             `json:"no_ack,omitempty"`
    66→	Template     string           `json:"template_owner,omitempty"` // Deprecated: stream templates are deprecated and will be removed in a future version.
    67→	Duplicates   time.Duration    `json:"duplicate_window,omitempty"`
    68→	Placement    *Placement       `json:"placement,omitempty"`
    69→	Mirror       *StreamSource    `json:"mirror,omitempty"`
    70→	Sources      []*StreamSource  `json:"sources,omitempty"`
    71→	Compression  StoreCompression `json:"compression"`
    72→	FirstSeq     uint64           `json:"first_seq,omitempty"`
    73→
    74→	// Allow applying a subject transform to incoming messages before doing anything else
    75→	SubjectTransform *SubjectTransformConfig `json:"subject_transform,omitempty"`
    76→
    77→	// Allow republish of the message after being sequenced and stored.
    78→	RePublish *RePublish `json:"republish,omitempty"`
    79→
    80→	// Allow higher performance, direct access to get individual messages. E.g. KeyValue
    81→	AllowDirect bool `json:"allow_direct"`
    82→	// Allow higher performance and unified direct access for mirrors as well.
    83→	MirrorDirect bool `json:"mirror_direct"`
    84→
    85→	// Allow KV like semantics to also discard new on a per subject basis
    86→	DiscardNewPer bool `json:"discard_new_per_subject,omitempty"`
    87→
    88→	// Optional qualifiers. These can not be modified after set to true.
    89→
    90→	// Sealed will seal a stream so no messages can get out or in.
    91→	Sealed bool `json:"sealed"`
    92→	// DenyDelete will restrict the ability to delete messages.
    93→	DenyDelete bool `json:"deny_delete"`
    94→	// DenyPurge will restrict the ability to purge messages.
    95→	DenyPurge bool `json:"deny_purge"`
    96→	// AllowRollup allows messages to be placed into the system and purge
    97→	// all older messages using a special msg header.
    98→	AllowRollup bool `json:"allow_rollup_hdrs"`
    99→
   100→	// The following defaults will apply to consumers when created against
   101→	// this stream, unless overridden manually.
   102→	// TODO(nat): Can/should we name these better?
   103→	ConsumerLimits StreamConsumerLimits `json:"consumer_limits"`
   104→
   105→	// AllowMsgTTL allows header initiated per-message TTLs. If disabled,
   106→	// then the `NATS-TTL` header will be ignored.
   107→	AllowMsgTTL bool `json:"allow_msg_ttl"`
   108→
   109→	// SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL sets the TTL of delete marker messages left behind by
   110→	// subject delete markers.
   111→	SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL time.Duration `json:"subject_delete_marker_ttl,omitempty"`
   112→
   113→	// AllowMsgCounter allows a stream to use (only) counter CRDTs.
   114→	AllowMsgCounter bool `json:"allow_msg_counter,omitempty"`
   115→
   116→	// AllowAtomicPublish allows atomic batch publishing into the stream.
   117→	AllowAtomicPublish bool `json:"allow_atomic,omitempty"`
   118→
   119→	// AllowMsgSchedules allows the scheduling of messages.
   120→	AllowMsgSchedules bool `json:"allow_msg_schedules,omitempty"`
   121→
   122→	// Metadata is additional metadata for the Stream.
   123→	Metadata map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
   124→}

   126→// clone performs a deep copy of the StreamConfig struct, returning a new clone with
   127→// all values copied.
   128→func (cfg *StreamConfig) clone() *StreamConfig {
   129→	clone := *cfg
   130→	if cfg.Placement != nil {
   131→		placement := *cfg.Placement
   132→		clone.Placement = &placement
   133→	}
   134→	if cfg.Mirror != nil {
   135→		mirror := *cfg.Mirror
   136→		clone.Mirror = &mirror
   137→	}
   138→	if len(cfg.Sources) > 0 {
   139→		clone.Sources = make([]*StreamSource, len(cfg.Sources))
   140→		for i, cfgSource := range cfg.Sources {
   141→			source := *cfgSource
   142→			clone.Sources[i] = &source
   143→		}
   144→	}
   145→	if cfg.SubjectTransform != nil {
   146→		transform := *cfg.SubjectTransform
   147→		clone.SubjectTransform = &transform
   148→	}
   149→	if cfg.RePublish != nil {
   150→		rePublish := *cfg.RePublish
   151→		clone.RePublish = &rePublish
   152→	}
   153→	if cfg.Metadata != nil {
   154→		clone.Metadata = make(map[string]string, len(cfg.Metadata))
   155→		for k, v := range cfg.Metadata {
   156→			clone.Metadata[k] = v
   157→		}
   158→	}
   159→	return &clone
   160→}

   439→// inflightSubjectRunningTotal stores a running total of inflight messages for a specific subject.
   440→type inflightSubjectRunningTotal struct {
   441→	bytes uint64 // Running total of inflight bytes for inflight messages.
   442→	ops   uint64 // Inflight operations, i.e. inflight messages for this subject. If this reaches zero, we can remove the running total.
   443→}
   444→
   445→// msgCounterRunningTotal stores a running total and a number of inflight
   446→// but not yet applied clustered proposals/operations for this counter.
   447→type msgCounterRunningTotal struct {
   448→	total   *big.Int       // Running total.
   449→	sources CounterSources // Last seen counter sources.
   450→	ops     uint64         // Inflight operations. If this reaches zero, we can remove the running total.
   451→}
   452→
   453→type sourceInfo struct {
   454→	name  string        // The name of the stream being sourced.
   455→	iname string        // The unique index name of this particular source.
   456→	cname string        // The name of the current consumer for this source.
   457→	sub   *subscription // The subscription to the consumer.
   458→
   459→	msgs  *ipQueue[*inMsg]    // Intra-process queue for incoming messages.
   460→	sseq  uint64              // Last stream message sequence number seen from the source.
   461→	dseq  uint64              // Last delivery (i.e. consumer's) sequence number.
   462→	lag   uint64              // 0 or number of messages pending (as last reported by the consumer) - 1.
   463→	err   *ApiError           // The API error that caused the last consumer setup to fail.
   464→	fails int                 // The number of times trying to setup the consumer failed.
   465→	last  atomic.Int64        // Time the consumer was created or of last message it received.
   466→	lreq  time.Time           // The last time setupMirrorConsumer/setupSourceConsumer was called.
   467→	qch   chan struct{}       // Quit channel.
   468→	sip   bool                // Setup in progress.
   469→	wg    sync.WaitGroup      // WaitGroup for the consumer's go routine.
   470→	sf    string              // The subject filter.
   471→	sfs   []string            // The subject filters.
   472→	trs   []*subjectTransform // The subject transforms.
   473→}

   558→
   559→// AddStream adds a stream for the given account.
   560→func (a *Account) addStream(config *StreamConfig) (*stream, error) {
   561→	return a.addStreamWithAssignment(config, nil, nil, false)
   562→}
   563→
   564→// AddStreamWithStore adds a stream for the given account with custome store config options.
   565→func (a *Account) addStreamWithStore(config *StreamConfig, fsConfig *FileStoreConfig) (*stream, error) {
   566→	return a.addStreamWithAssignment(config, fsConfig, nil, false)
   567→}
   568→
   569→func (a *Account) addStreamPedantic(config *StreamConfig, pedantic bool) (*stream, error) {
   570→	return a.addStreamWithAssignment(config, nil, nil, pedantic)
   571→}
   572→
   573→func (a *Account) addStreamWithAssignment(config *StreamConfig, fsConfig *FileStoreConfig, sa *streamAssignment, pedantic bool) (*stream, error) {
   574→	s, jsa, err := a.checkForJetStream()
   575→	if err != nil {
   576→		return nil, err
   577→	}
   578→
   579→	// If we do not have the stream currently assigned to us in cluster mode we will proceed but warn.
   580→	// This can happen on startup with restored state where on meta replay we still do not have
   581→	// the assignment. Running in single server mode this always returns true.
   582→	if !jsa.streamAssigned(config.Name) {
   583→		s.Debugf("Stream '%s > %s' does not seem to be assigned to this server", a.Name, config.Name)
   584→	}
   585→
   586→	// Sensible defaults.
   587→	ccfg, apiErr := s.checkStreamCfg(config, a, pedantic)
   588→	if apiErr != nil {
   589→		return nil, apiErr
   590→	}
   591→	cfg := &ccfg
   592→
   593→	singleServerMode := !s.JetStreamIsClustered() && s.standAloneMode()
   594→	if singleServerMode && cfg.Replicas > 1 {
   595→		return nil, ApiErrors[JSStreamReplicasNotSupportedErr]
   596→	}
   597→
   598→	// Make sure we are ok when these are done in parallel.
   599→	// We used to call Add(1) in the "else" clause of the "if loaded"
   600→	// statement. This caused a data race because it was possible
   601→	// that one go routine stores (with count==0) and another routine
   602→	// gets "loaded==true" and calls wg.Wait() while the other routine
   603→	// then calls wg.Add(1). It also could mean that two routines execute
   604→	// the rest of the code concurrently.
   605→	swg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
   606→	swg.Add(1)
   607→	v, loaded := jsa.inflight.LoadOrStore(cfg.Name, swg)
   608→	wg := v.(*sync.WaitGroup)
   609→	if loaded {
   610→		wg.Wait()
   611→		// This waitgroup is "thrown away" (since there was an existing one).
   612→		swg.Done()
   613→	} else {
   614→		defer func() {
   615→			jsa.inflight.Delete(cfg.Name)
   616→			wg.Done()
   617→		}()
   618→	}
   619→
   620→	js, isClustered := jsa.jetStreamAndClustered()
   621→	jsa.mu.Lock()
   622→	if mset, ok := jsa.streams[cfg.Name]; ok {
   623→		jsa.mu.Unlock()
   624→		// Check to see if configs are same.
   625→		ocfg := mset.config()
   626→
   627→		// set the index name on cfg since it would not contain a value for iname while the return from mset.config() does to ensure the DeepEqual works
   628→		for _, s := range cfg.Sources {
   629→			s.setIndexName()
   630→		}
   631→
   632→		// Hold lock, because we'll be reading from and writing to a shared object.
   633→		js.mu.Lock()
   634→		copyStreamMetadata(cfg, &ocfg)
   635→		deepEqual := reflect.DeepEqual(cfg, &ocfg)
   636→		js.mu.Unlock()
   637→		if deepEqual {
   638→			if sa != nil {
   639→				mset.setStreamAssignment(sa)
   640→			}
   641→			return mset, nil
   642→		} else {
   643→			return nil, ApiErrors[JSStreamNameExistErr]
   644→		}
   645→	}
   646→	jsa.usageMu.RLock()
   647→	selected, tier, hasTier := jsa.selectLimits(cfg.Replicas)
   648→	jsa.usageMu.RUnlock()
   649→	reserved := int64(0)
   650→	if !isClustered {
   651→		reserved = jsa.tieredReservation(tier, cfg)
   652→	}
   653→	jsa.mu.Unlock()
   654→
   655→	if !hasTier {
   656→		return nil, NewJSNoLimitsError()
   657→	}
   658→	js.mu.RLock()
   659→	if isClustered {
   660→		_, reserved = tieredStreamAndReservationCount(js.cluster.streams[a.Name], tier, cfg)
   661→	}
   662→	if err := js.checkAllLimits(&selected, cfg, reserved, 0); err != nil {
   663→		js.mu.RUnlock()
   664→		return nil, err
   665→	}
   666→	js.mu.RUnlock()
   667→	jsa.mu.Lock()
   668→	// Check for template ownership if present.
   669→	if cfg.Template != _EMPTY_ && jsa.account != nil {
   670→		if !jsa.checkTemplateOwnership(cfg.Template, cfg.Name) {
   671→			jsa.mu.Unlock()
   672→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("stream not owned by template")
   673→		}
   674→	}
   675→
   676→	// If mirror, check if the transforms (if any) are valid.
   677→	if cfg.Mirror != nil {
   678→		if len(cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms) == 0 {
   679→			if cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject) {
   680→				jsa.mu.Unlock()
   681→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject filter '%s' for the mirror %w", cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject, ErrBadSubject)
   682→			}
   683→		} else {
   684→			for _, st := range cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms {
   685→				if st.Source != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(st.Source) {
   686→					jsa.mu.Unlock()
   687→					return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid subject transform source '%s' for the mirror: %w", st.Source, ErrBadSubject)
   688→				}
   689→				// check the transform, if any, is valid
   690→				if st.Destination != _EMPTY_ {
   691→					if _, err = NewSubjectTransform(st.Source, st.Destination); err != nil {
   692→						jsa.mu.Unlock()
   693→						return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject transform from '%s' to '%s' for the mirror: %w", st.Source, st.Destination, err)
   694→					}
   695→				}
   696→			}
   697→		}
   698→	}
   699→
   700→	// Setup our internal indexed names here for sources and check if the transforms (if any) are valid.
   701→	for _, ssi := range cfg.Sources {
   702→		if len(ssi.SubjectTransforms) == 0 {
   703→			// check the filter, if any, is valid
   704→			if ssi.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(ssi.FilterSubject) {
   705→				jsa.mu.Unlock()
   706→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject filter '%s' for the source: %w", ssi.FilterSubject, ErrBadSubject)
   707→			}
   708→		} else {
   709→			for _, st := range ssi.SubjectTransforms {
   710→				if st.Source != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(st.Source) {
   711→					jsa.mu.Unlock()
   712→					return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject filter '%s' for the source: %w", st.Source, ErrBadSubject)
   713→				}
   714→				// check the transform, if any, is valid
   715→				if st.Destination != _EMPTY_ {
   716→					if _, err = NewSubjectTransform(st.Source, st.Destination); err != nil {
   717→						jsa.mu.Unlock()
   718→						return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject transform from '%s' to '%s' for the source: %w", st.Source, st.Destination, err)
   719→					}
   720→				}
   721→			}
   722→		}
   723→	}
   724→
   725→	// Check for overlapping subjects with other streams.
   726→	// These are not allowed for now.
   727→	if jsa.subjectsOverlap(cfg.Subjects, nil) {
   728→		jsa.mu.Unlock()
   729→		return nil, NewJSStreamSubjectOverlapError()
   730→	}
   731→
   732→	if !hasTier {
   733→		jsa.mu.Unlock()
   734→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no applicable tier found")
   735→	}
   736→
   737→	// Setup the internal clients.
   738→	c := s.createInternalJetStreamClient()
   739→	ic := s.createInternalJetStreamClient()
   740→
   741→	// Work out the stream ingest limits.
   742→	mlen := s.opts.StreamMaxBufferedMsgs
   743→	msz := uint64(s.opts.StreamMaxBufferedSize)
   744→	if mlen == 0 {
   745→		mlen = streamDefaultMaxQueueMsgs
   746→	}
   747→	if msz == 0 {
   748→		msz = streamDefaultMaxQueueBytes
   749→	}
   750→
   751→	qpfx := fmt.Sprintf("[ACC:%s] stream '%s' ", a.Name, config.Name)
   752→	mset := &stream{
   753→		acc:       a,
   754→		jsa:       jsa,
   755→		cfg:       *cfg,
   756→		js:        js,
   757→		srv:       s,
   758→		client:    c,
   759→		sysc:      ic,
   760→		tier:      tier,
   761→		stype:     cfg.Storage,
   762→		consumers: make(map[string]*consumer),
   763→		msgs: newIPQueue[*inMsg](s, qpfx+"messages",
   764→			ipqSizeCalculation(func(msg *inMsg) uint64 {
   765→				return uint64(len(msg.hdr) + len(msg.msg) + len(msg.rply) + len(msg.subj))
   766→			}),
   767→			ipqLimitByLen[*inMsg](mlen),
   768→			ipqLimitBySize[*inMsg](msz),
   769→		),
   770→		gets: newIPQueue[*directGetReq](s, qpfx+"direct gets"),
   771→		qch:  make(chan struct{}),
   772→		mqch: make(chan struct{}),
   773→		uch:  make(chan struct{}, 4),
   774→		sch:  make(chan struct{}, 1),
   775→	}
   776→
   777→	// Start our signaling routine to process consumers.
   778→	mset.sigq = newIPQueue[*cMsg](s, qpfx+"obs") // of *cMsg
   779→	go mset.signalConsumersLoop()
   780→
   781→	// For no-ack consumers when we are interest retention.
   782→	if cfg.Retention != LimitsPolicy {
   783→		mset.ackq = newIPQueue[uint64](s, qpfx+"acks")
   784→	}
   785→
   786→	// Check for input subject transform
   787→	if cfg.SubjectTransform != nil {
   788→		tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination)
   789→		if err != nil {
   790→			jsa.mu.Unlock()
   791→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("stream subject transform from '%s' to '%s': %w", cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination, err)
   792→		}
   793→		mset.itr = tr
   794→	}
   795→
   796→	// Check for RePublish.
   797→	if cfg.RePublish != nil {
   798→		tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination)
   799→		if err != nil {
   800→			jsa.mu.Unlock()
   801→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("stream republish transform from '%s' to '%s': %w", cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination, err)
   802→		}
   803→		// Assign our transform for republishing.
   804→		mset.tr = tr
   805→	}
   806→	storeDir := filepath.Join(jsa.storeDir, streamsDir, cfg.Name)
   807→	jsa.mu.Unlock()
   808→
   809→	// Bind to the user account.
   810→	c.registerWithAccount(a)
   811→	// Bind to the system account.
   812→	ic.registerWithAccount(s.SystemAccount())
   813→
   814→	// Create the appropriate storage
   815→	fsCfg := fsConfig
   816→	if fsCfg == nil {
   817→		fsCfg = &FileStoreConfig{}
   818→		// If we are file based and not explicitly configured
   819→		// we may be able to auto-tune based on max msgs or bytes.
   820→		if cfg.Storage == FileStorage {
   821→			mset.autoTuneFileStorageBlockSize(fsCfg)
   822→		}
   823→	}
   824→	fsCfg.StoreDir = storeDir
   825→	// Grab configured sync interval.
   826→	fsCfg.SyncInterval = s.getOpts().SyncInterval
   827→	fsCfg.SyncAlways = s.getOpts().SyncAlways
   828→	fsCfg.Compression = config.Compression
   829→	// Async flushing is only allowed if the stream has a sync log backing it.
   830→	fsCfg.AsyncFlush = !fsCfg.SyncAlways && config.Replicas > 1
   831→
   832→	if err := mset.setupStore(fsCfg); err != nil {
   833→		mset.stop(true, false)
   834→		return nil, NewJSStreamStoreFailedError(err)
   835→	}
   836→
   837→	// Create our pubAck template here. Better than json marshal each time on success.
   838→	if domain := s.getOpts().JetStreamDomain; domain != _EMPTY_ {
   839→		mset.pubAck = fmt.Appendf(nil, "{%q:%q,%q:%q,%q:", "stream", cfg.Name, "domain", domain, "seq")
   840→	} else {
   841→		mset.pubAck = fmt.Appendf(nil, "{%q:%q,%q:", "stream", cfg.Name, "seq")
   842→	}
   843→	end := len(mset.pubAck)
   844→	mset.pubAck = mset.pubAck[:end:end]
   845→
   846→	// Set our known last sequence.
   847→	var state StreamState
   848→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
   849→
   850→	// Possible race with consumer.setLeader during recovery.
   851→	mset.mu.Lock()
   852→	mset.lseq = state.LastSeq
   853→
   854→	// Ensure dedupe state is loaded.
   855→	mset.ddMu.Lock()
   856→	mset.rebuildDedupe()
   857→	mset.ddMu.Unlock()
   858→	mset.mu.Unlock()
   859→
   860→	// Set our stream assignment if in clustered mode.
   861→	reserveResources := true
   862→	if sa != nil {
   863→		mset.setStreamAssignment(sa)
   864→
   865→		// If the stream is resetting we must not double-account resources, they were already accounted for.
   866→		js.mu.Lock()
   867→		if sa.resetting {
   868→			reserveResources, sa.resetting = false, false
   869→		}
   870→		js.mu.Unlock()
   871→	}
   872→
   873→	// Setup our internal send go routine.
   874→	mset.setupSendCapabilities()
   875→
   876→	// Reserve resources if MaxBytes present.
   877→	if reserveResources {
   878→		mset.js.reserveStreamResources(&mset.cfg)
   879→	}
   880→
   881→	// Call directly to set leader if not in clustered mode.
   882→	// This can be called though before we actually setup clustering, so check both.
   883→	if singleServerMode {
   884→		if err := mset.setLeader(true); err != nil {
   885→			mset.stop(true, false)
   886→			return nil, err
   887→		}
   888→	}
   889→
   890→	// This is always true in single server mode.
   891→	if mset.IsLeader() {
   892→		// Send advisory.
   893→		var suppress bool
   894→		if !s.standAloneMode() && sa == nil {
   895→			if cfg.Replicas > 1 {
   896→				suppress = true
   897→			}
   898→		} else if sa != nil {
   899→			suppress = sa.responded
   900→		}
   901→		if !suppress {
   902→			mset.sendCreateAdvisory()
   903→		}
   904→	}
   905→
   906→	// Register with our account last.
   907→	jsa.mu.Lock()
   908→	jsa.streams[cfg.Name] = mset
   909→	jsa.mu.Unlock()
   910→
   911→	return mset, nil
   912→}
   913→
   914→// Composes the index name. Contains the stream name, subject filter, and transform destination
   915→// when the stream is external we will use the api prefix as part of the index name
   916→// (as the same stream name could be used in multiple JS domains)
   917→func (ssi *StreamSource) composeIName() string {
   918→	var iName = ssi.Name
   919→
   920→	if ssi.External != nil {
   921→		iName = iName + ":" + getHash(ssi.External.ApiPrefix)
   922→	}
   923→
   924→	source := ssi.FilterSubject
   925→	destination := fwcs
   926→
   927→	if len(ssi.SubjectTransforms) == 0 {
   928→		// normalize filter and destination in case they are empty
   929→		if source == _EMPTY_ {
   930→			source = fwcs
   931→		}
   932→		if destination == _EMPTY_ {
   933→			destination = fwcs
   934→		}
   935→	} else {
   936→		var sources, destinations []string
   937→
   938→		for _, tr := range ssi.SubjectTransforms {
   939→			trsrc, trdest := tr.Source, tr.Destination
   940→			if trsrc == _EMPTY_ {
   941→				trsrc = fwcs
   942→			}
   943→			if trdest == _EMPTY_ {
   944→				trdest = fwcs
   945→			}
   946→			sources = append(sources, trsrc)
   947→			destinations = append(destinations, trdest)
   948→		}
   949→		source = strings.Join(sources, "\f")
   950→		destination = strings.Join(destinations, "\f")
   951→	}
   952→
   953→	return strings.Join([]string{iName, source, destination}, " ")
   954→}
   955→
   956→// Sets the index name.
   957→func (ssi *StreamSource) setIndexName() {
   958→	ssi.iname = ssi.composeIName()
   959→}
   960→
   961→func (mset *stream) streamAssignment() *streamAssignment {
   962→	mset.mu.RLock()
   963→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
   964→	return mset.sa
   965→}
   966→
   967→func (mset *stream) setStreamAssignment(sa *streamAssignment) {
   968→	var node RaftNode
   969→	var peers []string
   970→
   971→	mset.mu.RLock()
   972→	js := mset.js
   973→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
   974→
   975→	if js != nil {
   976→		js.mu.RLock()
   977→		if sa.Group != nil {
   978→			node = sa.Group.node
   979→			peers = sa.Group.Peers
   980→		}
   981→		js.mu.RUnlock()
   982→	}
   983→
   984→	mset.mu.Lock()
   985→	defer mset.mu.Unlock()
   986→
   987→	mset.sa = sa
   988→	if sa == nil {
   989→		return
   990→	}
   991→
   992→	// Set our node.
   993→	mset.node = node
   994→	if mset.node != nil {
   995→		mset.node.UpdateKnownPeers(peers)
   996→	}
   997→
   998→	// Setup our info sub here as well for all stream members. This is now by design.
   999→	if mset.infoSub == nil {
  1000→		isubj := fmt.Sprintf(clusterStreamInfoT, mset.jsa.acc(), mset.cfg.Name)
  1001→		// Note below the way we subscribe here is so that we can send requests to ourselves.
  1002→		mset.infoSub, _ = mset.srv.systemSubscribe(isubj, _EMPTY_, false, mset.sysc, mset.handleClusterStreamInfoRequest)
  1003→	}
  1004→
  1005→	// Trigger update chan.
  1006→	select {
  1007→	case mset.uch <- struct{}{}:
  1008→	default:
  1009→	}
  1010→}
  1011→

  1017→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  1018→	return mset.mqch
  1019→}

  1030→// IsLeader will return if we are the current leader.
  1031→func (mset *stream) IsLeader() bool {
  1032→	mset.mu.RLock()
  1033→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  1034→	return mset.isLeader()
  1035→}
  1036→
  1037→// Lock should be held.
  1038→func (mset *stream) isLeader() bool {
  1039→	if mset.isClustered() {
  1040→		return mset.node.Leader()
  1041→	}
  1042→	return true
  1043→}
  1044→
  1045→// isLeaderNodeState should NOT be used normally, use isLeader instead.
  1046→// Returns whether the node thinks it is the leader, regardless of whether applies are up-to-date yet
  1047→// (unlike isLeader, which requires applies to be caught up).
  1048→// May be used to respond to clients after a leader change, when applying entries from a former leader.
  1049→// Lock should be held.
  1050→func (mset *stream) isLeaderNodeState() bool {
  1051→	if mset.isClustered() {
  1052→		return mset.node.State() == Leader
  1053→	}
  1054→	return true
  1055→}
  1056→
  1057→// TODO(dlc) - Check to see if we can accept being the leader or we should step down.
  1058→func (mset *stream) setLeader(isLeader bool) error {
  1059→	mset.mu.Lock()
  1060→	// If we are here we have a change in leader status.
  1061→	if isLeader {
  1062→		// Make sure we are listening for sync requests.
  1063→		// TODO(dlc) - Original design was that all in sync members of the group would do DQ.
  1064→		if mset.isClustered() {
  1065→			mset.startClusterSubs()
  1066→		}
  1067→
  1068→		// Setup subscriptions if we were not already the leader.
  1069→		if err := mset.subscribeToStream(); err != nil {
  1070→			if mset.isClustered() {
  1071→				// Stepdown since we have an error.
  1072→				mset.node.StepDown()
  1073→			}
  1074→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  1075→			return err
  1076→		}
  1077→	} else {
  1078→		// cancel timer to create the source consumers if not fired yet
  1079→		if mset.sourcesConsumerSetup != nil {
  1080→			mset.sourcesConsumerSetup.Stop()
  1081→			mset.sourcesConsumerSetup = nil
  1082→		} else {
  1083→			// Stop any source consumers
  1084→			mset.stopSourceConsumers()
  1085→		}
  1086→
  1087→		// Stop responding to sync requests.
  1088→		mset.stopClusterSubs()
  1089→		// Unsubscribe from direct stream.
  1090→		mset.unsubscribeToStream(false, false)
  1091→		// Clear catchup state
  1092→		mset.clearAllCatchupPeers()
  1093→	}
  1094→	mset.store.ResetState()
  1095→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  1096→
  1097→	// If we are interest based make sure to check consumers.
  1098→	// This is to make sure we process any outstanding acks.
  1099→	mset.checkInterestState()
  1100→
  1101→	return nil
  1102→}

  1104→// Lock should be held.
  1105→func (mset *stream) startClusterSubs() {
  1106→	if mset.syncSub == nil {
  1107→		mset.syncSub, _ = mset.srv.systemSubscribe(mset.sa.Sync, _EMPTY_, false, mset.sysc, mset.handleClusterSyncRequest)
  1108→	}
  1109→}

  1111→// Lock should be held.
  1112→func (mset *stream) stopClusterSubs() {

  1114→		mset.srv.sysUnsubscribe(mset.syncSub)
  1115→		mset.syncSub = nil
  1116→	}

  1130→// Helper to determine the max msg size for this stream if file based.
  1131→func (mset *stream) maxMsgSize() uint64 {
  1132→	maxMsgSize := mset.cfg.MaxMsgSize
  1133→	if maxMsgSize <= 0 {
  1134→		// Pull from the account.
  1135→		if mset.jsa != nil {
  1136→			if acc := mset.jsa.acc(); acc != nil {
  1137→				acc.mu.RLock()
  1138→				maxMsgSize = acc.mpay
  1139→				acc.mu.RUnlock()
  1140→			}
  1141→		}
  1142→		// If all else fails use default.
  1143→		if maxMsgSize <= 0 {
  1144→			maxMsgSize = MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE
  1145→		}
  1146→	}
  1147→	// Now determine an estimation for the subjects etc.
  1148→	maxSubject := -1
  1149→	for _, subj := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  1150→		if subjectIsLiteral(subj) {
  1151→			if len(subj) > maxSubject {
  1152→				maxSubject = len(subj)
  1153→			}
  1154→		}
  1155→	}
  1156→	if maxSubject < 0 {
  1157→		const defaultMaxSubject = 256
  1158→		maxSubject = defaultMaxSubject
  1159→	}
  1160→	// filestore will add in estimates for record headers, etc.
  1161→	return fileStoreMsgSizeEstimate(maxSubject, int(maxMsgSize))
  1162→}
  1163→
  1164→// If we are file based and the file storage config was not explicitly set
  1165→// we can autotune block sizes to better match. Our target will be to store 125%
  1166→// of the theoretical limit. We will round up to nearest 100 bytes as well.
  1167→func (mset *stream) autoTuneFileStorageBlockSize(fsCfg *FileStoreConfig) {
  1168→	var totalEstSize uint64
  1169→
  1170→	// MaxBytes will take precedence for now.
  1171→	if mset.cfg.MaxBytes > 0 {
  1172→		totalEstSize = uint64(mset.cfg.MaxBytes)
  1173→	} else if mset.cfg.MaxMsgs > 0 {
  1174→		// Determine max message size to estimate.
  1175→		totalEstSize = mset.maxMsgSize() * uint64(mset.cfg.MaxMsgs)
  1176→	} else if mset.cfg.MaxMsgsPer > 0 {
  1177→		fsCfg.BlockSize = uint64(defaultKVBlockSize)
  1178→		return
  1179→	} else {
  1180→		// If nothing set will let underlying filestore determine blkSize.
  1181→		return
  1182→	}
  1183→
  1184→	blkSize := (totalEstSize / 4) + 1 // (25% overhead)
  1185→	// Round up to nearest 100
  1186→	if m := blkSize % 100; m != 0 {
  1187→		blkSize += 100 - m
  1188→	}
  1189→	if blkSize <= FileStoreMinBlkSize {
  1190→		blkSize = FileStoreMinBlkSize
  1191→	} else if blkSize >= FileStoreMaxBlkSize {
  1192→		blkSize = FileStoreMaxBlkSize
  1193→	} else {
  1194→		blkSize = defaultMediumBlockSize
  1195→	}
  1196→	fsCfg.BlockSize = uint64(blkSize)
  1197→}
  1198→
  1199→// rebuildDedupe will rebuild any dedupe structures needed after recovery of a stream.
  1200→// Will be called lazily to avoid penalizing startup times.
  1201→// TODO(dlc) - Might be good to know if this should be checked at all for streams with no
  1202→// headers and msgId in them. Would need signaling from the storage layer.
  1203→// mset.mu and mset.ddMu locks should be held.
  1204→func (mset *stream) rebuildDedupe() {
  1205→	// We have some messages. Lookup starting sequence by duplicate time window.
  1206→	sseq := mset.store.GetSeqFromTime(time.Now().Add(-mset.cfg.Duplicates))
  1207→	if sseq == 0 {
  1208→		return
  1209→	}
  1210→
  1211→	var smv StoreMsg
  1212→	var state StreamState
  1213→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
  1214→
  1215→	for seq := sseq; seq <= state.LastSeq; seq++ {
  1216→		sm, err := mset.store.LoadMsg(seq, &smv)
  1217→		if err != nil {
  1218→			continue
  1219→		}
  1220→		var msgId string
  1221→		if len(sm.hdr) > 0 {
  1222→			if msgId = getMsgId(sm.hdr); msgId != _EMPTY_ {
  1223→				mset.storeMsgIdLocked(&ddentry{msgId, sm.seq, sm.ts})
  1224→			}
  1225→		}
  1226→		if seq == state.LastSeq {
  1227→			mset.lmsgId = msgId
  1228→		}
  1229→	}
  1230→}

  1263→func (mset *stream) sendCreateAdvisory() {

  1266→	template := mset.cfg.Template
  1267→	outq := mset.outq
  1268→	srv := mset.srv
  1269→	mset.mu.RUnlock()

  1273→	}
  1274→
  1275→	// finally send an event that this stream was created
  1276→	m := JSStreamActionAdvisory{
  1277→		TypedEvent: TypedEvent{
  1278→			Type: JSStreamActionAdvisoryType,
  1279→			ID:   nuid.Next(),
  1280→			Time: time.Now().UTC(),
  1281→		},
  1282→		Stream:   name,
  1283→		Action:   CreateEvent,
  1284→		Template: template,
  1285→		Domain:   srv.getOpts().JetStreamDomain,

  1288→	j, err := json.Marshal(m)
  1289→	if err != nil {
  1290→		return
  1291→	}
  1292→
  1293→	subj := JSAdvisoryStreamCreatedPre + "." + name
  1294→	outq.sendMsg(subj, j)
  1295→}
