  1359→// subjectsOverlap to see if these subjects overlap with existing subjects.
  1360→// Use only for non-clustered JetStream
  1361→// RLock minimum should be held.
  1362→func (jsa *jsAccount) subjectsOverlap(subjects []string, self *stream) bool {
  1363→	for _, mset := range jsa.streams {
  1364→		if self != nil && mset == self {
  1365→			continue
  1366→		}
  1367→		for _, subj := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  1368→			for _, tsubj := range subjects {
  1369→				if SubjectsCollide(tsubj, subj) {
  1370→					return true
  1371→				}
  1372→			}
  1373→		}
  1374→	}
  1375→	return false
  1376→}
  1377→
  1378→// StreamDefaultDuplicatesWindow default duplicates window.
  1379→const StreamDefaultDuplicatesWindow = 2 * time.Minute
  1380→
  1381→func (s *Server) checkStreamCfg(config *StreamConfig, acc *Account, pedantic bool) (StreamConfig, *ApiError) {
  1382→	lim := &s.getOpts().JetStreamLimits
  1383→
  1384→	if config == nil {
  1385→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration invalid"))
  1386→	}
  1387→	if !isValidName(config.Name) {
  1388→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream name is required and can not contain '.', '*', '>'"))
  1389→	}
  1390→	if len(config.Name) > JSMaxNameLen {
  1391→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream name is too long, maximum allowed is %d", JSMaxNameLen))
  1392→	}
  1393→	if len(config.Description) > JSMaxDescriptionLen {
  1394→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream description is too long, maximum allowed is %d", JSMaxDescriptionLen))
  1395→	}
  1396→
  1397→	var metadataLen int
  1398→	for k, v := range config.Metadata {
  1399→		metadataLen += len(k) + len(v)
  1400→	}
  1401→	if metadataLen > JSMaxMetadataLen {
  1402→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream metadata exceeds maximum size of %d bytes", JSMaxMetadataLen))
  1403→	}
  1404→
  1405→	cfg := *config
  1406→
  1407→	if _, err := cfg.Retention.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
  1408→		return cfg, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("invalid retention"))
  1409→	}
  1410→	if _, err := cfg.Discard.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
  1411→		return cfg, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("invalid discard policy"))
  1412→	}
  1413→	if _, err := cfg.Compression.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
  1414→		return cfg, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("invalid compression"))
  1415→	}
  1416→
  1417→	// Make file the default.
  1418→	if cfg.Storage == 0 {
  1419→		cfg.Storage = FileStorage
  1420→	}
  1421→	if _, err := cfg.Storage.MarshalJSON(); err != nil {
  1422→		return cfg, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("invalid storage type"))
  1423→	}
  1424→
  1425→	if cfg.Replicas == 0 {
  1426→		cfg.Replicas = 1
  1427→	}
  1428→	if cfg.Replicas > StreamMaxReplicas {
  1429→		return cfg, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("maximum replicas is %d", StreamMaxReplicas))
  1430→	}
  1431→	if cfg.Replicas < 0 {
  1432→		return cfg, NewJSReplicasCountCannotBeNegativeError()
  1433→	}
  1434→	if cfg.MaxMsgs == 0 || cfg.MaxMsgs < -1 {
  1435→		if pedantic && cfg.MaxMsgs < -1 {
  1436→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("max_msgs must be set to -1"))
  1437→		}
  1438→		cfg.MaxMsgs = -1
  1439→	}
  1440→	if cfg.MaxMsgsPer == 0 || cfg.MaxMsgsPer < -1 {
  1441→		if pedantic && cfg.MaxMsgsPer < -1 {
  1442→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("max_msgs_per_subject must be set to -1"))
  1443→		}
  1444→		cfg.MaxMsgsPer = -1
  1445→	}
  1446→	if cfg.MaxBytes == 0 || cfg.MaxBytes < -1 {
  1447→		if pedantic && cfg.MaxBytes < -1 {
  1448→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("max_bytes must be set to -1"))
  1449→		}
  1450→		cfg.MaxBytes = -1
  1451→	}
  1452→	if cfg.MaxMsgSize == 0 || cfg.MaxMsgSize < -1 {
  1453→		if pedantic && cfg.MaxMsgSize < -1 {
  1454→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("max_msg_size must be set to -1"))
  1455→		}
  1456→		cfg.MaxMsgSize = -1
  1457→	}
  1458→	if cfg.MaxConsumers == 0 || cfg.MaxConsumers < -1 {
  1459→		if pedantic && cfg.MaxConsumers < -1 {
  1460→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("max_consumers must be set to -1"))
  1461→		}
  1462→		cfg.MaxConsumers = -1
  1463→	}
  1464→	if cfg.MaxAge < 0 {
  1465→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("max age can not be negative"))
  1466→	}
  1467→	if cfg.MaxAge != 0 && cfg.MaxAge < 100*time.Millisecond {
  1468→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("max age needs to be >= 100ms"))
  1469→	}
  1470→	if cfg.Duplicates == 0 && cfg.Mirror == nil {
  1471→		maxWindow := StreamDefaultDuplicatesWindow
  1472→		if lim.Duplicates > 0 && maxWindow > lim.Duplicates {
  1473→			if pedantic {
  1474→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate window limits are higher than current limits"))
  1475→			}
  1476→			maxWindow = lim.Duplicates
  1477→		}
  1478→		if cfg.MaxAge != 0 && cfg.MaxAge < maxWindow {
  1479→			if pedantic {
  1480→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate window cannot be bigger than max age"))
  1481→			}
  1482→			cfg.Duplicates = cfg.MaxAge
  1483→		} else {
  1484→			cfg.Duplicates = maxWindow
  1485→		}
  1486→	}
  1487→	if cfg.Duplicates < 0 {
  1488→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("duplicates window can not be negative"))
  1489	}
  1490→	// Check that duplicates is not larger then age if set.
  1491→	if cfg.MaxAge != 0 && cfg.Duplicates > cfg.MaxAge {
  1492→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("duplicates window can not be larger then max age"))
  1493→	}
  1494→	if lim.Duplicates > 0 && cfg.Duplicates > lim.Duplicates {
  1495→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("duplicates window can not be larger then server limit of %v",
  1496→			lim.Duplicates.String()))
  1497→	}
  1498→	if cfg.Duplicates > 0 && cfg.Duplicates < 100*time.Millisecond {
  1499→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("duplicates window needs to be >= 100ms"))
  1500→	}
  1501→
  1502→	if cfg.DenyPurge && cfg.AllowRollup {
  1503→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("roll-ups require the purge permission"))
  1504→	}
  1505→
  1506→	// Counter is not compatible with some settings.
  1507→	if cfg.AllowMsgCounter {
  1508→		if cfg.Discard == DiscardNew {
  1509→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("counter stream cannot use discard new"))
  1510→		}
  1511→		if cfg.AllowMsgTTL {
  1512→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("counter stream cannot use message TTLs"))
  1513→		}
  1514→		if cfg.Retention != LimitsPolicy {
  1515→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("counter stream can only use limits retention"))
  1516→		}
  1517→	}
  1518→
  1519→	// Check for new discard new per subject, we require the discard policy to also be new.
  1520→	if cfg.DiscardNewPer {
  1521→		if cfg.Discard != DiscardNew {
  1522→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("discard new per subject requires discard new policy to be set"))
  1523→		}
  1524→		if cfg.MaxMsgsPer <= 0 {
  1525→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("discard new per subject requires max msgs per subject > 0"))
  1526→		}
  1527→	}
  1528→
  1529→	if cfg.SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL > 0 {
  1530→		if cfg.SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL < time.Second {
  1531→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject delete marker TTL must be at least 1 second"))
  1532→		}
  1533→		if !cfg.AllowMsgTTL {
  1534→			if pedantic {
  1535→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject delete marker cannot be set if message TTLs are disabled"))
  1536→			}
  1537→			cfg.AllowMsgTTL = true
  1538→		}
  1539→		if !cfg.AllowRollup {
  1540→			if pedantic {
  1541→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject delete marker cannot be set if roll-ups are disabled"))
  1542→			}
  1543→			cfg.AllowRollup, cfg.DenyPurge = true, false
  1544→		}
  1545→	} else if cfg.SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL < 0 {
  1546→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject delete marker TTL must not be negative"))
  1547→	}
  1548→
  1549→	if cfg.AllowMsgSchedules {
  1550→		if !cfg.AllowRollup {
  1551→			if pedantic {
  1552→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("message scheduling cannot be set if roll-ups are disabled"))
  1553→			}
  1554→			cfg.AllowRollup, cfg.DenyPurge = true, false
  1555→		}
  1556→	}
  1557→
  1558→	getStream := func(streamName string) (bool, StreamConfig) {
  1559→		var exists bool
  1560→		var cfg StreamConfig
  1561→		if s.JetStreamIsClustered() {
  1562→			if js, _ := s.getJetStreamCluster(); js != nil {
  1563→				js.mu.RLock()
  1564→				if sa := js.streamAssignment(acc.Name, streamName); sa != nil {
  1565→					cfg = *sa.Config.clone()
  1566→					exists = true
  1567→				}
  1568→				js.mu.RUnlock()
  1569→			}
  1570→		} else if mset, err := acc.lookupStream(streamName); err == nil {
  1571→			cfg = mset.cfg
  1572→			exists = true
  1573→		}
  1574→		return exists, cfg
  1575→	}
  1576→
  1577→	hasStream := func(streamName string) (bool, int32, []string) {
  1578→		exists, cfg := getStream(streamName)
  1579→		return exists, cfg.MaxMsgSize, cfg.Subjects
  1580→	}
  1581→
  1582→	var streamSubs []string
  1583→	var deliveryPrefixes []string
  1584→	var apiPrefixes []string
  1585→
  1586→	// Do some pre-checking for mirror config to avoid cycles in clustered mode.
  1587→	if cfg.Mirror != nil {
  1588→		if cfg.FirstSeq > 0 {
  1589→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithFirstSeqError()
  1590→		}
  1591→		if len(cfg.Subjects) > 0 {
  1592→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithSubjectsError()
  1593→		}
  1594→		if len(cfg.Sources) > 0 {
  1595→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithSourcesError()
  1596→		}
  1597→		if cfg.AllowMsgCounter {
  1598→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithCountersError()
  1599→		}
  1600→		if cfg.AllowMsgSchedules {
  1601→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithMsgSchedulesError()
  1602→		}
  1603→		if cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && len(cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms) != 0 {
  1604→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorMultipleFiltersNotAllowedError()
  1605→		}
  1606→		if cfg.SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL > 0 {
  1607→			// Delete markers cannot be configured on a mirror as it would result in new
  1608→			// tombstones which would use up sequence numbers, diverging from the origin
  1609→			// stream.
  1610→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject delete markers forbidden on mirrors"))
  1611→		}
  1612→		// Check subject filters overlap.
  1613→		for outer, tr := range cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms {
  1614→			if tr.Source != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(tr.Source) {
  1615→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorInvalidSubjectFilterError(fmt.Errorf("%w %s", ErrBadSubject, tr.Source))
  1616→			}
  1617→
  1618→			err := ValidateMapping(tr.Source, tr.Destination)
  1619→			if err != nil {
  1620→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorInvalidTransformDestinationError(err)
  1621→			}
  1622→
  1623→			for inner, innertr := range cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms {
  1624→				if inner != outer && SubjectsCollide(tr.Source, innertr.Source) {
  1625→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorOverlappingSubjectFiltersError()
  1626→				}
  1627→			}
  1628→		}
  1629→		// Do not perform checks if External is provided, as it could lead to
  1630→		// checking against itself (if sourced stream name is the same on different JetStream)
  1631→		if cfg.Mirror.External == nil {
  1632→			if !isValidName(cfg.Mirror.Name) {
  1633→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorInvalidStreamNameError()
  1634→			}
  1635→			// We do not require other stream to exist anymore, but if we can see it check payloads.
  1636→			exists, maxMsgSize, subs := hasStream(cfg.Mirror.Name)
  1637→			if len(subs) > 0 {
  1638→				streamSubs = append(streamSubs, subs...)
  1639→			}
  1640→			if exists {
  1641→				if cfg.MaxMsgSize > 0 && maxMsgSize > 0 && cfg.MaxMsgSize < maxMsgSize {
  1642→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorMaxMessageSizeTooBigError()
  1643→				}
  1644→			}
  1645→			// Determine if we are inheriting direct gets.
  1646→			if exists, ocfg := getStream(cfg.Mirror.Name); exists {
  1647→				if pedantic && cfg.MirrorDirect != ocfg.AllowDirect {
  1648→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("origin stream has direct get set, mirror has it disabled"))
  1649→				}
  1650→				cfg.MirrorDirect = ocfg.AllowDirect
  1651→			} else if js := s.getJetStream(); js != nil && js.isClustered() {
  1652→				// Could not find it here. If we are clustered we can look it up.
  1653→				js.mu.RLock()
  1654→				if cc := js.cluster; cc != nil {
  1655→					if as := cc.streams[acc.Name]; as != nil {
  1656→						if sa := as[cfg.Mirror.Name]; sa != nil {
  1657→							if pedantic && cfg.MirrorDirect != sa.Config.AllowDirect {
  1658→								js.mu.RUnlock()
  1659→								return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("origin stream has direct get set, mirror has it disabled"))
  1660→							}
  1661→							cfg.MirrorDirect = sa.Config.AllowDirect
  1662→						}
  1663→					}
  1664→				}
  1665→				js.mu.RUnlock()
  1666→			}
  1667→		} else {
  1668→			if cfg.Mirror.External.DeliverPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  1669→				deliveryPrefixes = append(deliveryPrefixes, cfg.Mirror.External.DeliverPrefix)
  1670→			}
  1671→
  1672→			if cfg.Mirror.External.ApiPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  1673→				apiPrefixes = append(apiPrefixes, cfg.Mirror.External.ApiPrefix)
  1674→			}
  1675→		}
  1676→	}
  1677→
  1678→	if len(cfg.Sources) > 0 {
  1679→		if cfg.AllowMsgSchedules {
  1680→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceWithMsgSchedulesError()
  1681→		}
  1682→	}
  1683→
  1684→	// check for duplicates
  1685→	var iNames = make(map[string]struct{})
  1686→	for _, src := range cfg.Sources {
  1687→		if !isValidName(src.Name) {
  1688→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceInvalidStreamNameError()
  1689→		}
  1690→		if _, ok := iNames[src.composeIName()]; !ok {
  1691→			iNames[src.composeIName()] = struct{}{}
  1692→		} else {
  1693→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceDuplicateDetectedError()
  1694→		}
  1695→		// Do not perform checks if External is provided, as it could lead to
  1696→		// checking against itself (if sourced stream name is the same on different JetStream)
  1697→		if src.External == nil {
  1698→			exists, maxMsgSize, subs := hasStream(src.Name)
  1699→			if len(subs) > 0 {
  1700→				streamSubs = append(streamSubs, subs...)
  1701→			}
  1702→			if exists {
  1703→				if cfg.MaxMsgSize > 0 && maxMsgSize > 0 && cfg.MaxMsgSize < maxMsgSize {
  1704→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceMaxMessageSizeTooBigError()
  1705→				}
  1706→			}
  1707→
  1708→			if src.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ && len(src.SubjectTransforms) != 0 {
  1709→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceMultipleFiltersNotAllowedError()
  1710→			}
  1711→
  1712→			for _, tr := range src.SubjectTransforms {
  1713→				if tr.Source != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(tr.Source) {
  1714→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceInvalidSubjectFilterError(fmt.Errorf("%w %s", ErrBadSubject, tr.Source))
  1715→				}
  1716→
  1717→				err := ValidateMapping(tr.Source, tr.Destination)
  1718→				if err != nil {
  1719→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceInvalidTransformDestinationError(err)
  1720→				}
  1721→			}
  1722→
  1723→			// Check subject filters overlap.
  1724→			for outer, tr := range src.SubjectTransforms {
  1725→				for inner, innertr := range src.SubjectTransforms {
  1726→					if inner != outer && subjectIsSubsetMatch(tr.Source, innertr.Source) {
  1727→						return StreamConfig{}, NewJSSourceOverlappingSubjectFiltersError()
  1728→					}
  1729→				}
  1730→			}
  1731→			continue
  1732→		} else {
  1733→			if src.External.DeliverPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  1734→				deliveryPrefixes = append(deliveryPrefixes, src.External.DeliverPrefix)
  1735→			}
  1736→			if src.External.ApiPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  1737→				apiPrefixes = append(apiPrefixes, src.External.ApiPrefix)
  1738→			}
  1739→		}
  1740→	}
  1741→
  1742→	// check prefix overlap with subjects
  1743→	for _, pfx := range deliveryPrefixes {
  1744→		if !IsValidPublishSubject(pfx) {
  1745→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidExternalDeliverySubjError(pfx)
  1746→		}
  1747→		for _, sub := range streamSubs {
  1748→			if SubjectsCollide(sub, fmt.Sprintf("%s.%s", pfx, sub)) {
  1749→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamExternalDelPrefixOverlapsError(pfx, sub)
  1750→			}
  1751→		}
  1752→	}
  1753→	// check if api prefixes overlap
  1754→	for _, apiPfx := range apiPrefixes {
  1755→		if !IsValidPublishSubject(apiPfx) {
  1756→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(
  1757→				fmt.Errorf("stream external api prefix %q must be a valid subject without wildcards", apiPfx))
  1758→		}
  1759→		if SubjectsCollide(apiPfx, JSApiPrefix) {
  1760→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamExternalApiOverlapError(apiPfx, JSApiPrefix)
  1761→		}
  1762→	}
  1763→
  1764→	// cycle check for source cycle
  1765→	toVisit := []*StreamConfig{&cfg}
  1766→	visited := make(map[string]struct{})
  1767→	overlaps := func(subjects []string, filter string) bool {
  1768→		if filter == _EMPTY_ {
  1769→			return true
  1770→		}
  1771→		for _, subject := range subjects {
  1772→			if SubjectsCollide(subject, filter) {
  1773→				return true
  1774→			}
  1775→		}
  1776→		return false
  1777→	}
  1778→
  1779→	for len(toVisit) > 0 {
  1780→		cfg := toVisit[0]
  1781→		toVisit = toVisit[1:]
  1782→		visited[cfg.Name] = struct{}{}
  1783→		for _, src := range cfg.Sources {
  1784→			if src.External != nil {
  1785→				continue
  1786→			}
  1787→			// We can detect a cycle between streams, but let's double check that the
  1788→			// subjects actually form a cycle.
  1789→			if _, ok := visited[src.Name]; ok {
  1790→				if overlaps(cfg.Subjects, src.FilterSubject) {
  1791→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(errors.New("detected cycle"))
  1792→				}
  1793→			} else if exists, cfg := getStream(src.Name); exists {
  1794→				toVisit = append(toVisit, &cfg)
  1795→			}
  1796→		}
  1797→		// Avoid cycles hiding behind mirrors
  1798→		if m := cfg.Mirror; m != nil {
  1799→			if m.External == nil {
  1800→				if _, ok := visited[m.Name]; ok {
  1801→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(errors.New("detected cycle"))
  1802→				}
  1803→				if exists, cfg := getStream(m.Name); exists {
  1804→					toVisit = append(toVisit, &cfg)
  1805→				}
  1806→			}
  1807→		}
  1808→	}
  1809→
  1810→	if len(cfg.Subjects) == 0 {
  1811→		if cfg.Mirror == nil && len(cfg.Sources) == 0 {
  1812→			cfg.Subjects = append(cfg.Subjects, cfg.Name)
  1813→		}
  1814→	} else {
  1815→		if cfg.Mirror != nil {
  1816→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSMirrorWithSubjectsError()
  1817→		}
  1818→
  1819→		// Check for literal duplication of subject interest in config
  1820→		// and no overlap with any JS or SYS API subject space.
  1821→		dset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(cfg.Subjects))
  1822→		for _, subj := range cfg.Subjects {
  1823→			// Make sure the subject is valid. Check this first.
  1824→			if !IsValidSubject(subj) {
  1825→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("invalid subject"))
  1826→			}
  1827→			if _, ok := dset[subj]; ok {
  1828→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("duplicate subjects detected"))
  1829→			}
  1830→			// Check for trying to capture everything.
  1831→			if subj == fwcs {
  1832→				if !cfg.NoAck {
  1833→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("capturing all subjects requires no-ack to be true"))
  1834→				}
  1835→				// Capturing everything also will require R1.
  1836→				if cfg.Replicas != 1 {
  1837→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("capturing all subjects requires replicas of 1"))
  1838→				}
  1839→			}
  1840→			// Also check to make sure we do not overlap with our $JS API subjects.
  1841→			if !cfg.NoAck {
  1842→				for _, namespace := range []string{"$JS.>", "$JSC.>", "$NRG.>"} {
  1843→					if SubjectsCollide(subj, namespace) {
  1844→						// We allow an exception for $JS.EVENT.> since these could have been created in the past.
  1845→						if !subjectIsSubsetMatch(subj, "$JS.EVENT.>") {
  1846→							return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subjects that overlap with jetstream api require no-ack to be true"))
  1847→						}
  1848→					}
  1849→				}
  1850→				if SubjectsCollide(subj, "$SYS.>") {
  1851→					if !subjectIsSubsetMatch(subj, "$SYS.ACCOUNT.>") {
  1852→						return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subjects that overlap with system api require no-ack to be true"))
  1853→					}
  1854→				}
  1855→			}
  1856→			// Mark for duplicate check.
  1857→			dset[subj] = struct{}{}
  1858→		}
  1859→	}
  1860→
  1861→	if len(cfg.Subjects) == 0 && len(cfg.Sources) == 0 && cfg.Mirror == nil {
  1862→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(
  1863→			fmt.Errorf("stream needs at least one configured subject or be a source/mirror"))
  1864→	}
  1865→
  1866→	// Check for MaxBytes required and it's limit
  1867→	if required, limit := acc.maxBytesLimits(&cfg); required && cfg.MaxBytes <= 0 {
  1868→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamMaxBytesRequiredError()
  1869→	} else if limit > 0 && cfg.MaxBytes > limit {
  1870→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamMaxStreamBytesExceededError()
  1871→	}
  1872→
  1873→	// Now check if we have multiple subjects they we do not overlap ourselves
  1874→	// which would cause duplicate entries (assuming no MsgID).
  1875→	if len(cfg.Subjects) > 1 {
  1876→		for _, subj := range cfg.Subjects {
  1877→			for _, tsubj := range cfg.Subjects {
  1878→				if tsubj != subj && SubjectsCollide(tsubj, subj) {
  1879→					return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("subject %q overlaps with %q", subj, tsubj))
  1880→				}
  1881→			}
  1882→		}
  1883→	}
  1884→
  1885→	// If we have a republish directive check if we can create a transform here.
  1886→	if cfg.RePublish != nil {
  1887→		// Check to make sure source is a valid subset of the subjects we have.
  1888→		// Also make sure it does not form a cycle.
  1889→		// Empty same as all.
  1890→		if cfg.RePublish.Source == _EMPTY_ {
  1891→			if pedantic {
  1892→				return StreamConfig{}, NewJSPedanticError(fmt.Errorf("republish source can not be empty"))
  1893→			}
  1894→			cfg.RePublish.Source = fwcs
  1895→		}
  1896→		var formsCycle bool
  1897→		for _, subj := range cfg.Subjects {
  1898→			if SubjectsCollide(cfg.RePublish.Destination, subj) {
  1899→				formsCycle = true
  1900→				break
  1901→			}
  1902→		}
  1903→		if formsCycle {
  1904→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration for republish destination forms a cycle"))
  1905→		}
  1906→		if _, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination); err != nil {
  1907→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration for republish with transform from '%s' to '%s' not valid", cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination))
  1908→		}
  1909→	}
  1910→
  1911→	// Check the subject transform if any
  1912→	if cfg.SubjectTransform != nil {
  1913→		if cfg.SubjectTransform.Source != _EMPTY_ && !IsValidSubject(cfg.SubjectTransform.Source) {
  1914→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamTransformInvalidSourceError(fmt.Errorf("%w %s", ErrBadSubject, cfg.SubjectTransform.Source))
  1915→		}
  1916→
  1917→		err := ValidateMapping(cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination)
  1918→		if err != nil {
  1919→			return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamTransformInvalidDestinationError(err)
  1920→		}
  1921→	}
  1922→
  1923→	// For now don't allow preferred server in placement.
  1924→	if cfg.Placement != nil && cfg.Placement.Preferred != _EMPTY_ {
  1925→		return StreamConfig{}, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("preferred server not permitted in placement"))
  1926→	}
  1927→
  1928→	return cfg, nil
  1929→}
  1930→
  1931→// Config returns the stream's configuration.
  1932→func (mset *stream) config() StreamConfig {
  1933→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  1934→	defer mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  1935→	return mset.cfg
  1936→}
  1937→
  1938→func (mset *stream) fileStoreConfig() (FileStoreConfig, error) {
  1939→	mset.mu.Lock()
  1940→	defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  1941→	fs, ok := mset.store.(*fileStore)
  1942→	if !ok {
  1943→		return FileStoreConfig{}, ErrStoreWrongType
  1944→	}
  1945→	return fs.fileStoreConfig(), nil
  1946→}
  1947→
  1948→// Do not hold jsAccount or jetStream lock
  1949→func (jsa *jsAccount) configUpdateCheck(old, new *StreamConfig, s *Server, pedantic bool) (*StreamConfig, error) {
  1950→	cfg, apiErr := s.checkStreamCfg(new, jsa.acc(), pedantic)
  1951→	if apiErr != nil {
  1952→		return nil, apiErr
  1953→	}
  1954→
  1955→	// Name must match.
  1956→	if cfg.Name != old.Name {
  1957→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration name must match original"))
  1958→	}
  1959→	// Can't change MaxConsumers for now.
  1960→	if cfg.MaxConsumers != old.MaxConsumers {
  1961→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not change MaxConsumers"))
  1962→	}
  1963→	// Can't change storage types.
  1964→	if cfg.Storage != old.Storage {
  1965→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not change storage type"))
  1966→	}
  1967→	// Can only change retention from limits to interest or back, not to/from work queue for now.
  1968→	if cfg.Retention != old.Retention {
  1969→		if old.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy || cfg.Retention == WorkQueuePolicy {
  1970→			return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not change retention policy to/from workqueue"))
  1971→		}
  1972→	}
  1973→	// Can not have a template owner for now.
  1974→	if old.Template != _EMPTY_ {
  1975→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update not allowed on template owned stream"))
  1976→	}
  1977→	if cfg.Template != _EMPTY_ {
  1978→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not be owned by a template"))
  1979→	}
  1980→	// Can not change from true to false.
  1981→	if !cfg.Sealed && old.Sealed {
  1982→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not unseal a sealed stream"))
  1983→	}
  1984→	// Can not change from true to false.
  1985→	if !cfg.DenyDelete && old.DenyDelete {
  1986→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not cancel deny message deletes"))
  1987→	}
  1988→	// Can not change from true to false.
  1989→	if !cfg.DenyPurge && old.DenyPurge {
  1990→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not cancel deny purge"))
  1991→	}
  1992→	// Check for mirror changes which are not allowed.
  1993→	// We will allow removing the mirror config to "promote" the mirror to a normal stream.
  1994→	if cfg.Mirror != nil && !reflect.DeepEqual(cfg.Mirror, old.Mirror) {
  1995→		return nil, NewJSStreamMirrorNotUpdatableError()
  1996→	}
  1997→
  1998→	// Check on new discard new per subject.
  1999→	if cfg.DiscardNewPer {
  2000→		if cfg.Discard != DiscardNew {
  2001→			return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("discard new per subject requires discard new policy to be set"))
  2002→		}
  2003→		if cfg.MaxMsgsPer <= 0 {
  2004→			return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("discard new per subject requires max msgs per subject > 0"))
  2005→		}
  2006→	}
  2007→
  2008→	// Check on the allowed message TTL status.
  2009→	if old.AllowMsgTTL && !cfg.AllowMsgTTL {
  2010→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("message TTL status can not be disabled"))
  2011→	}
  2012→
  2013→	// Can't change counter setting.
  2014→	if cfg.AllowMsgCounter != old.AllowMsgCounter {
  2015→		return nil, NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(fmt.Errorf("stream configuration update can not change message counter setting"))
  2016→	}
  2017→
  2018→	// Do some adjustments for being sealed.
  2019→	// Pedantic mode will allow those changes to be made, as they are deterministic and important to get a sealed stream.
  2020→	if cfg.Sealed {
  2021→		cfg.MaxAge = 0
  2022→		cfg.Discard = DiscardNew
  2023→		cfg.DenyDelete, cfg.DenyPurge = true, true
  2024→		cfg.AllowRollup = false
  2025→	}
  2026→
  2027→	// Check limits. We need some extra handling to allow updating MaxBytes.
  2028→
  2029→	// First, let's calculate the difference between the new and old MaxBytes.
  2030→	maxBytesDiff := max(cfg.MaxBytes, 0) - max(old.MaxBytes, 0)
  2031→	if maxBytesDiff < 0 {
  2032→		// If we're updating to a lower MaxBytes (maxBytesDiff is negative),
  2033→		// then set to zero so checkBytesLimits doesn't set addBytes to 1.
  2034→		maxBytesDiff = 0
  2035→	}
  2036→	// If maxBytesDiff == 0, then that means MaxBytes didn't change.
  2037→	// If maxBytesDiff > 0, then we want to reserve additional bytes.
  2038→
  2039→	// Save the user configured MaxBytes.
  2040→	newMaxBytes := cfg.MaxBytes
  2041→	maxBytesOffset := int64(0)
  2042→
  2043→	// We temporarily set cfg.MaxBytes to maxBytesDiff because checkAllLimits
  2044→	// adds cfg.MaxBytes to the current reserved limit and checks if we've gone
  2045→	// over. However, we don't want an addition cfg.MaxBytes, we only want to
  2046→	// reserve the difference between the new and the old values.
  2047→	cfg.MaxBytes = maxBytesDiff
  2048→
  2049→	// Check limits.
  2050→	js, isClustered := jsa.jetStreamAndClustered()
  2051→	jsa.mu.RLock()
  2052→	acc := jsa.account
  2053→	jsa.usageMu.RLock()
  2054→	selected, tier, hasTier := jsa.selectLimits(cfg.Replicas)
  2055→	if !hasTier && old.Replicas != cfg.Replicas {
  2056→		selected, tier, hasTier = jsa.selectLimits(old.Replicas)
  2057→	}
  2058→	jsa.usageMu.RUnlock()
  2059→	reserved := int64(0)
  2060→	if !isClustered {
  2061→		reserved = jsa.tieredReservation(tier, &cfg)
  2062→	}
  2063→	jsa.mu.RUnlock()
  2064→	if !hasTier {
  2065→		return nil, NewJSNoLimitsError()
  2066→	}
  2067→	js.mu.RLock()
  2068→	defer js.mu.RUnlock()
  2069→	if isClustered {
  2070→		_, reserved = tieredStreamAndReservationCount(js.cluster.streams[acc.Name], tier, &cfg)
  2071→	}
  2072→	// reservation does not account for this stream, hence add the old value
  2073→	if tier == _EMPTY_ && old.Replicas > 1 {
  2074→		reserved += old.MaxBytes * int64(old.Replicas)
  2075→	} else {
  2076→		reserved += old.MaxBytes
  2077→	}
  2078→	if err := js.checkAllLimits(&selected, &cfg, reserved, maxBytesOffset); err != nil {
  2079→		return nil, err
  2080→	}
  2081→	// Restore the user configured MaxBytes.
  2082→	cfg.MaxBytes = newMaxBytes
  2083→	return &cfg, nil
  2084→}
  2085→
  2086→// Update will allow certain configuration properties of an existing stream to be updated.
  2087→func (mset *stream) update(config *StreamConfig) error {
  2088→	return mset.updateWithAdvisory(config, true, false)
  2089→}
  2090→
  2091→func (mset *stream) updatePedantic(config *StreamConfig, pedantic bool) error {
  2092→	return mset.updateWithAdvisory(config, true, pedantic)
  2093→}
  2094→
  2095→// Update will allow certain configuration properties of an existing stream to be updated.
  2096→func (mset *stream) updateWithAdvisory(config *StreamConfig, sendAdvisory bool, pedantic bool) error {
  2097→	_, jsa, err := mset.acc.checkForJetStream()
  2098→	if err != nil {
  2099→		return err
  2100→	}
  2101→
  2102→	mset.mu.RLock()
  2103→	ocfg := mset.cfg
  2104→	s := mset.srv
  2105→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2106→
  2107→	cfg, err := mset.jsa.configUpdateCheck(&ocfg, config, s, pedantic)
  2108→	if err != nil {
  2109→		return NewJSStreamInvalidConfigError(err, Unless(err))
  2110→	}
  2111→
  2112→	// In the event that some of the stream-level limits have changed, yell appropriately
  2113→	// if any of the consumers exceed that limit.
  2114→	updateLimits := ocfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold != cfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold ||
  2115→		ocfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending != cfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending
  2116→	if updateLimits {
  2117→		var errorConsumers []string
  2118→		consumers := map[string]*ConsumerConfig{}
  2119→		if mset.js.isClustered() {
  2120→			for _, c := range mset.sa.consumers {
  2121→				consumers[c.Name] = c.Config
  2122→			}
  2123→		} else {
  2124→			for _, c := range mset.consumers {
  2125→				consumers[c.name] = &c.cfg
  2126→			}
  2127→		}
  2128→		for name, ccfg := range consumers {
  2129→			if ccfg.InactiveThreshold > cfg.ConsumerLimits.InactiveThreshold ||
  2130→				ccfg.MaxAckPending > cfg.ConsumerLimits.MaxAckPending {
  2131→				errorConsumers = append(errorConsumers, name)
  2132→			}
  2133→		}
  2134→		if len(errorConsumers) > 0 {
  2135→			// TODO(nat): Return a parsable error so that we can surface something
  2136→			// sensible through the JS API.
  2137→			return fmt.Errorf("change to limits violates consumers: %s", strings.Join(errorConsumers, ", "))
  2138→		}
  2139→	}
  2140→
  2141→	jsa.mu.RLock()
  2142→	if jsa.subjectsOverlap(cfg.Subjects, mset) {
  2143→		jsa.mu.RUnlock()
  2144→		return NewJSStreamSubjectOverlapError()
  2145→	}
  2146→	jsa.mu.RUnlock()
  2147→
  2148→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2149→	if mset.isLeader() {
  2150→		// Check for mirror promotion.
  2151→		if ocfg.Mirror != nil && cfg.Mirror == nil {
  2152→			mset.cancelMirrorConsumer()
  2153→			mset.mirror = nil
  2154→		}
  2155→
  2156→		// Now check for subject interest differences.
  2157→		current := make(map[string]struct{}, len(ocfg.Subjects))
  2158→		for _, s := range ocfg.Subjects {
  2159→			current[s] = struct{}{}
  2160→		}
  2161→		// Update config with new values. The store update will enforce any stricter limits.
  2162→
  2163→		// Now walk new subjects. All of these need to be added, but we will check
  2164→		// the originals first, since if it is in there we can skip, already added.
  2165→		for _, s := range cfg.Subjects {
  2166→			if _, ok := current[s]; !ok {
  2167→				if _, err := mset.subscribeInternal(s, mset.processInboundJetStreamMsg); err != nil {
  2168→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  2169→					return err
  2170→				}
  2171→			}
  2172→			delete(current, s)
  2173→		}
  2174→		// What is left in current needs to be deleted.
  2175→		for s := range current {
  2176→			if err := mset.unsubscribeInternal(s); err != nil {
  2177→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  2178→				return err
  2179→			}
  2180→		}
  2181→
  2182→		// Check for the Duplicates
  2183→		mset.ddMu.Lock()
  2184→		if cfg.Duplicates != ocfg.Duplicates && mset.ddtmr != nil {
  2185→			// Let it fire right away, it will adjust properly on purge.
  2186→			mset.ddtmr.Reset(time.Microsecond)
  2187→		}
  2188→		mset.ddMu.Unlock()
  2189→
  2190→		// Check for Sources.
  2191→		if len(cfg.Sources) > 0 || len(ocfg.Sources) > 0 {
  2192→			currentIName := make(map[string]struct{})
  2193→			needsStartingSeqNum := make(map[string]struct{})
  2194→
  2195→			for _, s := range ocfg.Sources {
  2196→				currentIName[s.iname] = struct{}{}
  2197→			}
  2198→			for _, s := range cfg.Sources {
  2199→				s.setIndexName()
  2200→				if _, ok := currentIName[s.iname]; !ok {
  2201→					// new source
  2202→					if mset.sources == nil {
  2203→						mset.sources = make(map[string]*sourceInfo)
  2204→					}
  2205→					mset.cfg.Sources = append(mset.cfg.Sources, s)
  2206→
  2207→					var si *sourceInfo
  2208→
  2209→					if len(s.SubjectTransforms) == 0 {
  2210→						si = &sourceInfo{name: s.Name, iname: s.iname, sf: s.FilterSubject}
  2211→					} else {
  2212→						si = &sourceInfo{name: s.Name, iname: s.iname}
  2213→						si.trs = make([]*subjectTransform, len(s.SubjectTransforms))
  2214→						si.sfs = make([]string, len(s.SubjectTransforms))
  2215→						for i := range s.SubjectTransforms {
  2216→							// err can be ignored as already validated in config check
  2217→							si.sfs[i] = s.SubjectTransforms[i].Source
  2218→							var err error
  2219→							si.trs[i], err = NewSubjectTransform(s.SubjectTransforms[i].Source, s.SubjectTransforms[i].Destination)
  2220→							if err != nil {
  2221→								mset.mu.Unlock()
  2222→								return fmt.Errorf("unable to get subject transform for source: %v", err)
  2223→							}
  2224→						}
  2225→					}
  2226→
  2227→					mset.sources[s.iname] = si
  2228→					needsStartingSeqNum[s.iname] = struct{}{}
  2229→				} else {
  2230→					// source already exists
  2231→					delete(currentIName, s.iname)
  2232→				}
  2233→			}
  2234→			// What is left in currentIName needs to be deleted.
  2235→			for iName := range currentIName {
  2236→				mset.cancelSourceConsumer(iName)
  2237→				delete(mset.sources, iName)
  2238→			}
  2239→			neededCopy := make(map[string]struct{}, len(needsStartingSeqNum))
  2240→			for iName := range needsStartingSeqNum {
  2241→				neededCopy[iName] = struct{}{}
  2242→			}
  2243→			mset.setStartingSequenceForSources(needsStartingSeqNum)
  2244→			for iName := range neededCopy {
  2245→				mset.setupSourceConsumer(iName, mset.sources[iName].sseq+1, time.Time{})
  2246→			}
  2247→		}
  2248→	}
  2249→
  2250→	// Check for a change in allow direct/mirror status.
  2251→	// These will run on all members, so just update as appropriate here.
  2252→	// We do make sure we are caught up under monitorStream() during initial startup.
  2253→	if cfg.AllowDirect != ocfg.AllowDirect {
  2254→		if cfg.AllowDirect {
  2255→			mset.subscribeToDirect()
  2256→		} else {
  2257→			mset.unsubscribeToDirect()
  2258→		}
  2259→	}
  2260→	if cfg.MirrorDirect != ocfg.MirrorDirect {
  2261→		if cfg.MirrorDirect {
  2262→			mset.subscribeToMirrorDirect()
  2263→		} else {
  2264→			mset.unsubscribeToMirrorDirect()
  2265→		}
  2266→	}
  2267→
  2268→	// Check for changes to RePublish.
  2269→	if cfg.RePublish != nil {
  2270→		// Empty same as all.
  2271→		if cfg.RePublish.Source == _EMPTY_ {
  2272→			cfg.RePublish.Source = fwcs
  2273→		}
  2274→		if cfg.RePublish.Destination == _EMPTY_ {
  2275→			cfg.RePublish.Destination = fwcs
  2276→		}
  2277→		tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination)
  2278→		if err != nil {
  2279→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  2280→			return fmt.Errorf("stream configuration for republish from '%s' to '%s': %w", cfg.RePublish.Source, cfg.RePublish.Destination, err)
  2281→		}
  2282→		// Assign our transform for republishing.
  2283→		mset.tr = tr
  2284→	} else {
  2285→		mset.tr = nil
  2286→	}
  2287→
  2288→	// Check for changes to subject transform
  2289→	if ocfg.SubjectTransform == nil && cfg.SubjectTransform != nil {
  2290→		tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination)
  2291→		if err != nil {
  2292→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  2293→			return fmt.Errorf("stream configuration for subject transform from '%s' to '%s': %w", cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination, err)
  2294→		}
  2295→		mset.itr = tr
  2296→	} else if ocfg.SubjectTransform != nil && cfg.SubjectTransform != nil &&
  2297→		(ocfg.SubjectTransform.Source != cfg.SubjectTransform.Source || ocfg.SubjectTransform.Destination != cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination) {
  2298→		tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination)
  2299→		if err != nil {
  2300→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  2301→			return fmt.Errorf("stream configuration for subject transform from '%s' to '%s': %w", cfg.SubjectTransform.Source, cfg.SubjectTransform.Destination, err)
  2302→		}
  2303→		mset.itr = tr
  2304→	} else if ocfg.SubjectTransform != nil && cfg.SubjectTransform == nil {
  2305→		mset.itr = nil
  2306→	}
  2307→
  2308→	js := mset.js
  2309→
  2310→	if targetTier := tierName(cfg.Replicas); mset.tier != targetTier {
  2311→		// In cases such as R1->R3, only one update is needed
  2312→		jsa.usageMu.RLock()
  2313→		_, ok := jsa.limits[targetTier]
  2314→		jsa.usageMu.RUnlock()
  2315→		if ok {
  2316→			// error never set
  2317→			_, reported, _ := mset.store.Utilization()
  2318→			jsa.updateUsage(mset.tier, mset.stype, -int64(reported))
  2319→			jsa.updateUsage(targetTier, mset.stype, int64(reported))
  2320→			mset.tier = targetTier
  2321→		}
  2322→		// else in case the new tier does not exist (say on move), keep the old tier around
  2323→		// a subsequent update to an existing tier will then move from existing past tier to existing new tier
  2324→	}
  2325→
  2326→	if mset.isLeader() && mset.sa != nil && ocfg.Retention != cfg.Retention && cfg.Retention == InterestPolicy {
  2327→		// Before we can update the retention policy for the consumer, we need
  2328→		// the replica count of all consumers to match the stream.
  2329→		for _, c := range mset.sa.consumers {
  2330→			if c.Config.Replicas > 0 && c.Config.Replicas != cfg.Replicas {
  2331→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  2332→				return fmt.Errorf("consumer %q replica count must be %d", c.Name, cfg.Replicas)
  2333→			}
  2334→		}
  2335→	}
  2336→
  2337→	// If atomic publish is disabled, delete any in-progress batches.
  2338→	if !cfg.AllowAtomicPublish {
  2339→		mset.deleteInflightBatches(false)
  2340→		mset.deleteBatchApplyState()
  2341→	}
  2342→
  2343→	// Now update config and store's version of our config.
  2344→	// Although we are under the stream write lock, we will also assign the new
  2345→	// configuration under mset.cfgMu lock. This is so that in places where
  2346→	// mset.mu cannot be acquired (like many cases in consumer.go where code
  2347→	// is under the consumer's lock), and the stream's configuration needs to
  2348→	// be inspected, one can use mset.cfgMu's read lock to do that safely.
  2349→	mset.cfgMu.Lock()
  2350→	mset.cfg = *cfg
  2351→	mset.cfgMu.Unlock()
  2352→
  2353→	// If we're changing retention and haven't errored because of consumer
  2354→	// replicas by now, whip through and update the consumer retention.
  2355→	if ocfg.Retention != cfg.Retention {
  2356→		toUpdate := make([]*consumer, 0, len(mset.consumers))
  2357→		for _, c := range mset.consumers {
  2358→			toUpdate = append(toUpdate, c)
  2359→		}
  2360→		var ss StreamState
  2361→		mset.store.FastState(&ss)
  2362→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2363→		for _, c := range toUpdate {
  2364→			c.mu.Lock()
  2365→			c.retention = cfg.Retention
  2366→			c.mu.Unlock()
  2367→			if c.retention == InterestPolicy {
  2368→				// If we're switching to interest, force a check of the
  2369→				// interest of existing stream messages.
  2370→				c.checkStateForInterestStream(&ss)
  2371→			}
  2372→		}
  2373→		mset.mu.Lock()
  2374→	}
  2375→
  2376→	// If we are the leader never suppress update advisory, simply send.
  2377→	if mset.isLeader() && sendAdvisory {
  2378→		mset.sendUpdateAdvisoryLocked()
  2379→	}
  2380→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2381→
  2382→	if js != nil {
  2383→		maxBytesDiff := max(cfg.MaxBytes, 0) - max(ocfg.MaxBytes, 0)
  2384→		if maxBytesDiff > 0 {
  2385→			// Reserve the difference
  2386→			js.reserveStreamResources(&StreamConfig{
  2387→				MaxBytes: maxBytesDiff,
  2388→				Storage:  cfg.Storage,
  2389→			})
  2390→		} else if maxBytesDiff < 0 {
  2391→			// Release the difference
  2392→			js.releaseStreamResources(&StreamConfig{
  2393→				MaxBytes: -maxBytesDiff,
  2394→				Storage:  ocfg.Storage,
  2395→			})
  2396→		}
  2397→	}
  2398→
  2399→	mset.store.UpdateConfig(cfg)
  2400→
  2401→	return nil
  2402→}
  2403→
  2404→// Small helper to return the Name field from mset.cfg, protected by
  2405→// the mset.cfgMu mutex. This is simply because we have several places
  2406→// in consumer.go where we need it.
  2407→func (mset *stream) getCfgName() string {
  2408→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  2409→	defer mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  2410→	return mset.cfg.Name
  2411→}
  2412→
  2413→// Purge will remove all messages from the stream and underlying store based on the request.
  2414→func (mset *stream) purge(preq *JSApiStreamPurgeRequest) (purged uint64, err error) {
  2415→	mset.mu.RLock()
  2416→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  2417→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2418→		return 0, errStreamClosed
  2419→	}
  2420→	if mset.cfg.Sealed {
  2421→		mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2422→		return 0, errors.New("sealed stream")
  2423→	}
  2424→	store, mlseq := mset.store, mset.lseq
  2425→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2426→
  2427→	if preq != nil {
  2428→		purged, err = mset.store.PurgeEx(preq.Subject, preq.Sequence, preq.Keep)
  2429→	} else {
  2430→		purged, err = mset.store.Purge()
  2431→	}
  2432→	if err != nil {
  2433→		return purged, err
  2434→	}
  2435→
  2436→	// Grab our stream state.
  2437→	var state StreamState
  2438→	store.FastState(&state)
  2439→	fseq, lseq := state.FirstSeq, state.LastSeq
  2440→
  2441→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2442→	// Check if our last has moved past what our original last sequence was, if so reset.
  2443→	if lseq > mlseq {
  2444→		mset.setLastSeq(lseq)
  2445→	}
  2446→
  2447→	// Clear any pending acks below first seq.
  2448→	mset.clearAllPreAcksBelowFloor(fseq)
  2449→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2450→
  2451→	// Purge consumers.
  2452→	// Check for filtered purge.
  2453→	if preq != nil && preq.Subject != _EMPTY_ {
  2454→		ss := store.FilteredState(fseq, preq.Subject)
  2455→		fseq = ss.First
  2456→	}
  2457→
  2458→	// Take a copy of cList to avoid o.purge() potentially taking the stream lock and
  2459→	// violating the lock ordering.
  2460→	mset.clsMu.RLock()
  2461→	cList := slices.Clone(mset.cList)
  2462→	mset.clsMu.RUnlock()
  2463→	for _, o := range cList {
  2464→		start := fseq
  2465→		o.mu.RLock()
  2466→		// we update consumer sequences if:
  2467→		// no subject was specified, we can purge all consumers sequences
  2468→		doPurge := preq == nil ||
  2469→			preq.Subject == _EMPTY_ ||
  2470→			// consumer filter subject is equal to purged subject
  2471→			// or consumer filter subject is subset of purged subject,
  2472→			// but not the other way around.
  2473→			o.isEqualOrSubsetMatch(preq.Subject)
  2474→		// Check if a consumer has a wider subject space than what we purged
  2475→		var isWider bool
  2476→		if !doPurge && preq != nil && o.isFilteredMatch(preq.Subject) {
  2477→			doPurge, isWider = true, true
  2478→			start = state.FirstSeq
  2479→		}
  2480→		o.mu.RUnlock()
  2481→		if doPurge {
  2482→			o.purge(start, lseq, isWider)
  2483→		}
  2484→	}
  2485→
  2486→	return purged, nil
  2487→}
  2488→
  2489→// RemoveMsg will remove a message from a stream.
  2490→// FIXME(dlc) - Should pick one and be consistent.
  2491→func (mset *stream) removeMsg(seq uint64) (bool, error) {
  2492→	return mset.deleteMsg(seq)
  2493→}
  2494→
  2495→// DeleteMsg will remove a message from a stream.
  2496→func (mset *stream) deleteMsg(seq uint64) (bool, error) {
  2497→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  2498→		return false, errStreamClosed
  2499→	}
  2500→	removed, err := mset.store.RemoveMsg(seq)
  2501→	if err != nil {
  2502→		return removed, err
  2503→	}
  2504→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2505→	mset.clearAllPreAcks(seq)
  2506→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2507→	return removed, err
  2508→}
  2509→
  2510→// EraseMsg will securely remove a message and rewrite the data with random data.
  2511→func (mset *stream) eraseMsg(seq uint64) (bool, error) {
  2512→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  2513→		return false, errStreamClosed
  2514→	}
  2515→	removed, err := mset.store.EraseMsg(seq)
  2516→	if err != nil {
  2517→		return removed, err
  2518→	}
  2519→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2520→	mset.clearAllPreAcks(seq)
  2521→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2522→	return removed, err
  2523→}
  2524→
  2525→// Are we a mirror?
  2526→func (mset *stream) isMirror() bool {
  2527→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  2528→	defer mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  2529→	return mset.cfg.Mirror != nil
  2530→}

  2532→func (mset *stream) sourcesInfo() (sis []*StreamSourceInfo) {
  2533→	mset.mu.RLock()
  2534→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2535→	sis = make([]*StreamSourceInfo, 0, len(mset.sources))
  2536→	for _, si := range mset.sources {
  2537→		sis = append(sis, mset.sourceInfo(si))
  2538→	}
  2539→	return sis
  2540→}
  2541→
  2542→// Lock should be held
  2543→func (mset *stream) sourceInfo(si *sourceInfo) *StreamSourceInfo {
  2544→	if si == nil {
  2545→		return nil
  2546→	}
  2547→
  2548→	var ssi = StreamSourceInfo{Name: si.name, Lag: si.lag, Error: si.err, FilterSubject: si.sf}
  2549→
  2550→	trConfigs := make([]SubjectTransformConfig, len(si.sfs))
  2551→	for i := range si.sfs {
  2552→		var destination string
  2553→		if si.trs[i] != nil {
  2554→			destination = si.trs[i].dest
  2555→		}
  2556→		trConfigs[i] = SubjectTransformConfig{si.sfs[i], destination}
  2557→	}
  2558→
  2559→	ssi.SubjectTransforms = trConfigs
  2560→
  2561→	// If we have not heard from the source, set Active to -1.
  2562→	if last := si.last.Load(); last == 0 {
  2563→		ssi.Active = -1
  2564→	} else {
  2565→		ssi.Active = time.Since(time.Unix(0, last))
  2566→	}
  2567→
  2568→	var ext *ExternalStream
  2569→	if mset.cfg.Mirror != nil {
  2570→		ext = mset.cfg.Mirror.External
  2571→	} else if ss := mset.streamSource(si.iname); ss != nil && ss.External != nil {
  2572→		ext = ss.External
  2573→	}
  2574→	if ext != nil {
  2575→		ssi.External = &ExternalStream{
  2576→			ApiPrefix:     ext.ApiPrefix,
  2577→			DeliverPrefix: ext.DeliverPrefix,
  2578→		}
  2579→	}
  2580→	return &ssi
  2581→}
  2582→
  2583→// Return our source info for our mirror.
  2584→func (mset *stream) mirrorInfo() *StreamSourceInfo {
  2585→	mset.mu.RLock()
  2586→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2587→	return mset.sourceInfo(mset.mirror)
  2588→}
  2589→
  2590→// retryDisconnectedSyncConsumers() will check if we have any disconnected
  2591→// sync consumers for either mirror or a source and will reset and retry to connect.
  2592→func (mset *stream) retryDisconnectedSyncConsumers(remoteDomain string) {
  2593→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2594→	defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  2595→
  2596→	// Only applicable if we are the stream leader.
  2597→	if !mset.isLeader() {
  2598→		return
  2599→	}
  2600→
  2601→	// Check mirrors first.
  2602→	if si := mset.mirror; si != nil {
  2603→		if si.sub == nil && !si.sip {
  2604→			if remoteDomain == _EMPTY_ || (mset.cfg.Mirror != nil && mset.cfg.Mirror.External.Domain() == remoteDomain) {
  2605→				// Need to reset
  2606→				si.fails = 0
  2607→				mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)
  2608→				mset.scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry()
  2609→			}
  2610→		}
  2611→	} else {
  2612→		for _, si := range mset.sources {
  2613→			ss := mset.streamSource(si.iname)
  2614→			if remoteDomain == _EMPTY_ || (ss != nil && ss.External.Domain() == remoteDomain) {
  2615→				// Need to reset
  2616→				si.fails = 0
  2617→				mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)
  2618→				mset.setupSourceConsumer(si.iname, si.sseq+1, time.Time{})
  2619→			}
  2620→		}
  2621→	}
  2622→}
  2623→
  2624→const (
  2625→	// Our consumer HB interval.
  2626→	sourceHealthHB = 1 * time.Second
  2627→	// How often we check and our stalled interval.
  2628→	sourceHealthCheckInterval = 10 * time.Second
  2629→)
  2630→
  2631→// Will run as a Go routine to process mirror consumer messages.
  2632→func (mset *stream) processMirrorMsgs(mirror *sourceInfo, ready *sync.WaitGroup) {
  2633→	s := mset.srv
  2634→	defer func() {
  2635→		mirror.wg.Done()
  2636→		s.grWG.Done()
  2637→	}()
  2638→
  2639→	// Grab stream quit channel.
  2640→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2641→	msgs, qch, siqch := mirror.msgs, mset.qch, mirror.qch
  2642→	// Set the last seen as now so that we don't fail at the first check.
  2643→	mirror.last.Store(time.Now().UnixNano())
  2644→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2645→
  2646→	// Signal the caller that we have captured the above fields.
  2647→	ready.Done()
  2648→
  2649→	// Make sure we have valid ipq for msgs.
  2650→	if msgs == nil {
  2651→		mset.mu.Lock()
  2652→		mset.cancelMirrorConsumer()
  2653→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2654→		return
  2655→	}
  2656→
  2657→	t := time.NewTicker(sourceHealthCheckInterval)
  2658→	defer t.Stop()
  2659→
  2660→	for {
  2661→		select {
  2662→		case <-s.quitCh:
  2663→			return
  2664→		case <-qch:
  2665→			return
  2666→		case <-siqch:
  2667→			return
  2668→		case <-msgs.ch:
  2669→			ims := msgs.pop()
  2670→			for _, im := range ims {
  2671→				if !mset.processInboundMirrorMsg(im) {
  2672→					break
  2673→				}
  2674→				im.returnToPool()
  2675→			}
  2676→			msgs.recycle(&ims)
  2677→		case <-t.C:
  2678→			mset.mu.RLock()
  2679→			var stalled bool
  2680→			if mset.mirror != nil {
  2681→				stalled = time.Since(time.Unix(0, mset.mirror.last.Load())) > sourceHealthCheckInterval
  2682→			}
  2683→			isLeader := mset.isLeader()
  2684→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2685→			// No longer leader.
  2686→			if !isLeader {
  2687→				mset.mu.Lock()
  2688→				mset.cancelMirrorConsumer()
  2689→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  2690→				return
  2691→			}
  2692→			// We are stalled.
  2693→			if stalled {
  2694→				mset.retryMirrorConsumer()
  2695→			}
  2696→		}
  2697→	}
  2698→}
  2699→
  2700→// Checks that the message is from our current direct consumer. We can not depend on sub comparison
  2701→// since cross account imports break.
  2702→func (si *sourceInfo) isCurrentSub(reply string) bool {
  2703→	return si.cname != _EMPTY_ && strings.HasPrefix(reply, jsAckPre) && si.cname == tokenAt(reply, 4)
  2704→}
  2705→
  2706→// processInboundMirrorMsg handles processing messages bound for a stream.
  2707→func (mset *stream) processInboundMirrorMsg(m *inMsg) bool {
  2708→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2709→	if mset.mirror == nil {
  2710→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2711→		return false
  2712→	}
  2713→	if !mset.isLeader() {
  2714→		mset.cancelMirrorConsumer()
  2715→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2716→		return false
  2717→	}
  2718→
  2719→	isControl := m.isControlMsg()
  2720→
  2721→	// Ignore from old subscriptions.
  2722→	// The reason we can not just compare subs is that on cross account imports they will not match.
  2723→	if !mset.mirror.isCurrentSub(m.rply) && !isControl {
  2724→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2725→		return false
  2726→	}
  2727→
  2728→	// Check for heartbeats and flow control messages.
  2729→	if isControl {
  2730→		var needsRetry bool
  2731→		// Flow controls have reply subjects.
  2732→		if m.rply != _EMPTY_ {
  2733→			mset.handleFlowControl(m)
  2734→		} else {
  2735→			// For idle heartbeats make sure we did not miss anything and check if we are considered stalled.
  2736→			if ldseq := parseInt64(getHeader(JSLastConsumerSeq, m.hdr)); ldseq > 0 && uint64(ldseq) != mset.mirror.dseq {
  2737→				needsRetry = true
  2738→			} else if fcReply := getHeader(JSConsumerStalled, m.hdr); len(fcReply) > 0 {
  2739→				// Other side thinks we are stalled, so send flow control reply.
  2740→				mset.outq.sendMsg(string(fcReply), nil)
  2741→			}
  2742→		}
  2743→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2744→		if needsRetry {
  2745→			mset.retryMirrorConsumer()
  2746→		}
  2747→		return !needsRetry
  2748→	}
  2749→
  2750→	sseq, dseq, dc, ts, pending := replyInfo(m.rply)
  2751→
  2752→	if dc > 1 {
  2753→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2754→		return false
  2755→	}
  2756→
  2757→	// Mirror info tracking.
  2758→	olag, osseq, odseq := mset.mirror.lag, mset.mirror.sseq, mset.mirror.dseq
  2759→	if sseq == mset.mirror.sseq+1 {
  2760→		mset.mirror.dseq = dseq
  2761→		mset.mirror.sseq++
  2762→	} else if sseq <= mset.mirror.sseq {
  2763→		// Ignore older messages.
  2764→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2765→		return true
  2766→	} else if mset.mirror.cname == _EMPTY_ {
  2767→		mset.mirror.cname = tokenAt(m.rply, 4)
  2768→		mset.mirror.dseq, mset.mirror.sseq = dseq, sseq
  2769→	} else {
  2770→		// If the deliver sequence matches then the upstream stream has expired or deleted messages.
  2771→		if dseq == mset.mirror.dseq+1 {
  2772→			mset.skipMsgs(mset.mirror.sseq+1, sseq-1)
  2773→			mset.mirror.dseq++
  2774→			mset.mirror.sseq = sseq
  2775→		} else {
  2776→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  2777→			mset.retryMirrorConsumer()
  2778→			return false
  2779→		}
  2780→	}
  2781→
  2782→	if pending == 0 {
  2783→		mset.mirror.lag = 0
  2784→	} else {
  2785→		mset.mirror.lag = pending - 1
  2786→	}
  2787→
  2788→	// Check if we allow mirror direct here. If so check they we have mostly caught up.
  2789→	// The reason we do not require 0 is if the source is active we may always be slightly behind.
  2790→	if mset.cfg.MirrorDirect && mset.mirrorDirectSub == nil && pending < dgetCaughtUpThresh {
  2791→		if err := mset.subscribeToMirrorDirect(); err != nil {
  2792→			// Disable since we had problems above.
  2793→			mset.cfg.MirrorDirect = false
  2794→		}
  2795→	}
  2796→
  2797→	// Do the subject transform if there's one
  2798→	if len(mset.mirror.trs) > 0 {
  2799→		for _, tr := range mset.mirror.trs {
  2800→			if tr == nil {
  2801→				continue
  2802→			} else {
  2803→				tsubj, err := tr.Match(m.subj)
  2804→				if err == nil {
  2805→					m.subj = tsubj
  2806→					break
  2807→				}
  2808→			}
  2809→		}
  2810→	}
  2811→
  2812→	s, js, stype := mset.srv, mset.js, mset.cfg.Storage
  2813→	node := mset.node
  2814→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2815→
  2816→	var err error
  2817→	if node != nil {
  2818→		if js.limitsExceeded(stype) {
  2819→			s.resourcesExceededError(stype)
  2820→			err = ApiErrors[JSInsufficientResourcesErr]
  2821→		} else {
  2822→			err = node.Propose(encodeStreamMsg(m.subj, _EMPTY_, m.hdr, m.msg, sseq-1, ts, true))
  2823→		}
  2824→	} else {
  2825→		err = mset.processJetStreamMsg(m.subj, _EMPTY_, m.hdr, m.msg, sseq-1, ts, nil, true)
  2826→	}
  2827→	if err != nil {
  2828→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "no space left") {
  2829→			s.Errorf("JetStream out of space, will be DISABLED")
  2830→			s.DisableJetStream()
  2831→			return false
  2832→		}
  2833→		if err != errLastSeqMismatch {
  2834→			mset.mu.RLock()
  2835→			accName, sname := mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name
  2836→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
  2837→			s.RateLimitWarnf("Error processing inbound mirror message for '%s' > '%s': %v",
  2838→				accName, sname, err)
  2839→		} else {
  2840→			// We may have missed messages, restart.
  2841→			if sseq <= mset.lastSeq() {
  2842→				mset.mu.Lock()
  2843→				mset.mirror.lag = olag
  2844→				mset.mirror.sseq = osseq
  2845→				mset.mirror.dseq = odseq
  2846→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  2847→				return false
  2848→			} else {
  2849→				mset.mu.Lock()
  2850→				mset.mirror.dseq = odseq
  2851→				mset.mirror.sseq = osseq
  2852→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  2853→				mset.retryMirrorConsumer()
  2854→			}
  2855→		}
  2856→	}
  2857→	return err == nil
  2858→}
  2859→
  2860→func (mset *stream) setMirrorErr(err *ApiError) {
  2861→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2862→	if mset.mirror != nil {
  2863→		mset.mirror.err = err
  2864→	}
  2865→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  2866→}
  2867→
  2868→// Cancels a mirror consumer.
  2869→//
  2870→// Lock held on entry
  2871→func (mset *stream) cancelMirrorConsumer() {
  2872→	if mset.mirror == nil {
  2873→		return
  2874→	}
  2875→	mset.cancelSourceInfo(mset.mirror)
  2876→}
  2877→
  2878→// Similar to setupMirrorConsumer except that it will print a debug statement
  2879→// indicating that there is a retry.
  2880→//
  2881→// Lock is acquired in this function
  2882→func (mset *stream) retryMirrorConsumer() error {
  2883→	mset.mu.Lock()
  2884→	defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  2885→	mset.srv.Debugf("Retrying mirror consumer for '%s > %s'", mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name)
  2886→	mset.cancelMirrorConsumer()
  2887→	return mset.setupMirrorConsumer()
  2888→}
  2889→
  2890→// Lock should be held.
  2891→func (mset *stream) skipMsgs(start, end uint64) {
  2892→	node, store := mset.node, mset.store
  2893→	// If we are not clustered we can short circuit now with store.SkipMsgs
  2894→	if node == nil {
  2895→		store.SkipMsgs(start, end-start+1)
  2896→		mset.lseq = end
  2897→		return
  2898→	}
  2899→
  2900→	// FIXME (dlc) - We should allow proposals of DeleteRange, but would need to make sure all peers support.
  2901→	// With syncRequest was easy to add bool into request.
  2902→	var entries []*Entry
  2903→	for seq := start; seq <= end; seq++ {
  2904→		entries = append(entries, newEntry(EntryNormal, encodeStreamMsg(_EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, nil, nil, seq-1, 0, false)))
  2905→		// So a single message does not get too big.
  2906→		if len(entries) > 10_000 {
  2907→			node.ProposeMulti(entries)
  2908→			// We need to re-create `entries` because there is a reference
  2909→			// to it in the node's pae map.
  2910→			entries = entries[:0]
  2911→		}
  2912→	}
  2913→	// Send all at once.
  2914→	if len(entries) > 0 {
  2915→		node.ProposeMulti(entries)
  2916→	}
  2917→}
  2918→
  2919→const (
  2920→	// Base retry backoff duration.
  2921→	retryBackOff = 5 * time.Second
  2922→	// Maximum amount we will wait.
  2923→	retryMaximum = 2 * time.Minute
  2924→)
  2925→
  2926→// Calculate our backoff based on number of failures.
  2927→func calculateRetryBackoff(fails int) time.Duration {
  2928→	backoff := time.Duration(retryBackOff) * time.Duration(fails*2)
  2929→	if backoff > retryMaximum {
  2930→		backoff = retryMaximum
  2931→	}
  2932→	return backoff
  2933→}
  2934→
  2935→// This will schedule a call to setupMirrorConsumer, taking into account the last
  2936→// time it was retried and determine the soonest setupMirrorConsumer can be called
  2937→// without tripping the sourceConsumerRetryThreshold. We will also take into account
  2938→// number of failures and will back off our retries.
  2939→// The mset.mirror pointer has been verified to be not nil by the caller.
  2940→//
  2941→// Lock held on entry
  2942→func (mset *stream) scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry() {
  2943→	// We are trying to figure out how soon we can retry. setupMirrorConsumer will reject
  2944→	// a retry if last was done less than "sourceConsumerRetryThreshold" ago.
  2945→	next := sourceConsumerRetryThreshold - time.Since(mset.mirror.lreq)
  2946→	if next < 0 {
  2947→		// It means that we have passed the threshold and so we are ready to go.
  2948→		next = 0
  2949→	}
  2950→	// Take into account failures here.
  2951→	next += calculateRetryBackoff(mset.mirror.fails)
  2952→
  2953→	// Add some jitter.
  2954→	next += time.Duration(rand.Intn(int(100*time.Millisecond))) + 100*time.Millisecond
  2955→
  2956→	time.AfterFunc(next, func() {
  2957→		mset.mu.Lock()
  2958→		mset.setupMirrorConsumer()
  2959→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  2960→	})
  2961→}
  2962→
  2963→// How long we wait for a response from a consumer create request for a source or mirror.
  2964→var srcConsumerWaitTime = 30 * time.Second
  2965→
  2966→// Setup our mirror consumer.
  2967→// Lock should be held.
  2968→func (mset *stream) setupMirrorConsumer() error {
  2969→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  2970→		return errStreamClosed
  2971→	}
  2972→	if mset.outq == nil {
  2973→		return errors.New("outq required")
  2974→	}
  2975→	// We use to prevent update of a mirror configuration in cluster
  2976→	// mode but not in standalone. This is now fixed. However, without
  2977→	// rejecting the update, it could be that if the source stream was
  2978→	// removed and then later the mirrored stream config changed to
  2979→	// remove mirror configuration, this function would panic when
  2980→	// accessing mset.cfg.Mirror fields. Adding this protection in case
  2981→	// we allow in the future the mirror config to be changed (removed).
  2982→	if mset.cfg.Mirror == nil {
  2983→		return errors.New("invalid mirror configuration")
  2984→	}
  2985→
  2986→	// If this is the first time
  2987→	if mset.mirror == nil {
  2988→		mset.mirror = &sourceInfo{name: mset.cfg.Mirror.Name}
  2989→	} else {
  2990→		mset.cancelSourceInfo(mset.mirror)
  2991→		mset.mirror.sseq = mset.lseq
  2992→	}
  2993→
  2994→	// If we are no longer the leader stop trying.
  2995→	if !mset.isLeader() {
  2996→		return nil
  2997→	}
  2998→
  2999→	mirror := mset.mirror
  3000→
  3001→	// We want to throttle here in terms of how fast we request new consumers,
  3002→	// or if the previous is still in progress.
  3003→	if last := time.Since(mirror.lreq); last < sourceConsumerRetryThreshold || mirror.sip {
  3004→		mset.scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry()
  3005→		return nil
  3006→	}
  3007→	mirror.lreq = time.Now()
  3008→
  3009→	// Determine subjects etc.
  3010→	var deliverSubject string
  3011→	ext := mset.cfg.Mirror.External
  3012→
  3013→	if ext != nil && ext.DeliverPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  3014→		deliverSubject = strings.ReplaceAll(ext.DeliverPrefix+syncSubject(".M"), "..", ".")
  3015→	} else {
  3016→		deliverSubject = syncSubject("$JS.M")
  3017→	}
  3018→
  3019→	// Now send off request to create/update our consumer. This will be all API based even in single server mode.
  3020→	// We calculate durable names apriori so we do not need to save them off.
  3021→
  3022→	var state StreamState
  3023→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
  3024→
  3025→	req := &CreateConsumerRequest{
  3026→		Stream: mset.cfg.Mirror.Name,
  3027→		Config: ConsumerConfig{
  3028→			DeliverSubject:    deliverSubject,
  3029→			DeliverPolicy:     DeliverByStartSequence,
  3030→			OptStartSeq:       state.LastSeq + 1,
  3031→			AckPolicy:         AckNone,
  3032→			AckWait:           22 * time.Hour,
  3033→			MaxDeliver:        1,
  3034→			Heartbeat:         sourceHealthHB,
  3035→			FlowControl:       true,
  3036→			Direct:            true,
  3037→			InactiveThreshold: sourceHealthCheckInterval,
  3038→		},
  3039→	}
  3040→
  3041→	// Only use start optionals on first time.
  3042→	if state.Msgs == 0 && state.FirstSeq == 0 {
  3043→		req.Config.OptStartSeq = 0
  3044→		if mset.cfg.Mirror.OptStartSeq > 0 {
  3045→			req.Config.OptStartSeq = mset.cfg.Mirror.OptStartSeq
  3046→		} else if mset.cfg.Mirror.OptStartTime != nil {
  3047→			req.Config.OptStartTime = mset.cfg.Mirror.OptStartTime
  3048→			req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartTime
  3049→		}
  3050→	}
  3051→	if req.Config.OptStartSeq == 0 && req.Config.OptStartTime == nil {
  3052→		// If starting out and lastSeq is 0.
  3053→		req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverAll
  3054→	}
  3055→
  3056→	// Filters
  3057→	if mset.cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ {
  3058→		req.Config.FilterSubject = mset.cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject
  3059→		mirror.sf = mset.cfg.Mirror.FilterSubject
  3060→	}
  3061→
  3062→	if lst := len(mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms); lst > 0 {
  3063→		sfs := make([]string, lst)
  3064→		trs := make([]*subjectTransform, lst)
  3065→
  3066→		for i, tr := range mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms {
  3067→			// will not fail as already checked before that the transform will work
  3068→			subjectTransform, err := NewSubjectTransform(tr.Source, tr.Destination)
  3069→			if err != nil {
  3070→				mset.srv.Errorf("Unable to get transform for mirror consumer: %v", err)
  3071→			}
  3072→			sfs[i] = tr.Source
  3073→			trs[i] = subjectTransform
  3074→		}

  3076→		mirror.trs = trs
  3077→		// If there was no explicit FilterSubject defined and we have a single
  3078→		// subject transform, use Config.FilterSubject instead of FilterSubjects
  3079→		// so that we can use the extended consumer create API down below.
  3080→		if req.Config.FilterSubject == _EMPTY_ && len(sfs) == 1 {

  3084→		}
  3085→	}
  3086→
  3087→	respCh := make(chan *JSApiConsumerCreateResponse, 1)
  3088→	reply := infoReplySubject()
  3089→	crSub, err := mset.subscribeInternal(reply, func(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  3090→		mset.unsubscribe(sub)
  3091→		_, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  3092→
  3093→		var ccr JSApiConsumerCreateResponse
  3094→		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &ccr); err != nil {

  3098→		}

  3100→		case respCh <- &ccr:
  3101→		default:
  3102→		}
  3103→	})

  3108→	}
  3109→
  3110→	var subject string
  3111→	if req.Config.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ {
  3112→		req.Config.Name = fmt.Sprintf("mirror-%s", createConsumerName())
  3113→		subject = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiConsumerCreateExT, mset.cfg.Mirror.Name, req.Config.Name, req.Config.FilterSubject)
  3114→	} else {
  3115→		subject = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiConsumerCreateT, mset.cfg.Mirror.Name)
  3116→	}
  3117→	if ext != nil {
  3118→		subject = strings.Replace(subject, JSApiPrefix, ext.ApiPrefix, 1)
  3119→		subject = strings.ReplaceAll(subject, "..", ".")
  3120→	}
  3121→
  3122→	// Marshal now that we are done with `req`.
  3123→	b, _ := json.Marshal(req)
  3124→
  3125→	// Reset
  3126→	mirror.msgs = nil
  3127→	mirror.err = nil
  3128→	mirror.sip = true
  3129→
  3130→	// Send the consumer create request
  3131→	mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(subject, _EMPTY_, reply, nil, b, nil, 0))
  3132→
  3133→	go func() {
  3134→
  3135→		var retry bool
  3136→		defer func() {
  3137→			mset.mu.Lock()
  3138→			// Check that this is still valid and if so, clear the "setup in progress" flag.
  3139→			if mset.mirror != nil {
  3140→				mset.mirror.sip = false
  3141→				// If we need to retry, schedule now
  3142→				if retry {
  3143→					mset.mirror.fails++
  3144→					// Cancel here since we can not do anything with this consumer at this point.
  3145→					mset.cancelSourceInfo(mset.mirror)
  3146→					mset.scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry()
  3147→				} else {
  3148→					// Clear on success.
  3149→					mset.mirror.fails = 0
  3150→				}
  3151→			}
  3152→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  3153→		}()
  3154→
  3155→		// Wait for previous processMirrorMsgs go routine to be completely done.
  3156→		// If none is running, this will not block.
  3157→		mirror.wg.Wait()
  3158→
  3159→		select {
  3160→		case ccr := <-respCh:
  3161→			mset.mu.Lock()
  3162→			// Mirror config has been removed.
  3163→			if mset.mirror == nil {
  3164→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  3165→				return
  3166→			}
  3167→			ready := sync.WaitGroup{}
  3168→			mirror := mset.mirror
  3169→			mirror.err = nil
  3170→			if ccr.Error != nil || ccr.ConsumerInfo == nil {
  3171→				mset.srv.Warnf("JetStream error response for create mirror consumer: %+v", ccr.Error)
  3172→				mirror.err = ccr.Error
  3173→				// Let's retry as soon as possible, but we are gated by sourceConsumerRetryThreshold
  3174→				retry = true
  3175→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  3176→				return
  3177→			} else {
  3178→				// Setup actual subscription to process messages from our source.
  3179→				qname := fmt.Sprintf("[ACC:%s] stream mirror '%s' of '%s' msgs", mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name, mset.cfg.Mirror.Name)
  3180→				// Create a new queue each time
  3181→				mirror.msgs = newIPQueue[*inMsg](mset.srv, qname)
  3182→				msgs := mirror.msgs
  3183→				sub, err := mset.subscribeInternal(deliverSubject, func(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  3184→					hdr, msg := c.msgParts(copyBytes(rmsg)) // Need to copy.
  3185→					if len(hdr) > 0 {
  3186→						// Remove any Nats-Expected- headers as we don't want to validate them.
  3187→						hdr = removeHeaderIfPrefixPresent(hdr, "Nats-Expected-")
  3188→						// Remove any Nats-Batch- headers, batching is not supported when mirroring.
  3189→						hdr = removeHeaderIfPrefixPresent(hdr, "Nats-Batch-")
  3190→					}
  3191→					mset.queueInbound(msgs, subject, reply, hdr, msg, nil, nil)
  3192→					mirror.last.Store(time.Now().UnixNano())
  3193→				})
  3194→				if err != nil {
  3195→					mirror.err = NewJSMirrorConsumerSetupFailedError(err, Unless(err))
  3196→					retry = true
  3197→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  3198→					return
  3199→				}
  3200→				// Save our sub.
  3201→				mirror.sub = sub
  3202→
  3203→				// When an upstream stream expires messages or in general has messages that we want
  3204→				// that are no longer available we need to adjust here.
  3205→				var state StreamState
  3206→				mset.store.FastState(&state)
  3207→
  3208→				// Check if we need to skip messages.
  3209→				if state.LastSeq != ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream {
  3210→					// Check to see if delivered is past our last and we have no msgs. This will help the
  3211→					// case when mirroring a stream that has a very high starting sequence number.
  3212→					if state.Msgs == 0 && ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream > state.LastSeq {
  3213→						mset.store.PurgeEx(_EMPTY_, ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream+1, 0)
  3214→						mset.lseq = ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream
  3215→					} else {
  3216→						mset.skipMsgs(state.LastSeq+1, ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream)
  3217→					}
  3218→				}
  3219→
  3220→				// Capture consumer name.
  3221→				mirror.cname = ccr.ConsumerInfo.Name
  3222→				mirror.dseq = 0
  3223→				mirror.sseq = ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream
  3224→				mirror.qch = make(chan struct{})
  3225→				mirror.wg.Add(1)
  3226→				ready.Add(1)
  3227→				if !mset.srv.startGoRoutine(
  3228→					func() { mset.processMirrorMsgs(mirror, &ready) },
  3229→					pprofLabels{
  3230→						"type":     "mirror",
  3231→						"account":  mset.acc.Name,
  3232→						"stream":   mset.cfg.Name,
  3233→						"consumer": mirror.cname,
  3234→					},
  3235→				) {
  3236→					ready.Done()
  3237→				}
  3238→			}
  3239→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  3240→			ready.Wait()
  3241→		case <-time.After(srcConsumerWaitTime):
  3242→			mset.unsubscribe(crSub)
  3243→			// We already waited 30 seconds, let's retry now.
  3244→			retry = true
  3245→		}
  3246→	}()
  3247→
  3248→	return nil
  3249→}

  3251→func (mset *stream) streamSource(iname string) *StreamSource {
  3252→	for _, ssi := range mset.cfg.Sources {
  3253→		if ssi.iname == iname {
  3254→			return ssi
  3255→		}
  3256→	}
  3257→	return nil
  3258→}
  3259→
  3260→// Lock should be held.
  3261→func (mset *stream) retrySourceConsumerAtSeq(iName string, seq uint64) {
  3262→	s := mset.srv
  3263→
  3264→	s.Debugf("Retrying source consumer for '%s > %s'", mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name)
  3265→
  3266→	// setupSourceConsumer will check that the source is still configured.
  3267→	mset.setupSourceConsumer(iName, seq, time.Time{})
  3268→}
  3269→
  3270→// Lock should be held.
  3271→func (mset *stream) cancelSourceConsumer(iname string) {
  3272→	if si := mset.sources[iname]; si != nil {
  3273→		mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)
  3274→		si.sseq, si.dseq = 0, 0
  3275→	}
  3276→}
  3277→
  3278→// The `si` has been verified to be not nil. The sourceInfo's sub will
  3279→// be unsubscribed and set to nil (if not already done) and the
  3280→// cname will be reset. The message processing's go routine quit channel
  3281→// will be closed if still opened.
  3282→//
  3283→// Lock should be held
  3284→func (mset *stream) cancelSourceInfo(si *sourceInfo) {
  3285→	if si.sub != nil {
  3286→		mset.unsubscribe(si.sub)
  3287→		si.sub = nil
  3288→	}
  3289→	mset.removeInternalConsumer(si)
  3290→	if si.qch != nil {
  3291→		close(si.qch)
  3292→		si.qch = nil
  3293→	}
  3294→	if si.msgs != nil {
  3295→		si.msgs.drain()
  3296→		si.msgs.unregister()
  3297→	}
  3298→	// If we have a schedule setup go ahead and delete that.
  3299→	if t := mset.sourceSetupSchedules[si.iname]; t != nil {
  3300→		t.Stop()
  3301→		delete(mset.sourceSetupSchedules, si.iname)
  3302→	}
  3303→}
  3304→
  3305→const sourceConsumerRetryThreshold = 2 * time.Second
  3306→
  3307→// This is the main function to call when needing to setup a new consumer for the source.
  3308→// It actually only does the scheduling of the execution of trySetupSourceConsumer in order to implement retry backoff
  3309→// and throttle the number of requests.
  3310→// Lock should be held.
  3311→func (mset *stream) setupSourceConsumer(iname string, seq uint64, startTime time.Time) {
  3312→	if mset.sourceSetupSchedules == nil {
  3313→		mset.sourceSetupSchedules = map[string]*time.Timer{}
  3314→	}
  3315→
  3316→	if _, ok := mset.sourceSetupSchedules[iname]; ok {
  3317→		// If there is already a timer scheduled, we don't need to do anything.
  3318→		return
  3319→	}
  3320→
  3321→	si := mset.sources[iname]
  3322→	if si == nil || si.sip { // if sourceInfo was removed or setup is in progress, nothing to do
  3323→		return
  3324→	}
  3325→
  3326→	// First calculate the delay until the next time we can
  3327→	var scheduleDelay time.Duration
  3328→
  3329→	if !si.lreq.IsZero() { // it's not the very first time we are called, compute the delay
  3330→		// We want to throttle here in terms of how fast we request new consumers
  3331→		if sinceLast := time.Since(si.lreq); sinceLast < sourceConsumerRetryThreshold {
  3332→			scheduleDelay = sourceConsumerRetryThreshold - sinceLast
  3333→		}
  3334→		// Is it a retry? If so, add a backoff
  3335→		if si.fails > 0 {
  3336→			scheduleDelay += calculateRetryBackoff(si.fails)
  3337→		}
  3338→	}
  3339→
  3340→	// Always add some jitter
  3341→	scheduleDelay += time.Duration(rand.Intn(int(100*time.Millisecond))) + 100*time.Millisecond
  3342→
  3343→	// Schedule the call to trySetupSourceConsumer
  3344→	mset.sourceSetupSchedules[iname] = time.AfterFunc(scheduleDelay, func() {
  3345→		mset.mu.Lock()
  3346→		defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  3347→
  3348→		delete(mset.sourceSetupSchedules, iname)
  3349→		mset.trySetupSourceConsumer(iname, seq, startTime)
  3350→	})
  3351→}
  3352→
  3353→// This is where we will actually try to create a new consumer for the source
  3354→// Lock should be held.
  3355→func (mset *stream) trySetupSourceConsumer(iname string, seq uint64, startTime time.Time) {
  3356→	// Ignore if closed or not leader.
  3357→	if mset.closed.Load() || !mset.isLeader() {
  3358→		return
  3359→	}
  3360→
  3361→	si := mset.sources[iname]
  3362→	if si == nil {
  3363→		return
  3364→	}
  3365→
  3366→	// Cancel previous instance if applicable
  3367→	mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)
  3368→
  3369→	ssi := mset.streamSource(iname)
  3370→	if ssi == nil {
  3371→		return
  3372→	}
  3373→
  3374→	si.lreq = time.Now()
  3375→
  3376→	// Determine subjects etc.
  3377→	var deliverSubject string
  3378→	ext := ssi.External
  3379→
  3380→	if ext != nil && ext.DeliverPrefix != _EMPTY_ {
  3381→		deliverSubject = strings.ReplaceAll(ext.DeliverPrefix+syncSubject(".S"), "..", ".")
  3382→	} else {
  3383→		deliverSubject = syncSubject("$JS.S")
  3384→	}

  3386→	req := &CreateConsumerRequest{
  3387→		Stream: si.name,
  3388→		Config: ConsumerConfig{
  3389→			DeliverSubject:    deliverSubject,
  3390→			AckPolicy:         AckNone,
  3391→			AckWait:           22 * time.Hour,
  3392→			MaxDeliver:        1,
  3393→			Heartbeat:         sourceHealthHB,
  3394→			FlowControl:       true,
  3395→			Direct:            true,
  3396→			InactiveThreshold: sourceHealthCheckInterval,
  3397→		},
  3398→	}
  3399→

  3403→		req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartTime
  3404→	} else if seq <= 1 {
  3405→		if ssi.OptStartSeq > 0 {
  3406→			req.Config.OptStartSeq = ssi.OptStartSeq
  3407→			req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartSequence
  3408→		} else {
  3409→			// We have not recovered state so check that configured time is less that our first seq time.
