  3411→			mset.store.FastState(&state)
  3412→			if ssi.OptStartTime != nil {
  3413→				if !state.LastTime.IsZero() && ssi.OptStartTime.Before(state.LastTime) {
  3414→					req.Config.OptStartTime = &state.LastTime
  3415→				} else {
  3416→					req.Config.OptStartTime = ssi.OptStartTime
  3417→				}
  3418→				req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartTime
  3419→			} else if state.FirstSeq > 1 && !state.LastTime.IsZero() {
  3420→				req.Config.OptStartTime = &state.LastTime
  3421→				req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartTime
  3422→			}
  3423→		}
  3424→
  3425→	} else {
  3426→		req.Config.OptStartSeq = seq
  3427→		req.Config.DeliverPolicy = DeliverByStartSequence
  3428→	}

  3430→	if ssi.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ {
  3431→		req.Config.FilterSubject = ssi.FilterSubject
  3432→	}
  3433→
  3434→	var filterSubjects []string
  3435→	for _, tr := range ssi.SubjectTransforms {
  3436→		filterSubjects = append(filterSubjects, tr.Source)
  3437→	}
  3438→	req.Config.FilterSubjects = filterSubjects
  3439→
  3440→	respCh := make(chan *JSApiConsumerCreateResponse, 1)
  3441→	reply := infoReplySubject()
  3442→	crSub, err := mset.subscribeInternal(reply, func(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  3443→		mset.unsubscribe(sub)
  3444→		_, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  3445→		var ccr JSApiConsumerCreateResponse
  3446→		if err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &ccr); err != nil {
  3447→			c.Warnf("JetStream bad source consumer create response: %q", msg)
  3448→			return
  3449→		}
  3450→		select {
  3451→		case respCh <- &ccr:
  3452→		default:
  3453→		}
  3454→	})
  3455→	if err != nil {
  3456→		si.err = NewJSSourceConsumerSetupFailedError(err, Unless(err))

  3458→		return
  3459→	}
  3460→
  3461→	var subject string
  3462→	if req.Config.FilterSubject != _EMPTY_ {
  3463→		req.Config.Name = fmt.Sprintf("src-%s", createConsumerName())
  3464→		subject = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiConsumerCreateExT, si.name, req.Config.Name, req.Config.FilterSubject)
  3465→	} else if len(req.Config.FilterSubjects) == 1 {
  3466→		req.Config.Name = fmt.Sprintf("src-%s", createConsumerName())
  3467→		// It is necessary to switch to using FilterSubject here as the extended consumer
  3468→		// create API checks for it, so as to not accidentally allow multiple filtered subjects.
  3469→		req.Config.FilterSubject = req.Config.FilterSubjects[0]
  3470→		req.Config.FilterSubjects = nil
  3471→		subject = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiConsumerCreateExT, si.name, req.Config.Name, req.Config.FilterSubject)
  3472→	} else {
  3473→		subject = fmt.Sprintf(JSApiConsumerCreateT, si.name)
  3474→	}
  3475→	if ext != nil {
  3476→		subject = strings.Replace(subject, JSApiPrefix, ext.ApiPrefix, 1)
  3477→		subject = strings.ReplaceAll(subject, "..", ".")
  3478→	}
  3479→
  3480→	// Marshal request.
  3481→	b, _ := json.Marshal(req)

  3484→	si.msgs = nil
  3485→	si.err = nil
  3486→	si.sip = true
  3487→
  3488→	// Send the consumer create request
  3489→	mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(subject, _EMPTY_, reply, nil, b, nil, 0))
  3490→
  3491→	go func() {
  3492→
  3493→		var retry bool
  3494→		defer func() {
  3495→			mset.mu.Lock()
  3496→			// Check that this is still valid and if so, clear the "setup in progress" flag.
  3497→			if si := mset.sources[iname]; si != nil {
  3498→				si.sip = false
  3499→				// If we need to retry, schedule now
  3500→				if retry {
  3501→					si.fails++

  3503→					mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)

  3505→				} else {
  3506→					// Clear on success.
  3507→					si.fails = 0
  3508→				}
  3509→			}
  3510→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  3511→		}()
  3512→

  3514→		case ccr := <-respCh:
  3515→			mset.mu.Lock()
  3516→			// Check that it has not been removed or canceled (si.sub would be nil)
  3517→			if si := mset.sources[iname]; si != nil {
  3518→				si.err = nil

  3520→					// Note: this warning can happen a few times when starting up the server when sourcing streams are
  3521→					// defined, this is normal as the streams are re-created in no particular order and it is possible
  3522→					// that a stream sourcing another could come up before all of its sources have been recreated.
  3523→					mset.srv.Warnf("JetStream error response for stream %s create source consumer %s: %+v", mset.cfg.Name, si.name, ccr.Error)
  3524→					si.err = ccr.Error
  3525→					// Let's retry as soon as possible, but we are gated by sourceConsumerRetryThreshold
  3526→					retry = true
  3527→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  3528→					return
  3529→				} else {
  3530→					// Check if our shared msg queue and go routine is running or not.
  3531→					if mset.smsgs == nil {
  3532→						qname := fmt.Sprintf("[ACC:%s] stream sources '%s' msgs", mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name)
  3533→						mset.smsgs = newIPQueue[*inMsg](mset.srv, qname)
  3534→						mset.srv.startGoRoutine(func() { mset.processAllSourceMsgs() },
  3535→							pprofLabels{
  3536→								"type":    "source",
  3537→								"account": mset.acc.Name,
  3538→								"stream":  mset.cfg.Name,
  3539→							},
  3540→						)
  3541→					}
  3542→
  3543→					// Setup actual subscription to process messages from our source.
  3544→					if si.sseq != ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream {
  3545→						si.sseq = ccr.ConsumerInfo.Delivered.Stream + 1
  3546→					}
  3547→					// Capture consumer name.
  3548→					si.cname = ccr.ConsumerInfo.Name
  3549→
  3550→					// Do not set si.sseq to seq here. si.sseq will be set in processInboundSourceMsg
  3551→					si.dseq = 0
  3552→					si.qch = make(chan struct{})
  3553→					// Set the last seen as now so that we don't fail at the first check.
  3554→					si.last.Store(time.Now().UnixNano())
  3555→
  3556→					msgs := mset.smsgs
  3557→					sub, err := mset.subscribeInternal(deliverSubject, func(sub *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  3558→						hdr, msg := c.msgParts(copyBytes(rmsg)) // Need to copy.
  3559→						mset.queueInbound(msgs, subject, reply, hdr, msg, si, nil)
  3560→						si.last.Store(time.Now().UnixNano())
  3561→					})
  3562→					if err != nil {
  3563→						si.err = NewJSSourceConsumerSetupFailedError(err, Unless(err))
  3564→						retry = true
  3565→						mset.mu.Unlock()
  3566→						return
  3567→					}
  3568→					// Save our sub.
  3569→					si.sub = sub
  3570→				}
  3571→			}
  3572→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  3573→		case <-time.After(srcConsumerWaitTime):
  3574→			mset.unsubscribe(crSub)
  3575→			// We already waited 30 seconds, let's retry now.
  3576→			retry = true
  3577→		}
  3578→	}()
  3579→}
  3580→
  3581→// This will process all inbound source msgs.
  3582→// We mux them into one go routine to avoid lock contention and high cpu and thread thrashing.
  3583→// TODO(dlc) make this more then one and pin sources to one of a group.
  3584→func (mset *stream) processAllSourceMsgs() {
  3585→	s := mset.srv
  3586→	defer s.grWG.Done()
  3587→
  3588→	mset.mu.RLock()
  3589→	msgs, qch := mset.smsgs, mset.qch
  3590→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  3591→
  3592→	t := time.NewTicker(sourceHealthCheckInterval)
  3593→	defer t.Stop()
  3594→
  3595→	// When we detect we are no longer leader, we will cleanup.
  3596→	// Should always return right after this is called.
  3597→	cleanUp := func() {
  3598→		mset.mu.Lock()
  3599→		defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  3600→		for _, si := range mset.sources {
  3601→			mset.cancelSourceConsumer(si.iname)
  3602→		}
  3603→		mset.smsgs.drain()
  3604→		mset.smsgs.unregister()
  3605→		mset.smsgs = nil
  3606→	}
  3607→
  3608→	for {
  3609→		select {
  3610→		case <-s.quitCh:
  3611→			return
  3612→		case <-qch:
  3613→			return
  3614→		case <-msgs.ch:
  3615→			ims := msgs.pop()
  3616→			for _, im := range ims {
  3617→				if !mset.processInboundSourceMsg(im.si, im) {
  3618→					// If we are no longer leader bail.
  3619→					if !mset.IsLeader() {
  3620→						msgs.recycle(&ims)
  3621→						cleanUp()
  3622→						return
  3623→					}
  3624→					break
  3625→				}
  3626→				im.returnToPool()
  3627→			}
  3628→			msgs.recycle(&ims)
  3629→		case <-t.C:
  3630→			// If we are no longer leader bail.
  3631→			if !mset.IsLeader() {
  3632→				cleanUp()
  3633→				return
  3634→			}
  3635→
  3636→			// Check health of all sources.
  3637→			var stalled []*sourceInfo
  3638→			mset.mu.RLock()
  3639→			for _, si := range mset.sources {
  3640→				if time.Since(time.Unix(0, si.last.Load())) > sourceHealthCheckInterval {
  3641→					stalled = append(stalled, si)
  3642→				}
  3643→			}
  3644→			numSources := len(mset.sources)
  3645→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
  3646→
  3647→			// This can happen on an update when no longer have sources.
  3648→			if numSources == 0 {
  3649→				cleanUp()
  3650→				return
  3651→			}
  3652→
  3653→			// We do not want to block here so do in separate Go routine.
  3654→			if len(stalled) > 0 {
  3655→				go func() {
  3656→					mset.mu.Lock()
  3657→					defer mset.mu.Unlock()
  3658→					for _, si := range stalled {
  3659→						mset.setupSourceConsumer(si.iname, si.sseq+1, time.Time{})
  3660→					}
  3661→				}()
  3662→			}
  3663→		}
  3664→	}
  3665→}
  3666→
  3667→// isControlMsg determines if this is a control message.
  3668→func (m *inMsg) isControlMsg() bool {
  3669→	return len(m.msg) == 0 && len(m.hdr) > 0 && bytes.HasPrefix(m.hdr, []byte("NATS/1.0 100 "))
  3670→}
  3671→
  3672→// Sends a reply to a flow control request.
  3673→func (mset *stream) sendFlowControlReply(reply string) {
  3674→	mset.mu.RLock()
  3675→	if mset.isLeader() && mset.outq != nil {
  3676→		mset.outq.sendMsg(reply, nil)
  3677→	}
  3678→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  3679→}
  3680→
  3681→// handleFlowControl will properly handle flow control messages for both R==1 and R>1.
  3682→// Lock should be held.
  3683→func (mset *stream) handleFlowControl(m *inMsg) {
  3684→	// If we are clustered we will send the flow control message through the replication stack.
  3685→	if mset.isClustered() {
  3686→		mset.node.Propose(encodeStreamMsg(_EMPTY_, m.rply, m.hdr, nil, 0, 0, false))
  3687→	} else {
  3688→		mset.outq.sendMsg(m.rply, nil)
  3689→	}
  3690→}
  3691→
  3692→// processInboundSourceMsg handles processing other stream messages bound for this stream.
  3693→func (mset *stream) processInboundSourceMsg(si *sourceInfo, m *inMsg) bool {
  3694→	mset.mu.Lock()
  3695→	// If we are no longer the leader cancel this subscriber.
  3696→	if !mset.isLeader() {
  3697→		mset.cancelSourceConsumer(si.iname)
  3698→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  3699→		return false
  3700→	}
  3701→
  3702→	isControl := m.isControlMsg()
  3703→
  3704→	// Ignore from old subscriptions.
  3705→	if !si.isCurrentSub(m.rply) && !isControl {
  3706→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  3707→		return false
  3708→	}
  3709→
  3710→	// Check for heartbeats and flow control messages.
  3711→	if isControl {
  3712→		var needsRetry bool
  3713→		// Flow controls have reply subjects.
  3714→		if m.rply != _EMPTY_ {
  3715→			mset.handleFlowControl(m)
  3716→		} else {
  3717→			// For idle heartbeats make sure we did not miss anything.
  3718→			if ldseq := parseInt64(getHeader(JSLastConsumerSeq, m.hdr)); ldseq > 0 && uint64(ldseq) != si.dseq {
  3719→				needsRetry = true
  3720→				mset.retrySourceConsumerAtSeq(si.iname, si.sseq+1)
  3721→			} else if fcReply := getHeader(JSConsumerStalled, m.hdr); len(fcReply) > 0 {
  3722→				// Other side thinks we are stalled, so send flow control reply.
  3723→				mset.outq.sendMsg(string(fcReply), nil)
  3724→			}
  3725→		}
  3726→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  3727→		return !needsRetry
  3728→	}
  3729→
  3730→	sseq, dseq, dc, _, pending := replyInfo(m.rply)
  3731→
  3732→	if dc > 1 {
  3733→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  3734→		return false
  3735→	}
  3736→
  3737→	// Tracking is done here.
  3738→	if dseq == si.dseq+1 {
  3739→		si.dseq++
  3740→		si.sseq = sseq
  3741→	} else if dseq > si.dseq {
  3742→		if si.cname == _EMPTY_ {
  3743→			si.cname = tokenAt(m.rply, 4)
  3744→			si.dseq, si.sseq = dseq, sseq
  3745→		} else {
  3746→			mset.retrySourceConsumerAtSeq(si.iname, si.sseq+1)
  3747→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  3748→			return false
  3749→		}
  3750→	} else {
  3751→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  3752→		return false
  3753→	}
  3754→
  3755→	if pending == 0 {
  3756→		si.lag = 0
  3757→	} else {
  3758→		si.lag = pending - 1
  3759→	}
  3760→	node := mset.node
  3761→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  3762→
  3763→	hdr, msg := m.hdr, m.msg
  3764→
  3765→	// If we are daisy chained here make sure to remove the original one.
  3766→	if len(hdr) > 0 {
  3767→		hdr = removeHeaderIfPresent(hdr, JSStreamSource)
  3768→		// Remove any Nats-Expected- headers as we don't want to validate them.
  3769→		hdr = removeHeaderIfPrefixPresent(hdr, "Nats-Expected-")
  3770→		// Remove any Nats-Batch- headers, batching is not supported when sourcing.
  3771→		hdr = removeHeaderIfPrefixPresent(hdr, "Nats-Batch-")
  3772→	}
  3773→	// Hold onto the origin reply which has all the metadata.
  3774→	hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSStreamSource, si.genSourceHeader(m.subj, m.rply))
  3775→
  3776→	// Do the subject transform for the source if there's one
  3777→	if len(si.trs) > 0 {
  3778→		for _, tr := range si.trs {
  3779→			if tr == nil {
  3780→				continue
  3781→			} else {
  3782→				tsubj, err := tr.Match(m.subj)
  3783→				if err == nil {
  3784→					m.subj = tsubj
  3785→					break
  3786→				}
  3787→			}
  3788→		}
  3789→	}
  3790→
  3791→	var err error
  3792→	// If we are clustered we need to propose this message to the underlying raft group.
  3793→	if node != nil {
  3794→		err = mset.processClusteredInboundMsg(m.subj, _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, nil, true)
  3795→	} else {
  3796→		err = mset.processJetStreamMsg(m.subj, _EMPTY_, hdr, msg, 0, 0, nil, true)
  3797→	}
  3798→
  3799→	if err != nil {
  3800→		s := mset.srv
  3801→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "no space left") {
  3802→			s.Errorf("JetStream out of space, will be DISABLED")
  3803→			s.DisableJetStream()
  3804→		} else {
  3805→			mset.mu.RLock()
  3806→			accName, sname, iName := mset.acc.Name, mset.cfg.Name, si.iname
  3807→			mset.mu.RUnlock()
  3808→
  3809→			// Can happen temporarily all the time during normal operations when the sourcing stream
  3810→			// is working queue/interest with a limit and discard new.
  3811→			// TODO - Improve sourcing to WQ with limit and new to use flow control rather than re-creating the consumer.
  3812→			if errors.Is(err, ErrMaxMsgs) || errors.Is(err, ErrMaxBytes) {
  3813→				// Do not need to do a full retry that includes finding the last sequence in the stream
  3814→				// for that source. Just re-create starting with the seq we couldn't store instead.
  3815→				mset.mu.Lock()
  3816→				mset.retrySourceConsumerAtSeq(iName, si.sseq)
  3817→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  3818→			} else {
  3819→				// Log some warning for errors other than errLastSeqMismatch or errMaxMsgs.
  3820→				if !errors.Is(err, errLastSeqMismatch) {
  3821→					s.RateLimitWarnf("Error processing inbound source %q for '%s' > '%s': %v",
  3822→						iName, accName, sname, err)
  3823→				}
  3824→				// Retry in all type of errors if we are still leader.
  3825→				if mset.isLeader() {
  3826→					// This will make sure the source is still in mset.sources map,
  3827→					// find the last sequence and then call setupSourceConsumer.
  3828→					iNameMap := map[string]struct{}{iName: {}}
  3829→					mset.setStartingSequenceForSources(iNameMap)
  3830→					mset.mu.Lock()
  3831→					mset.retrySourceConsumerAtSeq(iName, si.sseq+1)
  3832→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  3833→				}
  3834→			}
  3835→		}
  3836→		return false
  3837→	}
  3838→
  3839→	return true
  3840→}
  3841→
  3842→// Generate a new (2.10) style source header (stream name, sequence number, source filter, source destination transform).
  3843→func (si *sourceInfo) genSourceHeader(orig, reply string) string {
  3844→	var b strings.Builder
  3845→	iNameParts := strings.Split(si.iname, " ")
  3846→
  3847→	b.WriteString(iNameParts[0])
  3848→	b.WriteByte(' ')
  3849→	// Grab sequence as text here from reply subject.
  3850→	var tsa [expectedNumReplyTokens]string
  3851→	start, tokens := 0, tsa[:0]
  3852→	for i := 0; i < len(reply); i++ {
  3853→		if reply[i] == btsep {
  3854→			tokens, start = append(tokens, reply[start:i]), i+1
  3855→		}
  3856→	}
  3857→	tokens = append(tokens, reply[start:])
  3858→	seq := "1" // Default
  3859→	if len(tokens) == expectedNumReplyTokens && tokens[0] == "$JS" && tokens[1] == "ACK" {
  3860→		seq = tokens[5]
  3861→	}
  3862→	b.WriteString(seq)
  3863→
  3864→	b.WriteByte(' ')
  3865→	b.WriteString(iNameParts[1])
  3866→	b.WriteByte(' ')
  3867→	b.WriteString(iNameParts[2])
  3868→	b.WriteByte(' ')
  3869→	b.WriteString(orig)
  3870→	return b.String()
  3871→}
  3872→
  3873→// Original version of header that stored ack reply direct.
  3874→func streamAndSeqFromAckReply(reply string) (string, string, uint64) {
  3875→	tsa := [expectedNumReplyTokens]string{}
  3876→	start, tokens := 0, tsa[:0]
  3877→	for i := 0; i < len(reply); i++ {
  3878→		if reply[i] == btsep {
  3879→			tokens, start = append(tokens, reply[start:i]), i+1
  3880→		}
  3881→	}
  3882→	tokens = append(tokens, reply[start:])
  3883→	if len(tokens) != expectedNumReplyTokens || tokens[0] != "$JS" || tokens[1] != "ACK" {
  3884→		return _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, 0
  3885→	}
  3886→	return tokens[2], _EMPTY_, uint64(parseAckReplyNum(tokens[5]))
  3887→}
  3888→
  3889→// Extract the stream name, the source index name and the message sequence number from the source header.
  3890→// Uses the filter and transform arguments to provide backwards compatibility
  3891→func streamAndSeq(shdr string) (string, string, uint64) {
  3892→	if strings.HasPrefix(shdr, jsAckPre) {
  3893→		return streamAndSeqFromAckReply(shdr)
  3894→	}
  3895→	// New version which is stream index name <SPC> sequence
  3896→	fields := strings.Split(shdr, " ")
  3897→	nFields := len(fields)
  3898→
  3899→	if nFields != 2 && nFields <= 3 {
  3900→		return _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, 0
  3901→	}
  3902→
  3903→	if nFields >= 4 {
  3904→		return fields[0], strings.Join([]string{fields[0], fields[2], fields[3]}, " "), uint64(parseAckReplyNum(fields[1]))
  3905→	} else {
  3906→		return fields[0], _EMPTY_, uint64(parseAckReplyNum(fields[1]))
  3907→	}
  3908→
  3909→}
  3910→
  3911→// Lock should be held.
  3912→func (mset *stream) setStartingSequenceForSources(iNames map[string]struct{}) {
  3913→	var state StreamState
  3914→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
  3915→
  3916→	// Do not reset sseq here so we can remember when purge/expiration happens.
  3917→	if state.Msgs == 0 {
  3918→		for iName := range iNames {
  3919→			si := mset.sources[iName]
  3920→			if si == nil {
  3921→				continue
  3922→			} else {
  3923→				si.dseq = 0
  3924→			}
  3925→		}
  3926→		return
  3927→	}
  3928→
  3929→	var smv StoreMsg
  3930→	for seq := state.LastSeq; seq >= state.FirstSeq; {
  3931→		sm, err := mset.store.LoadPrevMsg(seq, &smv)
  3932→		if err == ErrStoreEOF || err != nil {
  3933→			break
  3934→		}
  3935→		seq = sm.seq - 1
  3936→		if len(sm.hdr) == 0 {
  3937→			continue
  3938→		}
  3939→
  3940→		ss := getHeader(JSStreamSource, sm.hdr)
  3941→		if len(ss) == 0 {
  3942→			continue
  3943→		}
  3944→		streamName, indexName, sseq := streamAndSeq(bytesToString(ss))
  3945→
  3946→		if _, ok := iNames[indexName]; ok {
  3947→			si := mset.sources[indexName]
  3948→			si.sseq = sseq
  3949→			si.dseq = 0
  3950→			delete(iNames, indexName)
  3951→		} else if indexName == _EMPTY_ && streamName != _EMPTY_ {
  3952→			for iName := range iNames {
  3953→				// TODO streamSource is a linear walk, to optimize later
  3954→				if si := mset.sources[iName]; si != nil && streamName == si.name ||
  3955→					(mset.streamSource(iName).External != nil && streamName == si.name+":"+getHash(mset.streamSource(iName).External.ApiPrefix)) {
  3956→					si.sseq = sseq
  3957→					si.dseq = 0
  3958→					delete(iNames, iName)
  3959→					break
  3960→				}
  3961→			}
  3962→		}
  3963→		if len(iNames) == 0 {
  3964→			break
  3965→		}
  3966→	}
  3967→}
  3968→
  3969→// Resets the SourceInfo for all the sources
  3970→// lock should be held.
  3971→func (mset *stream) resetSourceInfo() {
  3972→	// Reset if needed.
  3973→	mset.stopSourceConsumers()
  3974→	mset.sources = make(map[string]*sourceInfo)
  3975→
  3976→	for _, ssi := range mset.cfg.Sources {
  3977→		if ssi.iname == _EMPTY_ {
  3978→			ssi.setIndexName()
  3979→		}
  3980→
  3981→		var si *sourceInfo
  3982→
  3983→		if len(ssi.SubjectTransforms) == 0 {
  3984→			si = &sourceInfo{name: ssi.Name, iname: ssi.iname, sf: ssi.FilterSubject}
  3985→		} else {
  3986→			sfs := make([]string, len(ssi.SubjectTransforms))
  3987→			trs := make([]*subjectTransform, len(ssi.SubjectTransforms))
  3988→			for i, str := range ssi.SubjectTransforms {
  3989→				tr, err := NewSubjectTransform(str.Source, str.Destination)
  3990→				if err != nil {
  3991→					mset.srv.Errorf("Unable to get subject transform for source: %v", err)
  3992→				}
  3993→				sfs[i] = str.Source
  3994→				trs[i] = tr
  3995→			}
  3996→			si = &sourceInfo{name: ssi.Name, iname: ssi.iname, sfs: sfs, trs: trs}
  3997→		}
  3998→		mset.sources[ssi.iname] = si
  3999→	}
  4000→}
  4001→
  4002→// This will do a reverse scan on startup or leader election
  4003→// searching for the starting sequence number.
  4004→// This can be slow in degenerative cases.
  4005→// Lock should be held.
  4006→func (mset *stream) startingSequenceForSources() {
  4007→	if len(mset.cfg.Sources) == 0 {
  4008→		return
  4009→	}
  4010→
  4011→	// Always reset here.
  4012→	mset.resetSourceInfo()
  4013→
  4014→	var state StreamState
  4015→	mset.store.FastState(&state)
  4016→
  4017→	// Bail if no messages, meaning no context.
  4018→	if state.Msgs == 0 {
  4019→		return
  4020→	}
  4021→
  4022→	// For short circuiting return.
  4023→	expected := len(mset.cfg.Sources)
  4024→	seqs := make(map[string]uint64)
  4025→
  4026→	// Stamp our si seq records on the way out.
  4027→	defer func() {
  4028→		for sname, seq := range seqs {
  4029→			// Ignore if not set.
  4030→			if seq == 0 {
  4031→				continue
  4032→			}
  4033→			if si := mset.sources[sname]; si != nil {
  4034→				si.sseq = seq
  4035→				si.dseq = 0
  4036→			}
  4037→		}
  4038→	}()
  4039→
  4040→	update := func(iName string, seq uint64) {
  4041→		// Only update active in case we have older ones in here that got configured out.
  4042→		if si := mset.sources[iName]; si != nil {
  4043→			if _, ok := seqs[iName]; !ok {
  4044→				seqs[iName] = seq
  4045→			}
  4046→		}
  4047→	}
  4048→
  4049→	var smv StoreMsg
  4050→	for seq := state.LastSeq; ; {
  4051→		sm, err := mset.store.LoadPrevMsg(seq, &smv)
  4052→		if err == ErrStoreEOF || err != nil {
  4053→			break
  4054→		}
  4055→		seq = sm.seq - 1
  4056→		if len(sm.hdr) == 0 {
  4057→			continue
  4058→		}
  4059→		ss := getHeader(JSStreamSource, sm.hdr)
  4060→		if len(ss) == 0 {
  4061→			continue
  4062→		}
  4063→
  4064→		streamName, iName, sseq := streamAndSeq(bytesToString(ss))
  4065→		if iName == _EMPTY_ { // Pre-2.10 message header means it's a match for any source using that stream name
  4066→			for _, ssi := range mset.cfg.Sources {
  4067→				if streamName == ssi.Name || (ssi.External != nil && streamName == ssi.Name+":"+getHash(ssi.External.ApiPrefix)) {
  4068→					update(ssi.iname, sseq)
  4069→				}
  4070→			}
  4071→		} else {
  4072→			update(iName, sseq)
  4073→		}
  4074→		if len(seqs) == expected {
  4075→			return
  4076→		}
  4077→	}
  4078→}
  4079→
  4080→// Setup our source consumers.
  4081→// Lock should be held.
  4082→func (mset *stream) setupSourceConsumers() error {
  4083→	if mset.outq == nil {
  4084→		return errors.New("outq required")
  4085→	}
  4086→	// Reset if needed.
  4087→	for _, si := range mset.sources {
  4088→		if si.sub != nil {
  4089→			mset.cancelSourceConsumer(si.iname)
  4090→		}
  4091→	}
  4092→
  4093→	// If we are no longer the leader, give up
  4094→	if !mset.isLeader() {
  4095→		return nil
  4096→	}
  4097→
  4098→	mset.startingSequenceForSources()
  4099→
  4100→	// Setup our consumers at the proper starting position.
  4101→	for _, ssi := range mset.cfg.Sources {
  4102→		if si := mset.sources[ssi.iname]; si != nil {
  4103→			mset.setupSourceConsumer(ssi.iname, si.sseq+1, time.Time{})
  4104→		}
  4105→	}
  4106→
  4107→	return nil
  4108→}
  4109→
  4110→// Will create internal subscriptions for the stream.
  4111→// Lock should be held.
  4112→func (mset *stream) subscribeToStream() error {
  4113→	if mset.active {
  4114→		return nil
  4115→	}
  4116→	for _, subject := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  4117→		if _, err := mset.subscribeInternal(subject, mset.processInboundJetStreamMsg); err != nil {
  4118→			return err
  4119→		}
  4120→	}
  4121→	// Check if we need to setup mirroring.
  4122→	if mset.cfg.Mirror != nil {
  4123→		// setup the initial mirror sourceInfo
  4124→		mset.mirror = &sourceInfo{name: mset.cfg.Mirror.Name}
  4125→		sfs := make([]string, len(mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms))
  4126→		trs := make([]*subjectTransform, len(mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms))
  4127→
  4128→		for i, tr := range mset.cfg.Mirror.SubjectTransforms {
  4129→			// will not fail as already checked before that the transform will work
  4130→			subjectTransform, err := NewSubjectTransform(tr.Source, tr.Destination)
  4131→			if err != nil {
  4132→				mset.srv.Errorf("Unable to get transform for mirror consumer: %v", err)
  4133→			}
  4134→
  4135→			sfs[i] = tr.Source
  4136→			trs[i] = subjectTransform
  4137→		}
  4138→		mset.mirror.sfs = sfs
  4139→		mset.mirror.trs = trs
  4140→		// delay the actual mirror consumer creation for after a delay
  4141→		mset.scheduleSetupMirrorConsumerRetry()
  4142→	} else if len(mset.cfg.Sources) > 0 && mset.sourcesConsumerSetup == nil {
  4143→		// Setup the initial source infos for the sources
  4144→		mset.resetSourceInfo()
  4145→		// Delay the actual source consumer(s) creation(s) for after a delay if a replicated stream.
  4146→		// If it's an R1, this is done at startup and we will do inline.
  4147→		if mset.cfg.Replicas == 1 {
  4148→			mset.setupSourceConsumers()
  4149→		} else {
  4150→			mset.sourcesConsumerSetup = time.AfterFunc(time.Duration(rand.Intn(int(500*time.Millisecond)))+100*time.Millisecond, func() {
  4151→				mset.mu.Lock()
  4152→				mset.setupSourceConsumers()
  4153→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  4154→			})
  4155→		}
  4156→	}
  4157→	// Check for direct get access.
  4158→	// We spin up followers for clustered streams in monitorStream().
  4159→	if mset.cfg.AllowDirect {
  4160→		// The leader subscribes separately to a non-queue-group sub.
  4161→		if mset.cfg.Mirror == nil && mset.directLeaderSub == nil {
  4162→			dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectLeaderMsgGetT, mset.cfg.Name)
  4163→			if sub, err := mset.subscribeInternal(dsubj, mset.processDirectGetRequest); err == nil {
  4164→				mset.directLeaderSub = sub
  4165→			} else {
  4166→				return err
  4167→			}
  4168→		}
  4169→		if err := mset.subscribeToDirect(); err != nil {
  4170→			return err
  4171→		}
  4172→	}
  4173→
  4174→	mset.active = true
  4175→	return nil
  4176→}
  4177→
  4178→// Lock should be held.
  4179→func (mset *stream) subscribeToDirect() error {
  4180→	// We will make this listen on a queue group by default, which can allow mirrors to participate on opt-in basis.
  4181→	if mset.directSub == nil {
  4182→		dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectMsgGetT, mset.cfg.Name)
  4183→		if sub, err := mset.queueSubscribeInternal(dsubj, dgetGroup, mset.processDirectGetRequest); err == nil {
  4184→			mset.directSub = sub
  4185→		} else {
  4186→			return err
  4187→		}
  4188→	}
  4189→	// Now the one that will have subject appended past stream name.
  4190→	if mset.lastBySub == nil {
  4191→		dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectGetLastBySubjectT, mset.cfg.Name, fwcs)
  4192→		// We will make this listen on a queue group by default, which can allow mirrors to participate on opt-in basis.
  4193→		if sub, err := mset.queueSubscribeInternal(dsubj, dgetGroup, mset.processDirectGetLastBySubjectRequest); err == nil {
  4194→			mset.lastBySub = sub
  4195→		} else {
  4196→			return err
  4197→		}
  4198→	}
  4199→
  4200→	return nil
  4201→}
  4202→
  4203→// Lock should be held.
  4204→func (mset *stream) unsubscribeToDirect() {
  4205→	if mset.directSub != nil {
  4206→		mset.unsubscribe(mset.directSub)
  4207→		mset.directSub = nil
  4208→	}
  4209→	if mset.lastBySub != nil {
  4210→		mset.unsubscribe(mset.lastBySub)
  4211→		mset.lastBySub = nil
  4212→	}
  4213→}
  4214→
  4215→// Lock should be held.
  4216→func (mset *stream) subscribeToMirrorDirect() error {
  4217→	if mset.cfg.Mirror == nil {
  4218→		return nil
  4219→	}
  4220→	mirrorName := mset.cfg.Mirror.Name
  4221→
  4222→	// We will make this listen on a queue group by default, which can allow mirrors to participate on opt-in basis.
  4223→	if mset.mirrorDirectSub == nil {
  4224→		dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectMsgGetT, mirrorName)
  4225→		// We will make this listen on a queue group by default, which can allow mirrors to participate on opt-in basis.
  4226→		if sub, err := mset.queueSubscribeInternal(dsubj, dgetGroup, mset.processDirectGetRequest); err == nil {
  4227→			mset.mirrorDirectSub = sub
  4228→		} else {
  4229→			return err
  4230→		}
  4231→	}
  4232→	// Now the one that will have subject appended past stream name.
  4233→	if mset.mirrorLastBySub == nil {
  4234→		dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectGetLastBySubjectT, mirrorName, fwcs)
  4235→		// We will make this listen on a queue group by default, which can allow mirrors to participate on opt-in basis.
  4236→		if sub, err := mset.queueSubscribeInternal(dsubj, dgetGroup, mset.processDirectGetLastBySubjectRequest); err == nil {
  4237→			mset.mirrorLastBySub = sub
  4238→		} else {
  4239→			return err
  4240→		}
  4241→	}
  4242→
  4243→	return nil
  4244→}
  4245→
  4246→// Lock should be held.
  4247→func (mset *stream) unsubscribeToMirrorDirect() {
  4248→	if mset.mirrorDirectSub != nil {
  4249→		mset.unsubscribe(mset.mirrorDirectSub)
  4250→		mset.mirrorDirectSub = nil
  4251→	}
  4252→	if mset.mirrorLastBySub != nil {
  4253→		mset.unsubscribe(mset.mirrorLastBySub)
  4254→		mset.mirrorLastBySub = nil
  4255→	}
  4256→}
  4257→
  4258→// Stop our source consumers.
  4259→// Lock should be held.
  4260→func (mset *stream) stopSourceConsumers() {
  4261→	for _, si := range mset.sources {
  4262→		mset.cancelSourceInfo(si)
  4263→	}
  4264→}
  4265→
  4266→// Lock should be held.
  4267→func (mset *stream) removeInternalConsumer(si *sourceInfo) {
  4268→	if si == nil || si.cname == _EMPTY_ {
  4269→		return
  4270→	}
  4271→	si.cname = _EMPTY_
  4272→}
  4273→
  4274→// Will unsubscribe from the stream.
  4275→// Lock should be held.
  4276→func (mset *stream) unsubscribeToStream(stopping, shuttingDown bool) error {
  4277→	for _, subject := range mset.cfg.Subjects {
  4278→		mset.unsubscribeInternal(subject)
  4279→	}
  4280→	if mset.mirror != nil {
  4281→		mset.cancelSourceInfo(mset.mirror)
  4282→		mset.mirror = nil
  4283→	}
  4284→
  4285→	if len(mset.sources) > 0 {
  4286→		mset.stopSourceConsumers()
  4287→	}
  4288→	// Clear batching state.
  4289→	mset.deleteInflightBatches(shuttingDown)
  4290→
  4291→	if stopping {
  4292→		mset.deleteBatchApplyState()
  4293→
  4294→		// In case we had a direct get subscriptions.
  4295→		mset.unsubscribeToDirect()
  4296→		mset.unsubscribeToMirrorDirect()
  4297→	}
  4298→
  4299→	if mset.directLeaderSub == nil {
  4300→		// Always unsubscribe the leader sub.
  4301→		mset.unsubscribe(mset.directLeaderSub)
  4302→		mset.directLeaderSub = nil
  4303→	}
  4304→
  4305→	mset.active = false
  4306→	return nil
  4307→}
  4308→
  4309→// Lock should be held.
  4310→func (mset *stream) deleteInflightBatches(shuttingDown bool) {
  4311→	if mset.batches != nil {
  4312→		mset.batches.mu.Lock()
  4313→		for batchId, b := range mset.batches.group {
  4314→			// If shutting down, do fixup during startup. In-memory batches don't require manual cleanup.
  4315→			if shuttingDown {
  4316→				b.stopLocked()
  4317→			} else {
  4318→				b.cleanupLocked(batchId, mset.batches)
  4319→			}
  4320→		}
  4321→		mset.batches.mu.Unlock()
  4322→		mset.batches = nil
  4323→	}
  4324→}
  4325→
  4326→// Lock should be held.
  4327→func (mset *stream) deleteBatchApplyState() {
  4328→	if batch := mset.batchApply; batch != nil {
  4329→		// Need to return entries (if any) to the pool.
  4330→		for _, bce := range batch.entries {
  4331→			bce.ReturnToPool()
  4332→		}
  4333→		mset.batchApply = nil
  4334→	}
  4335→}

  4337→// Lock does NOT need to be held, we set the client on setup and never change it at this point.
  4338→func (mset *stream) subscribeInternal(subject string, cb msgHandler) (*subscription, error) {
  4339→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  4340→		return nil, errStreamClosed
  4341→	}
  4342→	if cb == nil {
  4343→		return nil, errInvalidMsgHandler
  4344→	}
  4345→	c := mset.client
  4346→	sid := int(mset.sid.Add(1))
  4347→	// Now create the subscription
  4348→	return c.processSub([]byte(subject), nil, []byte(strconv.Itoa(sid)), cb, false)
  4349→}
  4350→
  4351→// Lock does NOT need to be held, we set the client on setup and never change it at this point.
  4352→func (mset *stream) queueSubscribeInternal(subject, group string, cb msgHandler) (*subscription, error) {
  4353→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  4354→		return nil, errStreamClosed
  4355→	}
  4356→	if cb == nil {
  4357→		return nil, errInvalidMsgHandler
  4358→	}
  4359→	c := mset.client
  4360→	sid := int(mset.sid.Add(1))
  4361→	// Now create the subscription
  4362→	return c.processSub([]byte(subject), []byte(group), []byte(strconv.Itoa(sid)), cb, false)
  4363→}
  4364→
  4365→// This will unsubscribe us from the exact subject given.
  4366→// We do not currently track the subs so do not have the sid.
  4367→// This should be called only on an update.
  4368→// Lock does NOT need to be held, we set the client on setup and never change it at this point.
  4369→func (mset *stream) unsubscribeInternal(subject string) error {
  4370→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  4371→		return errStreamClosed
  4372→	}
  4373→	c := mset.client
  4374→	var sid []byte
  4375→	c.mu.Lock()
  4376→	for _, sub := range c.subs {
  4377→		if subject == string(sub.subject) {
  4378→			sid = sub.sid
  4379→			break
  4380→		}
  4381→	}
  4382→	c.mu.Unlock()
  4383→
  4384→	if sid != nil {
  4385→		return c.processUnsub(sid)
  4386→	}
  4387→	return nil
  4388→}
  4389→
  4390→// Lock should be held.
  4391→func (mset *stream) unsubscribe(sub *subscription) {
  4392→	if sub == nil || mset.closed.Load() {
  4393→		return
  4394→	}
  4395→	mset.client.processUnsub(sub.sid)
  4396→}
  4397→
  4398→func (mset *stream) setupStore(fsCfg *FileStoreConfig) error {
  4399→	mset.mu.Lock()
  4400→	mset.created = time.Now().UTC()
  4401→
  4402→	switch mset.cfg.Storage {
  4403→	case MemoryStorage:
  4404→		ms, err := newMemStore(&mset.cfg)
  4405→		if err != nil {
  4406→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  4407→			return err
  4408→		}
  4409→		mset.store = ms
  4410→	case FileStorage:
  4411→		s := mset.srv
  4412→		prf := s.jsKeyGen(s.getOpts().JetStreamKey, mset.acc.Name)
  4413→		if prf != nil {
  4414→			// We are encrypted here, fill in correct cipher selection.
  4415→			fsCfg.Cipher = s.getOpts().JetStreamCipher
  4416→		}
  4417→		oldprf := s.jsKeyGen(s.getOpts().JetStreamOldKey, mset.acc.Name)
  4418→		cfg := *fsCfg
  4419→		cfg.srv = s
  4420→		fs, err := newFileStoreWithCreated(cfg, mset.cfg, mset.created, prf, oldprf)
  4421→		if err != nil {
  4422→			mset.mu.Unlock()
  4423→			return err
  4424→		}
  4425→		mset.store = fs
  4426→	}
  4427→	// This will fire the callback but we do not require the lock since md will be 0 here.
  4428→	mset.store.RegisterStorageUpdates(mset.storeUpdates)
  4429→	mset.store.RegisterStorageRemoveMsg(func(seq uint64) {
  4430→		if mset.IsClustered() {
  4431→			if mset.IsLeader() {
  4432→				mset.mu.RLock()
  4433→				md := streamMsgDelete{Seq: seq, NoErase: true, Stream: mset.cfg.Name}
  4434→				mset.node.Propose(encodeMsgDelete(&md))
  4435→				mset.mu.RUnlock()
  4436→			}
  4437→		} else {
  4438→			mset.removeMsg(seq)
  4439→		}
  4440→	})
  4441→	mset.store.RegisterProcessJetStreamMsg(func(im *inMsg) {
  4442→		if mset.IsClustered() {
  4443→			if mset.IsLeader() {
  4444→				mset.processClusteredInboundMsg(im.subj, im.rply, im.hdr, im.msg, im.mt, false)
  4445→			}
  4446→		} else {
  4447→			mset.processJetStreamMsg(im.subj, im.rply, im.hdr, im.msg, 0, 0, im.mt, false)
  4448→		}
  4449→	})
  4450→	mset.mu.Unlock()
  4451→
  4452→	return nil
  4453→}
  4454→
  4455→// Called for any updates to the underlying stream. We pass through the bytes to the
  4456→// jetstream account. We do local processing for stream pending for consumers, but only
  4457→// for removals.
  4458→// Lock should not be held.
  4459→func (mset *stream) storeUpdates(md, bd int64, seq uint64, subj string) {
  4460→	// If we have a single negative update then we will process our consumers for stream pending.
  4461→	// Purge and Store handled separately inside individual calls.
  4462→	if md == -1 && seq > 0 && subj != _EMPTY_ {
  4463→		// We use our consumer list mutex here instead of the main stream lock since it may be held already.
  4464→		mset.clsMu.RLock()
  4465→		if mset.csl != nil {
  4466→			mset.csl.Match(subj, func(o *consumer) {
  4467→				o.decStreamPending(seq, subj)
  4468→			})
  4469→		} else {
  4470→			for _, o := range mset.cList {
  4471→				o.decStreamPending(seq, subj)
  4472→			}
  4473→		}
  4474→		mset.clsMu.RUnlock()
  4475→	} else if md < 0 {
  4476→		// Batch decrements we need to force consumers to re-calculate num pending.
  4477→		mset.clsMu.RLock()
  4478→		for _, o := range mset.cList {
  4479→			o.streamNumPendingLocked()
  4480→		}
  4481→		mset.clsMu.RUnlock()
  4482→	}
  4483→
  4484→	if mset.jsa != nil {
  4485→		mset.jsa.updateUsage(mset.tier, mset.stype, bd)
  4486→	}
  4487→}
  4488→
  4489→// NumMsgIds returns the number of message ids being tracked for duplicate suppression.
  4490→func (mset *stream) numMsgIds() int {
  4491→	mset.ddMu.Lock()
  4492→	defer mset.ddMu.Unlock()
  4493→	return len(mset.ddmap)
  4494→}
  4495→
  4496→// checkMsgId will process and check for duplicates.
  4497→// mset.ddMu lock should be held.
  4498→func (mset *stream) checkMsgId(id string) *ddentry {
  4499→	if id == _EMPTY_ || len(mset.ddmap) == 0 {
  4500→		return nil
  4501→	}
  4502→	return mset.ddmap[id]
  4503→}
  4504→
  4505→// Will purge the entries that are past the window.
  4506→// Should be called from a timer.
  4507→func (mset *stream) purgeMsgIds() {
  4508→	now := time.Now().UnixNano()
  4509→	mset.cfgMu.RLock()
  4510→	tmrNext := mset.cfg.Duplicates
  4511→	mset.cfgMu.RUnlock()
  4512→	window := int64(tmrNext)
  4513→
  4514→	mset.ddMu.Lock()
  4515→	defer mset.ddMu.Unlock()
  4516→
  4517→	for i, dde := range mset.ddarr[mset.ddindex:] {
  4518→		if now-dde.ts >= window {
  4519→			delete(mset.ddmap, dde.id)
  4520→		} else {
  4521→			mset.ddindex += i
  4522→			// Check if we should garbage collect here if we are 1/3 total size.
  4523→			if cap(mset.ddarr) > 3*(len(mset.ddarr)-mset.ddindex) {
  4524→				mset.ddarr = append([]*ddentry(nil), mset.ddarr[mset.ddindex:]...)
  4525→				mset.ddindex = 0
  4526→			}
  4527→			tmrNext = time.Duration(window - (now - dde.ts))
  4528→			break
  4529→		}
  4530→	}
  4531→	if len(mset.ddmap) > 0 {
  4532→		// Make sure to not fire too quick
  4533→		const minFire = 50 * time.Millisecond
  4534→		if tmrNext < minFire {
  4535→			tmrNext = minFire
  4536→		}
  4537→		if mset.ddtmr != nil {
  4538→			mset.ddtmr.Reset(tmrNext)
  4539→		} else {
  4540→			mset.ddtmr = time.AfterFunc(tmrNext, mset.purgeMsgIds)
  4541→		}
  4542→	} else {
  4543→		if mset.ddtmr != nil {
  4544→			mset.ddtmr.Stop()
  4545→			mset.ddtmr = nil
  4546→		}
  4547→		mset.ddmap = nil
  4548→		mset.ddarr = nil
  4549→		mset.ddindex = 0
  4550→	}
  4551→}
  4552→
  4553→// storeMsgId will store the message id for duplicate detection.
  4554→func (mset *stream) storeMsgId(dde *ddentry) {
  4555→	mset.ddMu.Lock()
  4556→	defer mset.ddMu.Unlock()
  4557→	mset.storeMsgIdLocked(dde)
  4558→}
  4559→
  4560→// storeMsgIdLocked will store the message id for duplicate detection.
  4561→// mset.ddMu lock should be held.
  4562→func (mset *stream) storeMsgIdLocked(dde *ddentry) {
  4563→	if mset.ddmap == nil {
  4564→		mset.ddmap = make(map[string]*ddentry)
  4565→	}
  4566→	mset.ddmap[dde.id] = dde
  4567→	mset.ddarr = append(mset.ddarr, dde)
  4568→	if mset.ddtmr == nil {
  4569→		mset.ddtmr = time.AfterFunc(mset.cfg.Duplicates, mset.purgeMsgIds)
  4570→	}
  4571→}

  4573→// Fast lookup of msgId.
  4574→func getMsgId(hdr []byte) string {
  4575→	return string(getHeader(JSMsgId, hdr))
  4576→}

  4578→// Fast lookup of expected last msgId.
  4579→func getExpectedLastMsgId(hdr []byte) string {
  4580→	return string(getHeader(JSExpectedLastMsgId, hdr))
  4581→}

  4583→// Fast lookup of expected stream.
  4584→func getExpectedStream(hdr []byte) string {

  4588→// Fast lookup of expected last sequence.
  4589→func getExpectedLastSeq(hdr []byte) (uint64, bool) {
  4590→	bseq := sliceHeader(JSExpectedLastSeq, hdr)
  4591→	if len(bseq) == 0 {
  4592→		return 0, false
  4593→	}
  4594→	return uint64(parseInt64(bseq)), true
  4595→}
  4596→
  4597→// Fast lookup of rollups.
  4598→func getRollup(hdr []byte) string {
  4599→	r := getHeader(JSMsgRollup, hdr)
  4600→	if len(r) == 0 {
  4601→		return _EMPTY_
  4602→	}
  4603→	return strings.ToLower(string(r))
  4604→}
  4605→
  4606→// Fast lookup of expected stream sequence per subject.
  4607→func getExpectedLastSeqPerSubject(hdr []byte) (uint64, bool) {
  4608→	bseq := sliceHeader(JSExpectedLastSubjSeq, hdr)
  4609→	if len(bseq) == 0 {
  4610→		return 0, false
  4611→	}
  4612→	return uint64(parseInt64(bseq)), true
  4613→}
  4614→
  4615→// Fast lookup of expected subject for the expected stream sequence per subject.
  4616→func getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr []byte) string {
  4617→	return bytesToString(sliceHeader(JSExpectedLastSubjSeqSubj, hdr))
  4618→}
  4619→
  4620→// Fast lookup of the message TTL from headers:
  4621→// - Positive return value: duration in seconds.
  4622→// - Zero return value: no TTL or parse error.
  4623→// - Negative return value: never expires.
  4624→func getMessageTTL(hdr []byte) (int64, error) {
  4625→	ttl := getHeader(JSMessageTTL, hdr)
  4626→	if len(ttl) == 0 {
  4627→		return 0, nil
  4628→	}
  4629→	return parseMessageTTL(bytesToString(ttl))
  4630→}
  4631→
  4632→// - Positive return value: duration in seconds.
  4633→// - Zero return value: no TTL or parse error.
  4634→// - Negative return value: never expires.
  4635→func parseMessageTTL(ttl string) (int64, error) {
  4636→	if strings.ToLower(ttl) == "never" {
  4637→		return -1, nil
  4638→	}
  4639→	dur, err := time.ParseDuration(ttl)
  4640→	if err == nil {
  4641→		if dur < time.Second {
  4642→			return 0, NewJSMessageTTLInvalidError()
  4643→		}
  4644→		return int64(dur.Seconds()), nil
  4645→	}
  4646→	t := parseInt64(stringToBytes(ttl))
  4647→	if t < 0 {
  4648→		// This probably means a parse failure, hence why
  4649→		// we have a special case "never" for returning -1.
  4650→		// Otherwise we can't know if it's a genuine TTL
  4651→		// that says never expire or if it's a parse error.
  4652→		return 0, NewJSMessageTTLInvalidError()
  4653→	}
  4654→	return t, nil
  4655→}
  4656→
  4657→// Fast lookup of the message Incr from headers.
  4658→// Return includes the value or nil, and success.
  4659→func getMessageIncr(hdr []byte) (*big.Int, bool) {
  4660→	incr := sliceHeader(JSMessageIncr, hdr)
  4661→	if len(incr) == 0 {
  4662→		return nil, true
  4663→	}
  4664→	var v big.Int
  4665→	return v.SetString(bytesToString(incr), 10)
  4666→}
  4667→
  4668→// Fast lookup of message schedule.
  4669→func getMessageSchedule(hdr []byte) (time.Time, bool) {
  4670→	if len(hdr) == 0 {
  4671→		return time.Time{}, true
  4672→	}
  4673→	val := bytesToString(sliceHeader(JSSchedulePattern, hdr))
  4674→	if val == _EMPTY_ {
  4675→		return time.Time{}, true
  4676→	}
  4677→	if !strings.HasPrefix(val, "@at ") {
  4678→		return time.Time{}, false
  4679→	}
  4680→	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val[4:])
  4681→	return t, err == nil
  4682→}
  4683→
  4684→// Fast lookup of the message schedule TTL from headers.
  4685→// The TTL is confirmed to be valid, but the raw TTL string is returned.
  4686→func getMessageScheduleTTL(hdr []byte) (string, bool) {
  4687→	ttl := getHeader(JSScheduleTTL, hdr)
  4688→	if len(ttl) == 0 {
  4689→		return _EMPTY_, true
  4690→	}
  4691→	if _, err := parseMessageTTL(bytesToString(ttl)); err != nil {
  4692→		return _EMPTY_, false
  4693→	}
  4694→	return string(ttl), true
  4695→}
  4696→
  4697→// Fast lookup of message schedule target.
  4698→func getMessageScheduleTarget(hdr []byte) string {
  4699→	if len(hdr) == 0 {
  4700→		return _EMPTY_
  4701→	}
  4702→	return string(getHeader(JSScheduleTarget, hdr))
  4703→}
  4704→
  4705→// Fast lookup of message scheduler.
  4706→func getMessageScheduler(hdr []byte) string {
  4707→	if len(hdr) == 0 {
  4708→		return _EMPTY_
  4709→	}
  4710→	return string(getHeader(JSScheduler, hdr))
  4711→}
  4712→
  4713→// Fast lookup of batch ID.
  4714→func getBatchId(hdr []byte) string {
  4715→	if len(hdr) == 0 {
  4716→		return _EMPTY_
  4717→	}
  4718→	return string(getHeader(JSBatchId, hdr))
  4719→}
  4720→
  4721→// Fast lookup of batch sequence.
  4722→func getBatchSequence(hdr []byte) (uint64, bool) {
  4723→	bseq := sliceHeader(JSBatchSeq, hdr)
  4724→	if len(bseq) == 0 {
  4725→		return 0, false
  4726→	}
  4727→	return uint64(parseInt64(bseq)), true
  4728→}

  4730→// Signal if we are clustered. Will acquire rlock.
  4731→func (mset *stream) IsClustered() bool {
  4732→	mset.mu.RLock()
  4733→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  4734→	return mset.isClustered()
  4735→}
  4736→
  4737→// Lock should be held.
  4738→func (mset *stream) isClustered() bool {
  4739→	return mset.node != nil
  4740→}

  4742→// Used if we have to queue things internally to avoid the route/gw path.
  4743→type inMsg struct {
  4744→	subj string
  4745→	rply string
  4746→	hdr  []byte
  4747→	msg  []byte
  4748→	si   *sourceInfo
  4749→	mt   *msgTrace
  4750→	seq  uint64 // seq that can be optionally used for sorting out-of-band.
  4751→}
  4752→
  4753→var inMsgPool = sync.Pool{
  4754→	New: func() any {
  4755→		return &inMsg{}
  4756→	},
  4757→}
  4758→
  4759→func (im *inMsg) returnToPool() {
  4760→	im.subj, im.rply, im.hdr, im.msg, im.si, im.mt = _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, nil, nil, nil, nil
  4761→	inMsgPool.Put(im)
  4762→}
  4763→
  4764→func (mset *stream) queueInbound(ib *ipQueue[*inMsg], subj, rply string, hdr, msg []byte, si *sourceInfo, mt *msgTrace) {
  4765→	im := inMsgPool.Get().(*inMsg)
  4766→	im.subj, im.rply, im.hdr, im.msg, im.si, im.mt = subj, rply, hdr, msg, si, mt
  4767→	if _, err := ib.push(im); err != nil {
  4768→		im.returnToPool()
  4769→		streamName := mset.cfg.Name
  4770→		mset.srv.RateLimitWarnf("Dropping messages due to excessive stream ingest rate on '%s' > '%s': %s", mset.acc.Name, streamName, err)
  4771→		if rply != _EMPTY_ {
  4772→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 429 Too Many Requests\r\n\r\n")
  4773→			b, _ := json.Marshal(&JSPubAckResponse{PubAck: &PubAck{Stream: streamName}, Error: NewJSStreamTooManyRequestsError()})
  4774→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(rply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, b, nil, 0))
  4775→		}
  4776→	}
  4777→}
  4778→
  4779→var dgPool = sync.Pool{
  4780→	New: func() any {
  4781→		return &directGetReq{}
  4782→	},
  4783→}
  4784→
  4785→// For when we need to not inline the request.
  4786→type directGetReq struct {
  4787→	// Copy of this is correct for this.
  4788→	req   JSApiMsgGetRequest
  4789→	reply string
  4790→}
  4791→
  4792→// processDirectGetRequest handles direct get request for stream messages.
  4793→func (mset *stream) processDirectGetRequest(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  4794→	if len(reply) == 0 {
  4795→		return
  4796→	}
  4797→	hdr, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  4798→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  4799→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Required Api Level\r\n\r\n")
  4800→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4801→		return
  4802→	}
  4803→	if len(msg) == 0 {
  4804→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Empty Request\r\n\r\n")
  4805→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4806→		return
  4807→	}
  4808→	var req JSApiMsgGetRequest
  4809→	err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &req)
  4810→	if err != nil {
  4811→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Malformed Request\r\n\r\n")
  4812→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4813→		return
  4814→	}
  4815→	// Check if nothing set.
  4816→	if req.Seq == 0 && req.LastFor == _EMPTY_ && req.NextFor == _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) == 0 && req.StartTime == nil {
  4817→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Empty Request\r\n\r\n")
  4818→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4819→		return
  4820→	}
  4821→	// Check we don't have conflicting options set.
  4822→	// We do not allow batch mode for lastFor requests.
  4823→	if (req.Seq > 0 && req.LastFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4824→		(req.Seq > 0 && req.StartTime != nil) ||
  4825→		(req.StartTime != nil && req.LastFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4826→		(req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && req.NextFor != _EMPTY_) ||
  4827→		(req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && req.Batch > 0) ||
  4828→		(req.LastFor != _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0) ||
  4829→		(req.NextFor != _EMPTY_ && len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0) ||
  4830→		(req.UpToSeq > 0 && req.UpToTime != nil) {
  4831→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Bad Request\r\n\r\n")
  4832→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4833→		return
  4834→	}
  4835→
  4836→	// Reject request if we can't guarantee the precondition of min last sequence.
  4837→	if req.MinLastSeq > 0 && mset.lastSeq() < req.MinLastSeq {
  4838→		// We are not up-to-date yet, and don't know how long it will take us to be.
  4839→
  4840→		// If we're not the leader, we can redirect to the leader for them to answer.
  4841→		// But only on the original stream, mirrors only error.
  4842→		if !mset.isMirror() && !mset.isLeaderNodeState() {
  4843→			// Copy since we're sending the same bytes.
  4844→			msg = copyBytes(msg)
  4845→			dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectLeaderMsgGetT, mset.getCfgName())
  4846→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(dsubj, _EMPTY_, reply, nil, msg, nil, 0))
  4847→			return
  4848→		}
  4849→
  4850→		// Otherwise, simply reject the request so the client can retry.
  4851→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Min Last Sequence\r\n\r\n")
  4852→		mset.srv.sendDelayedErrResponse(mset.account(), reply, hdr, _EMPTY_, errRespDelay)
  4853→		return
  4854→	}
  4855→
  4856→	inlineOk := c.kind != ROUTER && c.kind != GATEWAY && c.kind != LEAF
  4857→	if !inlineOk {
  4858→		dg := dgPool.Get().(*directGetReq)
  4859→		dg.req, dg.reply = req, reply
  4860→		mset.gets.push(dg)
  4861→	} else {
  4862→		mset.getDirectRequest(&req, reply)
  4863→	}
  4864→}
  4865→
  4866→// This is for direct get by last subject which is part of the subject itself.
  4867→func (mset *stream) processDirectGetLastBySubjectRequest(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  4868→	if len(reply) == 0 {
  4869→		return
  4870→	}
  4871→	var minLastSeq uint64
  4872→	hdr, msg := c.msgParts(rmsg)
  4873→	if errorOnRequiredApiLevel(hdr) {
  4874→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Required Api Level\r\n\r\n")
  4875→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4876→		return
  4877→	}
  4878→
  4879→	// This version expects no payload, unless min last seq is specified.
  4880→	if len(msg) != 0 {
  4881→		var req JSApiMsgGetRequest
  4882→		err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &req)
  4883→		if err != nil {
  4884→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Malformed Request\r\n\r\n")
  4885→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4886→			return
  4887→		}
  4888→
  4889→		if req.MinLastSeq == 0 {
  4890→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Bad Request\r\n\r\n")
  4891→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4892→			return
  4893→		}
  4894→		minLastSeq = req.MinLastSeq
  4895→	}
  4896→	// Extract the key.
  4897→	var key string
  4898→	for i, n := 0, 0; i < len(subject); i++ {
  4899→		if subject[i] == btsep {
  4900→			if n == 4 {
  4901→				if start := i + 1; start < len(subject) {
  4902→					key = subject[i+1:]
  4903→				}
  4904→				break
  4905→			}
  4906→			n++
  4907→		}
  4908→	}
  4909→	if len(key) == 0 {
  4910→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 408 Bad Request\r\n\r\n")
  4911→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4912→		return
  4913→	}
  4914→
  4915→	req := JSApiMsgGetRequest{LastFor: key, MinLastSeq: minLastSeq}
  4916→
  4917→	// Reject request if we can't guarantee the precondition of min last sequence.
  4918→	if req.MinLastSeq > 0 && mset.lastSeq() < req.MinLastSeq {
  4919→		// We are not up-to-date yet, and don't know how long it will take us to be.
  4920→
  4921→		// If we're not the leader, we can redirect to the leader for them to answer.
  4922→		// But only on the original stream, mirrors only error.
  4923→		if !mset.isMirror() && !mset.isLeaderNodeState() {
  4924→			dsubj := fmt.Sprintf(JSDirectLeaderMsgGetT, mset.getCfgName())
  4925→			msg, _ = json.Marshal(req)
  4926→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(dsubj, _EMPTY_, reply, nil, msg, nil, 0))
  4927→			return
  4928→		}
  4929→
  4930→		// Otherwise, simply reject the request so the client can retry.
  4931→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 412 Min Last Sequence\r\n\r\n")
  4932→		mset.srv.sendDelayedErrResponse(mset.account(), reply, hdr, _EMPTY_, errRespDelay)
  4933→		return
  4934→	}
  4935→
  4936→	inlineOk := c.kind != ROUTER && c.kind != GATEWAY && c.kind != LEAF
  4937→	if !inlineOk {
  4938→		dg := dgPool.Get().(*directGetReq)
  4939→		dg.req, dg.reply = req, reply
  4940→		mset.gets.push(dg)
  4941→	} else {
  4942→		mset.getDirectRequest(&req, reply)
  4943→	}
  4944→}
  4945→
  4946→// For direct get batch and multi requests.
  4947→const (
  4948→	dg   = "NATS/1.0\r\nNats-Stream: %s\r\nNats-Subject: %s\r\nNats-Sequence: %d\r\nNats-Time-Stamp: %s\r\n\r\n"
  4949→	dgb  = "NATS/1.0\r\nNats-Stream: %s\r\nNats-Subject: %s\r\nNats-Sequence: %d\r\nNats-Time-Stamp: %s\r\nNats-Num-Pending: %d\r\nNats-Last-Sequence: %d\r\n\r\n"
  4950→	eob  = "NATS/1.0 204 EOB\r\nNats-Num-Pending: %d\r\nNats-Last-Sequence: %d\r\n\r\n"
  4951→	eobm = "NATS/1.0 204 EOB\r\nNats-Num-Pending: %d\r\nNats-Last-Sequence: %d\r\nNats-UpTo-Sequence: %d\r\n\r\n"
  4952→)
  4953→
  4954→// Handle a multi request.
  4955→func (mset *stream) getDirectMulti(req *JSApiMsgGetRequest, reply string) {
  4956→	// TODO(dlc) - Make configurable?
  4957→	const maxAllowedResponses = 1024
  4958→
  4959→	// We hold the lock here to try to avoid changes out from underneath of us.
  4960→	mset.mu.RLock()
  4961→	defer mset.mu.RUnlock()
  4962→	// Grab store and name.
  4963→	store, name, s := mset.store, mset.cfg.Name, mset.srv
  4964→
  4965→	// Grab MaxBytes
  4966→	mb := req.MaxBytes
  4967→	if mb == 0 && s != nil {
  4968→		// Fill in with the server's MaxPending.
  4969→		mb = int(s.opts.MaxPending)
  4970→	}
  4971→
  4972→	upToSeq := req.UpToSeq
  4973→	// If we have UpToTime set get the proper sequence.
  4974→	if req.UpToTime != nil {
  4975→		upToSeq = store.GetSeqFromTime((*req.UpToTime).UTC())
  4976→		// Avoid selecting a first sequence that will take us to before the stream first
  4977→		// sequence, otherwise we can return messages after the supplied UpToTime.
  4978→		if upToSeq <= mset.state().FirstSeq {
  4979→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 No Results\r\n\r\n")
  4980→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  4981→			return
  4982→		}
  4983→		// We need to back off one since this is used to determine start sequence normally,
  4984→		// whereas here we want it to be the ceiling.
  4985→		upToSeq--
  4986→	}
  4987→	// If not set, set to the last sequence and remember that for EOB.
  4988→	if upToSeq == 0 {
  4989→		var state StreamState
  4990→		mset.store.FastState(&state)
  4991→		upToSeq = state.LastSeq
  4992→	}
  4993→
  4994→	seqs, err := store.MultiLastSeqs(req.MultiLastFor, upToSeq, maxAllowedResponses)
  4995→	if err != nil {
  4996→		var hdr []byte
  4997→		if err == ErrTooManyResults {
  4998→			hdr = []byte("NATS/1.0 413 Too Many Results\r\n\r\n")
  4999→		} else {
  5000→			hdr = []byte(fmt.Sprintf("NATS/1.0 500 %v\r\n\r\n", err))
  5001→		}
  5002→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5003→		return
  5004→	}
  5005→	if len(seqs) == 0 {
  5006→		hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 No Results\r\n\r\n")
  5007→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5008→		return
  5009→	}
  5010→
  5011→	np, lseq, sentBytes, sent := uint64(len(seqs)-1), uint64(0), 0, 0
  5012→	for _, seq := range seqs {
  5013→		if seq < req.Seq {
  5014→			if np > 0 {
  5015→				np--
  5016→			}
  5017→			continue
  5018→		}
  5019→		var svp StoreMsg
  5020→		sm, err := store.LoadMsg(seq, &svp)
  5021→		if err != nil {
  5022→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 Message Not Found\r\n\r\n")
  5023→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5024→			return
  5025→		}
  5026→
  5027→		hdr := sm.hdr
  5028→		ts := time.Unix(0, sm.ts).UTC()
  5029→
  5030→		if len(hdr) == 0 {
  5031→			hdr = fmt.Appendf(nil, dgb, name, sm.subj, sm.seq, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), np, lseq)
  5032→		} else {
  5033→			hdr = copyBytes(hdr)
  5034→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSStream, name)
  5035→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSubject, sm.subj)
  5036→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSequence, strconv.FormatUint(sm.seq, 10))
  5037→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSTimeStamp, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
  5038→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSNumPending, strconv.FormatUint(np, 10))
  5039→			hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSLastSequence, strconv.FormatUint(lseq, 10))
  5040→		}
  5041→		// Decrement num pending. This is optimization and we do not continue to look it up for these operations.
  5042→		if np > 0 {
  5043→			np--
  5044→		}
  5045→		// Track our lseq
  5046→		lseq = sm.seq
  5047→		// Send out our message.
  5048→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, sm.msg, nil, 0))
  5049→		// Check if we have exceeded max bytes.
  5050→		sentBytes += len(sm.subj) + len(sm.hdr) + len(sm.msg)
  5051→		if sentBytes >= mb {
  5052→			break
  5053→		}
  5054→		sent++
  5055→		if req.Batch > 0 && sent >= req.Batch {
  5056→			break
  5057→		}
  5058→	}
  5059→
  5060→	// Send out EOB
  5061→	hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, eobm, np, lseq, upToSeq)
  5062→	mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5063→}
  5064→
  5065→// Do actual work on a direct msg request.
  5066→// This could be called in a Go routine if we are inline for a non-client connection.
  5067→func (mset *stream) getDirectRequest(req *JSApiMsgGetRequest, reply string) {
  5068→	// Handle multi in separate function.
  5069→	if len(req.MultiLastFor) > 0 {
  5070→		mset.getDirectMulti(req, reply)
  5071→		return
  5072→	}
  5073→
  5074→	mset.mu.RLock()
  5075→	store, name, s := mset.store, mset.cfg.Name, mset.srv
  5076→	mset.mu.RUnlock()
  5077→
  5078→	var seq uint64
  5079→	// Lookup start seq if AsOfTime is set.
  5080→	if req.StartTime != nil {
  5081→		seq = store.GetSeqFromTime(*req.StartTime)
  5082→	} else {
  5083→		seq = req.Seq
  5084→	}
  5085→
  5086→	wc := subjectHasWildcard(req.NextFor)
  5087→	// For tracking num pending if we are batch.
  5088→	var np, lseq, validThrough uint64
  5089→	var isBatchRequest bool
  5090→	batch := req.Batch
  5091→	if batch == 0 {
  5092→		batch = 1
  5093→	} else {
  5094→		// This is a batch request, capture initial numPending.
  5095→		isBatchRequest = true
  5096→		np, validThrough = store.NumPending(seq, req.NextFor, false)
  5097→	}
  5098→
  5099→	// Grab MaxBytes
  5100→	mb := req.MaxBytes
  5101→	if mb == 0 && s != nil {
  5102→		// Fill in with the server's MaxPending.
  5103→		mb = int(s.opts.MaxPending)
  5104→	}
  5105→	// Track what we have sent.
  5106→	var sentBytes int
  5107→
  5108→	// Loop over batch, which defaults to 1.
  5109→	for i := 0; i < batch; i++ {
  5110→		var (
  5111→			svp StoreMsg
  5112→			sm  *StoreMsg
  5113→			err error
  5114→		)
  5115→		if seq > 0 && req.NextFor == _EMPTY_ {
  5116→			// Only do direct lookup for first in a batch.
  5117→			if i == 0 {
  5118→				sm, err = store.LoadMsg(seq, &svp)
  5119→			} else {
  5120→				// We want to use load next with fwcs to step over deleted msgs.
  5121→				sm, seq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(fwcs, true, seq, &svp)
  5122→			}
  5123→			// Bump for next loop if applicable.
  5124→			seq++
  5125→		} else if req.NextFor != _EMPTY_ {
  5126→			sm, seq, err = store.LoadNextMsg(req.NextFor, wc, seq, &svp)
  5127→			seq++
  5128→		} else {
  5129→			// Batch is not applicable here, this is checked before we get here.
  5130→			sm, err = store.LoadLastMsg(req.LastFor, &svp)
  5131→		}
  5132→		if err != nil {
  5133→			// For batches, if we stop early we want to do EOB logic below.
  5134→			if batch > 1 && i > 0 {
  5135→				break
  5136→			}
  5137→			hdr := []byte("NATS/1.0 404 Message Not Found\r\n\r\n")
  5138→			mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5139→			return
  5140→		}
  5141→
  5142→		ts := time.Unix(0, sm.ts).UTC()
  5143→		var hdr []byte
  5144→		if !req.NoHeaders {
  5145→			hdr = sm.hdr
  5146→			if isBatchRequest {
  5147→				if len(hdr) == 0 {
  5148→					hdr = fmt.Appendf(nil, dgb, name, sm.subj, sm.seq, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), np, lseq)
  5149→				} else {
  5150→					hdr = copyBytes(hdr)
  5151→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSStream, name)
  5152→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSubject, sm.subj)
  5153→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSequence, strconv.FormatUint(sm.seq, 10))
  5154→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSTimeStamp, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
  5155→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSNumPending, strconv.FormatUint(np, 10))
  5156→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSLastSequence, strconv.FormatUint(lseq, 10))
  5157→				}
  5158→				// Decrement num pending. This is optimization and we do not continue to look it up for these operations.
  5159→				np--
  5160→			} else {
  5161→				if len(hdr) == 0 {
  5162→					hdr = fmt.Appendf(nil, dg, name, sm.subj, sm.seq, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
  5163→				} else {
  5164→					hdr = copyBytes(hdr)
  5165→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSStream, name)
  5166→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSubject, sm.subj)
  5167→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSSequence, strconv.FormatUint(sm.seq, 10))
  5168→					hdr = genHeader(hdr, JSTimeStamp, ts.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
  5169→				}
  5170→			}
  5171→		}
  5172→		// Track our lseq
  5173→		lseq = sm.seq
  5174→		// Send out our message.
  5175→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, sm.msg, nil, 0))
  5176→		// Check if we have exceeded max bytes.
  5177→		sentBytes += len(sm.subj) + len(sm.hdr) + len(sm.msg)
  5178→		if sentBytes >= mb {
  5179→			break
  5180→		}
  5181→	}
  5182→
  5183→	// If batch was requested send EOB.
  5184→	if isBatchRequest {
  5185→		// Update if the stream's lasts sequence has moved past our validThrough.
  5186→		if mset.lastSeq() > validThrough {
  5187→			np, _ = store.NumPending(seq, req.NextFor, false)
  5188→		}
  5189→		hdr := fmt.Appendf(nil, eob, np, lseq)
  5190→		mset.outq.send(newJSPubMsg(reply, _EMPTY_, _EMPTY_, hdr, nil, nil, 0))
  5191→	}
  5192→}
  5193→
  5194→// processInboundJetStreamMsg handles processing messages bound for a stream.
  5195→func (mset *stream) processInboundJetStreamMsg(_ *subscription, c *client, _ *Account, subject, reply string, rmsg []byte) {
  5196→	hdr, msg := c.msgParts(copyBytes(rmsg)) // Need to copy.
  5197→	if mt, traceOnly := c.isMsgTraceEnabled(); mt != nil {
  5198→		// If message is delivered, we need to disable the message trace headers
  5199→		// to prevent a trace event to be generated when a stored message
  5200→		// is delivered to a consumer and routed.
  5201→		if !traceOnly {
  5202→			disableTraceHeaders(c, hdr)
  5203→		}
  5204→		// This will add the jetstream event while in the client read loop.
  5205→		// Since the event will be updated in a different go routine, the
  5206→		// tracing object will have a separate reference to the JS trace
  5207→		// object.
  5208→		mt.addJetStreamEvent(mset.name())
  5209→	}
  5210→	mset.queueInbound(mset.msgs, subject, reply, hdr, msg, nil, c.pa.trace)
  5211→}
  5212→
  5213→var (
  5214→	errLastSeqMismatch   = errors.New("last sequence mismatch")
  5215→	errMsgIdDuplicate    = errors.New("msgid is duplicate")
  5216→	errStreamClosed      = errors.New("stream closed")
  5217→	errInvalidMsgHandler = errors.New("undefined message handler")
  5218→	errStreamMismatch    = errors.New("expected stream does not match")
  5219→	errMsgTTLDisabled    = errors.New("message TTL disabled")
  5220→)
  5221→
  5222→// processJetStreamMsg is where we try to actually process the stream msg.
  5223→func (mset *stream) processJetStreamMsg(subject, reply string, hdr, msg []byte, lseq uint64, ts int64, mt *msgTrace, sourced bool) (retErr error) {
  5224→	if mt != nil {
  5225→		// Only the leader/standalone will have mt!=nil. On exit, send the
  5226→		// message trace event.
  5227→		defer func() {
  5228→			mt.sendEventFromJetStream(retErr)
  5229→		}()
  5230→	}
  5231→
  5232→	if mset.closed.Load() {
  5233→		return errStreamClosed
  5234→	}
  5235→
  5236→	mset.mu.Lock()
  5237→	s, store := mset.srv, mset.store
  5238→
  5239→	traceOnly := mt.traceOnly()
  5240→	bumpCLFS := func() {
  5241→		// Do not bump if tracing and not doing message delivery.
  5242→		if traceOnly {
  5243→			return
  5244→		}
  5245→		mset.clMu.Lock()
  5246→		mset.clfs++
  5247→		mset.clMu.Unlock()
  5248→	}
  5249→
  5250→	// Apply the input subject transform if any
  5251→	if mset.itr != nil {
  5252→		ts, err := mset.itr.Match(subject)
  5253→		if err == nil {
  5254→			// no filtering: if the subject doesn't map the source of the transform, don't change it
  5255→			subject = ts
  5256→		}
  5257→	}
  5258→
  5259→	var accName string
  5260→	if mset.acc != nil {
  5261→		accName = mset.acc.Name
  5262→	}
  5263→
  5264→	js, jsa, doAck := mset.js, mset.jsa, !mset.cfg.NoAck
  5265→	name, stype := mset.cfg.Name, mset.cfg.Storage
  5266→	maxMsgSize := int(mset.cfg.MaxMsgSize)
  5267→	numConsumers := len(mset.consumers)
  5268→	interestRetention := mset.cfg.Retention == InterestPolicy
  5269→	allowMsgCounter, allowMsgSchedules := mset.cfg.AllowMsgCounter, mset.cfg.AllowMsgSchedules
  5270→	// Snapshot if we are the leader and if we can respond.
  5271→	isLeader, isSealed := mset.isLeaderNodeState(), mset.cfg.Sealed
  5272→	isClustered := mset.isClustered()
  5273→	canConsistencyCheck := !isClustered || traceOnly
  5274→	canRespond := doAck && len(reply) > 0 && isLeader
  5275→	outq := mset.outq
  5276→
  5277→	var resp = &JSPubAckResponse{}
  5278→
  5279→	var batchId string
  5280→	var batchSeq uint64
  5281→	if len(hdr) > 0 {
  5282→		// Populate batch details.
  5283→		if batchId = getBatchId(hdr); batchId != _EMPTY_ {
  5284→			batchSeq, _ = getBatchSequence(hdr)
  5285→			// Disable consistency checking if this was already done
  5286→			// earlier as part of the batch consistency check.
  5287→			canConsistencyCheck = traceOnly
  5288→		}
  5289→	}
  5290→
  5291→	// Bail here if sealed.
  5292→	if canConsistencyCheck && isSealed {
  5293→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  5294→		if canRespond && outq != nil {
  5295→			resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5296→			resp.Error = ApiErrors[JSStreamSealedErr]
  5297→			b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5298→			outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5299→		}
  5300→		return ApiErrors[JSStreamSealedErr]
  5301→	}
  5302→
  5303→	var buf [256]byte
  5304→	pubAck := append(buf[:0], mset.pubAck...)
  5305→
  5306→	// If this is a non-clustered msg and we are not considered active, meaning no active subscription, do not process.
  5307→	if lseq == 0 && ts == 0 && !mset.active {
  5308→		mset.mu.Unlock()
  5309→		return nil
  5310→	}
  5311→
  5312→	// For clustering the lower layers will pass our expected lseq. If it is present check for that here.
  5313→	var clfs uint64
  5314→	if lseq > 0 {
  5315→		clfs = mset.getCLFS()
  5316→		if lseq != (mset.lseq + clfs) {
  5317→			isMisMatch := true
  5318→			// We may be able to recover here if we have no state whatsoever, or we are a mirror.
  5319→			// See if we have to adjust our starting sequence.
  5320→			if mset.lseq == 0 || mset.cfg.Mirror != nil {
  5321→				var state StreamState
  5322→				mset.store.FastState(&state)
  5323→				if state.FirstSeq == 0 {
  5324→					mset.store.Compact(lseq + 1)
  5325→					mset.lseq = lseq
  5326→					isMisMatch = false
  5327→				}
  5328→			}
  5329→			// Really is a mismatch.
  5330→			if isMisMatch {
  5331→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5332→				if canRespond && outq != nil {
  5333→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5334→					resp.Error = ApiErrors[JSStreamSequenceNotMatchErr]
  5335→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5336→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5337→				}
  5338→				return errLastSeqMismatch
  5339→			}
  5340→		}
  5341→	}
  5342→
  5343→	// If we have received this message across an account we may have request information attached.
  5344→	// For now remove. TODO(dlc) - Should this be opt-in or opt-out?
  5345→	if len(hdr) > 0 {
  5346→		hdr = removeHeaderIfPresent(hdr, ClientInfoHdr)
  5347→	}
  5348→
  5349→	// Process additional msg headers if still present.
  5350→	var msgId string
  5351→	var incr *big.Int
  5352→	var rollupSub, rollupAll bool
  5353→
  5354→	if len(hdr) > 0 {
  5355→		// Certain checks have already been performed if in clustered mode, so only check if not.
  5356→		// Note, for cluster mode but with message tracing (without message delivery), we need
  5357→		// to do this check here since it was not done in processClusteredInboundMsg().
  5358→		if canConsistencyCheck {
  5359→			// Counter increments.
  5360→			// Only supported on counter streams, and payload must be empty (if not coming from a source).
  5361→			var ok bool
  5362→			if incr, ok = getMessageIncr(hdr); !ok {
  5363→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5364→				apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrInvalidError()
  5365→				if canRespond {
  5366→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5367→					resp.Error = apiErr
  5368→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5369→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5370→				}
  5371→				return apiErr
  5372→			} else if incr != nil && !sourced {
  5373→				// Only do checks if the message isn't sourced. Otherwise, we need to store verbatim.
  5374→				if !allowMsgCounter {
  5375→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5376→					apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrDisabledError()
  5377→					if canRespond {
  5378→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5379→						resp.Error = apiErr
  5380→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5381→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5382→					}
  5383→					return apiErr
  5384→				} else if len(msg) > 0 {
  5385→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5386→					apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrPayloadError()
  5387→					if canRespond {
  5388→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5389→						resp.Error = apiErr
  5390→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5391→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5392→					}
  5393→					return apiErr
  5394→				} else {
  5395→					// Check for incompatible headers.
  5396→					var doErr bool
  5397→					if getRollup(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
  5398→						getExpectedStream(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
  5399→						getExpectedLastMsgId(hdr) != _EMPTY_ ||
  5400→						getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr) != _EMPTY_ {
  5401→						doErr = true
  5402→					} else if _, ok := getExpectedLastSeq(hdr); ok {
  5403→						doErr = true
  5404→					} else if _, ok := getExpectedLastSeqPerSubject(hdr); ok {
  5405→						doErr = true
  5406→					}
  5407→
  5408→					if doErr {
  5409→						mset.mu.Unlock()
  5410→						apiErr := NewJSMessageIncrInvalidError()
  5411→						if canRespond {
  5412→							resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5413→							resp.Error = apiErr
  5414→							b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5415→							outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5416→						}
  5417→						return apiErr
  5418→					}
  5419→				}
  5420→			}
  5421→
  5422→			// Expected stream.
  5423→			if sname := getExpectedStream(hdr); sname != _EMPTY_ && sname != name {
  5424→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5425→				if canRespond {
  5426→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5427→					resp.Error = NewJSStreamNotMatchError()
  5428→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5429→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5430→				}
  5431→				return errStreamMismatch
  5432→			}
  5433→
  5434→			// TTL'd messages are rejected entirely if TTLs are not enabled on the stream.
  5435→			// Shouldn't happen in clustered mode since we should have already caught this
  5436→			// in processClusteredInboundMsg, but needed here for non-clustered etc.
  5437→			if ttl, _ := getMessageTTL(hdr); !sourced && ttl != 0 && !mset.cfg.AllowMsgTTL {
  5438→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5439→				if canRespond {
  5440→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5441→					resp.Error = NewJSMessageTTLDisabledError()
  5442→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5443→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5444→				}
  5445→				return errMsgTTLDisabled
  5446→			}
  5447→
  5448→			// Expected last sequence per subject.
  5449→			if seq, exists := getExpectedLastSeqPerSubject(hdr); exists {
  5450→				// Allow override of the subject used for the check.
  5451→				seqSubj := subject
  5452→				if optSubj := getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr); optSubj != _EMPTY_ {
  5453→					seqSubj = optSubj
  5454→				}
  5455→
  5456→				// TODO(dlc) - We could make a new store func that does this all in one.
  5457→				var smv StoreMsg
  5458→				var fseq uint64
  5459→				sm, err := store.LoadLastMsg(seqSubj, &smv)
  5460→				if sm != nil {
  5461→					fseq = sm.seq
  5462→				}
  5463→				if err == ErrStoreMsgNotFound && seq == 0 {
  5464→					fseq, err = 0, nil
  5465→				}
  5466→				if err != nil || fseq != seq {
  5467→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5468→					if canRespond {
  5469→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5470→						resp.Error = NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceError(fseq)
  5471→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5472→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5473→					}
  5474→					return fmt.Errorf("last sequence by subject mismatch: %d vs %d", seq, fseq)
  5475→				}
  5476→			} else if getExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectForSubject(hdr) != _EMPTY_ {
  5477→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5478→				apiErr := NewJSStreamExpectedLastSeqPerSubjectInvalidError()
  5479→				if canRespond {
  5480→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5481→					resp.Error = apiErr
  5482→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5483→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5484→				}
  5485→				return apiErr
  5486→			}
  5487→
  5488→			// Expected last sequence.
  5489→			if seq, exists := getExpectedLastSeq(hdr); exists && seq != mset.lseq {
  5490→				mlseq := mset.lseq
  5491→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5492→				if canRespond {
  5493→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5494→					resp.Error = NewJSStreamWrongLastSequenceError(mlseq)
  5495→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5496→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5497→				}
  5498→				return fmt.Errorf("last sequence mismatch: %d vs %d", seq, mlseq)
  5499→			}
  5500→
  5501→			// Message scheduling.
  5502→			if schedule, ok := getMessageSchedule(hdr); !ok {
  5503→				mset.mu.Unlock()
  5504→				apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesPatternInvalidError()
  5505→				if !allowMsgSchedules {
  5506→					apiErr = NewJSMessageSchedulesDisabledError()
  5507→				}
  5508→				if canRespond {
  5509→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5510→					resp.Error = apiErr
  5511→					b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5512→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5513→				}
  5514→				return apiErr
  5515→			} else if !schedule.IsZero() {
  5516→				if !allowMsgSchedules {
  5517→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5518→					apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesDisabledError()
  5519→					if canRespond {
  5520→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5521→						resp.Error = apiErr
  5522→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5523→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5524→					}
  5525→					return apiErr
  5526→				} else if scheduleTtl, ok := getMessageScheduleTTL(hdr); !ok {
  5527→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5528→					apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesTTLInvalidError()
  5529→					if canRespond {
  5530→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5531→						resp.Error = apiErr
  5532→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5533→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5534→					}
  5535→					return apiErr
  5536→				} else if scheduleTtl != _EMPTY_ && !mset.cfg.AllowMsgTTL {
  5537→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5538→					if canRespond {
  5539→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5540→						resp.Error = NewJSMessageTTLDisabledError()
  5541→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5542→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5543→					}
  5544→					return errMsgTTLDisabled
  5545→				} else if scheduleTarget := getMessageScheduleTarget(hdr); scheduleTarget == _EMPTY_ ||
  5546→					!IsValidPublishSubject(scheduleTarget) || SubjectsCollide(scheduleTarget, subject) {
  5547→					mset.mu.Unlock()
  5548→					apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesTargetInvalidError()
  5549→					if canRespond {
  5550→						resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5551→						resp.Error = apiErr
  5552→						b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5553→						outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5554→					}
  5555→					return apiErr
  5556→				} else {
  5557→					match := slices.ContainsFunc(mset.cfg.Subjects, func(subj string) bool {
  5558→						return SubjectsCollide(subj, scheduleTarget)
  5559→					})
  5560→					if !match {
  5561→						mset.mu.Unlock()
  5562→						apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesTargetInvalidError()
  5563→						if canRespond {
  5564→							resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5565→							resp.Error = apiErr
  5566→							b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5567→							outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5568→						}
  5569→						return apiErr
  5570→					}
  5571→
  5572→					// Add a rollup sub header if it doesn't already exist.
  5573→					// Otherwise, it must exist already as a rollup on the subject.
  5574→					if rollup := getRollup(hdr); rollup == _EMPTY_ {

  5577→						mset.mu.Unlock()
  5578→						apiErr := NewJSMessageSchedulesRollupInvalidError()
  5579→						if canRespond {
  5580→							resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5581→							resp.Error = apiErr
  5582→							b, _ := json.Marshal(resp)
  5583→							outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5584→						}

  5586→					}
  5587→				}
  5588→			}
  5589→		}
  5590→

  5596→				var seq uint64
  5597→				mset.ddMu.Lock()

  5599→				if dde != nil {
  5600→					seq = dde.seq
  5601→				}

  5604→					mset.mu.Unlock()

  5606→						response := append(pubAck, strconv.FormatUint(dde.seq, 10)...)
  5607→						response = append(response, ",\"duplicate\": true}"...)
  5608→						outq.sendMsg(reply, response)
  5609→					}

  5611→				}
  5612→			}
  5613→		}
  5614→

  5619→				mset.mu.Unlock()

  5621→				if canRespond {
  5622→					resp.PubAck = &PubAck{Stream: name}
  5623→					resp.Error = NewJSStreamWrongLastMsgIDError(last)

  5625→					outq.sendMsg(reply, b)
  5626→				}

  5629→		}
